opportunity_title: 03/08 Math Starter Kit Packing
description_short: 03/08 Math Starter Kit Packing Session Welcome to a KiTNETiC volunteering opportunity! Volunteering Session Information: What? Volunteers will be hand-packing Math Starter Kits for elementary schools! When? March 8th from 4:00 - 5:15 PM Where?…
description_long: 03/08 Math Starter Kit Packing Session Welcome to a KiTNETiC volunteering opportunity! Volunteering Session Information: What? Volunteers will be hand-packing Math Starter Kits for elementary schools! When? March 8th from 4:00 - 5:15 PM Where? Liberty Recreation Center, Room A Location? 2601 Glencliff Dr, Plano, TX 75075 Volunteer Specific Information: Please make sure to arrive on time. The earliest our doors will open is 3:55 PM. Please DO NOT arrive earlier than 3:55 PM for preparation purposes. Please treat the materials and kits with care. Please bring any service-hours forms, so we can sign them off. More information will be released via email. Please check your email frequently! We will be capping the volunteer amount to 30. Each form counts as ONE participant. If you are in a group with 2+ people, each person in your group MUST fill out a separate form. Please share with friends and family! Who is KiTNETiC? KiTNETiC is an up-and-coming volunteering-based nonprofit that aims to solve real-world issues with hands-on kits. We reach out to various partners to deliver our kits to those in need. Questions? Connect with us via email at kitnetic.us@gmail.com.

opportunity_title: 10/17 National Bink-A-Thon - Plano, TX
description_short: As part of a grant from the MrBallen Foundation we are hosting a blanket making event across eight states. This is for the Plano, TX location. We will make blankets to give to children and teens who have been traumatized by violent crimes. Blankets…
description_long: As part of a grant from the MrBallen Foundation we are hosting a blanket making event across eight states. This is for the Plano, TX location. We will make blankets to give to children and teens who have been traumatized by violent crimes. Blankets are the fleece-tied type. We will provide fleece. Bring your open heart to help us make blankets to give to these kids as a reminder that they are not invisible or forgotten and that they matter! ALL ages are welcome. Children/teens need to have one adult per 4 children. This does count for community service hours through schools, service orgs and the court system. We are working on getting sponsors for lunch. We also need help setting up the day before - hauling items from storage to the event location, and clean up after. You do not need to be crafty. You need a warm heart, a pair of sharp scissors and that's it! If you want to help in other ways, feel free to contact Cheryl Bean.

opportunity_title: 20 SERVICE HOURS OPPORTUNITY -- HOST A BAKE SALE (READ DESCRIPTION)
description_short: Bake goods, sell goods, and receive 20 service hours! We are Caring for Moms, a 501(c)(3) organization that is dedicated to bettering the lives of mothers in-need. To fulfill the 20 hours, you must: Host a bake sale! (Can be with friends &…
description_long: Bake goods, sell goods, and receive 20 service hours! We are Caring for Moms, a 501(c)(3) organization that is dedicated to bettering the lives of mothers in-need. To fulfill the 20 hours, you must: Host a bake sale! (Can be with friends & family or by yourself) Gather at least USD$50 in sales Donate the funds to: https://midwivesforhaiti.org/donate/ Midwives for Haiti (the organization linked above is a credited 501(c)(3) organization, and the organization we are partnered with so all funds will go to them at this time) After we've received confirmation of your donation, send us (caringformomsus@gmail.com) an email with the name you donated under and we will be able to provide you with your 20 service hours! NOTE: These 20 service hours are contingent on you raising at least USD$50 in sales Thank you so much for joining our cause in reducing maternal health disparities globally ❤

opportunity_title: 2026 Dallas YMCA Turkey Trot - Thanksgiving Day 11/26/26
description_short: DALLAS YMCA TURKEY TROT Welcome to the Dallas YMCA Turkey Trot! For over 55 years, the YMCA Dallas Turkey Trot has been a Thanksgiving tradition for families, friends, and athletes of all levels. Join us this year for our annual 5K or 8-mile race,…
description_long: DALLAS YMCA TURKEY TROT Welcome to the Dallas YMCA Turkey Trot! For over 55 years, the YMCA Dallas Turkey Trot has been a Thanksgiving tradition for families, friends, and athletes of all levels. Join us this year for our annual 5K or 8-mile race, and experience the joy of giving back to your community while enjoying a fun-filled day of racing and celebration. A Race That Gives Back Our event is more than just a race; it's an opportunity to make a difference in the lives of those around us. All proceeds from the Turkey Trot go directly to the YMCA of Metropolitan Dallas, supporting programs and initiatives that help build strong, healthy, and connected communities.

opportunity_title: 2026 Dallas/ Fort Worth Autism Speaks Empower Walk & 5K
description_short: The Autism Speaks Empower Walk & 5K Run is more than just a walk or run — it's a celebration of community, inclusion, and progress. This exciting event combines everything you love about the traditional Autism Speaks Walk with the added energy…
description_long: The Autism Speaks Empower Walk & 5K Run is more than just a walk or run — it's a celebration of community, inclusion, and progress. This exciting event combines everything you love about the traditional Autism Speaks Walk with the added energy of a 5K run! Whether you're walking, running, or cheering from the sidelines, you'll be part of an inspiring experience filled with connection and purpose. The event features a wide range of activities for all ages and abilities, including: 🌐 Resource Fair A robust lineup of tools, services, and supports that span the entire autism lifespan. 🤝 Friendship Bracelet Station Create and share bracelets to celebrate unity and connection. 🦸‍♂️ Superhero Sprint A fun, fast-paced race for all ages— with capes! 📸 Photo Opportunities Capture unforgettable memories at our themed selfie stations. 🎉 And So Much More! Games, entertainment, community partners, and surprises around every corner. Join us as we take bold steps toward a world where all people with autism can reach their full potential. Come for the cause, stay for the community — and leave empowered. To SignUp: Please use this link - https://signup.com/go/pwooJrm

opportunity_title: 2026 Executive Leadership - North Texas Events
description_short: This group of individuals, alongside the Campaign Chair and American Diabetes Association (ADA) staff, will further the mission of ADA by raising awareness and funds through the 2026 signature campaigns and events. 2026 Events: Step Out Walk: North…
description_long: This group of individuals, alongside the Campaign Chair and American Diabetes Association (ADA) staff, will further the mission of ADA by raising awareness and funds through the 2026 signature campaigns and events. 2026 Events: Step Out Walk: North Texas - April 25 Tour de Cure: Texas - October 24 State of Diabetes - November TBD OUR MISSION: To prevent and cure diabetes and to improve the lives of all people affected by diabetes. ROLE & RESPONSIBILITIES OF ELT MEMBER INCLUDE: Acquisition & Retention Serve as the Executive Champion for your company and commit to a company fundraising team. Serve as company liaison to secure impactful sponsorship of your chosen event. In collaboration with ADA staff, assist in recruiting and engaging other like-minded leaders to invest and get involved with the American Diabetes Association Leverage your influence in the community to open doors to new companies & leaders. Benefits: Opportunity to take a leadership role at the only national organization of its kind in the diabetes space. Provides best-in-class employee engagement opportunities for your leadership and staff members. Networking opportunities with local businesses and communities impacted by diabetes. Knowledge gained or enhanced about the work that is transforming how we prevent and cure diabetes and improve the lives of all people affected by diabetes. Opportunity for you and your company to project a positive corporate image in the community and local media. Opportunity to make a significant impact on ADA’s goals to change the lives of those impacted by this disease.

opportunity_title: 2026 Komen Dallas Race for the Cure
description_short: The Susan G. Komen Dallas Race for the Cure is made possible by an amazing team of volunteers who help make this event a success. Our event day volunteers are individuals who are passionate about the fight against breast cancer and want to lend a…
description_long: The Susan G. Komen Dallas Race for the Cure is made possible by an amazing team of volunteers who help make this event a success. Our event day volunteers are individuals who are passionate about the fight against breast cancer and want to lend a hand to help us make the Race a success from start to finish! There are many ways to get involved at this year’s Race and ensure it’s a day to remember! Ways to Get Involved: Can you help us the weeks before the Race? Volunteer at a Packet Pick-up! Set up the site before the event Man various event tents: Hope Village, VIP, Mission We Remember, etc. Monitor the course or distribute water to runners MULTIPLE shifts available! Great opportunity for groups

opportunity_title: 2026 Planning Committee- North Texas Events
description_short: The Planning Committee’s main objective is to assist in planning and organizing the event day details. Planning committee volunteers will work to enhance the participant experience on event day. 2026 American Diabetes Association Events: Step Out…
description_long: The Planning Committee’s main objective is to assist in planning and organizing the event day details. Planning committee volunteers will work to enhance the participant experience on event day. 2026 American Diabetes Association Events: Step Out Walk: North Texas - April 25 Tour de Cure: Texas - October 24 State of Diabetes - November TBD Some examples of the subcommittees/responsibilities are: Food & Fun: plan fun games, arrange activities and entertainment throughout the day, help to secure in-kind breakfast, lunch, and grocery items for rest stops, entertainment on-site and on the route, work with local staff on the event program, including speakers VIP & Mission: work with team committee to ensure Red participants are invited to speak at the opening ceremony, plan a mission moment, secure a motivational speaker, and secure enhancements to the Living Red and Champion tents like massage therapists, specialty food items, decoration, ensure branding and mission is integrated at event Community Outreach: helps with outreach to Bike Shops or Clubs, manages training events, generates buzz via the digital community, helps promote the event within the community, including social media. Volunteer Recruitment: Reach out to volunteer groups, schools, community organizations to ensure all event day volunteer positions are covered. Actively communicate with volunteers before, during, and after the event.

opportunity_title: A Friendly Visit to a Hospice Patient - DeSoto
description_short: VITAS Healthcare is looking for kind and caring volunteers to help terminally ill patients and their families. When you make friendly visits, provide a listening ear, and offer the gift of friendship at a crucial time, you can make a real difference…
description_long: VITAS Healthcare is looking for kind and caring volunteers to help terminally ill patients and their families. When you make friendly visits, provide a listening ear, and offer the gift of friendship at a crucial time, you can make a real difference in another's life. Yours, too! Take advantage of numerous volunteer opportunities, including: Sitting with a patient while a caregiver takes a break Making friendly visits to patients and their families at home or in a nursing home VITAS provides free training and orientation for volunteers to help learn the skills required to be an outstanding hospice volunteer. For students, volunteering also strengthens your resume for a post-college career.

opportunity_title: A Hospice Patient Visit with your Certified Pet
description_short: VITAS Healthcare invites friendly and well-behaved pets to join the Paw Pals program, in which animals and their humans learn how to visit patients at the end of life. A registered VITAS Paw Pals visitor can offer comfort, bring back memories,…
description_long: VITAS Healthcare invites friendly and well-behaved pets to join the Paw Pals program, in which animals and their humans learn how to visit patients at the end of life. A registered VITAS Paw Pals visitor can offer comfort, bring back memories, encourage activity and provide unconditional love to VITAS patients. Your visits leave behind memories of you and your pet, and rekindle thoughts of other pets. Pet visits can sometimes reach a patient when no one else can. Our Paw Pals brighten the day for everyone when they visit. All kinds of pets are invited to join the VITAS Paw Pals Club. Paw Pals must be well-groomed, at least one-year old, and have up-to-date health records and vaccines.

opportunity_title: A Memory Bear Stitched Creates a Forever Friend for a Hospice Loved One
description_short: VITAS Healthcare is looking for volunteers who love to sew and want to share their skills with hospice patients and their families. A Memory Bear, sewn by a volunteer with compassion and love, is a forever friend who can ease a hurting heart after…
description_long: VITAS Healthcare is looking for volunteers who love to sew and want to share their skills with hospice patients and their families. A Memory Bear, sewn by a volunteer with compassion and love, is a forever friend who can ease a hurting heart after the death of a VITAS hospice patient. Sewn from a patient's favorite shirt, dress, sports jersey or work uniform, a Memory Bear can evoke the closeness of a loved one, bring a smile to someone in distress, or offer a sense of security to a child who feels alone after the loss of a parent or sibling. This is a great opportunity for you to give back to your community, use your creative skills, and strengthen your resume. By volunteering, you can ke the biggest difference in another's life. For more information about VITAS Volunteer Services or to apply, visit: https://www.vitas.com/volunteer.

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: A neighbor near Cedar Hill, TX would welcome a hand with lawn and yard care · Ref IWTMYL-MR0
description_short: A neighbor in Cedar Hill, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A neighbor in Cedar Hill, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming. Your kindness makes a real difference in keeping a neighbor's outdoor space beautiful. This is a home shared by a household of four, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Cedar Hill, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-MR0&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-MR0

About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Dallas, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. opportunity_title: ASSISTANT MARKETING MANAGER (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Reports To: Marketing Manager Assistant Marketing Manager supports the marketing function by planning, executing, and optimizing campaigns that drive brand awareness, user engagement, and community growth. This role bridges strategy and…
description_long: Reports To: Marketing Manager Assistant Marketing Manager supports the marketing function by planning, executing, and optimizing campaigns that drive brand awareness, user engagement, and community growth. This role bridges strategy and implementation — ensuring that all marketing efforts align with our brand’s mindful and conscious voice. He/She will work closely with the marketing, content, and partnership teams to develop integrated marketing plans, track performance, and coordinate outreach campaigns for practitioners, seekers, and wellness events. Key Responsibilities: Marketing Strategy & Campaigns Assist in developing and executing marketing campaigns across digital channels (social, email, paid, content, partnerships). Support the content and creative teams in crafting campaigns aligned with wellness themes, seasonal trends, and community needs. Coordinate event promotions , including conscious workshops, retreats, and marketplace features. Manage and track campaign calendars, deliverables, and performance reports. Digital Marketing & Analytics Collaborate on SEO, SEM, and social media optimization strategies. Support email marketing , including segmentation, automation, and A/B testing. Monitor campaign performance using analytics tools (Google Analytics, Meta Business Suite, GoHighLevel, etc.) and prepare regular performance dashboards Partnership & Community Outreach Assist in building and maintaining relationships with wellness practitioners , holistic brands , and event organizers . Coordinate cross-promotional collaborations , influencer campaigns, and affiliate marketing activities. Support community engagement initiatives — newsletters, webinars, and wellness events. Brand & Creative Coordination Ensure brand consistency across all communication materials. Collaborate with designers and content creators to produce visually aligned, meaningful marketing assets. Manage digital content updates on the platform and social media channels. Budget & Administration Support in budget tracking , vendor coordination, and campaign cost management. Research trends, competitors, and new opportunities within the wellness and conscious living market. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field. 2–4 years of experience in digital marketing, brand management, or community marketing (experience in wellness, lifestyle, or startup sectors preferred). Strong understanding of digital marketing tools (Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, Canva, GoHIghLevel, etc.). Excellent organizational and multitasking skills with attention to detail. Passion for wellness, holistic health, and conscious living is highly valued. Strong analytical and reporting skills. Excellent written and verbal communication abilities. Preferred Qualifications Experience working in a startup or online platform environment. Knowledge of SEO , content marketing , and social media strategy in the wellness space. Familiarity with wellness modalities (yoga, meditation, Ayurveda, Reiki, energy healing, etc.). Certification or coursework in digital marketing , holistic wellness , or brand storytelling . Experience managing community or influencer marketing programs.

opportunity_title: ATTENTION STUDENTS!! Volunteers Several Opportunities ARLINGTON, TX
description_short: Our hospice team is looking to expand our team. We are looking for caring, compassionate people who love to help others. We have several opportunities available between our 3 offices. We are looking for people for the following opportunities. Weekly…

We have several opportunities available between our 3 offices. We are looking for people for the following opportunities. Weekly phone calls to check on our families and report to supervisor if any needs are not met. We service a very large area. We would love to have you join our team. We believe in putting the patients first. Our entire staff is dedicated to giving the best end of life care to our patients and their families. That includes giving them a life with quality until that day comes.

opportunity_title: Accounts Executive
description_short: Job Title: Accounts Executive Department: Operations Department Location: Remote Employment Type: Volunteer / Unpaid position Commitment: 6-10 hours/week for 3–6 months About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is a non-profit…
description_long: Job Title: Accounts Executive Department: Operations Department Location: Remote Employment Type: Volunteer / Unpaid position Commitment: 6-10 hours/week for 3–6 months About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is a non-profit organization deeply committed to global orphanage support, guided by the Menzies 5 Keys of Needs. Our overarching mission is to lead the way in creating eco-regenerative conscious communities, serving as secure sanctuaries for orphans and vulnerable families. With a focus on addressing trauma and promoting holistic healing, we strive to foster environments where individuals can thrive and flourish. As part of the Menzies Mission Non-profit Organization, My Source Network stands as an innovative digital platform dedicated to holistic healing. By connecting individuals with a diverse network of practitioners and resources, we facilitate access to various healing modalities and transformative events. Our platform empowers users to embark on personalized wellness journeys, fostering growth and well-being in every individual we serve. Join our team and help us create exceptional digital experiences for our users! About the role The Accounts Executive will be responsible for managing the organization’s day-to-day accounting operations, ensuring that all financial records are accurate, organized, and up to date. This role plays a key part in maintaining the integrity of MSN’s financial data and supporting compliance, reporting, and audit requirements. The ideal candidate will have strong attention to detail, a solid understanding of bookkeeping principles, and experience working with accounting and payment platforms. Key Responsibilities Maintain accurate and timely records of all income, expenses, and financial adjustments. Reconcile Stripe payouts with bank statements and ensure accuracy across payment platforms. Support external Chartered Accountant (CA) or accounting partners with: Sales Tax filings (state-wise) OFAC, FinCEN, and 1099-K documentation via Stripe CCPA and GDPR compliance support Manage vendor and partner invoices, ensuring all payments are logged and properly categorized. Prepare monthly MIS (Management Information System) reports and financial summaries. Track and report event-related security deposits, including holds and releases. Assist with audit preparation and maintain compliance-related financial documentation. Qualifications Education: Bachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or Business Administration (equivalent to B.Com / M.Com). Experience: 1–3 years of experience in bookkeeping, financial operations, or within fintech/startup environments. Certifications: Pursuing or holding a CPA or CMA certification is a plus. Skills and Competencies Proficiency in QuickBooks Online , Xero , FreshBooks , and Google Sheets/Excel . Working knowledge of Stripe , PayPal , or Square , particularly in reconciling payouts and transaction fees. Basic understanding of Sales Tax (state-level) and 1099 reporting . Strong organizational and analytical skills with high attention to detail. Ability to maintain accurate records for compliance, reimbursements, and audits. Excellent communication and time management skills. Additional Information Familiarity with compliance documentation (OFAC, FinCEN, CCPA, GDPR) preferred. Why Join Us? Innovative Projects: Work on cutting-edge projects that make a real impact. Growth Opportunities: Opportunities for professional growth and development. Collaborative Environment: Join a team of passionate and talented professionals. Flexible Work: Enjoy a flexible work environment with options for remote work. Application Process To apply, review the job description and submit your resume, portfolio, and cover letter to hr@menziesmission.org . Ensure accurate contact information for application status updates. If selected, be prepared to discuss qualifications and align with the organization's mission. For questions, contact hr@menziesmission.org. We look forward to reviewing your application. Equal Opportunity Employer: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Accommodation for the application process is available upon request.

opportunity_title: Add Sunshine to Someone's Day! Become a Hospice Companion Volunteer!
description_short: Looking to make a difference in the life of someone in your community? Become an Elara Caring Hospice Volunteer! Our volunteers enhance the lives of those receiving our services by providing compassionate visits. If you have an interest in serving…
description_long: Looking to make a difference in the life of someone in your community? Become an Elara Caring Hospice Volunteer! Our volunteers enhance the lives of those receiving our services by providing compassionate visits. If you have an interest in serving persons facing end of life issues and being a part of a fantastic hospice team, we want to hear from you! Please consider giving a little time each week to a hospice patient who would enjoy social visits. Read a book with them, help write a letter to a friend, share memories, listen. Individuals receiving hospice care appreciate the additional care and support of a volunteer. Thank you!

opportunity_title: Addison, TX: Host a Foreign Exchange Student Addison, TX and surrounding areas!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Administrative Assistance Needed to Support Hospice Organization
description_short: Freedom Hospice is looking for dependable administrative volunteers to work in our office in North Richland Hills once or twice a week for several hours on an ongoing basis. Our hospice is growing and we need volunteers to help support our hospice…
description_long: Freedom Hospice is looking for dependable administrative volunteers to work in our office in North Richland Hills once or twice a week for several hours on an ongoing basis. Our hospice is growing and we need volunteers to help support our hospice team with admit packets, filing, paperwork, answering phones, mailings and various other projects. Please contact Jon Glover, Director of Benevolent Services for more information.

opportunity_title: Administrative Externship
description_short: Job Description: As a Hospice Administrative Volunteer, you will play a crucial role in supporting the administrative functions of our hospice organization. This role is an excellent opportunity for college students looking to gain real-world…
description_long: Job Description: As a Hospice Administrative Volunteer, you will play a crucial role in supporting the administrative functions of our hospice organization. This role is an excellent opportunity for college students looking to gain real-world experience in a healthcare setting while contributing to a mission of compassion and care. You will work closely with our administrative team to help ensure the smooth operation of our hospice services. Tasks may include: Answering phones Filing Scanning Faxing Data entry Mailings Arts and craft projects Key Responsibilities: Phone and email communication: Handle incoming calls and emails, providing courteous and helpful responses, and directing inquiries to the appropriate staff members. Filing and organization: Maintain organized and easily accessible filing systems for administrative documents, ensuring efficiency in record retrieval. Administrative tasks: Perform general office tasks such as photocopying, scanning, faxing, and maintaining office supplies. Data entry and record keeping: Assist in maintaining and updating patient records, volunteer logs, and other essential documents accurately and confidentially. Assist with mailings: Prepare and send out mailings, including thank-you letters, newsletters, and informational materials. Assist with projects: Assist volunteer coordinator with special projects for our patients such as making cards, writing letters, putting gifts together, etc. Support staff meetings: Attend staff meetings and assist in preparing agendas, taking minutes, and distributing meeting materials. Event support: Assist in planning and organizing hospice events, including fundraisers, awareness campaigns, and volunteer appreciation events. Requirements: Minimum age: 16 In-office interview In-office orientation/training Valid driver's license/ID Qualifications: A commitment to the mission and values of the hospice organization. Strong organizational and communication skills. Attention to detail and the ability to handle confidential information. Proficiency in basic computer skills, including word processing and data entry. Friendly and compassionate demeanor. Reliable and punctual. Ability to work collaboratively in a team and take direction from staff members. Time Commitment: We are very flexible. Volunteers are expected to commit to a minimum of 1 hour per week. This opportunity will help develop skills such as: Compassion Understanding patient care Understanding how a hospice business operates. Working in an office setting Verbal and written communication Basic computer skills How This Opportunity Can Help College Students: Professional experience: Gain practical experience in an administrative role within a healthcare organization, enhancing your resume and career prospects. Skill development: Develop valuable skills in data management, communication, and organization that are transferable to various professions. Networking: Connect with professionals in the healthcare field and build a network that can be beneficial for future job opportunities. Community engagement: Make a meaningful contribution to your local community and experience the rewards of giving back. Flexible schedule: This volunteer opportunity allows for flexible scheduling, making it manageable alongside college coursework and other commitments. Mentorship: Learn from experienced administrators and professionals who can provide guidance and mentorship as you consider your career path. College level courses that may be related to this position: Nursing Administrative work Compensation and Benefits Volunteer retention Gain knowledge of healthcare and how a hospice is run. Attain credit hours (if required by your instructors and/or institution) Work closely with patients, nurses, social workers, hospice staff, etc. The opportunity to make a meaningful impact in the lives of hospice patients and their families. Gain valuable experience in end-of-life care. Ongoing training and support to enhance your skills and knowledge. A supportive and compassionate team environment. If you or someone you know might be interested, please contact Divinity Hospice at 817-438-7463 or tazi@divinityhospice.net.

opportunity_title: Administrative Hospice Volunteer in Office
description_short: Build your administrative skills while assisting in the office. Know the insights of an hospice office and learn basic computer skills. We will provide you on hand training and guidance throughout your experience. This opportunity will be a great…
description_long: Build your administrative skills while assisting in the office. Know the insights of an hospice office and learn basic computer skills. We will provide you on hand training and guidance throughout your experience. This opportunity will be a great resume builder! Start as soon as possible. YOU build your own schedule. Assist weekly or even monthly. To Get Started: Application Phone Interview Background Check Orientation Administrative Duties: Assembling marketing and admission packets, etc. Assisting with filing, typing, and answering the phones Organizing newsletter mailing for Bereavement Services Assembling workshop manual and guide books We provide full training in Hospice philosophy and volunteer duties. Orientation and on-going training provided! Background and reference check required. We will work with your schedule! We have many ways for volunteers to engage and change the lives of our patients, follow our Volunteer Match page or visit our website www.trinityhospice.com/volunteers

opportunity_title: Administrative Support Specialist
description_short: TWCUYC is seeking an organized volunteer to provide administrative support to our leadership team. This role helps ensure programs, volunteers, and participant services operate efficiently. What You’ll Do Organize digital files and documents Assist…
description_long: TWCUYC is seeking an organized volunteer to provide administrative support to our leadership team. This role helps ensure programs, volunteers, and participant services operate efficiently. What You’ll Do Organize digital files and documents Assist with data entry and record keeping Update spreadsheets and tracking systems Schedule meetings and appointments Assist with correspondence and follow up tasks Support special projects and organizational initiatives What We’re Looking For Strong attention to detail Excellent organizational skills Proficiency with Google Workspace or Microsoft Office Ability to maintain confidentiality Administrative experience is preferred but not required Time Commitment Minimum of 5 hours per week.

opportunity_title: Administrative Volunteer
description_short: Homage Hospice is dedicated to providing care and comfort to terminally ill patients and their families in their homes or wherever home may be. We provide the highest quality of care possible by employing a team of healthcare professionals to…
description_long: Homage Hospice is dedicated to providing care and comfort to terminally ill patients and their families in their homes or wherever home may be. We provide the highest quality of care possible by employing a team of healthcare professionals to administer comfort and supportive care to individuals. Homage Hospice strives to meet the physical, emotional, spiritual and social needs of patients and family members who share the emotional trauma of a terminal illness. Homage Hospice is committed to educate the community and increase awareness of the hospice concept of care. Volunteers are a vital part of Homage Hospice, providing a special kind of care and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening ear to patients and families, assist with daily household activities for patients, provide grief support, serve as a companion to loved ones or share their personal expertise to help promote hospice care, the time given to Homage Hospice families or in our local office by our volunteers can be the most rewarding experiences of their lives. It takes a very special, caring person to give of themselves as unselfishly as our Homage Hospice patient care and administrative volunteers do. If you'd like to be a part of the Homage Hospice Volunteer Team, here are some examples of the activities you can assist with. * reading to a patient, or holding a hand * staying with a patient while their family members take a needed break or keep an appointment * shopping or doing errands for a patient * listening * helping patients write or tell their life stories * light household chores * providing help with makeup and hair * Veterans are always needed to share their stories with other veterans at bed side * working on crafts or other projects * clerical and other office duties If you would like to volunteer for Homage Hospice, please follow these brief steps; * Step 1. Respond to this volunteer request via email * Step 2. Wait and our Volunteer Coordinator will contact you with-in 24-48hrs.

description_short: Volunteers get to make their own schedule! Administrative volunteers may assist with ancillary and office activities that support direct patient care activities which could include (but are not restricted to): Answering phones Assisting with patient…
description_long: Volunteers get to make their own schedule! Administrative volunteers may assist with ancillary and office activities that support direct patient care activities which could include (but are not restricted to): Answering phones Assisting with patient and family mailings Filing Assisting the Bereavement and Volunteer programs with administrative tasks as needed Data entry

opportunity_title: Adopt a Lawn
description_short: Adopt A Lawn is general lawn care of a senior citizen's yard during the growing season. This is a great project for individuals, families, church groups, companies or teens. Adopt a Lawn for the growing season or get a group together and help up mow…
description_long: Adopt A Lawn is general lawn care of a senior citizen's yard during the growing season. This is a great project for individuals, families, church groups, companies or teens. Adopt a Lawn for the growing season or get a group together and help up mow for a day! Help a Senior Citizen maintain their lawn and their pride by providing support mowing, weeding and edging. We also have opportunities for hedge and tree trimming. Many of our clients are disabled and cannot afford to have someone maintain their lawn. They do not want their home to become the neighborhood eye sore and this simple act of service goes a long way by enabling them to continue living independently in their own home. Yards are typically mowed 2-3 times per month. Frequency is dictated by the growth of the lawn. This opportunity is open to teenagers with adult supervision. Volunteers may mow any day of the week that is convenient for them. All adult volunteers must pass a criminal background check. Clients are located in one of the following 11 cities: Keller, Southlake, Colleyville, Grapevine, Bedford, Hurst, Euless, North Richland Hills, Richland Hills, Watauga, and Haltom City.

opportunity_title: Adoption Counselor (REMOTE)
description_short: Adoption Counselor Remote Volunteer Position Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the…
description_long: Adoption Counselor Remote Volunteer Position Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As an adoption counselor, you’ll play a vital role on the adoption team by reviewing adoption applications and guiding applicants through the adoption process. Adoption Counselors help the pets in the DPA foster program find homes of their own, and help match make families to their new furry best friends. Team: Adoption Team Location Remote/Virtual: Remote/Virtual - Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST timezone. Time Commitment: Between 6-15 Hours a week Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. After one year of service we issue a professional letter of recommendation from our Director of Volunteer Management and Human Resources, as well as a LinkedIn recommendation(s) as applicable. Reports to: Adoption Counselor Lead Position Responsibilities: Review, approve, and handle adoption applications for assigned pets Communicate with applicants and foster parents Partner with the Foster team to help match applicants with pets that fit their lifestyles Manage the contract, payment, and adoption finalization process Assisting with any other duties needed to help drive Dallas Pets Alive!’s mission and support our values Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Strong sense of organization Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device The following activities are required: The ability to communicate information and ideas so others will understand. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values. Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: DPA! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and the communities we serve. We embrace challenges and lead with positive intent, welcoming all people who have a caring and compassionate dedication to animal welfare. Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. The golden rule is expected: Treat all living beings as you would like to be treated. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org. Watch our video to learn more: Dallas Pets Alive!

opportunity_title: Adoption Counselor (REMOTE)
description_short: Adoption Counselor Remote Volunteer Position Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the…
description_long: Adoption Counselor Remote Volunteer Position Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As an adoption counselor, you’ll play a vital role on the adoption team by reviewing adoption applications and guiding applicants through the adoption process. Adoption Counselors help the pets in the DPA foster program find homes of their own, and help match make families to their new furry best friends. Team: Adoption Team Location Remote/Virtual: Remote/Virtual - Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST timezone. Time Commitment: Between 6-15 Hours a week Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. After one year of service we issue a professional letter of recommendation from our Director of Volunteer Management and Human Resources, as well as a LinkedIn recommendation(s) as applicable. Reports to: Adoption Counselor Lead Position Responsibilities: Review, approve, and handle adoption applications for assigned pets Communicate with applicants and foster parents Partner with the Foster team to help match applicants with pets that fit their lifestyles Manage the contract, payment, and adoption finalization process Assisting with any other duties needed to help drive Dallas Pets Alive!’s mission and support our values Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Strong sense of organization Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device The following activities are required: The ability to communicate information and ideas so others will understand. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values. Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: DPA! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and the communities we serve. We embrace challenges and lead with positive intent, welcoming all people who have a caring and compassionate dedication to animal welfare. Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. The golden rule is expected: Treat all living beings as you would like to be treated. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org. Watch our video to learn more: Dallas Pets Alive!

opportunity_title: Adoption Counselors Needed
description_short: Help cats find new forever homes at our weekly adoption events! New Beginnings Cat Rescue is a non-profit organization that rescues cats and kittens from local shelters when they are running out of time. We hold adoption events at the Garland…
description_long: Help cats find new forever homes at our weekly adoption events! New Beginnings Cat Rescue is a non-profit organization that rescues cats and kittens from local shelters when they are running out of time. We hold adoption events at the Garland PetSmart every Saturday and Sunday. Volunteer 5+ hours one or more Saturdays/Sundays each month to assist at adoptions. Duties involve setting up tables, assembling cages, maintaining cages throughout the day, playing with the cats, and educating the public about the importance of spay/neuter. Adoptions are held every Saturday from 11am to 4pm and Sunday from 12pm to 4pm, but volunteers arrive 30 minutes early to set up and stay 30 minutes after to clean up. During the event, Counselors interview each potential adopter to help them find the right cat for their lifestyle/family. If the potential adopter meets all of the adoption requirements, the counselor guides the adopter in paperwork completion and makes arrangements for the cat to be taken home. Training is required for this position and you will work with a more experienced adoption counselor until you are comfortable working alone. Adoption Counselors also maintain the cages throughout the shift and play with the cats during slow times. If you live in the Garland or DFW area and would like to help with this rewarding task, please consider volunteering with New Beginnings Cat Rescue. To find out more about us and to submit a volunteer application, please see our website at: https://nbcrtx.org/volunteer-application

opportunity_title: Adult Mentorship | Empower Young Girls to Be Confident Leaders!
description_short: Lemon Aide Society empowers girls to lead by mentoring them as they plan and execute service projects in our community. We are looking for women willing to be the leaders and mentors for our Lemon Aides. Adult Mentors are female leaders who have…
description_long: Lemon Aide Society empowers girls to lead by mentoring them as they plan and execute service projects in our community. We are looking for women willing to be the leaders and mentors for our Lemon Aides. Adult Mentors are female leaders who have experience working with girls. They embody the Lemon Aide Society’s core beliefs and recognize that their influence can change the trajectory of a girl's life. Adult Mentors focus on relationship building - offering guidance and support as our Lemon Aides and Teen Mentors learn about themselves, their community, and how they can make a difference in the world. Adult mentors will lead a group in our monthly program, and the time commitment is approximately 5 hours a month throughout the school year . Responsibilities: Provide mentorship to Lemon Aides and Teen Mentors Attend monthly group meetings and assist girls in implementing the Lemon Aide Society’s leadership and social justice curriculum Facilitate leadership training sessions and guide project leaders through the stages of project development and evaluation Commitment Details: Adult Mentors lead a group of girls in a monthly meeting (in person) as well as prepare girls to lead the service project each month (typically on Zoom). Depending on the age of the group, expected volunteer time is approximately 5 hours/month. All supplies and curriculum provided. Meetings are held once a month on a weekend day. Approximately 2 hours. Mentoring session are held at your convenience (and the girls' availability) primarily on zoom. These are usually weekday evenings or weekends. Sessions are typically about 45 minutes. Learn more at our website: https://www.lemonaidesociety.org/adult-mentorship

description_short: Craft the Messages That Connect Our Community TEDxSouthlake is looking for an Adult Volunteer Content Lead to oversee email marketing and website messaging. This role will help shape how we communicate with our audience through newsletters,…
description_long: Craft the Messages That Connect Our Community TEDxSouthlake is looking for an Adult Volunteer Content Lead to oversee email marketing and website messaging. This role will help shape how we communicate with our audience through newsletters, automated emails, and website content. You’ll work closely with the marketing team to ensure every message reflects the spirit and voice of TEDxSouthlake. Responsibilities: Manage and support email campaigns using platforms like Mailchimp Help write and edit email newsletters, announcements, and website copy Maintain and organize the email list and audience segments Monitor email performance (open rates, click rates, conversions) Coordinate messaging across email, website, and marketing campaigns Ideal Candidate: A strong communicator with experience in email marketing, content writing, or digital communications. Familiarity with Mailchimp, email automation, or audience segmentation is a plus. Location : Remote, but the desire to meet in person for the event in Southlake, TX, in November 2026. Time Commitment : Approximately 2-5 hours/week, including virtual monthly meetings. Apply Now: Send your resume and a brief introduction to info@TEDxSouthlake.com

opportunity_title: Advocate for Hospice Patients by making Check-in Phone Calls
description_short: VITAS Healthcare is looking for caring individuals to share their voice and time as Proactive Caller Volunteers to help terminally ill patients and their families. If you enjoy talking on the phone and have time to make check-in calls, you may be a…
description_long: VITAS Healthcare is looking for caring individuals to share their voice and time as Proactive Caller Volunteers to help terminally ill patients and their families. If you enjoy talking on the phone and have time to make check-in calls, you may be a perfect Proactive Caller Volunteer. Our Proactive volunteers make "check-in" phone calls to our hospice patients and their family members. You can work from the comfort of your own home, apartment or dorm room or come into the VITAS office. You'll call patients at home, asking how they are doing, checking on needed supplies, listening and identifying any service concerns. You'll relay any concerns back to the office. VITAS provides a free volunteer orientation and training to help you learn the skills required to be an outstanding volunteer caller. Volunteering can be personally rewarding as well as strengthen your resume for future opportunities. For more information about VITAS Volunteer Services, visit https://www.vitas.com/volunteer.

description_short: Make a Difference from Anywhere—Become a Proactive Caller Volunteer with VITAS Do you enjoy meaningful conversations and want to make a positive impact? VITAS is seeking compassionate individuals to serve as Proactive Caller Volunteers , offering…
description_long: Make a Difference from Anywhere—Become a Proactive Caller Volunteer with VITAS Do you enjoy meaningful conversations and want to make a positive impact? Learn more about VITAS: www.vitas.com/volunteer

opportunity_title: Aftercare Administrative Assistant (Hybrid)
description_short: Aftercare Administrative Assistant Hybrid Volunteer Opportunity Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability and provide a healthy…
description_long: Aftercare Administrative Assistant Hybrid Volunteer Opportunity Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability and provide a healthy and loving environment for their pet. Dogs Matter is the only non-profit agency in Texas offering free temporary foster care placement and aftercare support services specifically for pets of people with addictions receiving substance abuse treatment. Dogs Matter has become a department of Dallas Pets Alive (DPA). With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to Dogs Matter, you directly impact our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dogs Matter visit https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ Are you an organized multitasker with a caring heart and a knack for making things run smoothly? As our Aftercare Administrative Associate, you'll support clients transitioning from treatment by managing essential medical admin tasks, processing orders for food and supplies, and working closely with our recovery coaches. You'll be the reliable behind-the-scenes helper whose work makes a major difference in the next chapter in our clients’ journeys. About Dogs Matter: https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ About Dallas Pets Alive : https://dallaspetsalive.org Team: Dogs Matter Location: Hybrid, local to the Dallas-Fort Worth area Time Commitment: 2-5 hours a week Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: Lead Recovery Coach and/or Director of Operations Works with: This role requires collaboration with our clients, Recovery Coaches, and the Dogs Matter team. Primary Responsibilities: Medical Admin Assistance : Maintain and update records, schedule follow‑up appointments, and manage healthcare-related correspondence. Ensure accuracy and confidentiality in all documentation. Order Processing & Inventory Support: Process orders for essential food and supplies—tracking inventory, placing orders, and ensuring timely deliveries to help clients stay focused on their recovery. Collaboration with Recovery Coach: Work in tandem with the recovery coach to coordinate care tasks and support smooth client transitions. Administrative Organization: Help manage schedules, track supplies, handle administrative errands, and support reporting or documentation needs to keep the aftercare program running smoothly. Client Interaction Support: Field basic client inquiries, provide information on food/supply delivery schedules, and assist with administrative needs that help reinforce a caring, supportive environment. Qualifications/Skills: Handle confidential and sensitive information with professionalism, discretion, and integrity Passionate about the Dogs Matter mission and supporting clients with empathy, respect, and grace Reliable and responsive, with the ability to respond to client needs within 48 hours during scheduled hours Collaborative, supportive, and team-oriented Excellent verbal and written communication skills Prior experience in medical admin, aftercare coordination, client support, or familiarity with recovery environments is a plus! Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Must be at least 18 years of age What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Allen, TX: a neighbor could use a hand with yard work · Ref IWTMYL-K5H
description_short: In Allen, TX, a lawn has become difficult to keep up with, and a nearby volunteer could provide meaningful relief with a short, straightforward visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming. A little bit of your time makes a…
description_long: In Allen, TX, a lawn has become difficult to keep up with, and a nearby volunteer could provide meaningful relief with a short, straightforward visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming. A little bit of your time makes a big difference in a neighbor's day. This is a home shared by a household of two, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. A tidy, well-kept yard makes a visible difference the same day and helps a neighbor keep their home comfortable and cared for. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Allen, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-K5H&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-K5H

opportunity_title: Ambassadors of Smiles
description_short: Want to make a positive difference in someone's life with just your smile or the holding of someone's hand? It's really that easy to brighten the lives of hospice patients! Terminally ill patients may have a limited life expectancy, but they want to…
description_long: Want to make a positive difference in someone's life with just your smile or the holding of someone's hand? It's really that easy to brighten the lives of hospice patients! Terminally ill patients may have a limited life expectancy, but they want to feel connected to people and a part of life for as long as possible. Freedom Hospice is looking for caring and compassionate people to visit patients in their homes and nursing facilities to provide a comforting smile, and a caring presence. Volunteers can engage patients with interesting conversation, playing of card/board games, arts & crafts, reading, etc. Volunteers participate in an intensive on-line training program that will ensure that he/she feels confident and ready to interact with our patients. Patient assignments are tailored to match volunteer's geographic preferences and schedule. To Get Started Right Away, Please go to our Volunteer Application at https://www.volgistics.com/ex/portal.dll/ap?ap=1126865281

description_short: Somewhere in Grand Prairie, TX, an older neighbor is looking out at a yard that has become harder to keep up with — and a nearby volunteer could offer real, practical help. This is a simple request: a hand keeping the lawn tidy, safe, and easy to…
description_long: Somewhere in Grand Prairie, TX, an older neighbor is looking out at a yard that has become harder to keep up with — and a nearby volunteer could offer real, practical help. This is a simple request: a hand keeping the lawn tidy, safe, and easy to move around. A neighbor in Grand Prairie could use a hand with their yard. This neighbor reached out through our voicemail helpline; the summary here was generated from what they shared on that call using AI, and reviewed to protect their privacy before posting. Specifically, this opportunity may include lawn and yard help. Equipment availability is still being confirmed. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Keeping the grass down and the paths clear does more than tidy things up — it means safer footing on the way to the mailbox or the car and one less thing weighing on someone who wants to stay comfortable and independent at home. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Grand Prairie, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service.
