opportunity_title: Animal Intake Admin
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As our Animal Intake Admin you’ll play an important role in intaking animals into the foster program and this role will allow you to play a major part in ensuring animals are intaked into the program and ready to rock and roll! Team: Foster Team Reports to : Foster Director Works with : Location : Remote/Virtual - Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST timezone. Time Commitment: Between 6-15 Hours a week Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. After one year of service we issue a professional letter of recommendation from our Director of Volunteer Management and Human Resources, as well as a LinkedIn recommendation(s) as applicable. Position Responsibilities: Monitoring the “new pulls” Slack channel for new pull requests Inputting the animal into ShelterLuv Using templates to send new pull emails to fosters which introduce them to the team, inform them of logistics for picking up their new foster animal, and provide information about foster caretaking Assisting with any other duties needed to help drive Dallas Pets Alive!’s mission Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device The following activities are required: Ability to operate a computer and/or mobile device. The ability to communicate information and ideas so others will understand. What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: DPA! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and the communities we serve. We embrace challenges and lead with positive intent, welcoming all people who have a caring and compassionate dedication to animal welfare. Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. The golden rule is expected: Treat all living beings as you would like to be treated. DPA! has a zero tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Animation Program Lead (with tech ability)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: Animation Program Lead (Volunteer – Technical Focus) We are seeking an experienced and organized Animation Program Lead with technical ability to support and guide the launch of our Animation Program. This volunteer role plays a central part in coordinating creative efforts, supporting technical workflows, and ensuring the program develops smoothly during its startup phase. This is a 6-9 month minimum commitment, with the opportunity to renew. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys combining leadership, technology, and creative collaboration in a mission-driven environment. Key Responsibilities: Provide leadership and coordination for the animation program during its startup phase. Support and guide animators and script writers on technical and workflow-related matters. Help establish tools, processes, and timelines for animation and content production. Collaborate with internal teams to align creative output with program goals and organizational messaging. Review and provide feedback on animated content and scripts to ensure quality and consistency. Support basic troubleshooting related to animation or AI-assisted tools as needed. Track progress across projects and help ensure deadlines are met. Contribute to documentation and process improvements for future program cycles. Qualifications: Experience in animation, digital media, or creative technology. Comfort with technical tools and workflows, including AI-assisted creative tools. Strong organizational, communication, and leadership skills. Ability to coordinate across creative and non-creative team members. Comfortable working independently in a remote environment. Prior experience leading projects or creative teams is an asset. Interest in mission-driven, educational, or mental health–focused content is strongly valued. Interest in astrology and unconventional/spiritual frameworks as tools for storytelling and character development.

opportunity_title: Animators (with AI experience)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking creative and motivated Animators with AI experience to support the launch of our Animation Program. This volunteer role is part of a program startup phase, focused on building engaging animated content that supports our mission, storytelling, and educational initiatives. This is a 6-9 month minimum commitment, with the opportunity to renew. This is a great opportunity for individuals who enjoy blending creativity with emerging technology and want hands-on experience contributing to a growing, mission-driven program. Key Responsibilities: Create short-form animated content aligned with program goals and organizational messaging. Us e AI-assisted animation tools to support concept development, storyboarding, or production. Collaborate with internal teams to translate ideas, scripts, or educational concepts into engaging visuals. Support the development of animation styles, templates, and workflows for the program. Assist with revisions and refinements based on feedback. Ensure content is accessible, appropriate, and aligned with organizational values. Maintain organized files and documentation related to animation projects. Contribute creative ideas to help shape and grow the animation program during its startup phase. Qualifications: Experience with animation (2D, motion graphics, or similar formats). Familiarity with AI-powered creative tools (e.g., AI-assisted animation, generative visuals, or workflow tools). Creative storytelling skills with strong attention to detail. Ability to work independently while collaborating with a remote team. Comfortable receiving and implementing feedback. Interest in mission-driven, educational, or mental health–focused content is a plus. Interest in astrology and unconventional/spiritual frameworks as tools for storytelling and character development.

opportunity_title: Apartment Life Coordinator (Multiple Locations & Flexible Opportunities)
description_short: Are you passionate about building community and making a meaningful impact where you live? Apartment Life (AL) is looking for Coordinators to serve in apartment communities across the country. Events and Ministry Coordinators get to create and…
description_long: Are you passionate about building community and making a meaningful impact where you live? Apartment Life (AL) is looking for Coordinators to serve in apartment communities across the country. Events and Ministry Coordinators get to create and provide an excellent Apartment Life (AL) experience to residents and staff in an apartment community! Coordinators are passionate about the vision and mission of AL and combine this passion with skill to plan consistently excellent and irresistible events, provide exceptional personal communication, and offer acts of genuine care and radical hospitality for those in their scope of service- ultimately impacting the community and the lives of apartment residents. We are seeking mission-minded, highly relational, and responsible coordinators who enjoy meeting new people, planning social activities, and helping people experience God's unconditional love. Coordinators get to partake in doing "business as a ministry" by faithfully weaving together excellence in business with loving their neighbors well. What You’ll Do: Plan and host consistent, engaging community events Build relationships with residents and property staff Provide care, support, and hospitality to those in your community Serve as a positive and encouraging presence within the apartment community Who We’re Looking For: Individuals or couples who enjoy connecting with others Organized, proactive, and relational Passionate about serving and building community Able to manage responsibilities with consistency and excellence What You’ll Receive: Reduced/complimentary housing or hourly compensation (varies by community and opportunity) Training and ongoing support from Apartment Life A meaningful opportunity to impact lives and build community 👉 Interested in learning more or applying? Visit our landing page to explore available opportunities in your location and apply: https://ministryhire.com/company-jobs/apartment-life

opportunity_title: Apply to Become A Part of the KiTNETiC Team! (LEADERSHIP OPPORTUNITY)
description_short: Are you looking for a leadership position? Passionate about creating change in your community? Experienced in social media or outreach management? Then, we're looking for you! KiTNETiC is looking for ambitious individuals 13+ years old to be a part…
description_long: Are you looking for a leadership position? Passionate about creating change in your community? Experienced in social media or outreach management? Then, we're looking for you! KiTNETiC is looking for ambitious individuals 13+ years old to be a part of our team. REQUIREMENTS AND MORE INFORMATION BELOW! Who is KiTNETiC? KiTNETiC is a youth-led nonprofit that unites volunteers to pack supply and comfort kits for various community causes such as education and healthcare. We host monthly packing sessions and reach out to community partners. To date, we have packed around 19,000 kits across five states. What kind of leadership positions do we have available? Please remember that no position is paid. These positions serve as leadership opportunities for young individuals.* Bolded positions are positions in high demand! Social Media Manager - Manage our social media and photography. Event Planner - Plan packing sessions and outreach events. Kit Scientist Design kits that our organization packs and distributes to communities in need. Expansion - Help expand our organization across the nation. Treasurer - Manage our finances and yearly budgets. Graphic Designer - Design social media and event posts. For more information, please visit this document: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HnrwI8wastPwtf_R4cXvQVDu0XVH2yrrhjwurWTZBI4/edit?usp=sharing REQUIREMENTS! - MUST BE MET Applicants must be based in the DFW area. Applicants must be at least in the eighth grade. Applicants MUST fill out the official application form before August 1st @ 11:59 PM CST: https://forms.gle/wA8AKQLiNWpvZnLU8 Applicants should expect to dedicate at least an hour to KiTNETiC operations each week and attend monthly meetings. For more information, please email kitnetic.us@gmail.com.

opportunity_title: Arlington Hospice Patient/ Caregiver Companion
description_short: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily…
description_long: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily responsibilities and stress. Making a difference as a hospice volunteer begins with the desire to help the dying and their families. Hospice volunteers also provide a number of services such as bereavement support and help in the office. 16 yrs and up for Administrative Volunteer and 18 yrs and Older for Patient Support Volunteer. I am looking forward to meeting you very soon! Call with any questions or email me at jwilliams@newcenturyhospice.com. Jennifer Williams Volunteer Coordinator 817-246-9100

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Arlington, TX - Hospice Volunteer
description_short: Looking for Volunteers to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving…
description_long: Looking for Volunteers to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: Arlington, TX: Host an Exchange Student-Volunteer families needed in Arlington area!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Arts and Crafts | Volunteer with Lifted Hospice | Grapevine/HEB
description_short: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Our Memory Care Patients love to do arts and crafts and we're looking for individuals to volunteer their time…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Our Memory Care Patients love to do arts and crafts and we're looking for individuals to volunteer their time assisting with crafts. We aren't looking for Picasso; just people passionate about helping brighten someone's day and a willingness to sit with our patients! Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: Spend quality time with elderly patients in care facilities assisting with arts and crafts. Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life. Contact Hannah Morris at hannah.morris@liftedhospice.com or 214-656-8402 for more information.

opportunity_title: Assist Fund Raising Team with Event Preparation for Lone Star Bark Happening on May 3, 2026
description_short: Assisting Fund-Raising Team with preparations for Lone Star Bark held on May 3, 2026. Come join our Fund-Raising Team for an exciting fun filled time helping with preparation for our annual Lone Star Bark Event, happening on Sunday, May 3, 2026!…
description_long: Assisting Fund-Raising Team with preparations for Lone Star Bark held on May 3, 2026. Come join our Fund-Raising Team for an exciting fun filled time helping with preparation for our annual Lone Star Bark Event, happening on Sunday, May 3, 2026! Preparation includes making gift baskets, stuffing bags and other fun preparation activities!

opportunity_title: Assist a Hospice Caregiver with a Needed Break (In Homes Around Metroplex)
description_short: VITAS Healthcare is looking for kind and caring people to help terminally ill patients and their families - just by being there. Many of our patients live at home with family members or friends who rarely get the opportunity to leave the house…
description_long: VITAS Healthcare is looking for kind and caring people to help terminally ill patients and their families - just by being there. Many of our patients live at home with family members or friends who rarely get the opportunity to leave the house because they are devoted to caring for their ill loved ones. Relief care (sitting with a patient while the caregiver takes a break) is one of our biggest volunteer needs. For caregivers to have the opportunity to run an errand, go see a movie, go to a doctor's appontment or go on an outing can work wonders to renew their energy and better equip them for the responsibilities of caregving. VITAS Volunteer orientation is provided free of charge to help you learn the skills required to be an outstanding hospice volunteer. Time and dates are ongoing and flexible. Locations are in homes throughout the greater Dallas area. We are especially looking for volunteers who would be available during daytime, weekday hours but weekends or evenings are also helpful. For more information about VITAS Volunteer Services visit: https://www.vitas.com/volunteer.

opportunity_title: Assist at NeuroFit Gym, an Adaptive Fitness Gym
description_short: Volunteer Opportunities Volunteers contributed to success of NFF. We rely on volunteers to assist our clients at our adaptive fitness gym, NeuroFit. We welcome college students. Our facility serves as an educational training site for healthcare…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunities Volunteers contributed to success of NFF. We rely on volunteers to assist our clients at our adaptive fitness gym, NeuroFit. We welcome college students. Our facility serves as an educational training site for healthcare students, offering opportunities for internships and capstone projects. We provide students and volunteers with direct, hands-on experience working with individuals affected by neurological conditions. This allows them to apply their academic knowledge with practical application in a real-world clinical environment. Whether you want to volunteer to use your skills or to develop a variety of skills, to meet new people or to reap the rewards of lending a hand by better serving our community, we need YOU! Here are some volunteer opportunities (not inclusive): Assist clients with equipment Interact with clients Set clients on machines Setup/put away equipment Help with cleanup and prepare gym for next day

opportunity_title: Assistant, Rescue (Owner Surrender Board)
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As a Rescue Admin, you provide the organization with a dedicated representative to work hand in hand with the administration side of a continued positive partnership of Dallas Pets Alive! and our shelter partners. Team: Rescue Time Commitment: Estimate 4-6 hours per week. Must be able to commit to a minimum of 6 months of volunteer service. Reports to: Our Rescue Director, Vice President of Animal Operations, and Executive Director. Works with: This volunteer will work with pet owners that have scheduled owner surrender appointments at Dallas Animal Services. Responsibilities: Monitor owner surrender board Work with pet owners to gather additional information about the pets in need Complete brief write-ups with photos of shelter pets in need and add to DPA’s Trello networking albums (ideally daily but if not feasible as frequently as possible) Add shelter candidates to Trello “Pending” albums for review and approval to network to fosters Maintain Owner Surrender Trello boards (Dogs In Need & Cats In Need) for candidates, adding and removing pets based on availability Qualifications/Skills: Must be at least 18 years of age Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Ability and willingness to maintain consistent availability in case of emergencies. Have some related animal/rescue group experience, preferred Ability to work virtually ( Email, Slack, Phone ). A flexible work schedule to answer shelter partner correspondence during the work day is preferred but not required. Must be organized and skilled at record-keeping What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Attention Veterans:  We are looking for Veterans who would like to visit with our Veteran patients.
description_short: We are commited to honoring our Veteran patients to end of life and even afterward in collaboration with our bereavement team. We appreciate other Veterans in the community that have a desire to volunteer and give of their time in the form of…
description_long: We are commited to honoring our Veteran patients to end of life and even afterward in collaboration with our bereavement team. We appreciate other Veterans in the community that have a desire to volunteer and give of their time in the form of visiting with our Veteran patients and their families, assisting with special appreciation events including all Veteran Holidays, and other various projects to honor the life of a veteran. Completion of an application and background check is required for all volunteer candidates as a policy to protect our patients' safety. Please join us on our mission to remember and honor the lives of our Veteran patients and their families so that they may have memories of peace, comfort, and caring hearts at a very tender time in life.

opportunity_title: Auction Procurement Volunteer, Soirée
description_short: Position Requirements An organized, proactive individual who is comfortable reaching out to businesses to request donations in support of the DPA Soirée. Outreach may include email, phone, or in-person communication—guidance and sample scripts will…
description_long: Position Requirements An organized, proactive individual who is comfortable reaching out to businesses to request donations in support of the DPA Soirée. Outreach may include email, phone, or in-person communication—guidance and sample scripts will be provided. No prior experience or existing business connections are required. A genuine interest in supporting Dallas Pets Alive, Dogs Matter, and Humane Society of Dallas County is essential. Position Purpose Support the success of the Soirée auction by helping source and secure live and silent auction items, as well as contributions for the wine and whiskey pull. Your Role Help build a dynamic and engaging auction by securing donations from businesses and community partners who: Offer products, services, or experiences suitable for auction Value giving back to the community Support animal welfare initiatives Volunteer Goals (Participate in as many as possible) Reach out to businesses and organizations to request donations Help secure live and silent auction items Source bottles for the wine and whiskey pull Track outreach efforts and responses Identify new donor opportunities and partnerships Why Get Involved Make a meaningful impact for animals in need Build relationships with local businesses and community partners Gain hands-on experience in fundraising and event planning Be part of a collaborative, mission-driven team

opportunity_title: BILINGUAL HOSPICE PATIENT COMPANION VOLUNTEERS NEEDED FOR DALLAS, COLLIN, ROCKWALL, HUNT AND KAUFMAN COUNTIES!
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Hope Health Care provides high-quality "whole-istic" hospice care for patients in the Dallas, Collin, Rockwall, Hunt and Kaufman counties, in either the home or facility setting. We are in need of…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Hope Health Care provides high-quality "whole-istic" hospice care for patients in the Dallas, Collin, Rockwall, Hunt and Kaufman counties, in either the home or facility setting. We are in need of Spanish speaking volunteers who can provide companionship to our Spanish-speaking patients. TIME COMMITMENT: 1 to 2 hour a week. Any time you can volunteer to provide some companionship to a patient is greatly needed and appreciated. LOCATION: Serve in your community. We strive to match you with a patient that resides in our near your community so that your drive time is as little as possible. NO HOSPICE EXPERIENCE NECESSARY: Have the heart to serve but new to hospice? Rest assured you will receive all the training necessary to become a competent and confident hospice volunteer!

opportunity_title: Be A Companion for A Hospice Patient - Dallas
description_short: Trinity Hospice is seeking kind, compassionate, and dependable volunteers to be friendly visitors to our hospice patients. As a hospice volunteer, you will be an important part of the hospice team. As a Friendly Visitor Volunteer, you can provide…
description_long: Trinity Hospice is seeking kind, compassionate, and dependable volunteers to be friendly visitors to our hospice patients. As a hospice volunteer, you will be an important part of the hospice team. As a Friendly Visitor Volunteer, you can provide much-needed companionship by visiting with patients in their homes. Activities that our volunteers do include: Engaging in friendly conversation Reading and writing with the patient Playing games Sharing hobbies Playing music Watching TV/Movies with patient Helping with household duties the patient might not be able to do Errands Caregiver relief And Much Much More!!! We provide full training in Hospice philosophy and volunteer duties. Orientation and on-going training provided! Background and reference check required. Volunteer will be driving to patients home for visits, so you will need a reliable mode of transportation. We will work with your schedule! Volunteers must be 18 years or older to volunteer directly with patients. No previous training or volunteer work required. You must have your own reliable vehicle and be willing to travel to visit patients. We are not taking any court ordered community service volunteers at this time. Below is a list of the communities that we serve: We have many ways for volunteers to engage and change the lives of our patients, follow our Volunteer Match page or visit our website www.trinityhospice.com/volunteers.

opportunity_title: Be Someone's Hero
description_short: Not all heroes wear scrubs. Sometimes they simply sit, listen and care. Sometimes the most powerful gift you can give is simply your presence. Freedom Hospice is looking for compassionate volunteers to support patients and families receiving hospice…
description_long: Not all heroes wear scrubs. Sometimes they simply sit, listen and care. Sometimes the most powerful gift you can give is simply your presence. Freedom Hospice is looking for compassionate volunteers to support patients and families receiving hospice care. As a hospice volunteer, you can provide comfort, companionship and dignity during life's most meaningful moments. Ways you can help: Sit and visit with patients Read listen or share conversation Capture the unique story of patients Help with small office tasks Share special skills like music, crafts, pet therapy, etc. Why volunteer with hospice? Because no one should face the end of life alone. Your time and kindness can bring peace, connection and comfort to someone who truly needs it. Flexible scheduling Free on-line training provided No medical experience required - just a caring heart. If you are ready to make a difference that truly matters, we'd love to hear from you. To apply, complete our on-line application found at https://www.volgistics.com/appform/898610724

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Be a Friend to Hospice Patients
description_short: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the…
description_long: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: Be a Hero! Volunteer for the Bold For Gold Tour 3K  to Fight Childhood Cancer!
description_short: Be Bold. Go Gold. Give Hope! Join Jacob Way Organization on Saturday, December 5, 2026 , for the historic national launch of the Bold For Gold Tour in Arlington, Texas! This high-energy 3K walk and community celebration will shine a light on…
description_long: Be Bold. Go Gold. Give Hope! Join Jacob Way Organization on Saturday, December 5, 2026 , for the historic national launch of the Bold For Gold Tour in Arlington, Texas! This high-energy 3K walk and community celebration will shine a light on childhood cancer and provide critical support to families in need. We are looking for passionate, energetic volunteers to help us make this inaugural event a massive success. As a volunteer, you will be the heart and soul of the event, creating a welcoming and fun atmosphere for hundreds of participants. Volunteer Responsibilities We have a variety of roles available to match your interests and skills! No experience is necessary—just a positive attitude and a willingness to help. Roles include: Event Greeters & Guides Registration & Check-In Crew Route Marshals & Cheer Squad Water Station Crew "Stuff the Sleigh" Donation Team Finish Line Celebration Team Setup & Teardown Ninjas Please note: Final volunteer positions may be updated according to event needs. Qualifications A positive, friendly, and enthusiastic attitude. A passion for helping others and supporting our community. Must be reliable, punctual, and a team player. All volunteers must attend a mandatory virtual training session before the event. What You'll Get All volunteers will receive: An official Bold for Gold Tour Volunteer T-Shirt . Lunch and snacks to keep you energized. A fun, rewarding experience being part of a historic community event. Event Details Date: Saturday, December 5, 2026 Time: Volunteer shifts are from approx. 7:45Am to 2:00 PM CST. Location: Bob Cooke Park, 1817 New York Avenue, Arlington, TX 76010. Ready to be a hero? Sign up today and help us turn Arlington GOLD!

opportunity_title: Be a Host Family to a High School Exchange Student
description_short: Are you looking to make a meaningful difference in the world? Would you like to learn more about other cultures? Hosting a foreign exchange student is a great way to start! ETC works with the U.S. Department of State to find homes for high school…
description_long: Are you looking to make a meaningful difference in the world? Would you like to learn more about other cultures? Hosting a foreign exchange student is a great way to start! ETC works with the U.S. Department of State to find homes for high school foreign exchange students coming to the USA on a J-1 visa. We have students arriving from a variety of countries, ages 14-18, who are looking for an American family to share this once in a lifetime experience with. What does being a host family entail? Ensure a safe and nurturing home environment for the student Provide students with a room (can be shared with a similar aged host sibling) & his/her own bed 3 meals per day Transportation to and from school and for extracurricular activities Give love and support to the student Explore cultural differences and similarities Families with or without children are accepted What are the students like? Bring their own spending money and health insurance Have good English skills Are highly screened for the program Excited to meet their American family! Why should we become a host family? Learn a new culture by tasting new foods, listening to new music, and speaking a new language Share your culture with an exchange student Add another member to your family Help an exchange student get an American education and the experience of a lifetime! Still not totally convinced? See what The Rogers Family (former host family) has to say about their experience: "Our exchange student has been such a blessing in our lives. We had no idea we were missing anything until she came into our lives. Having a host daughter has made us laugh harder than we ever have before. It’s been so special to see my kids love her, bond with her, and my daughter’s closest confident in a time of her life. She has become not just a daughter to me, but my friend." Watch our video to learn more: https://youtu.be/2hGfElh3Cgc

org_name: The American Red Cross - East Texas Region
opportunity_title: Be a Lifeline in Times of Crisis - Join the Red Cross Disaster Action Team in Crandall Texas!
description_short: Are you a people person with a knack for leadership? Put your HR skills and passion for helping others to work with the American Red Cross! As a Disaster Action Team (DAT) volunteer, you’ll be on the front lines, providing compassionate support to…
description_long: Are you a people person with a knack for leadership? Put your HR skills and passion for helping others to work with the American Red Cross! As a Disaster Action Team (DAT) volunteer, you’ll be on the front lines, providing compassionate support to individuals and families affected by disasters-from home fires to large-scale emergencies. The Disaster Workforce Engagement team is expanding across North Texas and seeking individuals with experience in HR, team building, training, project management, disaster relief and community engagement. This is a continuous volunteer opportunity, and you are able to set your availability to meet the needs of your schedule. Individuals and groups are welcome to join our team. If you are volunteering as a group, you will be able to serve in small groups. Training Required We offer virtual and in-person training for our Disaster Action Team. How to Sign Up Please visit "The Application Site" to start your application to become a Red Cross Volunteer. Our application can be completed in 15 minutes! Make sure to verify your phone number and email address, read and agree to Red Cross Policies. A member of our team will reach out shortly. Watch our video to learn more: Red Cross Disaster Action Teams

This is a continuous volunteer opportunity, and you are able to set your availability to meet the needs of your schedule. Individuals and groups are welcome to join our team. If you are volunteering as a group, you will be able to serve in small groups. Training Required We offer virtual and in-person training for our Disaster Action Team. How to Sign Up Please visit "The Application Site" to start your application to become a Red Cross Volunteer. Our application can be completed in 15 minutes! Make sure to verify your phone number and email address, read and agree to Red Cross Policies. A member of our team will reach out shortly. Watch our video to learn more: Red Cross Disaster Action Teams

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Be a Light to Others - Join Our Hospice Volunteer Team!
description_short: Looking for a rewarding volunteer position...look no further! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence,…
description_long: Looking for a rewarding volunteer position...look no further! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Volunteer Opportunity: Direct Patient Care - Sitting with patients, reading, working puzzles, watching tv/movies and sharing life stories. This may also include Caregiver Respite where you will visit with the patient while the caregiver runs errands, takes time for themselves or goes to appointments. Must be able to pass a drug screen, background check, and be at least 17 years old. If you are interested in this wonderful volunteer opportunity, please email Faith Oates at Faith.Oates@ehab.com Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description Enhabit Hospice is looking for a volunteer to provide friendship to patients during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. Serving the entire DFW area For more information, please contact: Faith Oates - Volunteer Coordinator and Bereavement Services 469.677.3100 Faith.Oates@ehab.com

opportunity_title: Be a Presence of Comfort: Become a Bedside Companion Volunteer with Trinity Hospice
description_short: Be a Presence of Comfort: Become a Bedside Companion Volunteer with Trinity Hospice Are you a compassionate individual with a heart for helping others? Trinity Hospice is seeking caring volunteers to provide bedside companionship to our patients.…
description_long: Be a Presence of Comfort: Become a Bedside Companion Volunteer with Trinity Hospice Are you a compassionate individual with a heart for helping others? Trinity Hospice is seeking caring volunteers to provide bedside companionship to our patients. Your presence, kindness, and quiet support can make a world of difference during a vulnerable time. What does a Bedside Companion Volunteer do? Our Bedside Companion Volunteers provide invaluable support to patients by: Being present: Spending time with patients, offering a comforting and supportive presence. Providing companionship: Listening to stories, sharing conversations, reading aloud, or simply sitting quietly with patients. Offering emotional support: Providing a listening ear and a compassionate heart, offering comfort and reassurance. Creating a peaceful environment: Helping to create a calm and relaxing atmosphere for patients. Respecting patient preferences: Honoring individual needs and preferences for interaction, respecting silence when desired. Offering respite for families: Providing a brief respite for family caregivers, allowing them time for errands, appointments, or self-care. Why Volunteer with Trinity Hospice as a Bedside Companion? Meaningful Impact: Your presence and compassion can provide invaluable comfort and support to patients and their families. Personal Fulfillment: Experience the deep satisfaction of making a real difference in someone's life during a sensitive time. Flexible Scheduling: We offer flexible volunteer opportunities to fit your availability. Comprehensive Training: We provide thorough training and ongoing support to equip you with the skills and knowledge you need. Be Part of a Team: Join a compassionate community of volunteers and staff dedicated to providing exceptional care. Who are We Looking For? We welcome individuals from all backgrounds who are: Compassionate and empathetic. Good listeners. Respectful and patient. Reliable and dependable. Committed to maintaining confidentiality. How to Get Involved: If you are interested in becoming a Bedside Companion Volunteer, we would love to hear from you! Call us: 469.726.4402 Visit our website: www.TrinityHospice.com Email us: bkaiser@TrinityHospice.com Offer the gift of your presence. Become a Bedside Companion Volunteer with Trinity Hospice Trinity Hospice - Providing Comfort and Care in the DFW

opportunity_title: Be a Superhero - Volunteer in Hospice!
description_short: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the…
description_long: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: Beat the Event Helper
description_short: Volunteer Opportunity: Beat the Heat Community Event – MediSec Join MediSec as a volunteer for our Beat the Heat Community Event and help us engage with the local community while promoting awareness of healthcare cybersecurity and public health.…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunity: Beat the Heat Community Event – MediSec Join MediSec as a volunteer for our Beat the Heat Community Event and help us engage with the local community while promoting awareness of healthcare cybersecurity and public health. Volunteers will: Welcome and engage with attendees throughout the event. Help explain MediSec's mission and community initiatives. Assist with event activities and outreach. Support event setup, organization, and cleanup as needed. Create a fun, welcoming, and informative environment for families and community members. No prior experience is required—just enthusiasm, a positive attitude, and a willingness to help! This is a great opportunity for students looking to gain volunteer experience, develop communication skills, and make a meaningful impact in their community. Organization: MediSec Event: Beat the Heat Community Event Date: Saturday, July 19 Time: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM Location: Sidecar Social, Frisco, TX We look forward to having you join us as we serve our community and share MediSec's mission of protecting patient safety through healthcare cybersecurity awareness.

opportunity_title: Become a CHOICE Business Champion! Share your career experience with a high-school student!
description_short: A "business champion" is a business professional that works with a mentor-student team as the subject of a meaningful Research a Business project. This consists of just three 30-minute phone calls along with a half-day career shadow. We are…
description_long: A "business champion" is a business professional that works with a mentor-student team as the subject of a meaningful Research a Business project. This consists of just three 30-minute phone calls along with a half-day career shadow. We are interested in showing students what it means to work in your field! For a nine-week period during the course of the school year, roughly January to March, our mentors and mentees (high school juniors and seniors) have the opportunity to research an existing business. The goals of this project are to uncover students’ particular business aptitudes, provide them with the opportunity to connect with a real-world business and learn how to interview, and teach conflict resolution techniques. At the completion of the project, students will participate in a career shadow with their business champion, something most underprivileged youth never have the opportunity to do. Here, students can see first-hand what a corporate environment looks like and what a college degree has the potential to offer. Stronger workforce. Stronger community. Better for business. You can be part of mentoring students to turn education into endless possibility by supplementing their schoolwork with real-world experience and knowledge. Together, we can help today’s at-risk students become tomorrow’s workplace stars.

org_name: THE HAPPY PET PROJECT, POWERED BY TEXAS GOOD DEEDS PROJECT INC
opportunity_title: Become a Hero for Pets in Need as a Foster!
description_short: *This opportunity requires individuals to be located in the Dallas Fort Worth Area of Texas.** Fostering changes lives-starting with yours! When you open your home to a pet through The Happy Pet Project, you’re not just saving one animal; you’re…
description_long: *This opportunity requires individuals to be located in the Dallas Fort Worth Area of Texas.** Fostering changes lives-starting with yours! When you open your home to a pet through The Happy Pet Project, you’re not just saving one animal; you’re strengthening our community and bringing hope to countless furry friends. Fostering is your chance to make a direct and lasting impact. Whether it’s providing a safe space for healing, preparing a pet for their forever home, or simply offering love to those who need it most, YOU are the difference-maker. No experience? No problem! We’ll be with you every step of the way, offering guidance, resources, and support as you learn and grow with your foster companion. Together, we can create a world where every pet feels safe, loved, and cared for. Sign up today and start making a difference in the lives of pets and people in your community.

opportunity_title: Become a Hospice Companion Volunteer! Bring Comfort and Joy to those who Need It!
description_short: Hospice Companion Volunteers bring human connection back to patients that have lost access to their community because of their condition. It's a vital role and much needed, maybe even in your own neighborhood. Hospice patients live in a variety of…
description_long: Hospice Companion Volunteers bring human connection back to patients that have lost access to their community because of their condition. It's a vital role and much needed, maybe even in your own neighborhood. Hospice patients live in a variety of settings, from residential homes, to apartments, to assisted living facilities, to nursing homes, but because of how serious their conditions are, they often can't go out to connect with friends and family like most of us can. That is where caring volunteers like you come in! Just because someone is on hospice doesn't mean they are actively dying or that they have given up on life. They are just living with a very serious illness and they need a lot of help to have as good a quality of life as they can. As anyone could imagine, a good quality of life for people often includes time with friends and family, sharing memories, having good conversations, listening to music, or just talking about their day. For hospice patients, many of whom can't leave their home, or sometimes even their bed, the community has to come to them. You can be a part of that community that brightens their day and gives a little relief from the boredom, loneliness, and pain of their condition. It's a medicine given from one heart to another, no medical license required. Our Companion Volunteers make those critical connections just by showing up ready to listen, to engage, to share, and to learn. We also make sure you are trained and prepared for being in a hospice patient's home. Hospice can be a scary word, but it doesn't have to be. There is so much joy to offer and receive in the final chapter of life, so think about inquiring today.

description_short: Hope Health Care can can help you build your resume and help you gain valuable experience in working with family and friends of hospice patients. Bereavement Follow-up Phone Volunteers make phone calls to the family and friends of a loved one who…
description_long: Hope Health Care can can help you build your resume and help you gain valuable experience in working with family and friends of hospice patients. Bereavement Follow-up Phone Volunteers make phone calls to the family and friends of a loved one who has recently died to provide comfort and support, refer to bereavement counselor if appropriate and to request a positive review on social media sites. Volunteers must be emotionally mature, and sensitive with excellent communication skills. These phone calls are made from the volunteers home or office, however the volunteer must have access to a computer and the internet. This opportunity can be done from your home or one of two of our offices, Garland or Greenville.

opportunity_title: Big Climb Dallas- Blood Cancer United
description_short: Big Climb is more than a stair climb-it's a moment of purpose. Volunteers for the Big Climb will support event activities such a registration, food and equipment stations. Throughout the Big Climb event climbers and volunteers will be lifted by…

Throughout the Big Climb event climbers and volunteers will be lifted by stories of survivorship, resilience, and the real impact of your efforts with Blood Cancer United. Around you, fellow climbers and volunteers will be cheering you on-every step a symbol of strength, every landing a reminder of hope. Volunteers will assist with photo stations, water stops along the climb route and present finisher medals alongside survivors, caregivers, and teammates-knowing their climb is part of something bigger.

description_short: VITAS is looking for kind and caring people to help terminally ill patients and their families. Friendly visits, simple errands, providing a listening ear and the gift of friendship at a crucial time can make a real difference in another's life.…
description_long: VITAS is looking for kind and caring people to help terminally ill patients and their families. Friendly visits, simple errands, providing a listening ear and the gift of friendship at a crucial time can make a real difference in another's life. Yours, too! VITAS volunteer activities: sit with a patient while the caregiver takes a break; telephone support, friendly visits to patients and their families at home or in a nursing home; driving a patient to appointments; accompanying a patient to a church function, wedding, beauty shop, play, ballgame or run errands.

opportunity_title: Blanket Maker in Plano, TX - Crochet + Knit
description_short: We make blankets and give them to children and teens who need a reminder that they matter, are not invisible, worthy of love, and that someone cares. Ill, abused, homeless, experiencing trauma. Blankets can be anywhere from 36"x36" (preemies and…
description_long: We make blankets and give them to children and teens who need a reminder that they matter, are not invisible, worthy of love, and that someone cares. Ill, abused, homeless, experiencing trauma. Blankets can be anywhere from 36"x36" (preemies and babies) up to a twin-sized blanket, 45"x60" (children and teens - those are the ones we ALWAYS NEED - the BIG ONES for BIG KIDS). Blankets can be sewn, crocheted, knitted, or quilted! Blankets, or "Binkies" as they're called, are delivered to our Binky Patrol chapters across the U.S. The Area Coordinators then organize a group of people to sew labels onto each blanket. Once the labels are on, the blankets are delivered by yet more volunteers to local hospitals, crisis centers, police and fire departments, and other local agencies. The blankets are then given to children and teens in need of comfort. If you want to help us more, you can also have us send you labels to add and perhaps - if there is no chapter near you, you can deliver them to a local group that really needs them. We work with social services in most states and the groups they are tied to. We are also MrBallen Foundation partners and tied to all of the organizations they serve. This is a great way for students to earn Community Service hours! https://binkypatrol.org/community-serivce/ Gather a group of your friends or family members, take a quick trip to your local fabric store, pick out some fleece, bring it home, make some blankets and deliver or ship them to a Binky Patrol chapter near you! If you sew you can make quilts. if you knit or crochet you can make afghans. Please be sure to use machine washable and dryable materials in all blankets. We'll be glad to help you find a chapter near you or help coordinate delivery. Contact us today - txcheryl@binkypatrol.org Thank you!

We work with social services in most states and the groups they are tied to. We are also MrBallen Foundation partners and tied to all of the organizations they serve. This is a great way for students to earn Community Service hours! https://binkypatrol.org/community-serivce/ Gather a group of your friends or family members, take a quick trip to your local fabric store, pick out some fleece, bring it home, make some blankets and deliver or ship them to a Binky Patrol chapter near you! If you sew you can make quilts. if you knit or crochet you can make afghans. Please be sure to use machine washable and dryable materials in all blankets. We'll be glad to help you find a chapter near you or help coordinate delivery. Contact us today - txcheryl@binkypatrol.org Thank you!

opportunity_title: Blanket Maker in Plano, TX - Sew + Quilt
description_short: We make blankets and give them to children and teens who need a reminder that they matter, are not invisible, worthy of love, and that someone cares. Ill, abused, homeless, experiencing trauma. Blankets can be anywhere from 36"x36" (preemies and…
description_long: We make blankets and give them to children and teens who need a reminder that they matter, are not invisible, worthy of love, and that someone cares. Ill, abused, homeless, experiencing trauma. Blankets can be anywhere from 36"x36" (preemies and babies) up to a twin-sized blanket, 45"x60" (children and teens - those are the ones we ALWAYS NEED - the BIG ONES for BIG KIDS). Blankets can be sewn, crocheted, knitted, or quilted! Blankets, or "Binkies" as they're called, are delivered to our Binky Patrol chapters across the U.S. The Area Coordinators then organize a group of people to sew labels onto each blanket. Once the labels are on, the blankets are delivered by yet more volunteers to local hospitals, crisis centers, police and fire departments, and other local agencies. The blankets are then given to children and teens in need of comfort. If you want to help us more, you can also have us send you labels to add and perhaps - if there is no chapter near you, you can deliver them to a local group that really needs them. We work with social services in most states and the groups they are tied to. We are also MrBallen Foundation partners and tied to all of the organizations they serve. This is a great way for students to earn Community Service hours! https://binkypatrol.org/community-serivce/ Gather a group of your friends or family members, take a quick trip to your local fabric store, pick out some fleece, bring it home, make some blankets and deliver or ship them to a Binky Patrol chapter near you! If you sew you can make quilts. if you knit or crochet you can make afghans. Please be sure to use machine washable and dryable materials in all blankets. We'll be glad to help you find a chapter near you or help coordinate delivery. Contact us today - txcheryl@binkypatrol.org Thank you!

org_name: American Red Cross - North Texas Region
opportunity_title: Blood Donor Ambassador Needed in Carrollton Texas
description_short: Volunteer Responsibilities Assist with blood donor registration, maintain appointment flow, maintain canteen, monitor for reactions, assist with appointment reminders and future registrations. Project Details Your help is needed at blood drives!…
description_long: Volunteer Responsibilities Assist with blood donor registration, maintain appointment flow, maintain canteen, monitor for reactions, assist with appointment reminders and future registrations. Project Details Your help is needed at blood drives! From start to finish, Donor Ambassadors provide a friendly face for blood donors, whether they’re greeting and registering donors at check-in or thanking them for rolling up their sleeves and donating lifesaving blood at the refreshments table. Requirements: Volunteers must be 16+ years old. Must be willing and able to complete training shifts and two or more blood drive shifts a month for at least three months. Must be an excellent communicator with a service mindset. Must be flexible to adapt to the changing needs of the blood to drive throughout the day.

opportunity_title: Board of Directors
description_short: WAAW Foundation Board 3.0 Recruitment Join Our Board of Directors Working to Advance STEM Education for African Women (WAAW) Foundation is a leading international non-profit dedicated to bridging the gender gap in technology and innovation. We…
description_long: WAAW Foundation Board 3.0 Recruitment Join Our Board of Directors Working to Advance STEM Education for African Women (WAAW) Foundation is a leading international non-profit dedicated to bridging the gender gap in technology and innovation. We believe that empowering African women in STEM is the most effective catalyst for sustainable economic growth and poverty alleviation across the continent. Since our inception in 2007, we have built a pan-African movement that provides high-impact training, mentorship, and scholarship opportunities to thousands of young women. WAAW Foundation’s footprint extends across the continent, turning potential into tangible leadership through data-driven results: Breadth of Reach : We have empowered over 60,000 secondary school girls with essential tech skills and STEM knowledge. Geographic Footprint : We maintain active operations in 29 African countries across West, East, Southern, Central and North Africa. Community/College Hubs : We have established 114 STEM Chapters in higher institutions, serving as vital centers for mentorship and peer-to-peer learning in communities across Africa. Leadership Pipeline : Over 1,000 Fellows and 196 Scholars are pursuing STEM degrees/careers and leading community-based initiatives. Educational Support : Nearly 3,000 teachers have been trained in modern STEM pedagogy and blended learning strategies to redefine classroom engagement. As we enter this next phase of growth—Board 3.0—we are expanding our global leadership team. We are seeking visionary, high-influence leaders with a passion for gender equity and social innovation to provide robust governance, strategic direction, and the fundraising support necessary to scale our impact. Board Composition & Global Representation Our Board is intentionally designed to ensure geographic diversity and strong coverage across key skill areas. West Africa Representative East Africa Representative Southern Africa Representative USA-based Member Europe-based Member WAAW Alumna (Fellow/Scholar) WAAW Teacher or Parent of a Beneficiary Board Members SkillSet, Roles and Responsibilities If you have experience in any of these skills, you are encouraged to apply. HR & Organizational Management : The People and Culture Leader - Guide organizational design, leadership development and talent strategy to support sustainable growth. Program Management & Impact Evaluation : The Program Delivery Expert - Ensure programs are measurable, impactful, and scalable. Finance and Accounting : The Financial Steward - Ensure financial transparency and sustainability. Oversee audits and regulatory compliance. Fundraising & Strategy : Drive fundraising and high-level partnerships with institutional donors/foundations, and ensure long-term growth strategy. Philanthropy and Global Partnerships : The Philanthropic Ambassador - Strengthen philanthropy, donor engagement, advocacy and global visibility. Legal Expertise and Board Governance : Drive mentorship and community engagement. Represent beneficiaries and provide program insight. Board Member Expectations Attend quarterly board meetings Participate in (monthly) subcommittee meetings and strategic discussions Support strategic planning and governance Make a personal contribution or leverage networks to support fundraising Serve as an ambassador for WAAW Foundation Commit approximately 2-4 hours per week What We Are Looking For 10+ years of professional experience (varies by role) Previous Board Experience (if any) Strong leadership and strategic thinking skills Experience in nonprofit, corporate or social impact sectors Passion for advancing STEM education for African women and girls Ability to contribute networks, expertise and influence Why Join the WAAW Foundation Board? Serving on the WAAW Foundation Board is a transformative opportunity for leaders who want to move beyond advocacy into active ownership of Africa’s digital future. As a member of our global board, you will: Shape the Future of STEM Education in Africa: You aren't just an observer; you are an architect of the continent’s digital transformation. You will provide strategic oversight for programs across 29 African nations. Your leadership ensures that the next decade of African innovation is inclusive, equitable, and globally competitive. Collaborate with Global Leaders and Change-Makers: Joining this board places you at the center of an elite ecosystem. You will work alongside senior tech executives, high-level diplomats, and social innovation pioneers. This is a unique space to exchange insights with an international network of peers, fostering partnerships that drive gender equity on a global scale. Contribute to a High-Impact Mission: Your involvement has a direct, measurable ROI on human capital, and you’ll help scale a movement that has already reached 60,000+ girls. You will play a pivotal role in our goal to foster innovators who will create thousands of jobs, transforming local economies through the power of STEM. Gain Specialized Nonprofit Governance Experience: Serving on a pan-African board offers a masterclass in navigating complex, multi-jurisdictional governance. You will gain deep experience in international NGO compliance, strategic fundraising, and fiscal stewardship across diverse regulatory environments—refining your ability to lead through complexity and operational excellence. Increase Professional Visibility: Represent WAAW Foundation at international summits, global tech conferences, and high-level diplomatic dialogues. This role positions you as a leading champion for social innovation, amplifying your personal brand as a visionary leader in the global development space. Influence Continental Policy : Gain a seat at the table in shaping pan-African STEM strategies. WAAW Foundation is a key contributor to the African Union’s STISA 2034 task force, giving you a direct voice in high-level policy discussions. Drive Generational Change : Directly contribute to a mandate aimed at building a legacy of economic independence for African women. Our Vision : To create a world where African women are formidable global leaders, transforming lives through technology, science, and innovation. We invite you to be the architect of that vision. How to Apply Interested board candidates should complete the WAAW Foundation Board of Directors: Expression of Interest Form

org_name: Creative Visions Social Services & Consultants, Inc.,
opportunity_title: Board of Management Volunteer
description_short: Want to be the type of person to make a difference in the community and in the lives of youth and families. CVSS is the place for you! The Board of Directors of Creative Visions Social Services & Consultants, Incorporation is responsible for the…
description_long: Want to be the type of person to make a difference in the community and in the lives of youth and families. CVSS is the place for you! The Board of Directors of Creative Visions Social Services & Consultants, Incorporation is responsible for the successful and effective operation of Creative Visions Social Services & Consultants, Incorporation in the achievement of this purpose. Title: Member, Creative Visions Social Services & Consultants, Incorporation (CVSS) Board of Managers. Purpose: To serve as a voting member of the group with authority and responsibility to assist in the implementation of policies, procedures, and regulations for the conduct of Creative Visions Social Services & Consultants, Incorporation; to raise funds through personal contributions and participation in the campaign structure to finance the organization and its programs. Meetings Regularly - Bi-monthly board meetings (average 6 annually) (Board Meeting or Virtual) Attendance : 6 times a year, Fundraising - Standing Committee meetings - Special Events (as scheduled) -Occasional CVSS program events (as a board representative) Become a Volunteer join our board at cvssdfw.org
