opportunity_title: Breast Cancer Awareness Outreach
description_short: Stronger Than Cancer is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting breast cancer patients through comfort care kits, education, and community outreach. We are seeking student volunteers to lead breast cancer awareness outreach events on campus, or in the…
description_long: Stronger Than Cancer is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting breast cancer patients through comfort care kits, education, and community outreach. We are seeking student volunteers to lead breast cancer awareness outreach events on campus, or in the community, or at public locations with prior permission. Volunteers will plan and host informational tables, distribute educational materials, and engage others in conversations about early detection and screening. Training, guidance, and outreach materials will be provided by Stronger Than Cancer.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Brighten Someone's Day - Become a Hospice Volunteer
description_short: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the…
description_long: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: Brighten Someone's Day: Volunteer with Lifted Hospice
description_short: Opportunity Description: Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Your time can make a…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: * Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. * Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. * Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: * Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. * Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. * A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? * Make a lasting impact in your community. * Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life. Contact Hannah Morris at hannah.morris@liftedhospice.com or 214-656-8402 for more information. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held at organization location(s) No Donation

opportunity_title: Brighten Someone's Day: Volunteer with Lifted Hospice | Garland & Surrounding Areas
description_short: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. Options of activities to do with patient: playing games/cards, listening to music (or singing/playing instruments), Veteran services, arts & crafts, reading to patient, etc... Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life.

opportunity_title: Brightening Days Through Hospice Visits
description_short: Hospice care is centered on comfort, compassion, and living fully—every single day. At Halcyon Home, our hospice team focuses on managing symptoms while supporting patients and their families with dignity, kindness, and heart. Hospice is not about…
description_long: Hospice care is centered on comfort, compassion, and living fully—every single day. At Halcyon Home, our hospice team focuses on managing symptoms while supporting patients and their families with dignity, kindness, and heart. Hospice is not about giving up; it’s about embracing meaningful moments and honoring life. Our volunteers play a special role by spending quality time with patients facing terminal illness. From reading a favorite book and sharing conversations to enjoying hobbies and simple moments together, volunteers bring companionship, comfort, and a sense of normalcy into each visit. Just as importantly, volunteers offer families a chance to recharge—whether that means running errands, attending appointments, or simply taking a much-needed break—knowing their loved one is in caring company. Volunteers are often the highlight of the day for both patients and families. Opportunities are available in patients’ homes or in senior community settings, allowing you to make a genuine difference wherever you are placed. No prior experience is needed—training and orientation are provided. If you’re ready to brighten lives and be part of something meaningful, we’d love to hear from you. Please contact our Volunteer Team at Tford@halcyonhome.com .

opportunity_title: Bringing joy to Hospice Patients
description_short: Volunteers can serve in multiple roles and have described their work as gratifying and meaningful. In addition to providing compassion, they can spend time with the patient, reading, listening to music, and sharing life stories. Whatever way we can…
description_long: Volunteers can serve in multiple roles and have described their work as gratifying and meaningful. In addition to providing compassion, they can spend time with the patient, reading, listening to music, and sharing life stories. Whatever way we can serve our patients is beneficial and our volunteers can choose the days and times that work best with their personal schedule. We provide an orientation to prepare our volunteers, instill confidence, and to help them feel comfortable in carrying out their responsibilities.

opportunity_title: BuildHer Women in STEM
description_short: Our mission is to empower girls to explore STEM in fun, hands-on ways, whether at home or online. Through creative projects, challenges, tutoring, and conversations with inspiring women in science, technology, engineering, and math, we aim to spark…
description_long: Our mission is to empower girls to explore STEM in fun, hands-on ways, whether at home or online. Through creative projects, challenges, tutoring, and conversations with inspiring women in science, technology, engineering, and math, we aim to spark curiosity, build confidence, and show that girls belong in every corner of STEM. We want to make learning interactive, exciting, and accessible, so every girl can discover her potential and see herself as a future innovator. To be a volunteer go to the website below and fill out the form after clicking be a voluteer box. https://www.buildherus.org/

opportunity_title: Building South Asian Civic Engagement in Texas
description_short: Volunteer with SAAVETX Education Fund Help Build Educated Voters Across Texas SAAVETX Education Fund is a nonpartisan organization dedicated to strengthening civic participation within South Asian communities across Texas. We believe informed voters…
description_long: Volunteer with SAAVETX Education Fund Help Build Educated Voters Across Texas SAAVETX Education Fund is a nonpartisan organization dedicated to strengthening civic participation within South Asian communities across Texas. We believe informed voters build stronger communities — and volunteers are at the heart of that work. We are seeking committed volunteers to support voter education and engagement efforts through: 📞 Phone banking to provide clear, accessible voter information 💬 Text banking and relational outreach to connect with community members ✍🏽 Postcard writing campaigns to increase voter awareness 🚪 Door-to-door canvassing in targeted communities 📲 Digital outreach and social media engagement Volunteers help South Asians across Texas understand what’s on the ballot, how to vote, and why their voice matters. This is nonpartisan civic education focused on empowering informed participation. Who We’re Looking For Individuals committed to civic engagement and community empowerment Volunteers who are dependable and able to follow through on commitments Strong communicators who can engage respectfully across diverse communities Multilingual volunteers are especially encouraged to apply No prior experience is required — training is provided. What matters most is reliability, professionalism, and a commitment to building long-term community power. What You’ll Gain Hands-on experience in civic engagement and community organizing Training in voter education and outreach strategies Opportunities to take on leadership roles The ability to make measurable impact in your community Flexible scheduling available. Some evening and weekend shifts required during peak election cycles.

opportunity_title: CEDAR HILL: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most
description_short: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most Become a Hospice Patient Volunteer Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an…
description_long: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most Become a Hospice Patient Volunteer Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an Alpha Omega Hospice Patient Volunteer, you'll become an important part of a patient's care team by offering companionship, support, and a caring presence to individuals facing serious illness. Why Hospice Volunteering? Build meaningful relationships with patients and families Make a positive impact during an important time in someone's life Develop communication, empathy, and active listening skills Share your time, talents, and compassion with others Experience the personal fulfillment that comes from serving your community Help reduce loneliness and improve quality of life for patients What Do Volunteers Do? Provide companionship through: Conversation and life-story sharing Reading, music, games, and activities Watching television together Listening and offering emotional support Simply being a caring presence No healthcare or volunteer experience is required. Volunteer Commitment Flexible scheduling Visit 1 time per week or 2 times per month Typical visits last 30–60 minutes Volunteer opportunities available near your preferred area What We Provide One-on-one patient matching within approximately 15 minutes of your preferred location Volunteer Coordinator accompaniment on your first visit Ongoing support and guidance Comprehensive training provided at no cost Requirements provided for FREE: Online Application Background Check TB Test (Skin or Blood Test) Drug Screening (THC not tested) Driver's License Online Volunteer Training "You are not just volunteering your time—you are providing comfort, dignity, companionship, and human connection to someone who truly needs it."

opportunity_title: COMMISSIONED Sales & Marketing Opportunity REMOTE | Ground Floor Growth Lane| Pathway to Partnership
description_short: Are you a builder ? A disruptor ? A change-maker ? Do you love impacting others through storytelling, building relationships, and spreading a little sunshine wherever you go? Let's talk soon! We’re looking for a dynamic Sales & Marketing leader…
description_long: Are you a builder ? A disruptor ? A change-maker ? Do you love impacting others through storytelling, building relationships, and spreading a little sunshine wherever you go? Let's talk soon! We’re looking for a dynamic Sales & Marketing leader who brings: ✨ Influence and marketing energy ✨ The gift of gab and strong people skills ✨ The art of follow-through ✨ The ability to spot opportunity a mile away ✨ A passion for growth, partnerships, and purpose-driven work Step into our growing digital metaverse where we are creating social, emotional, and learning-centered environments for children navigating life changes and BIG gigantic emotions. We create spaces where children can see themselves, feel supported, and discover their strength. 🌟 This is what your future can hold. 🌟 It’s time for a change. 🌟 It’s time to build something meaningful. 📩 Connect with Glorya today: info@grangelworld.com

opportunity_title: CONTENT WRITER (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Reports To: Editor- Content Team Content Writer will be responsible to craft high-quality written content for our platform. You will create engaging, informative, and SEO-friendly content that resonates with seekers while showcasing our holistic…
description_long: Reports To: Editor- Content Team Content Writer will be responsible to craft high-quality written content for our platform. You will create engaging, informative, and SEO-friendly content that resonates with seekers while showcasing our holistic partners’ offerings Key Responsibilities: Research, write, and publish SEO-friendly content including: Blog posts on wellness, mindfulness, and holistic practices Healer and event profiles / service descriptions Website pages and landing content Social media posts, newsletters, and email campaigns Collaborate with SEO Lead to optimize content for target keywords and search intent Ensure all content reflects brand voice, tone, and values Conduct research on holistic practices, trends, and competitors Edit and proofread content to maintain high-quality standards Maintain content calendar and deliverables within agreed deadlines Coordinate with Marketing and Design teams for multimedia content integration Incorporate user feedback and analytics insights to improve content engagement Skills & Qualifications: Proven experience as a Content Writer, Copywriter, or similar role Excellent writing, editing, and proofreading skills Understanding of SEO best practices and keyword research Familiarity with CMS platforms (WordPress, Webflow, etc.) Ability to research and write on wellness, holistic, or lifestyle topics Strong organizational and time-management skills Creativity and storytelling ability Knowledge of social media content creation is a plus

opportunity_title: CPA / Financial Steward
description_short: The primary goal of this role is to transition the organization's financial management from basic spreadsheets to professional accounting standards. This position is critical for ensuring compliance, financial transparency, and "grant readiness" for…
description_long: The primary goal of this role is to transition the organization's financial management from basic spreadsheets to professional accounting standards. This position is critical for ensuring compliance, financial transparency, and "grant readiness" for the organization. Responsibilities Accounting Standards : Moving current financial data from spreadsheets to professional accounting systems and standards. Compliance & Reporting : Supporting the preparation of IRS Form 990 . Grant Readiness : Ensuring all financial records are audit-ready and meet the requirements of potential grantors. Financial Oversight : Strengthening cash flow control and improving financial forecasting to support scaling. Strategic Support : Providing senior finance leadership to assist the COO and CEO in making data-driven decisions Time Commitment The expected time commitment ranges from 8 to 10 hours per month . Benefits Performing the role of CPA / Financial Steward for Transition Trails offers several professional and personal benefits for the individual in the position: Impactful Leadership Role : You serve as a senior finance leader, providing critical strategic support to the CEO and COO to drive data-driven organizational decisions. System Transformation Experience : This role offers the opportunity to lead a significant transition, moving the organization from basic spreadsheet management to professional, audit-ready accounting standards. Mission-Driven Contribution : By ensuring "grant readiness" and compliance (such as IRS Form 990 support), you directly enable the organization to secure funding and scale its impact within the Salesforce training and nonprofit sectors. Flexible Engagement : The role is designed for a fractional model , typically requiring 8 to 30 hours per month , enabling a high-level professional contribution with a flexible, part-time commitment. Expertise in Specialized Accounting : You gain or apply deep expertise in nonprofit-specific financial requirements, including fund accounting for restricted grants and tracking complex grant life cycles. Strategic Visibility : You help address critical organizational challenges, such as managing "margin pressure" and improving "cash visibility," making your contribution highly visible and valuable to leadership. Qualifications The qualifications for a CPA / Financial Steward include: Professional Certifications & Education Active CPA License : Essential for providing high-level financial oversight and ensuring the organization meets professional accounting standards. Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree : Typically in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. Technical Experience Senior Finance Leadership : Experience acting as a senior leader (such as a CFO) who can provide strategic support to executive leadership (COO/CEO). Nonprofit Accounting Expertise : Deep knowledge of nonprofit-specific financial requirements, including IRS Form 990 preparation and fund accounting for restricted and unrestricted grants. Systems Transition : Proven ability to migrate financial management from manual spreadsheets to professional, audit-ready accounting systems. Specialized Skills Grant Management : Proficiency in tracking grant life cycles (e.g., from "Submitted" to "Awarded" stages) and integrating these cycles into comprehensive financial reports. Financial Strategy : Advanced skills in cash flow forecasting , "cash visibility," and managing "margin pressure" during periods of scaling. Audit Readiness : The ability to ensure all financial records are compliant and meet the stringent requirements of potential grantors. Communication & Soft Skills Data-Driven Decision Support : Ability to translate complex financial data into actionable insights for non-financial executives. Strategic Collaboration : Experience working alongside operations teams to align financial oversight with organizational growth.

opportunity_title: Cage Cleaners Needed
description_short: Get your weekly dose of cat love from New Beginnings Cat Rescue! NBCR maintains the adoption center at the Garland PetSmart. The cats and kittens need daily attention, feeding, watering, grooming, cleaning litter boxes, and of course play and social…
description_long: Get your weekly dose of cat love from New Beginnings Cat Rescue! NBCR maintains the adoption center at the Garland PetSmart. The cats and kittens need daily attention, feeding, watering, grooming, cleaning litter boxes, and of course play and social interaction. We have cage cleaners come in twice a day. Most morning cage cleaners come in before 11am and evening cage cleaners come in after 5pm. As each cage is cleaned the kitty comes out to explore, play with toys and use scratching posts. Cleaners sanitize the cage, shake out the beds and provide clean bedding if needed, scoop the litterbox, and give the kitty fresh food and water. We train each person for this position, and while it isn't the easiest job, it certainly has its rewards! You get one-on-one time with the kitties and who appreciates a clean environment as much as a cat? If you live in the Garland or DFW area and would like to help with this task, please consider volunteering with New Beginnings Cat Rescue. To find out more about us and to submit a volunteer application, please see our website at: https://nbcrtx.org/volunteer-application

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice of Texas
opportunity_title: Call Grieving Families
description_short: Use your talents for listening and providing caring support for grieving families of Hospice patients who have passed. Come into our office and work under the direction of our Bereavement team to support the families of former hospice patients who…
description_long: Use your talents for listening and providing caring support for grieving families of Hospice patients who have passed. Come into our office and work under the direction of our Bereavement team to support the families of former hospice patients who we follow for 13 months after the death.

opportunity_title: Call for Power Chat Volunteers in Dallas!
description_short: At Girls Empowerment Network we strive to inspire girls to think creatively and critically about their futures by giving them a glimpse into the working world. That's where you come in! We are looking for dynamic and engaging professionals to…
description_long: At Girls Empowerment Network we strive to inspire girls to think creatively and critically about their futures by giving them a glimpse into the working world. That's where you come in! We are looking for dynamic and engaging professionals to participate in Power Chats on Tuesday, March 3 and Thursday, March 5. When you register , you’ll have the ability to choose the region, school location, date, and time of day that works best for you to participate in Power Chats. What exactly are Power Chats? They’re a structured discussion activity which allows youth and adult volunteers to connect in smaller groups, ask questions, talk about their interests, and share career insight. Girls get the chance to ask questions such as: what's your career?; what makes you feel confident?; what does leadership mean to you?; what insight would you share with your 6th grade self?; and what makes you feel powerful? After you register using this form , we’ll reach out one week prior to your selected Power Chats date to e-introduce you to the Girls Empowerment Network staff member leading Power Chats. You'll also receive detailed location information and a Power Chats training handout reviewing what to expect and best practices for engaging with girls. Please complete this registration form its entirety to signal your commitment to volunteering with Radiant G Circle Power Chats! Registration will close on Monday, February 23 at 9AM.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Can you play an instrument? Play for our Hospice Patients!
description_short: We are seeking musicians to volunteer their time and talent to perform for hospice patients residing in a local assisted living facility. Music therapy benefits hospice patients by reducing pain, anxiety, and depression while increasing relaxation,…
description_long: We are seeking musicians to volunteer their time and talent to perform for hospice patients residing in a local assisted living facility. Music therapy benefits hospice patients by reducing pain, anxiety, and depression while increasing relaxation, enhancing memory, communication, and quality of life. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship and music of volunteers. Volunteers often read to patients, hold their hands, listen or play music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Watch our video to learn more: Hospice - Something More

description_short: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily…
description_long: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily responsibilities and stress. Making a difference as a hospice volunteer begins with the desire to help the dying and their families. description_short: Join us on Saturday, September 12th as we honor the nearly 3,000 lives lost in the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001, as well as the countless first responders and service members who answered the call. In partnership with the National…
description_long: Join us on Saturday, September 12th as we honor the nearly 3,000 lives lost in the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001, as well as the countless first responders and service members who answered the call. In partnership with the National Cemetery Administration, volunteer at National Cemeteries, VA Grant funded Cemeteries, or at a local cemetery across the nation. Patriot Day is not just a day of remembrance. It's a day of service — honoring their sacrifice through action.

opportunity_title: Cat Cleaning and TLC
description_short: The SPCA of Texas is in need of volunteers to assist with daily cat care and socialization to improve their adoptability. We also ask our volunteers to help distribute enrichment items to the cats. We offer flexible shifts throughout the week,…
description_long: The SPCA of Texas is in need of volunteers to assist with daily cat care and socialization to improve their adoptability. We also ask our volunteers to help distribute enrichment items to the cats. We offer flexible shifts throughout the week, running from 9 am to 6 pm. Volunteers aged 10 and above are welcome to join us. For those under 15, participation alongside a parent or guardian, aged 18 or older, is mandatory. Younger volunteers, aged 10 to 11, will primarily focus on cat handling after receiving training. Volunteers aged 12 and older will have the opportunity to handle both dogs and cats. To begin volunteering, you'll need to create a volunteer account and complete initial steps. There's a $15 volunteer fee per person to cover the initial onboarding costs. After completing these steps, you'll be eligible to sign up for an Orientation session, where you'll receive further training and information.

opportunity_title: Cat Foster Parent
description_short: Texas CARES needs great foster parents to help care for our cats and kittens in your home. They will stay with you until a great permanent home is found through the Adopt-a-Pet program. All medical care is paid by Texas CARES. We also have the need…
description_long: Texas CARES needs great foster parents to help care for our cats and kittens in your home. They will stay with you until a great permanent home is found through the Adopt-a-Pet program. All medical care is paid by Texas CARES. We also have the need for kitty babysitters...if you can't devote a ton of time to foster, we can use you as a fill in when our fosters need a break or are on vacation. This is our busiest time of year, as we are already starting to take in pregnant cats or cats with young kittens.

opportunity_title: Cat Foster Parent
description_short: We are always looking for more foster homes so that we can help more cats. Help save the lives of homeless cats and kittens by providing a foster home. Our animals stay in foster homes until a forever home is found for them at our weekly adoption…
description_long: We are always looking for more foster homes so that we can help more cats. Help save the lives of homeless cats and kittens by providing a foster home. Our animals stay in foster homes until a forever home is found for them at our weekly adoption events. Foster parents provide temporary care for cats and kittens in their own home. Some kitties need as little as two weeks of care, while others may need care for several months. Fostering is flexible enough for most lifestyles. By offering your time, energy and home to an animal in need, you prepare the animal for adoption into a permanent home by helping them to learn to be part of a family. NBCR provides all veterinary care and medicine for foster animals, plus plenty of support to foster volunteers. How does the foster process work? When NBCR receives animals who are not ready to be put up for adoption, we contact our foster volunteers. There will be plenty of opportunities to foster, so there is no pressure to take an animal if your family situation does not allow it at a certain time. Our volunteers receive information about the animal’s condition and needs. Foster families may need to bring the animal periodically to be examined by the veterinarian, receive vaccinations or other treatment as well as public meet and greet events at the Garland PetSmart on Saturdays and Sundays If you live in the Garland or DFW area and would like to help with this rewarding task, please consider volunteering with New Beginnings Cat Rescue. To find out more about us and to submit application, please see our website at: https://nbcrtx.org/volunteer-application

org_name: SHARE! High School Student Exchange Program, Exchange Students
opportunity_title: Change a student’s life and your own, through the power of cultural exchange
description_short: Make a global impact right from your home. 🌍 SHARE! High School Exchange Program is seeking volunteer host families and adults to welcome international high school exchange students (ages 14–18) for a semester or full academic year. This is a unique…
description_long: Make a global impact right from your home. 🌍 SHARE! High School Exchange Program is seeking volunteer host families and adults to welcome international high school exchange students (ages 14–18) for a semester or full academic year. This is a unique cultural exchange opportunity where you can support a student’s American experience while gaining a new global perspective—without leaving home. Students come from countries such as France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Japan, Brazil, Vietnam, Bolivia, Hong Kong, Czech Republic, and Poland and are excited to attend a local U.S. high school and become part of daily family life. Why Host an Exchange Student? Hosting is a meaningful way to: Promote cultural exchange and global understanding Support international students studying in the U.S. Build lifelong international relationships Create a rewarding experience for your family Program Benefits ✔ Students are carefully screened and selected ✔ Students speak English and are motivated to engage ✔ Students have their own spending money for personal expenses ✔ Students are covered by medical insurance ✔ Ongoing support from local Exchange Program Coordinators (EPCs) ✔ 24/7 program assistance Who Can Volunteer as a Host Family? We are looking for welcoming, supportive homes , including: Families with or without children Single adults or single parents Retired couples and empty nesters No prior hosting experience required. Host Family Responsibilities Host families provide: A safe and supportive home environment A bed (shared or private room) Meals and inclusion in daily family life Encouragement and cultural connection Time Commitment Short-term "Welcome Families" Semester (5–6 months) Academic year (10 months) Ready to make a difference? 📞 800-941-3738 🌐 sharesouthwest.org/contact Learn more, ask questions, or request student profiles today!

opportunity_title: Cinco De Mayo Soup Bowl Fiesta
description_short: 🌮 Cinco de Mayo Taco Bowl Volunteer Event — Soup Angels (Dallas, TX) Date: Saturday, May 2 Time: 1:00–3:00 p.m. Location: Chef Irvin’s Home (Lakewood, Dallas) About Soup Angels Soup Angels is a brand‑new, grassroots outreach organization based in…
description_long: 🌮 Cinco de Mayo Taco Bowl Volunteer Event — Soup Angels (Dallas, TX) Date: Saturday, May 2 Time: 1:00–3:00 p.m. Location: Chef Irvin’s Home (Lakewood, Dallas) About Soup Angels Soup Angels is a brand‑new, grassroots outreach organization based in East Dallas. We prepare chef‑crafted meals and deliver them directly to our unhoused neighbors with dignity, compassion, and joy. Every bowl we make becomes a moment of nourishment, connection, and hope. Volunteer Opportunity Join us for a fun, hands‑on volunteer event as we prepare Cinco de Mayo Taco Bowls for distribution across Dallas. This is a lively, community‑driven kitchen session — no cooking experience required, just a good heart and a willingness to help. What Volunteers Will Do Assemble fresh, delicious Taco Bowls Portion, pack, and prepare meals for outreach Enjoy a joyful, music‑filled, community atmosphere Support a brand‑new organization serving neighbors experiencing homelessness Optional: Stay afterward to help with the first round of deliveries Why It Matters Cinco de Mayo weekend is one of our biggest outreach pushes of the spring. Every volunteer helps us increase the number of meals we can share with neighbors who often go days without a warm, dignified plate of food. Your two hours of service directly translate into more bowls, more smiles, and more care delivered across Dallas. Who We’re Looking For Individuals, friends, coworkers, and families Teens welcome with an adult Anyone who loves community, food, and joyful service People who want to make a real impact in a short amount of time Location Chef Irvin’s home kitchen in the Lakewood neighborhood of Dallas. (Address provided upon registration.) How to Join Sign up on Idealist, then invite a friend, coworker, neighbor — or share the opportunity on your social media. The more hands we have, the more neighbors we can feed.

description_short: Mi Escuelita Preschool warmly invites you and your team to come read to our preschool scholars! Reading to young children fosters language development, builds communication and social skills, and ignites a love for learning. Studies show that early…
description_long: Mi Escuelita Preschool warmly invites you and your team to come read to our preschool scholars! Reading to young children fosters language development, builds communication and social skills, and ignites a love for learning. Studies show that early reading also supports cognitive growth, encourages imagination, and prepares children for academic success. Community role models play a vital role in this journey by showing young children the value of reading and learning. Join us in creating these impactful moments and helping set the foundation for their success in school and beyond! Contact Erika Graciano, egraciano@miescuelita.org , or call at 214-526-0220 x108 for more details. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held at organization location(s) Event can be held virtually Donation Requested Donations are not required, but they are greatly appreciated and help support our students and classrooms.

opportunity_title: Client Services Coordinator
description_short: Customer Service Heroes / Extraordinaires -- We Need Your SuperPower to Help Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our customer service efforts . Your kind heart and organized work style can make a real…
description_long: Customer Service Heroes / Extraordinaires -- We Need Your SuperPower to Help Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our customer service efforts . Your kind heart and organized work style can make a real difference. Client Services Coordinator: Works directly with our clients to process their applications via email and phone calls. Our prospective clients submit applications for our services (critical home repairs) and the Client Services Coordinator validates their inputs and compares it against our protocols. Mostly remote; On occasion, this volunteer may be asked to travel to a client's home or need to attend volunteer meetings in-person. Prior experience with Sales Force and the Google suite of tools is helpful but we can also provide training. Having a computer and telephone is required. Mission: As a national organization, Rebuilding Together is a leading nonprofit in safe and healthy housing. Across the country, Rebuilding Together’s affiliates complete ~10,000 rebuild projects each year. Rebuilding Together North Texas revitalizes neighborhoods by bringing volunteers and communities together to repair homes and rebuild the lives of low-income homeowners in the Denton, Dallas, Tarrant, Collin, and Rockwall counties.

opportunity_title: Co-Director of Marketing
description_short: Help Shape the Vision Behind TEDxSouthlake TEDxSouthlake is seeking an Adult Volunteer Co-Director of Marketing to help guide the strategy, storytelling, and overall direction of our marketing and communications efforts. This leadership role…
description_long: Help Shape the Vision Behind TEDxSouthlake TEDxSouthlake is seeking an Adult Volunteer Co-Director of Marketing to help guide the strategy, storytelling, and overall direction of our marketing and communications efforts. This leadership role collaborates closely with the marketing team and board to ensure that our messaging, campaigns, and outreach reflect the spirit of TEDxSouthlake. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys thinking strategically while empowering a creative volunteer team. Responsibilities : Help refine marketing strategy, brand voice, and storytelling direction Collaborate with team leads across marketing, content, and social media Coordinate and oversee the social media posting calendar, ensuring content is scheduled and aligned across platforms Help maintain a central marketing calendar for campaigns, announcements, and key milestones Support planning and execution of campaigns for speaker reveals, ticket sales, and community engagement across owned media Guide and support volunteer contributors, helping to keep projects organized and moving forward Identify opportunities to amplify reach through partnerships, community engagement, and cross-platform storytelling Monitor high-level performance insights and help adjust strategy based on what resonates with the audience Ideal Candidate: A strategic thinker with experience in marketing, communications, brand strategy, or nonprofit leadership. Someone who enjoys mentoring others and translating big ideas into coordinated action. Location : Remote, but the desire to meet in person for the event in Southlake, TX, in November 2026. Time Commitment : Approximately 2-5 hours/week, including virtual monthly meetings. Apply Now: Send your resume and a brief introduction to info@TEDxSouthlake.com

opportunity_title: College Students: Volunteer with Lifted Hospice | Dallas
description_short: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Our volunteers sit and visit with patients, one on one activities, or group activities at the Memory Care/Assisted Living facilities. Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. Options of activities to do with patient: playing games/cards, listening to music (or singing/playing instruments), Veteran services, arts & crafts, reading to patient, etc... Group activities at facility: arts & crafts, games, music Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life.

No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life.

opportunity_title: College Students: Volunteer with Lifted Hospice | Plano & Surrounding Areas
description_short: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Our volunteers sit and visit with patients, one on one activities, or group activities at the Memory Care/Assisted Living facilities. Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. Options of activities to do with patient: playing games/cards, listening to music (or singing/playing instruments), Veteran services, arts & crafts, reading to patient, etc... Group activities at facility: arts & crafts, games, music Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life.

opportunity_title: Colleyville, TX: Host an Exchange Student-Volunteer families needed for GCISD for new school year!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Collin County, TX - Drive cancer patients to and from treatment
description_short: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their…
description_long: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their appointments. Most rides are Monday-Friday during the day. You choose the rides that fit your schedule, with a suggested minimum of 2 rides per month. Volunteer drivers must: Be age 18-84 Have a valid driver’s license and safe driving record Use a reliable, insured vehicle Pass background and motor vehicle records checks Complete online training and use our online ride scheduling tool Learn more at cancer.org/drive. We need you now more than ever. Watch our video to learn more: Hope Behind The Wheel

description_short: Kindful Health is a privately owned hospice company that is focused on providing patients and their families with the full promise of the hospice benefit in the State of Texas. We are quickly growing and in need of musically inclined volunteers like…

We are quickly growing and in need of musically inclined volunteers like you to help us with the most important aspect of hospice: community. If you are interested in making a positive difference in the Fort Worth community by playing music to or singing with our hospice patients then this volunteer opportunity is for you! Please let us know if you are interested and I will reach out promptly to tell you all about how your musical talents are so needed for our Hospice patients and their families!

opportunity_title: Community & Outreach Manager
description_short: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. Community & Outreach Manager: Acts as…
description_long: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. Community & Outreach Manager: Acts as the brand ambassador, fostering engagement by responding to comments, engaging with followers, and managing online conversations. Supports event promotion through social media, email outreach, and community engagement. Helps increase attendance and awareness of the event and the nonprofit’s mission. Mission: As a national organization, Rebuilding Together is a leading nonprofit in safe and healthy housing. Across the country, Rebuilding Together’s affiliates complete ~10,000 rebuild projects each year. Rebuilding Together North Texas revitalizes neighborhoods by bringing volunteers and communities together to repair homes and rebuild the lives of low-income homeowners in the Denton, Dallas, Tarrant, Collin, and Rockwall counties.

opportunity_title: Community Advocate for Black Health & Liberation - W.H.E.W
description_short: Key Responsibilities: ("Assisting with community outreach events, providing administrative support for health programs, or participating in advocacy campaigns.") Qualifications: ("A passion for reproductive justice, strong communication skills, and…
description_long: Key Responsibilities: ("Assisting with community outreach events, providing administrative support for health programs, or participating in advocacy campaigns.") Qualifications: ("A passion for reproductive justice, strong communication skills, and a commitment to centering Black communities.") Training provided: ("All volunteers will receive an orientation on our mission and specific project training.") Time Commitment: ("We are looking for a commitment of 5 hours per month.") Qualifications A deep commitment to reproductive justice and Black liberation. Strong communication skills and an ability to engage with diverse community members. Reliability and a proactive "collective leadership" mindset. Local to Texas or the South (for in-person roles) or digitally savvy (for remote support). Why Volunteer with Us? By joining W.H.E.W., you aren’t just "helping"—you are participating in a bold new force for the future. You will gain experience in grassroots organizing and be part of a legacy built on nearly two decades of leadership.

opportunity_title: Community Ambassadors  - The Extra Money Expo
description_short: Help Us Build the Future of Local Business: Join Our Outreach Team! The Extra Money Expo is more than just an event—it’s a platform for small business owners to help provide local families with access to opportunities that will accelerate their path…
description_long: Help Us Build the Future of Local Business: Join Our Outreach Team! The Extra Money Expo is more than just an event—it’s a platform for small business owners to help provide local families with access to opportunities that will accelerate their path to success. We are currently looking for Community Ambassadors to help us connect with the businesses, speakers, and partners who will make this year’s experience truly transformative. We aren't looking for "salespeople"—we are looking for people who care about the local ecosystem and want to help us invite the right voices to the gathering. What you’ll be doing: Facilitating Connections: You’ll be reaching out to potential partners to see how they can contribute to and benefit from this community-driven event. Creating Opportunities: You’ll help ensure that the right business owners get the exposure they need to flourish. We’ve Got Your Back We want you to feel confident and prepared when you reach out. To make this process seamless and stress-free, we provide: The "Connection Toolkit": Easy-to-use email templates and conversation guides that keep your outreach authentic and human-centered. Flexible Engagement Options: We have a variety of ways for businesses to get involved, ensuring there is a comfortable fit for every budget and goal. Early Access Benefits: You’ll be able to offer exclusive early-involvement incentives to the people you contact. The "Impact Appreciation" Program Your time and effort directly fuel the growth of this event. To honor your commitment to our community, we offer a 10% Impact Stipend for every partner or exhibitor who joins the Expo through your outreach. This is our way of ensuring that your contribution to the success of your fellow business owners is recognized and valued. Ready to help us grow? If you’re passionate about connecting people and helping small businesses thrive, we would love to have you on the team.

opportunity_title: Community Canvassing Volunteer
description_short: Volunteer Opportunity, April 25 TWCUYC is seeking community volunteers to support a non-partisan civic education and outreach effort in Dallas County. On April 25th at 9:30 AM, volunteers will join us in the Bonton community, Precinct 3029, to help…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunity, April 25 TWCUYC is seeking community volunteers to support a non-partisan civic education and outreach effort in Dallas County. On April 25th at 9:30 AM, volunteers will join us in the Bonton community, Precinct 3029, to help share clear, accurate information about the voting process and available resources. Our goal is simple. Make sure neighbors have access to information and remove barriers that prevent people from participating in their community. Event Details Date: April 25 Time: 9:30 AM Meet-Up Location: William Blair “Rochester” Park 3000 Rochester St, Dallas, TX 75215 Canvassing Area: Precinct 3029, Bonton Community What Volunteers Will Do Volunteers will work in small teams to engage community members. Activities may include: Door-to-door community outreach Sharing voter education materials and resources Providing information about polling locations and voting requirements Helping community members check voter registration status Connecting residents to resources for obtaining identification documents Encouraging respectful conversations about civic participation No prior canvassing experience is required. Training and materials will be provided. Time Commitment This is a single-day opportunity on April 25. Plan for a 3 to 4-hour canvassing shift following a brief orientation. Who Should Volunteer We welcome volunteers who: Care about strengthening their community Enjoy talking with neighbors and meeting new people Believe everyone should have access to civic information Want to help remove barriers, such as a lack of ID or voting information Students, community leaders, faith groups, and first-time volunteers are all welcome. What Volunteers Receive Orientation and outreach training Canvassing materials and talking points Community leadership experience Opportunities to connect with local partners The chance to support informed civic participation in Dallas Contact 📞 972-559-9201 📧 Volunteer@twcuyc.org

opportunity_title: Community Engagement Lead
description_short: BUiLT International is seeking a Community Engagement Lead to help strengthen and grow one of the most dynamic technology communities in the Dallas–Fort Worth region. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys connecting people, building…
description_long: BUiLT International is seeking a Community Engagement Lead to help strengthen and grow one of the most dynamic technology communities in the Dallas–Fort Worth region. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys connecting people, building relationships, and creating welcoming environments where professionals can thrive. As Community Engagement Lead, you will help cultivate relationships with BUiLT members, event attendees, and partner organizations while ensuring that new professionals entering the community feel seen, connected, and valued. Responsibilities Welcome and connect attendees at BUiLT events such as Black Tech Thursday Help onboard new members into the BUiLT community Facilitate introductions between professionals, founders, and leaders in the ecosystem Gather feedback from attendees to improve community experiences Support community conversations across digital platforms Ideal Candidate Strong communicator who enjoys bringing people together Passionate about technology, professional growth, and community leadership Organized and proactive in relationship-building Interested in expanding their professional network within the tech ecosystem What You’ll Gain Direct access to one of the fastest-growing tech communities in DFW Networking opportunities with founders, engineers, and technology leaders Leadership experience within a nationally recognized tech organization

opportunity_title: Community Engagement Volunteers Needed | Get Volunteer Hours
description_short: We need more volunteers at our community events! Our events take place in the Dallas-Fprt Worth Metropolitan area, so you must be in this area. Opportunities range from tabling to voter registration, etc. This is a great way to get involved in the…
description_long: We need more volunteers at our community events! Our events take place in the Dallas-Fprt Worth Metropolitan area, so you must be in this area. Opportunities range from tabling to voter registration, etc. This is a great way to get involved in the community, make an impact, and engage with community members, all while having lots of fun. We tend to participate in very exciting and popular events. We will be signing off on volunteer hours! Please reach out to learn more about the various events we will be hosting.

opportunity_title: Community Events Coordinator
description_short: Making Dallas a No-Kill city is a big job and as a volunteer-based organization, we need a lot of great volunteers to do it. As an Adoption Events Coordinator, you'll support the assistant events director and development director in the Dallas Pets…
description_long: Making Dallas a No-Kill city is a big job and as a volunteer-based organization, we need a lot of great volunteers to do it. As an Adoption Events Coordinator, you'll support the assistant events director and development director in the Dallas Pets Alive events, to maximize opportunities for networking, donor relations, adoptable animals’ exposure, DPA Awareness, and fundraising. Team: Events Reports to: Community Engagement Manager Works with: Correspondence with Community Engagement Manager on a regular basis. Close interaction with DPA volunteers. Ongoing interaction with the public, venue hosts, and DPA supporters. Responsibilities: Sending request to foster families for confirmation of attendance for each adoption event Following up as required on questions and concerns raised by foster families concerning current and upcoming events Sending confirmation to leadership and adoption events team weekly of expected attendance at each event Regularly attending events to ensure consistency of process, adherence to DPA event policies, and to maintain relationship with event leads and volunteers Leading events on a periodic basis (after several events and training) Assisting with any other duties needed to help drive Dallas Pets Alive!’s mission Qualifications/Skills: HS diploma/equivalent or higher degree of education required Must be proficient with use of email and have regular phone access Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfareMust not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Dallas Pets Alive! Musts: Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times A motivational, can-do attitude to represent Dallas Pets Alive! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare Time Commitment: Varies per event

description_short: Food Distribution every 3rd Saturday. We need at least 4 people to assist with the sorting and placing food items into bags. After the preparation is completed the volunteers will travel to 4520 Gus Thomason for the distribution of the food to the…
description_long: Food Distribution every 3rd Saturday. We need at least 4 people to assist with the sorting and placing food items into bags. After the preparation is completed the volunteers will travel to 4520 Gus Thomason for the distribution of the food to the residents. We practice social distancing, wearing of masks and gloves. Water provided.

opportunity_title: Community Outreach & Follow-Up Volunteer for 1MC Dallas
description_short: We're looking for a driven, organized volunteer to lead our ecosystem outreach initiative. This role is perfect for someone who loves making connections, doesn't get discouraged easily, and wants to make a real impact on Dallas's small business…
description_long: We're looking for a driven, organized volunteer to lead our ecosystem outreach initiative. This role is perfect for someone who loves making connections, doesn't get discouraged easily, and wants to make a real impact on Dallas's small business community. What You'll Do Research local small business support organizations, City of Dallas programs, nonprofit partners, and entrepreneurial ecosystem resources Send a prepared outreach email on behalf of 1MC Dallas to each identified organization Follow up persistently (5-7 times) with contacts who don't respond Track outreach in a simple spreadsheet (template provided) Report back with responses, partnership interest, and any leads worth escalating What We're Looking For Someone comfortable sending professional emails and following up without feeling awkward about it Basic research skills — knowing how to find the right contact person at an organization Organized enough to track who's been contacted and when A genuine interest in entrepreneurship, small business, or community development is a big plus No prior experience required — just persistence and a good attitude Time Commitment Approximately 3–5 hours per week, flexible schedule, fully remote. This is a great opportunity for a retiree or a professional looking to give back. Why This Matters Your work will directly help Dallas's earliest-stage founders connect with the resources that can change their business trajectory.

opportunity_title: Community Outreach & Partnership Specialist
description_short: TWCUYC is seeking a volunteer to help build relationships with businesses, community organizations, faith communities, and service providers. This role will help expand resources and opportunities for the communities we serve. What You’ll Do…
description_long: TWCUYC is seeking a volunteer to help build relationships with businesses, community organizations, faith communities, and service providers. This role will help expand resources and opportunities for the communities we serve. What You’ll Do Research potential community partners Contact organizations and businesses regarding partnership opportunities Assist with sponsorship and donation outreach Help identify volunteer recruitment opportunities Attend community events when available Maintain partnership and outreach records What We’re Looking For Strong communication skills Professional phone and email etiquette Comfortable networking and building relationships Self-motivated and dependable Experience in outreach, business development, fundraising, sales, or community engagement is preferred but not required Time Commitment Minimum of 5 hours per week.

opportunity_title: Community Outreach Coordinator
description_short: Gateway Alliance Project is a startup non-profit 501(c)(3) organization geared at improving the roads of re-entry to formerly incarcerated women, and by providing diversion alternatives for at-risk youth prior to engagement with the carceral system.…
description_long: Gateway Alliance Project is a startup non-profit 501(c)(3) organization geared at improving the roads of re-entry to formerly incarcerated women, and by providing diversion alternatives for at-risk youth prior to engagement with the carceral system. We offer a variety of life skills trainings, and are happy to announce the opening of our transitional home for women April 2024. We are in serach of a Community Outreach Coordinator to connect our organization with the surrounding community. Duties include brainstorming potential partnerships or methods for engagement with the local community, maintaining a calendar for outreach events and working closely with community partners to plan and execute various outreach events.

opportunity_title: Community Outreach Volunteer
description_short: Connect Foundation is seeking compassionate and dependable volunteers to support community outreach efforts throughout the Dallas-Fort Worth area. Volunteers may assist with distributing emergency kits to individuals experiencing homelessness and…
description_long: Connect Foundation is seeking compassionate and dependable volunteers to support community outreach efforts throughout the Dallas-Fort Worth area. Volunteers may assist with distributing emergency kits to individuals experiencing homelessness and others facing crisis situations, as well as supporting outreach events, community engagement efforts, donation organization, and other service initiatives. This opportunity is ideal for individuals who are passionate about helping others, serving their community, and making a direct impact through hands-on volunteer work. Both individual volunteers and groups are encouraged to apply.

opportunity_title: Community representative in Dallas, TX
description_short: EurAupair is one of the oldest and largest au pair organizations in the USA. We are non-profit and designated by the US State Department's exchange program. Our mission is to improve understanding among people of different countries through cultural…
description_long: EurAupair is one of the oldest and largest au pair organizations in the USA. We are non-profit and designated by the US State Department's exchange program. Our mission is to improve understanding among people of different countries through cultural exchange across America. EurAupair is looking for a local representative to work with au pairs and host families in Dallas, TX Must live within 1hour drive from the posted location Good communication skills No previous experience needed Responsibilities: Meet with the au pair every month and contact the family every month. Provide guidance to host families and Au Pairs before and during their program year, among other similar tasks Benefits: Very flexible hours approximately 6 hours per month Reimbursement: $900.00 / year and per family Establishing relationships with local business, civic groups, clubs and other organizations as you promote awareness of the EurAupair Program For more information, visit our website at www.euraupair.com/counselors

org_name: Medical City Hospice and Family Care
opportunity_title: Companion for Hospice patients in the Plano area
description_short: Many people at the end of their lives are unable to go out and see their friends and do the hobbies they loved so much. If you become a Hospice Volunteer, you can go to them! No matter where they live or how much they are still able to do, people on…
description_long: Many people at the end of their lives are unable to go out and see their friends and do the hobbies they loved so much. If you become a Hospice Volunteer, you can go to them! No matter where they live or how much they are still able to do, people on hospice benefit from the friendly presence of volunteers. Volunteer with Medical City Hospice and Family Care and provide companionship and caring presence to hospice patients living at a DFW community or at home in McKinney. You will receive free orientation and training and will be able to meet patients to read, share stories, listen to music, or provide respite for family members. Your companionship and caring presence ensures that patients spend the end of their life in dignity and comfort. Medical City Hospice and Family Care has many other locations in the DFW area and surrounding cities. Training and orientation will be scheduled to fit to your schedule. Contact Medical City Hospice and Family Care 469-872-4642 PLEASE CHECK YOUR SPAM INBOX.

We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and we are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. This is a flexible schedule and can be as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Feel free to contact Tonya Choate at 682-777-0722 or send an email to tchoate@ThreeOaksHospice.com to learn more.

opportunity_title: Compassion in Action: Hospice Patient Visits
description_short: Hospice care is centered on comfort, compassion, and living fully—every single day. At Halcyon Home, our hospice team focuses on managing symptoms while supporting patients and their families with dignity, kindness, and heart. Hospice is not about…
description_long: Hospice care is centered on comfort, compassion, and living fully—every single day. At Halcyon Home, our hospice team focuses on managing symptoms while supporting patients and their families with dignity, kindness, and heart. Hospice is not about giving up; it’s about embracing meaningful moments and honoring life. Our volunteers play a special role by spending quality time with patients facing terminal illness. From reading a favorite book and sharing conversations to enjoying hobbies and simple moments together, volunteers bring companionship, comfort, and a sense of normalcy into each visit. Just as importantly, volunteers offer families a chance to recharge—whether that means running errands, attending appointments, or simply taking a much-needed break—knowing their loved one is in caring company. Volunteers are often the highlight of the day for both patients and families. Opportunities are available in patients’ homes or in senior community settings, allowing you to make a genuine difference wherever you are placed. No prior experience is needed—training and orientation are provided. If you’re ready to brighten lives and be part of something meaningful, we’d love to hear from you. Please contact our Volunteer Team at Tford@halcyonhome.com .

opportunity_title: Compassionate Hearts Initiative
description_short: Job description: At Anointed Health Group, we believe every individual deserves dignity, comfort, and compassionate support during life’s most meaningful moments. Our hospice volunteers play a vital role in bringing this mission to life by offering…
description_long: Job description: At Anointed Health Group, we believe every individual deserves dignity, comfort, and compassionate support during life’s most meaningful moments. Our hospice volunteers play a vital role in bringing this mission to life by offering presence, encouragement, and care to patients and their families. Hospice Volunteers provide compassionate, non-medical support to patients facing life-limiting illnesses, as well as their families and caregivers. Volunteers work alongside our interdisciplinary hospice team to enhance quality of life through emotional, social, and practical support. By becoming a hospice volunteer, you are helping provide comfort, dignity, and support during one of life’s most important journeys. Your time and presence can make a lasting impact. Key Responsibilities Offer companionship through conversation, reading, music, or simply being present Provide emotional support to patients and their families Assist with light, non-clinical tasks as appropriate Give caregivers respite time by staying with patients Support office staff with administrative tasks or special projects Observe and report any concerns to the hospice team Qualifications Compassionate, dependable, and respectful of diverse backgrounds and beliefs Strong communication and listening skills Ability to follow written and verbal instructions Must be 18 years or older Reliable transportation (for patient-facing roles) Willingness to complete orientation and background check Time Commitment Flexible scheduling based on your availability Opportunities available weekdays, evenings, and weekends Ongoing, recurring volunteer opportunities Work Location: In person

opportunity_title: Couple Shuffle 5k/10k/15k- Omaha Volunteer Assignments
description_short: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and…
description_long: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and each race is a chance to create a ripple effect of positivity - encouraging families to get moving, friends to bond over fitness, and communities to come together around a shared goal: better health. We couldn’t make any of this happen without the help of incredible volunteers like you. We’re currently seeking volunteers for our upcoming event - and we need YOUR help to bring it to life. Whether you’re signing up solo, with your family, or rallying a group of coworkers or classmates, there’s a spot for everyone to get involved and make a difference. As a thank-you, every volunteer gets ONE of the following: One future race entries (Bodies Race) A $20 Amazon gift card BONUS: Community service hours signed off Volunteer Roles - Here’s How You Can Help: Aid Station Crew - Highest Need Keep our runners fueled and focused. Hand out water, pick up discarded cups, and bring positive energy to everyone who passes by. Every high five counts! Course Monitor Be the spirit of the course! Guide runners on the right path and cheer them on with signs, music, or even a fun costume. You’ll be keeping the course safe and fun. Lead Bike Volunteer - High Priority Lead the way! Use your own bike and helmet to guide runners throughout the course. You’ll pre-ride the route in advance and be a key player on race day. Course Photographer If you love capturing special moments, this one’s for you. Bring your own camera and head to a scenic course spot to catch the smiles, the grit, and the joy of race day. Finish Line Volunteer Be part of the celebration! Hand out medals, manage the awards table, and help runners feel the reward of their hard work. We’re looking for individuals, friend groups, student organizations, businesses, and families who want to be part of something bigger. Come spend your morning making memories, supporting wellness in your city, and cheering on every single finish line victory. Let’s build a healthier community, together. Sign up to volunteer today - we can’t do it without you!

opportunity_title: Court Appointed Community Service (CCS)
description_short: The SPCA of Texas is currently open to Court Ordered Community Service (CCS) volunteers at both our Dallas (2400 Lone Star Dr., Dallas, TX 75212) and Ellis (2570 Farm to Market Rd 878, Waxahachie, TX 75165) locations. CCS volunteers must be a…
description_long: The SPCA of Texas is currently open to Court Ordered Community Service (CCS) volunteers at both our Dallas (2400 Lone Star Dr., Dallas, TX 75212) and Ellis (2570 Farm to Market Rd 878, Waxahachie, TX 75165) locations. CCS volunteers must be a minimum of 10 years old and will exclusively perform non-animal handling tasks. For volunteers under 15, participation alongside a parent or guardian, aged 18 or older, is required, and we only offer service at our Dallas location. Our Ellis County Animal Care Center requires all volunteers to be at least 18 years of age or older. Given our work with a vulnerable population, we can only accept individuals with non-violent offenses such as basic traffic violations, DWI, minor possession of alcohol, and select minor drug-related charges (evaluated on a case-by-case basis). For Court Appointed Community Service volunteers, there is a required $35 volunteer fee to cover the associated costs of initiating a new volunteer (including background checks, volunteer insurance, etc.). Additionally, all CCS volunteers must undergo a non-refundable background check before commencing their service.
