opportunity_title: Creative Content Creator
description_short: CREATIVE CONTENT CREATOR We are seeking a talented and reliable volunteer Creative Content Creator to support two mission-driven brands: Ripples in Africa ( ripplesinafrica.org ) — a nonprofit dedicated to community impact across Africa Y aji Spice…
description_long: CREATIVE CONTENT CREATOR We are seeking a talented and reliable volunteer Creative Content Creator to support two mission-driven brands: Ripples in Africa ( ripplesinafrica.org ) — a nonprofit dedicated to community impact across Africa Y aji Spice ( yajispice.com | blog.yajispice.com) — a West African-inspired spice and recipe brand Scope of Work: Yaji Spice ---2–3 images per week (blog posts and social media) — 1 video per week optimized for Facebook, Instagram, Pinterest, LinkedIn, and TikTok — Seasonal graphics for Shopify store — Product label design as needed Ripples in Africa — Blog post images and social media videos on an ongoing basis Ideal Candidate: — Proficient in graphic design and video editing (Canva, Adobe, CapCut, or equivalent) — Familiar with content specs across major social platforms — Able to maintain a consistent weekly workflow independently — Portfolio demonstrating visual content creation required — Interest in food, culture, or international nonprofit work is a plus This is an unpaid remote volunteer position with flexible scheduling, estimated at 3–5 hours per week. It offers an excellent opportunity to expand your portfolio across multiple brands and content types. To apply, please send a brief introduction and links to your portfolio or relevant work samples to: 📧 Stephie@ripplesinafrica.org yajispice.com | blog.yajispice.com | ripplesinafrica.org

opportunity_title: Creative Designer / Visual Designer (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Location: Remote Visual Designer, will be the creative mind behind the visual storytelling of our events. Your work will directly shape the visual identity of our platform and bring our offerings to life. You'll design posters and promotional…
description_long: Location: Remote Visual Designer, will be the creative mind behind the visual storytelling of our events. Your work will directly shape the visual identity of our platform and bring our offerings to life. You'll design posters and promotional materials that resonate with our audience's values—balance, authenticity, and spiritual growth. Key Responsibilities: Design engaging and visually cohesive posters for workshops, retreats, online classes, festivals, and community events. Collaborate with event coordinators and marketing teams to understand event themes, speaker profiles, and branding needs. Ensure all designs align with the platform’s holistic, calm, and nature-inspired brand aesthetics. Create multiple versions or formats for social media, email marketing, and print distribution. Manage a design calendar and deliver high-quality assets on time. Stay up to date with visual trends in wellness, spirituality, and conscious branding. Requirements : Proven experience in graphic design, particularly poster development (portfolio required). Proficiency in tools like Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, Canva, or Figma. Strong eye for typography, color harmony, and layout. Ability to interpret spiritual, natural, and emotional themes into visual form. Understanding of branding, especially in wellness or holistic industries. Excellent communication and time management skills. Added bonus - Background or interest in wellness, yoga, meditation, or holistic practices. Experience in motion graphics or short-form video creation. Familiarity with content marketing or social media strategy.

opportunity_title: DARCC Winter 2026 Volunteer Training
description_short: DARCC's Volunteer Training will take place February 23rd - March 12th. The training is a total of 40 hours, will be virtual (via Zoom), and is required by the Office of the Attorney General (OAG) for anyone who is interested in volunteering for…
description_long: DARCC's Volunteer Training will take place February 23rd - March 12th. The training is a total of 40 hours, will be virtual (via Zoom), and is required by the Office of the Attorney General (OAG) for anyone who is interested in volunteering for agencies that work directly with sexual assault survivors. Our volunteers can assist survivors by answering calls from our 24/7 crisis hotline, accompanying survivors during a sexual assault forensic exam, and by providing legal advocacy (e.g., court accompaniment, accompaniment to law enforcement agencies for initial reports/interviews) for survivors. Participants must attend all 40 hours and receive a certificate of completion before volunteering. Background checks will be done for all applicants.

opportunity_title: DFW Brain Tumor Walk & Race
description_short: Be part of something meaningful. Volunteer with us! Join us for the DFW Brain Tumor Walk and Race , hosted by the National Brain Tumor Society, and help create a powerful day of hope, remembrance, and community. This annual 5K walk/run brings…
description_long: Be part of something meaningful. Volunteer with us! Join us for the DFW Brain Tumor Walk and Race , hosted by the National Brain Tumor Society, and help create a powerful day of hope, remembrance, and community. This annual 5K walk/run brings together patients, survivors, caregivers, families, and supporters—all united to conquer and cure brain tumors. 📍 Location: AT&T Stadium, Arlington 📅 Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026 We’re looking for enthusiastic volunteers to help bring this special event to life. Whether you can give a few hours or the whole morning, your support makes a real difference. Volunteer roles may include: Assisting at registration and the t-shirt tent Supporting food and beverage stations Course and venue set-up, including signage Course marshal and water stations Crowd control and participant support Helping with family-friendly activities And much more! No prior experience is needed—just a positive attitude and a willingness to help. Volunteers receive event training, support, and the gratitude of a community deeply impacted by brain tumors. 👉 Register to volunteer for free and be part of a day that truly matters. Questions? Contact us at DFWinfo@braintumor.org Together, we can make this an unforgettable event for everyone who attends.

opportunity_title: DFW Metroplex- Hospice Volunteers & Student Interns
description_short: Hospice volunteers do something rare: they make sure no Texan faces the end of life alone. Concho Hearts Hospice — locally owned and nurse-founded — is building volunteer teams in all 20 of our communities, from Fort Worth to Houston, Austin to…
description_long: Hospice volunteers do something rare: they make sure no Texan faces the end of life alone. Concho Hearts Hospice — locally owned and nurse-founded — is building volunteer teams in all 20 of our communities, from Fort Worth to Houston, Austin to Texarkana, San Angelo to the Rio Grande Valley. There's a role for almost anyone: Patient companions — weekly visits for conversation, music, or quiet presence Bereavement volunteers — supporting grieving families after a loss Veteran-to-veteran volunteers — if you've served, your presence means something to a fellow veteran that no one else can offer Office & outreach volunteers — behind-the-scenes support that keeps care flowing Students: we host social work, nursing, counseling, and chaplaincy interns — field coordinators, let's talk. Full training and support provided. Requirements: 18+, background check Time is the one thing everyone runs out of. Give someone yours.

opportunity_title: Dallas Coffee and Snacks at the VA
description_short: Join Soldiers’ Angels every Friday at the Dallas VA Medical Center as we serve coffee, donuts, and healthy snacks to veterans throughout the hospital. It’s a simple way to say thank you—and bring a smile to those who’ve served us. Volunteer Details:…
description_long: Join Soldiers’ Angels every Friday at the Dallas VA Medical Center as we serve coffee, donuts, and healthy snacks to veterans throughout the hospital. It’s a simple way to say thank you—and bring a smile to those who’ve served us. Volunteer Details: 📍 Dallas VA Medical Center 4500 S Lancaster Rd, Dallas, TX 75216 🕗 Fridays | 8:00 AM – 11:30 AM 👔 Attire: Business Casual A Soldiers’ Angels lead volunteer will be on-site to welcome you, guide you, and make sure your experience is meaningful and fun! Important: The VA requires an Occasional Volunteer Form submitted at least 7 days prior to the event. 📩 Contact Thom at tstraley@soldiersangels.org to get started and confirm your spot.

opportunity_title: Dallas, TX - Drive cancer patients to and from treatment
description_short: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their…
description_long: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their appointments. Most rides are Monday-Friday during the day. You choose the rides that fit your schedule, with a suggested minimum of 2 rides per month. Volunteer drivers must: Be age 18-84 Have a valid driver’s license and safe driving record Use a reliable, insured vehicle Pass background and motor vehicle records checks Complete online training and use our online ride scheduling tool Learn more at cancer.org/drive. We need you now more than ever. Watch our video to learn more: Hope Behind The Wheel

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Dallas, TX: a neighbor could use a hand with yard help · Ref IWTMYL-LAE
description_short: In Dallas, TX, a lawn has become difficult to keep up with, and a nearby volunteer could provide meaningful relief with a short, straightforward visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and well-maintained. You're bringing a smile to a…
description_long: In Dallas, TX, a lawn has become difficult to keep up with, and a nearby volunteer could provide meaningful relief with a short, straightforward visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and well-maintained. You're bringing a smile to a neighbor's face with your kindness. This is a home shared by a household of two, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. A tidy, well-kept yard makes a visible difference the same day and helps a neighbor keep their home comfortable and cared for. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Dallas, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-LAE&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-LAE

opportunity_title: Dallas-Fort Worth, TX - Fundraising Walk Volunteers Needed!
description_short: We are hosting the Annual Dallas-Fort Worth VisionWalk at Reverchon Park on March 21, 2026! We are looking for volunteers to help out with day-of execution of the event! This may include passing out refreshments, helping with registration, and…
description_long: We are hosting the Annual Dallas-Fort Worth VisionWalk at Reverchon Park on March 21, 2026! We are looking for volunteers to help out with day-of execution of the event! This may include passing out refreshments, helping with registration, and aiding participants along the walk route. To learn more: Give.FightingBlindness.org/DallasFortWorthVisionWalk or FightingBlindness.org Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held at a volunteer group location (e.g. office, park) Donation Requested

description_short: We would like to work with your company or corporation for a volunteer day. Are you looking for an opportunity to serve your community and create teamwork in a different setting outside of your daily work? We can take up to 15 people in our location…

opportunity_title: Death Doula Volunteer
description_short: We would love to have you as a Death Doula Volunteer ! “Our worst fear isn’t the end of life but the end of memories” – Author Tom Rachman Navigating end-of-life care can take an emotional toll on patients, families, and loved ones. As you know,…
description_long: We would love to have you as a Death Doula Volunteer ! “Our worst fear isn’t the end of life but the end of memories” – Author Tom Rachman Navigating end-of-life care can take an emotional toll on patients, families, and loved ones. As you know, end-of-life doula can be a great resource to add to your loved one’s hospice care team. Not many people are even aware of end-of-life doulas, but they can be a wonderful source of comfort for families and patients alike. Hospice Teams and Doulas can and often do work hand in hand. This is where we need your help! Volunteers are a required part of Hospice and therefore we can help you gain Hospice Patient experiences as you build your Death Doula profession. The process is simple! 1) Fill out the online interest form. 2) I will then contact you & set up a time to chat via phone so that you can introduce yourself & ask any questions. 3) Complete required onboarding documents and training. 4) Meet in person for coffee or tea (orientation) 5) We will schedule a day/time that works for us both to do your 1st Patient visit together! We look forward to you joining our Awesome Volunteer Team! It's truly an amazing and humbling experience.

opportunity_title: Denton County, TX - Drive cancer patients to and from treatment
description_short: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their…
description_long: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their appointments. Most rides are Monday-Friday during the day. You choose the rides that fit your schedule, with a suggested minimum of 2 rides per month. Volunteer drivers must: Be age 18-84 Have a valid driver’s license and safe driving record Use a reliable, insured vehicle Pass background and motor vehicle records checks Complete online training and use our online ride scheduling tool Learn more at cancer.org/drive. We need you now more than ever. Watch our video to learn more: Hope Behind The Wheel

opportunity_title: DevOps Engineer
description_short: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer)** Role Overview Menzies is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented DevOps Engineer to strengthen the reliability, automation, and resilience of our AWS-hosted web application. This role…
description_long: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer)** Role Overview Menzies is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented DevOps Engineer to strengthen the reliability, automation, and resilience of our AWS-hosted web application. This role bridges our development and cybersecurity (DevSecOps) functions to ensure smooth, secure, and repeatable deployments from GitHub to AWS. The DevOps Engineer will be responsible for building CI/CD pipelines, standardizing branch workflows, managing environment consistency, and overseeing critical infrastructure maintenance tasks such as patching, configuration drift prevention, AMI management, and continuity planning. This is an excellent opportunity for a technically curious volunteer to gain real-world DevOps and cloud experience while contributing to a mission-driven nonprofit organization. Key Responsibilities CI/CD Pipeline and Deployment Automation Design, implement, and maintain CI/CD pipelines using AWS CodePipeline, CodeBuild, and/or GitHub Actions. Collaborate with developers to define and enforce a branching strategy (feature/*, uat, main) ensuring consistent deployments and clean releases. Create and apply Pull Request templates and merge policies to improve testing and review discipline. Automate environment setup and deployment processes using Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform or AWS CloudFormation. Integrate security and quality checks (e.g., dependency scanning, secret validation, and SAST/DAST tools) into the pipeline in collaboration with the DevSecOps Engineer. Configuration Management and Environment Health Manage and monitor AWS Elastic Beanstalk and related AWS environments for stability, scalability, and performance. Maintain AMI versions and enforce configuration consistency across development, UAT, and production environments to avoid drift. Use AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager or equivalent automation scripts to ensure all EC2 instances are patched and compliant. Configure CloudWatch dashboards and alerts to track uptime, cost, and performance trends. Work closely with the DevSecOps Engineer to respond to AWS Security Hub , GuardDuty , or Config findings related to infrastructure. Backup, Disaster Recovery, and Continuity Implement and maintain automated backup routines for application environments, configuration data, and RDS instances. Conduct test restorations to validate backup integrity and ensure that recovery procedures function as expected. Develop, document, and periodically update disaster recovery (DR) procedures, including restoration workflows for AWS environments. Draft and maintain an IT continuity plan outlining recovery timelines, dependencies, and escalation steps. Generate a monthly continuity and environment health report , summarizing patching, backup verification, and key system metrics. Required Qualifications Candidates should demonstrate one or more of the following: Bachelor’s degree (or current enrollment) in Computer Science, Information Technology , or a related discipline. Entry-level certification or equivalent hands-on experience such as: AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner or AWS Certified SysOps Administrator – Associate CompTIA Security+ (preferred baseline for all technical volunteers) A GitHub or GitLab portfolio demonstrating experience with CI/CD automation, scripting, or AWS environments. Technical Expectations: Proficiency with AWS services (Elastic Beanstalk, EC2, S3, IAM, RDS, CloudWatch). Familiarity with Git and version-control workflows. Basic scripting ability in Bash, Python, or PowerShell . Understanding of CI/CD concepts , cloud security basics , patching , and infrastructure reliability . Strong documentation and communication habits. Preferred Qualifications Experience automating deployments with AWS CodePipeline , CodeBuild , or GitHub Actions . Familiarity with Terraform or CloudFormation for Infrastructure-as-Code. Working knowledge of AWS Systems Manager , Backup , and Patch Manager . Exposure to monitoring tools (e.g., CloudWatch). Experience planning disaster recovery or continuity exercises . Prior experience in a DevOps or cloud operations volunteer or professional role. Personal Attributes Proactive and resourceful, with strong attention to operational detail. Communicates clearly across development, security, and management teams. Eager to learn modern DevOps tools and best practices. Organized, dependable, and committed to following through on deliverables. Values transparency, documentation, and collaborative problem-solving.

opportunity_title: DevOps Engineer
description_short: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer) About the Organization Menzies Philanthropic Foundation (Menzies Mission) is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting orphans and vulnerable families through resources, education, and…
description_long: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer) About the Organization Menzies Philanthropic Foundation (Menzies Mission) is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting orphans and vulnerable families through resources, education, and holistic development rooted in the Menzies 7 Keys of Needs (Housing, Health & Nutrition, Education, Safety, Family, Faith, and Love). Believing that self-actualization can break cycles of poverty and trauma, we provide mental and emotional support, healing-centered guidance, and programs that help individuals realize their potential and transform their future. In partnership with our social-impact company, Me Is We , we are developing a regenerative financial ecosystem to ensure long-term sustainability and expansion for our mission. At the heart of this ecosystem is My Source Network —a global holistic wellness community platform for healing and mindful connection. Through practitioners, conscious events, and transformational resources, MSN supports individuals on their journeys toward balance, empowerment, and belonging, cultivating a worldwide community rooted in healing, authenticity, and collaboration. We are an impact-driven organization that values innovation, accountability, and collaboration. If you are passionate about meaningful work and creating scalable positive change, we would be honored to have you join our team. All interviews will take place virtually through Application Process To apply, review the job description and submit your resume, portfolio, and cover letter to hr@menziesmission.org . Ensure accurate contact information for application status updates. If selected, be prepared to discuss qualifications and alignment with the organization's mission. For questions, contact hr@menziesmission.org. We look forward to reviewing your application. Equal Opportunity Employer: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Accommodation for the application process is available upon request. Google Meet . Please note that the session will be recorded to support our evaluation and documentation process.*

opportunity_title: DevSecOps Engineer
description_short: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer)** Role Overview We are seeking a motivated DevSecOps Engineer to strengthen the security and reliability of our cloud applications and CI/CD workflows. This position continues the…
description_long: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer)** Role Overview We are seeking a motivated DevSecOps Engineer to strengthen the security and reliability of our cloud applications and CI/CD workflows. This position continues the ongoing security automation efforts established under the current DevSecOps role. The ideal candidate combines foundational security knowledge with curiosity and a willingness to learn new tools and processes while contributing directly to meaningful non-profit technology projects. Key Responsibilities Integrate and maintain security automation within CI/CD pipelines, including: Static analysis (SAST) for code vulnerabilities. Dynamic testing (DAST) for runtime exposures. Dependency and vulnerability scanning using Semgrep , OWASP ZAP , and Dependabot . Secret scanning to detect exposed keys, tokens, or credentials before release. Collaborate with developers and DevOps to triage and remediate vulnerabilities identified in builds or environments. Contribute to secure GitHub branching, approval, and dependency-management workflows. Monitor and interpret findings from AWS-native security services such as Security Hub, GuardDuty, Config, CloudTrail, and CloudWatch to detect misconfigurations or suspicious activity. Support least-privilege IAM configurations and secure AWS role usage. Document security standards, scanning procedures, and remediation guidelines. Participate in incident response simulations and post-incident analysis. Required Qualifications CompTIA Security+ certification (or higher). Understanding of Git workflows and CI/CD concepts. Familiarity with at least one scanning or automation tool (e.g., OWASP ZAP, Semgrep, Trivy, or Snyk). Basic awareness of AWS security services , IAM principles , and secure configuration practices. Scripting or automation experience in Bash, Python, or PowerShell . Strong analytical mindset, attention to detail, and commitment to continuous learning. Preferred Qualifications Coursework or prior experience in cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP). Exposure to Infrastructure-as-Code or container security concepts. Interest in pursuing advanced certifications such as AWS Security Specialty , CySA+ . Experience contributing to open-source or volunteer security projects. Personal Attributes Security-first mindset with proactive problem-solving. Communicates clearly and collaborates respectfully with developers and operations. Thrives in a remote, asynchronous team environment. Curious, adaptable, and passionate about building secure, ethical technology solutions.

opportunity_title: Development Coordinator (Donor Focus)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking a highly motivated and relationship-oriented individual to join our team as a Development Coordinator (Donor Focus) on a volunteer basis. This remote role is perfect for someone passionate about building strong connections with supporters and contributing directly to the sustainability and growth of our mission. As the Development Coordinator, you will play a crucial role in cultivating, engaging, and stewarding our valued donors, ensuring they feel connected to our impact and inspired to continue their support. Your efforts will directly empower our fundraising initiatives, enabling us to expand our reach and deepen our impact in the communities we serve. Key Responsibilities: Identify, research, and qualify prospective individual and corporate donors. Manage and maintain accurate donor records within our CRM system, ensuring data integrity and timely updates. Develop and implement personalized donor communication strategies, including thank you notes, impact reports, and appeal letters. Assist in the planning, coordination, and execution of fundraising campaigns, events, and donor engagement activities. Support the development of compelling fundraising materials, presentations, and proposals. Track and report on donor engagement metrics and fundraising progress. Cultivate and nurture relationships with existing donors to encourage continued and increased support. Collaborate closely with the Board of Directors and executive team on fundraising strategies and initiatives. Actively contribute to a positive and collaborative remote team environment. Qualifications: Proven experience in fundraising, donor relations, sales, marketing, or a related field with a strong focus on relationship building. Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively, both verbally and in writing, with diverse audiences. Experience with donor management software or CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce, Raiser's Edge) is highly preferred. Exceptional organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail, especially in data management and communication. Strong interpersonal skills with a professional and empathetic demeanor. Ability to work independently, take initiative, and manage multiple tasks in a remote setting. Proficiency in Microsoft Outlook and other Microsoft Office Suite applications (e.g., Word, Excel, PowerPoint), as well as Google Workspace (e.g., Docs, Sheets, Slides). Passion for SelfSoulLiberate's mission and a commitment to health equity and mental wellness. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration.

opportunity_title: Digital Copywriter
description_short: Paws in the City is looking for a Digital Copywriter! This role is responsible for creating compelling written content that drives engagement, donations, and awareness across digital channels. Commitment: Approximately 2–4 hours per month, with…
description_long: Paws in the City is looking for a Digital Copywriter! This role is responsible for creating compelling written content that drives engagement, donations, and awareness across digital channels. Commitment: Approximately 2–4 hours per month, with flexibility based on campaign needs. Responsibilities: Write and edit content for social media, email campaigns, and website updates Develop messaging that aligns with PAWS brand voice and mission Collaborate with the marketing team on campaigns and promotions Optimize content for clarity, engagement, and consistency Assist with storytelling for adoptable animals and success stories

org_name: Now I Lay Me Down To Sleep
opportunity_title: Digital Retouch Artist Volunteer | Remote Opportunity to Support Families Experiencing Infant Loss
description_short: Use your editing skills to create lasting memories for families experiencing the loss of a baby. Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep (NILMDTS) is seeking compassionate Digital Retouch Artist volunteers to provide professional editing services for remembrance…
description_long: Use your editing skills to create lasting memories for families experiencing the loss of a baby. Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep (NILMDTS) is seeking compassionate Digital Retouch Artist volunteers to provide professional editing services for remembrance photography sessions. Though this role happens behind the scenes, the impact is immeasurable . Your work helps create beautiful images that families may cherish for a lifetime. As a Digital Retouch Artist, you'll use your technical skills and artistic eye to thoughtfully edit remembrance photographs with care and sensitivity. What You'll Do: Professionally edit remembrance images submitted by volunteer photographers, medical providers, or families using Adobe Photoshop Perform sensitive and detailed retouching work according to NILMDTS standards Complete editing assignments within requested turnaround times Maintain image confidentiality and professionalism Communicate with staff and volunteers as needed Why This Role Matters: For many families, these images become priceless keepsakes and tangible reminders of the love they have for their baby. Your work helps transform photographs into treasured memories. Qualifications: Experience with photo editing and retouching Proficiency in editing using Adobe Photoshop Strong attention to detail Ability to handle emotionally sensitive subject matter Dependable communication and timely completion of assignments Compassion and commitment to serving families Training & Support NILMDTS provides: Retouching guidelines and standards Volunteer support and educational resources Ongoing connection to a mission-driven volunteer community Adobe Photoshop reimbursement with minimum editing qualifications met Time Commitment Assignments are completed remotely and can be accepted based on your availability and schedule. The turnaround time once a session is picked up is 2 weeks. Volunteers and families can submit up to 20 images for retouching. About Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep provides remembrance photography to families experiencing the loss of a baby through a network of compassionate volunteer photographers and digital artists serving families around the world.

opportunity_title: Direct Patient Volunteer - Southwest (Duncanville, DeSoto, Cedar Hill)
description_short: Volunteers get to make their own schedule! Our Non-medical Hospice Direct Care Volunteer provides support to patients living with a terminal illness, in addition to their caregivers and family. Support is provided in a variety of ways, including…
description_long: Volunteers get to make their own schedule! Our Non-medical Hospice Direct Care Volunteer provides support to patients living with a terminal illness, in addition to their caregivers and family. Support is provided in a variety of ways, including companionship, socialization, and caregiver relief. There is no medical background needed - our Hospice volunteers do not interact with the patient in any medical or personal hygiene capacity (i.e., volunteers do not give baths, get the patient in/out of bed, or to/from the bathroom, give medication). Some examples of what a Direct Care Volunteer could do: Provide companionship Socialization and/or support to hospice patients and their families/caregivers Reading to the patient Playing games Writing letters to or on behalf of the patient Assisting with light household duties (watering plants, walking pets, washing dishes, emptying and taking out the garbage, etc.

org_name: Information Technology Disaster Resource Center, Inc
opportunity_title: Disaster Tech Response Team - DFW
description_short: When disaster strikes, communities need more than food and water — they need to communicate, coordinate, and recover. The Information Technology Disaster Resource Center (ITDRC) deploys teams of volunteer technology professionals to…
description_long: When disaster strikes, communities need more than food and water — they need to communicate, coordinate, and recover. The Information Technology Disaster Resource Center (ITDRC) deploys teams of volunteer technology professionals to disaster-affected communities across the United States. We restore internet connectivity, set up temporary communications infrastructure, and provide technical support to families, nonprofits, and emergency responders on the ground — often within 24–48 hours of a declared disaster. What You'll Do Depending on your skills and availability, you may: Deploy or support temporary Wi-Fi and network infrastructure in disaster-impacted areas Provide remote or on-site IT support to displaced residents and relief organizations Help set up device charging stations, computer labs, or communications hubs Assist with cybersecurity, data recovery, or systems administration for affected enterprises Contribute remotely to logistics, coordination, project management, or documentation Who We're Looking For We welcome Tech Professionals of all backgrounds, including: Network Engineers and Systems Administrators Cybersecurity Professionals Web Developers and Software Engineers Help Desk and Tech Support Specialists Project Managers and Operations leads with a tech background No two deployments are the same — your skills will be matched to real needs in the field. Flexible, On Your Terms ITDRC is a fully volunteer-driven organization. You choose when and how you serve. Deployments can be remote or on-site, short-term or extended. We work around your schedule, your expertise, and your capacity. Training is provided. New volunteers receive orientation and onboarding so you're prepared before your first deployment. Join thousands of volunteers nationwide who are using their tech skills to rebuild lives after disaster. Visit ITDRC.org to learn more and apply.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Do You Karaoke?  Lead and Entertain Seniors!
description_short: Sing your heart out with seniors in an assisted living facility. Lead them in the classics and perform your favorites. You will do this in the local nursing home closest to your zip code. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We…
description_long: Sing your heart out with seniors in an assisted living facility. Lead them in the classics and perform your favorites. You will do this in the local nursing home closest to your zip code. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Watch our video to learn more: Hospice - Something More

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Do You Play A Musical Instrument?  Play for Hospice Patients
description_short: Use your musical talent to give quality of life to hospice patients. Whether you are a vocalist or you play an instrument, you can bring the joy of live music to patients for comfort, joy, and relaxation. Music can reduce pain, bring calm, stir the…
description_long: Use your musical talent to give quality of life to hospice patients. Whether you are a vocalist or you play an instrument, you can bring the joy of live music to patients for comfort, joy, and relaxation. Music can reduce pain, bring calm, stir the imagination, and bring back wonderful memories. Music volunteers are needed for one-on-one or duet assignment with hospice patients. Requirements: Volunteer Training Modules (at-home online) TB Test - free Background Check - free Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers can read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Watch our video to learn more: Hospice - Something More

opportunity_title: Do you like to Quilt, Sew, Knit, or Crochet for babies?
description_short: Whether you enjoy quilting, sewing, knitting, crochet, or would like to learn those skills, come volunteer with us! LOVE THY BABY specializes in meeting the needs of babies facing health or economic crisis. We also need people to iron, fold, and…
description_long: Whether you enjoy quilting, sewing, knitting, crochet, or would like to learn those skills, come volunteer with us! LOVE THY BABY specializes in meeting the needs of babies facing health or economic crisis. We also need people to iron, fold, and sort at our work meetings, 10-2 every Thursday in Farmers Branch. NOTE: Many of our volunteers make items at home and either mail them to us or bring them to our work meetings. Learn more on our Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/lovethybaby or read about all the programs in our Love Thy Neighbor, Inc. organization by visiting our website at www.LTNLoveThyNeighbor.org .

opportunity_title: DoNot Stop 5k/10k & Half Marathon - Dallas Volunteer Assignment
description_short: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and…
description_long: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and each race is a chance to create a ripple effect of positivity - encouraging families to get moving, friends to bond over fitness, and communities to come together around a shared goal: better health. We couldn’t make any of this happen without the help of incredible volunteers like you. We’re currently seeking volunteers for our upcoming event - and we need YOUR help to bring it to life. Whether you’re signing up solo, with your family, or rallying a group of coworkers or classmates, there’s a spot for everyone to get involved and make a difference. As a thank-you, every volunteer gets ONE of the following: Two future race entries (Bodies Race) A $20 Amazon gift card BONUS: Community service hours signed off Volunteer Roles - Here’s How You Can Help: Aid Station Crew - Highest Need Keep our runners fueled and focused. Hand out water, pick up discarded cups, and bring positive energy to everyone who passes by. Every high five counts! Course Monitor Be the spirit of the course! Guide runners on the right path and cheer them on with signs, music, or even a fun costume. You’ll be keeping the course safe and fun. Lead Bike Volunteer - High Priority Lead the way! Use your own bike and helmet to guide runners throughout the course. You’ll pre-ride the route in advance and be a key player on race day. Course Photographer If you love capturing special moments, this one’s for you. Bring your own camera and head to a scenic course spot to catch the smiles, the grit, and the joy of race day. Finish Line Volunteer Be part of the celebration! Hand out medals, manage the awards table, and help runners feel the reward of their hard work. We’re looking for individuals, friend groups, student organizations, businesses, and families who want to be part of something bigger. Come spend your morning making memories, supporting wellness in your city, and cheering on every single finish line victory. Let’s build a healthier community, together. Sign up to volunteer today - we can’t do it without you!

org_name: THE HAPPY PET PROJECT, POWERED BY TEXAS GOOD DEEDS PROJECT INC
opportunity_title: Dog  Behavior Evaluator
description_short: About the Role: The Happy Pet Project is seeking experienced, hands-on volunteers to serve as Dog Evaluators . This critical role helps ensure that the dogs we rescue are a good fit for our programs, supports our shelter partners, and sets up our…
description_long: About the Role: The Happy Pet Project is seeking experienced, hands-on volunteers to serve as Dog Evaluators . This critical role helps ensure that the dogs we rescue are a good fit for our programs, supports our shelter partners, and sets up our fosters for success. As a Dog Evaluator, you’ll use your knowledge and experience to perform behavioral assessments on dogs of all sizes — helping the rescue make informed decisions for sustainable care. What You’ll Do: Conduct hands-on evaluations of dogs at partner shelters to assess behavior and temperament. Work with dogs ranging from 5–100 lbs , both indoors and outdoors, year-round. Document observations and provide feedback to support foster placement and care decisions by our leadership team. Collaborate with shelter staff to understand each dog’s background and needs. Requirements: Located in the DFW area of Texas and able to travel to partner shelters. Dog handling experience required ; knowledge of canine body language is a bonus. Comfortable handling dogs of all sizes, indoors and outdoors, year-round. In-person training required (2–4 hours). Flexible schedule — you can sign up to evaluate dogs based on your availability and shelter operating hours after training. Why Volunteer with Us: Dog Evaluators play a key role in the success of our rescue efforts , ensuring that each dog is set up for a happy, safe future in foster or adoptive homes. Your expertise directly helps save lives while supporting both our rescue and community partners.

opportunity_title: Dog Walking Heroes and Cleaning Crew
description_short: The SPCA of Texas is seeking dedicated volunteers to provide love and attention to our furry friends throughout the week (Monday-Friday). With over 200 animals in our care at any given time, we need volunteers to assist with caring for dogs awaiting…
description_long: The SPCA of Texas is seeking dedicated volunteers to provide love and attention to our furry friends throughout the week (Monday-Friday). With over 200 animals in our care at any given time, we need volunteers to assist with caring for dogs awaiting adoption, including socializing, walking, playing, reinforcing basic commands, brushing, feeding, and cleaning up as needed. We offer flexible shifts every day of the week, from 9 am to 6 pm. Volunteers aged 10 and above are welcome to participate. For those under 15, volunteering alongside a parent or guardian aged 18 or older is required. For younger volunteers, aged 10 to 11, opportunities primarily focus on cat handling after training, while those aged 12 and older can handle both dogs and cats. To begin volunteering, you'll need to create a volunteer account and complete a few initial steps. There is a $15 volunteer fee per person to cover the initial costs of onboarding new volunteers. Once these steps are completed, you'll be eligible to sign up for an Orientation session, where you'll receive further training and information.

opportunity_title: Dogs Matter Foster Coordinator (Virtual)
description_short: Making Dallas a No-Kill city is a big job and as a volunteer-based organization, we need a lot of great volunteers to do it. Dallas Pets Alive relies heavily on foster homes to save animals from euthanasia. As a Dogs Matter Foster Coordinator, you…
description_long: Making Dallas a No-Kill city is a big job and as a volunteer-based organization, we need a lot of great volunteers to do it. Dallas Pets Alive relies heavily on foster homes to save animals from euthanasia. As a Dogs Matter Foster Coordinator, you will be making a huge impact on this mission by assisting our partner Dogs Matter with networking for fosters. *To be considered, please complete our official volunteer application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ Team: Foster Reports to: Dogs Matter Team, Foster Director, Assistant VP of Animal Operations VP of Animal Operations and the Executive Director. Works with: Will have regular and continuous contact virtually or over the phone with team leaders Role Summary: The Dogs Matter Foster Coordinator serves as the primary liaison between Dallas Pets Alive and Dogs Matter. This role ensures that every incoming Dogs Matter pet is documented, supported, and placed with an approved foster through clear communication, organized workflows, and consistent follow through. Key Responsibilities: Serve as the main point of contact for all Dogs Matter foster inquiries and “tags” Receive assigned tasks through Doobert, gather all information and photos, and upload to Trello Tag social media team members in Trello when promotion is needed Maintain and update the Dogs in Need Google Album and Trello board Confirm foster approval and coordinate handoffs between Dogs Matter, fosters, and DPA Communicate with fosters and/or volunteers via Slack, email, Facebook groups, and direct outreach Post foster needs in the DPA Foster Facebook Group and relevant breed or community pages Respond to and support inquiries sent to foster-apps@ regarding Dogs Matters fosters Ensure medical records, surrender forms, foster agreements, and other documentation are obtained and in the right hands Track foster placement progress and follow up as needed What Success Looks Like: Pets are processed quickly, accurately, and consistently across systems Fosters feel supported and informed throughout the inquiry and intake process Dogs Matter receives timely updates and clear communication Trello, Doobert, Slack, and ShelterLuv remain organized and up to date Pets are placed efficiently and safely into approved foster homes, foster parents, and other volunteers. Qualifications/Skills: HS diploma/equivalent required with related animal/rescue group experience Ability to work virtually (phone, email) Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Dallas Pets Alive! Musts: Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times A motivational, can-do attitude to represent Dallas Pets Alive! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare Time Commitment: Up to 10 - 15 hours per week

opportunity_title: Donor Outreach Volunteer, Soiree
description_short: DPA Soirée Donor Outreach Volunteer Position Requirements A well-connected individual or someone comfortable making introductions, with a desire to support Dallas Pets Alive, Dogs Matter, and Humane Society of Dallas County. This role is ideal for…
description_long: DPA Soirée Donor Outreach Volunteer Position Requirements A well-connected individual or someone comfortable making introductions, with a desire to support Dallas Pets Alive, Dogs Matter, and Humane Society of Dallas County. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys connecting people and supporting a meaningful cause. No cold outreach required—this is all about leveraging existing relationships. Position Purpose Support the success of the DPA Soirée by expanding our network of donors and partners through your personal and professional connections. Your Role Help grow our reach and fundraising efforts by introducing DPA to individuals and businesses who: Care about animal welfare Are open to supporting or partnering with the event Have the connections, influence, or resources to make an impact Volunteer Goals (Participate in as many as possible) Identify potential donors within your personal or professional network Make introductions to businesses, brands, or individuals Facilitate warm connections or share contact information when appropriate Help uncover new partnership or sponsorship opportunities Why Get Involved Make a meaningful impact for animals in need Strengthen your personal and professional relationships Expand your network and sphere of influence Be part of a collaborative, mission-driven team

opportunity_title: Donor Relationship Coordinator
description_short: Here at Dallas Pets Alive we rescue and help the healthy and treatable pets at greatest risk for euthanasia at our partner shelters. We also provide assistance and resources to pet owners having problems so significant that they need to surrender…
description_long: Here at Dallas Pets Alive we rescue and help the healthy and treatable pets at greatest risk for euthanasia at our partner shelters. We also provide assistance and resources to pet owners having problems so significant that they need to surrender their pets to a shelter. We are looking for professional and passionate volunteers to help make North Texas a No-Kill community. In our rescue program, we focus on: Rescuing healthy and treatable pets at North Texas shelters at the greatest risk of being euthanized Reducing owner surrender through our Positive Alternatives to Shelter Surrender (PASS) program Opening kennel space by transporting pets to partners in the U.S. and Canada in need of adoptable animals Challenging the way our community views rescue animals. We believe that adoption is not only the right thing to do but THE thing to do! PURPOSE OF THE POSITION: The Donor Relationship Coordinator will support the donor management side of fund development by supporting: donor campaigns, communications, and appreciation. The position works closely with the Development Director, Donor Appreciation Team, Marketing Director, and Donors. SUMMARY: Making North Texas No-Kill is a big job and we need great staff to do it. As our Donor Relationship Coordinator you will be responsible for ensuring that our donors get the appreciation and feel connected to the impact of their gifts. This role supports DPA’s fundraising processes that ensure the progress of fundraising campaigns, donor retention, relationships with donors, and help us to raise necessary funds for our mission. TIME REQUIREMENT: 5-8 hours a week. This is a hybrid/remote position. Department and/or Team: Development Reports to: Development Director Works with: Development Director, Donor Appreciation Team, Marketing Director, and Donors across DPA, Dogs Matter, and HSDC Responsibilities: Supporting donor appreciation team with reports to send emailed/postal Thank Yous Managing periodic fundraising mailings: tracking and evaluating results Supporting donor communications including new donor welcomes and Champion updates Supporting donor appreciation events Assisting with any other event/fundraising/donor support duties needed to help drive our collective missions and support our values CONTACTS WITH OTHERS: Correspondence with community and donors seeking services, business partners, CEO, Board of Directors, Leadership, and volunteers. CONTROLS OVER WORK : This position reports to the Development Director Qualifications/Skills: Must be at least 21 years of age Experience working with confidential and sensitive information Experience with donor management or CRM programs is helpful Access to a computer and ability/willingness to master new development software/database applications Microsoft Excel / Word / Powerpoint/Google software / Gmail Able to operate independently and yet demonstrate strong team / collaboration skills Is detail-oriented, possesses good follow-up skills, and is able to manage deadlines Takes a proactive approach and is not afraid to ask questions or offer suggestions Is committed to seeking new ideas, monitoring industry trends, and recommending best practices to help innovate our email campaigns Dallas Pets Alive! Musts: Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times A motivational, can-do attitude to represent Dallas Pets Alive! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Time Commitment: 5-8 hours per week, average over a month

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Duncanville, TX: a neighbor could use a hand with lawn care · Ref IWTMYL-N10
description_short: A neighbor in Duncanville, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A neighbor in Duncanville, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and well-maintained. You're helping a neighbor feel more comfortable and proud of their home. This is a home shared by a household of two, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Duncanville, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-N10&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-N10

opportunity_title: EDITOR - CONTENT TEAM (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Reports To: Marketing Manager Editor will play a pivotal role in shaping our brand’s narrative and voice across all content touchpoints. He/She will ensure every piece of content — from blogs and practitioner profiles to newsletters, event…
description_long: Reports To: Marketing Manager Editor will play a pivotal role in shaping our brand’s narrative and voice across all content touchpoints. He/She will ensure every piece of content — from blogs and practitioner profiles to newsletters, event promotions, and wellness guides — reflects our brand’s authenticity, compassion, and integrity. He/She will collaborate closely with writers, practitioners, and the marketing team to maintain editorial excellence, accuracy, and alignment with our conscious living values. Key Responsibilities Editorial Oversight: Review and refine all written content including articles, practitioner spotlights, wellness resources, product descriptions, and event write-ups. Voice & Tone Consistency: Uphold a compassionate, trustworthy, and empowering voice that resonates with our wellness community. Editorial Calendar: Plan and manage the publishing schedule in coordination with the marketing and partnerships teams. SEO & Discoverability: Edit with SEO best practices in mind to enhance content visibility and organic reach. Collaboration with Writers & Practitioners: Guide contributors (internal and external) to align their submissions with editorial standards and brand messaging. Fact-Checking & Ethical Integrity: Ensure all wellness-related claims are accurate, responsibly represented, and backed by credible sources or practitioner expertise. Content Workflow: Oversee editing, approvals, and publication workflow for all content channels — web, social, and email. Performance Monitoring: Track engagement metrics and collaborate with marketing on strategies to elevate content impact. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in English, Journalism, Communications, Marketing, or related field. 5 years of experience in editing, content management, or digital publishing (preferably in wellness, lifestyle, or conscious living sectors). Excellent written and verbal communication skills with an eye for detail and tone. Familiarity with SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Google Analytics) and CMS platforms (WordPress, Webflow, or similar). Strong understanding or personal interest in wellness, holistic health, mindfulness, and conscious living. Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced startup environment. Preferred Qualifications Prior experience editing or managing content in the wellness, holistic health, lifestyle, or conscious living industries. Understanding of alternative healing modalities (e.g., Ayurveda, Reiki, yoga, meditation, mindfulness, energy healing). Experience collaborating with wellness practitioners, coaches, or holistic brands . Familiarity with content marketing and storytelling for community-based or platform-driven businesses. Strong sense of ethical communication in wellness — ensuring credibility, inclusivity, and compassion in content. Proficiency with content planning tools (Flowlu) and marketing analytics platforms (Google Analytics, GoHighLevel). Experience in visual storytelling or coordination with designers and social media teams. Awareness of SEO and user intent within wellness-related search trends. Certification or coursework in wellness communication, holistic health, or digital marketing is a plus.

opportunity_title: EXPERIENCED MARKETING VOLUNTEER WANTED!
description_short: MARKETING VOLUNTEER WANTED! Do you have a passion for marketing and a heart for serving your community? We are Hope Health Care, providing superior hospice care in Dallas, Collin, Rockwall and Hunt counties. We are looking for a marketing volunteer,…
description_long: MARKETING VOLUNTEER WANTED! Do you have a passion for marketing and a heart for serving your community? We are Hope Health Care, providing superior hospice care in Dallas, Collin, Rockwall and Hunt counties. We are looking for a marketing volunteer, with real world experience, to assist our Marketing department, remotely and in our Garland office as needed, to increase awareness of our mission and the compassionate care we provide to patients and families in these communities. We're looking for someone with experience in creating and/or maintaining: Social media presence Marketing event preparations Community outreach and event promotion Creative marketing communications Digital marketing Your expertise can make a meaningful difference by helping educate healthcare providers, patients and families and community agencies about the many benefits of hospice care that Hope can provide. Whether you can volunteer a few hours a week or more, the time you give and the skills you share will have a lasting impact. If you're interested in using your marketing experience to support a mission that truly matters, we'd love to hear from you! Please contact our Volunteer Coordinator, Rita West at 214-274-7696 to learn more about this exciting volunteer opportunity.

opportunity_title: Embajadores por Mil Mujeres - Dallas
description_short: Mil Mujeres Mil Mujeres es una organización sin ánimo de lucro que ofrece servicios legales a bajo costo a la comunidad latina y promueve la lucha por la defensa de los derechos de inmigrantes víctimas de crímenes violentos en los Estados Unidos a…
description_long: Mil Mujeres Mil Mujeres es una organización sin ánimo de lucro que ofrece servicios legales a bajo costo a la comunidad latina y promueve la lucha por la defensa de los derechos de inmigrantes víctimas de crímenes violentos en los Estados Unidos a través de diferentes campañas a nivel nacional con fines educativos y de información. Descripción del rol: Buscamos personas proactivas con habilidades de comunicación en español (inglés preferible pero no indispensable), vocación de servicio social e interés en participar en campañas, eventos y entrevistas en medios de comunicación promoviendo información relevante sobre la defensa y protección de los derechos de la comunidad inmigrante en los Estados Unidos. Beneficios: Haz parte de nuestro grupo de embajadores y podrás mejorar tu perfil profesional, obtener recomendaciones laborales, ampliar tu red de contactos, certificarte como voluntario, acceder a descuentos en asesorías legales migratorias, clases de inglés, servicios de terapia, clases de ciudadanía, conocer otras culturas y formar parte de nuestra comunidad a nivel nacional. Reembolso de costos de transporte. Proceso: Envía tu hoja de vida actualizada, agendaremos una entrevista para conocerte y responder a tus preguntas. Criterio de selección: Nuestros embajadores serán elegidos por su vocación de servicio, compromiso con la comunidad, entusiasmo, liderazgo y voluntad para trabajar en nuestra actividades. Buscamos personas gestoras de cambio que promuevan la información en la comunidad. Modalidad: 100% presencial Disponibilidad: Para la atención a eventos durante los fines de semana, actividades en consulados, ferias latinas, escuelas e iglesias.

opportunity_title: EmpowHER Fundraising Volunteer
description_short: As a fundraising volunteer, your current responsibilities are as follows: Make at least one fundraiser each month. This includes setting up online fundraisers, coordinating with brands or businesses, or helping organize fundraising events that…
description_long: As a fundraising volunteer, your current responsibilities are as follows: Make at least one fundraiser each month. This includes setting up online fundraisers, coordinating with brands or businesses, or helping organize fundraising events that support refugees in crisis. With this role, there is a lot of creative freedom! We will help you on ideas if you get stuck. You can earn up to 4 hours for every fundraiser setup or major fundraising task (if you feel that your work deserves more hours like coordinating with a brand or managing a larger campaign) let us know because an executive may be able to override it. Communicate with one of the founders or the volunteer coordinator once you have an idea going. They’ll help you set up your fundraiser and make sure everything is going well. Because we are a fairly new organization, we still need YOUR help to help spread the word. If you have any friends that may be interested in volunteering with us, send them the volunteer link !

opportunity_title: EmpowHER Graphic Design Volunteer
description_short: As a social media volunteer, your current responsibilities are as follows: Make at least two social media posts each week. They must be related to a recent event hosted by our organization or a topic related to refugees, or feminism (scroll down for…
description_long: As a social media volunteer, your current responsibilities are as follows: Make at least two social media posts each week. They must be related to a recent event hosted by our organization or a topic related to refugees, or feminism (scroll down for more content ideas). Your post must be engaging and ORIGINAL . If you follow any trends from other creators, make sure to give credit in the caption. You can earn up to 1 hour for every post (if you feel that your work deserves more hours, let us know, and an executive may be able to override it). Because we are a fairly new organization, we still need YOUR help to help spread the word. If you have any friends that may be interested in volunteering with us, send them the volunteer link !

opportunity_title: EmpowHER Writer Volunteer
description_short: As a writer, your current responsibilities are as follows: Make one article each month. They must be related to a recent event hosted by our organization or a topic related to refugees, or feminism. You have complete freedom in choosing your topic,…
description_long: As a writer, your current responsibilities are as follows: Make one article each month. They must be related to a recent event hosted by our organization or a topic related to refugees, or feminism. You have complete freedom in choosing your topic, but make sure to ask an executive for approval first. Your writing must be engaging and ORIGINAL . We do not condone the use of plagiarized work (e.g., copying directly), but you are free to use it for ideas or information as long as it is cited properly. All articles/essays must be below 2000 words. There is no minimum, but if we feel that your work must be longer, you will be notified. Because we are a fairly new organization, we still need YOUR help to help spread the word. If you have any friends that may be interested in volunteering with us, send them the volunteer link !

opportunity_title: Environmental & Climate Justice Internship OR Team Member
description_short: Are you passionate about creating positive change for communities and the environment? Join our team as an Environmental & Climate Justice Intern/Team Member and contribute to meaningful research, community engagement, and impactful content…
description_long: Are you passionate about creating positive change for communities and the environment? Join our team as an Environmental & Climate Justice Intern/Team Member and contribute to meaningful research, community engagement, and impactful content creation! Responsibilities : Research : Conduct in-depth research on environmental justice issues and initiatives. Analyze data and gather insights to support informed decision-making. Community Involvement : Engage with local communities and stakeholders to understand their concerns and needs. Collaborate on projects that address environmental disparities. Content Creation : Develop compelling content, including articles, social media posts, and multimedia materials, to raise awareness about relevant environmental justice topics and ongoing initiatives. Requirements : Passion : Demonstrated commitment to environmental justice and social equity. Communication Skills : Strong written and verbal communication skills to effectively convey complex issues to diverse audiences. Collaborative Spirit : Ability to work in a team-oriented and community-focused environment. Time Commitment : Must be available at least 5 hours per week. Must be living in the United States. Applicants that are currently pursuing / have earned a degree in Environmental Science are preferred, however we encourage applicants of all educational backgrounds to apply!
