org_name: THE HAPPY PET PROJECT, POWERED BY TEXAS GOOD DEEDS PROJECT INC
opportunity_title: Event Engagement Volunteer
description_short: Volunteer Opportunity: Open Volunteer (Events and engagement) About the Role: Love animals? Love exploring your community? Become an Open Volunteer with The Happy Pet Project and help us make a difference while having fun! This role is perfect for…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunity: Open Volunteer (Events and engagement) About the Role: Love animals? Love exploring your community? Become an Open Volunteer with The Happy Pet Project and help us make a difference while having fun! This role is perfect for weekend warriors who want flexibility, enjoy meeting new people, and are ready to share their passion for animals at events across DFW. What You’ll Do at Events: Help set up and break down event tables and booths. Transport adoptable dogs safely to and from events. Share information about our rescue, our mission, and the dogs we serve. Engage with community members, inspire support, and help raise funds and adoptions. Don’t worry — we’ll teach you everything you need to know! What We’re Looking For: Friendly, approachable, and outgoing personality. (Looking to get more comfortable in these skills? We can help you with that!) Dependable and flexible — willing to volunteer a couple of hours on weekends. Passion for animals and a desire to make a positive impact in the community. Enjoy connecting with people and representing our rescue with enthusiasm. Time Commitment: Events are typically a couple of hours on weekends and are spread across DFW. This role is flexible so you can sign up for what works for you - but best suited for volunteers who are dependable and ready to engage with the public. Why Volunteer with Us: As an Open Volunteer, you’ll help dogs find loving homes, expand our rescue’s reach, and strengthen the connections that make our community a better place for pets and people alike — all while having fun exploring DFW and meeting amazing people.

opportunity_title: Event Host
description_short: Paws in the City is looking for Event Hosts! This role is responsible for planning, coordinating, and executing fundraising events that drive awareness and support for PAWS. Event Hosts are outgoing, well-connected individuals who are excited to…
description_long: Paws in the City is looking for Event Hosts! This role is responsible for planning, coordinating, and executing fundraising events that drive awareness and support for PAWS. Event Hosts are outgoing, well-connected individuals who are excited to bring creative ideas to life and engage the community. Commitment: On average, 4–6 hours per event. Time is typically split between event-day activities and virtual coordination leading up to the event. Responsibilities: Coordinate with event partners and venues for planning, marketing, and fundraising efforts Collaborate with PAWS on staffing needs, featured animals, and fundraising strategies Capture and share social media content during events Lead event execution with energy, organization, and a strong community presence

opportunity_title: Event Photographer & Videographer - Dallas TX National Rebuilding Day (April 25, 2026)
description_short: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. On-Site Photographer & Videographer:…
description_long: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. On-Site Photographer & Videographer: Captures high-quality photos & video footage during live events or on-location shoots. Responsible for operating cameras, framing shots, managing lighting and audio conditions, and documenting key moments as directed. Works with the production or creative team to ensure all required footage is recorded for later editing and distribution. Mission: As a national organization, Rebuilding Together is a leading nonprofit in safe and healthy housing. Across the country, Rebuilding Together’s affiliates complete ~10,000 rebuild projects each year. Rebuilding Together North Texas revitalizes neighborhoods by bringing volunteers and communities together to repair homes and rebuild the lives of low-income homeowners in the Denton, Dallas, Tarrant, Collin, and Rockwall counties.

opportunity_title: Event Planning with Lifted Hospice | DFW
description_short: Opportunity Description: Join our events team!! We're looking for volunteers to help plan, coordinate and execute events that serve our patients in DFW. You can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey, and…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Join our events team!! We're looking for volunteers to help plan, coordinate and execute events that serve our patients in DFW. You can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey, and events is a great way to connect with our patients and create a meaningful experience! Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. What You’ll Do: Collaborate ideas for hosting events for our patients Coordinate with the Volunteer Coordinator Prep supplies necessary Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life. Contact Hannah Morris at hannah.morris@liftedhospice.com or 214-656-8402 for more information.

opportunity_title: Event Volunteer
description_short: What if the reason a dog gets adopted… is because you showed up? Every weekend across DFW, there’s a moment where someone locks eyes with a dog and says, “That’s the one.” Those moments don’t happen by accident. They happen because volunteers create…
description_long: What if the reason a dog gets adopted… is because you showed up? Every weekend across DFW, there’s a moment where someone locks eyes with a dog and says, “That’s the one.” Those moments don’t happen by accident. They happen because volunteers create the space for them. From adoption events to happy hours to shelter days, our events are flexible and varied. You sign up for what works with your schedule. No experience required. Just heart and reliability. Sometimes showing up for a few hours changes a dog’s entire life. Ready to get started?

opportunity_title: Event Volunteers for the 2026 Day of Empathy
description_short: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is looking for volunteers to help with Texas Day of Empathy 2026, taking place on Saturday, April 18, 2026, from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM BridgeBuilders 6601 Bexar St, Dallas, Texas 75215 Day of Empathy…
description_long: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is looking for volunteers to help with Texas Day of Empathy 2026, taking place on Saturday, April 18, 2026, from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM BridgeBuilders 6601 Bexar St, Dallas, Texas 75215 Day of Empathy is a community-centered event focused on empathy, education, and connection. The event will include a community resource fair, a panel discussion on Texas parole, voter engagement, HIV education and awareness, food, music, and community resources. Volunteers help make this event welcoming, organized, and impactful for everyone attending. Volunteer roles may include: Event setup and breakdown Helping set up tables, chairs, tents, signage, and materials before the event, and assisting with cleanup after. Greeters and registration Welcoming guests, helping with check-in, handing out materials, and directing attendees. Resource fair support Helping community organizations get set up, assisting with materials, and keeping the resource area organized. Food and beverage support Helping with food setup, distribution, and keeping serving areas clean. Parking and crowd support Helping guide guests, supporting event flow, and assisting staff as needed. Photography/videography Capturing photos or video during the event. Experience is helpful but not required. General volunteers Helping wherever needed to keep the event running smoothly. Volunteer requirements: Must be at least 15 years old Friendly and reliable Able to work as part of a team Comfortable working outdoors if needed Why volunteer: Support a meaningful community event Meet community leaders and organizations Gain event and outreach experience Earn volunteer hours if needed Help connect people to real resources About TWCUYC The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation works to embolden unseen and silenced communities through housing stability, education, community engagement, advocacy, and recovery support. Sign up to volunteer and help make Texas Day of Empathy 2026 a success.

opportunity_title: Events Coordinator
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking proactive and organized Events Coordinators to join our team on a volunteer basis. This remote role is ideal for enthusiastic individuals who are interested in mental health advocacy and want hands-on experience supporting the planning and execution of quarterly mental health panel discussions. These panels feature mental health professionals, subject-matter experts, and community voices , with each event centered around a different mental health topic (e.g., anxiety, burnout, youth mental health, cultural perspectives). The role plays a key part in ensuring each panel is thoughtfully planned, smoothly executed, and impactful for attendees. Key Responsibilities: Help manage logistics, including scheduling, material preparation, and attendee registration. Support the creation and distribution of promotional materials for events. Coordinate with internal teams and external partners to ensure smooth event operations. Assist in on-the-day event support, which may include virtual or in-person tasks depending on the event type. Maintain organized records of event details, budgets, and participant information. Contribute to post-event evaluations and reporting. Qualifications: Strong organizational and time management skills with attention to detail. Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. Proficiency in Microsoft Outlook and other Microsoft Office Suite applications (e.g., Word, Excel, PowerPoint), as well as Google Workspace (e.g., Docs, Sheets, Slides). Prior experience in event planning, coordination, or a related field is a plus, but not required. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration.

opportunity_title: Events and Ministry Coordinator (Multiple Locations & Flexible Opportunities)
description_short: Job Description: Are you passionate about building community and making a meaningful impact where you live? Apartment Life (AL) is looking for Coordinators to serve in apartment communities across the country. Events and Ministry Coordinators get to…
description_long: Job Description: Are you passionate about building community and making a meaningful impact where you live? Apartment Life (AL) is looking for Coordinators to serve in apartment communities across the country. Events and Ministry Coordinators get to create and provide an excellent Apartment Life (AL) experience to residents and staff in an apartment community! Coordinators are passionate about the vision and mission of AL and combine this passion with skill to plan consistently excellent and irresistible events, provide exceptional personal communication, and offer acts of genuine care and radical hospitality for those in their scope of service- ultimately impacting the community and the lives of apartment residents. We are seeking mission-minded, highly relational, and responsible coordinators who enjoy meeting new people, planning social activities, and helping people experience God's unconditional love. Coordinators get to partake in doing "business as a ministry" by faithfully weaving together excellence in business with loving their neighbors well. What You’ll Do: Plan and host consistent, engaging community events Build relationships with residents and property staff Provide care, support, and hospitality to those in your community Serve as a positive and encouraging presence within the apartment community Who We’re Looking For: Individuals or couples who enjoy connecting with others Organized, proactive, and relational Passionate about serving and building community Able to manage responsibilities with consistency and excellence What You’ll Receive: Reduced/complimentary housing or hourly compensation (varies by community and opportunity) Training and ongoing support from Apartment Life A meaningful opportunity to impact lives and build community 👉 Interested in learning more or applying? Visit our landing page to explore available opportunities in your location and apply: https://ministryhire.com/company-jobs/apartment-life

description_short: About Apartment Life: Apartment Life is a faith-based, non-profit organization that has been serving the apartment industry since 2000. We believe every individual is created for community and that we are called to love our neighbor as ourselves.…
description_long: About Apartment Life: Apartment Life is a faith-based, non-profit organization that has been serving the apartment industry since 2000. We believe every individual is created for community and that we are called to love our neighbor as ourselves. Our goal is to live this out while providing strong business value to apartment owners. As a faith-based organization, we are committed to Fair Housing guidelines to ensure that all residents are treated fairly, equally, and consistently. All of our employees are trained in Fair Housing guidelines. Find out what makes us special! opportunity_title: Every newborn deserves a warm blanket and clothing!
description_short: LOVE THY BABY is seeking companies and individuals to conduct clothing drives/baby showers or raise money to purchase materials to make blankets and quilts for newborns who are born sick, premature and/or into poverty. Please consider making a…
description_long: LOVE THY BABY is seeking companies and individuals to conduct clothing drives/baby showers or raise money to purchase materials to make blankets and quilts for newborns who are born sick, premature and/or into poverty. Please consider making a financial donation today! Address: Love Thy Baby P.O. Box 1732, Coppell, TX 75019. We are a 501(c)3 and can provide tax receipts for donations. One hundred years from now, it will not matter what my bank account was, the sort of house I lived in, or the kind of car I drove, but it MAY be different because I was important in the life of a child! Additional information at www.LTNLoveThyNeighbor.org/programs/love-thy-baby or our Facebook page at www.facebook.com/lovethybaby. Information about all the programs of Love Thy Neighbor, Inc. is available on our website at www.LTNLoveThyNeighbor .org .

opportunity_title: FUN ROLE!   ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT TO FOUNDER *must speak fluent english" USA Based ONLY
description_short: Administrative Assistant to the Founder – GRANGEL WORLD 📍 Frisco, Texas Area | Part-Time / Internship Opportunity Are you creative, organized, proactive, and ready to be part of something meaningful and exciting? GRANGEL WORLD is looking for a…
description_long: Administrative Assistant to the Founder – GRANGEL WORLD 📍 Frisco, Texas Area | Part-Time / Internship Opportunity Are you creative, organized, proactive, and ready to be part of something meaningful and exciting? GRANGEL WORLD is looking for a dedicated Administrative Assistant to support the Founder in a fun, fast-moving, purpose-driven environment. This is an excellent opportunity for a college student, intern, or emerging professional who wants hands-on experience in entrepreneurship, branding, media, nonprofit collaboration, and digital storytelling. What You’ll Help With: Assist with fun and engaging social media campaigns Help manage and organize emails / correspondence Monitor inboxes and flag important opportunities Support our growing Grandbassador community Assist with scheduling, follow-up, and founder priorities Learn about our unique SEL (Social Emotional Learning) metaverse for children Help with creative ideas, promotions, and community outreach What We’re Looking For: Positive attitude and strong communication skills Organized and dependable Comfortable with email, Canva, social media, Google Docs, and basic tech tools Eager to learn and grow Self-starter who enjoys helping build something special Interest in children’s brands, education, media, or nonprofits is a plus Why This Opportunity Is Special: Direct mentorship from the Founder Learn real-world business operations Gain resume-building experience Be part of a mission helping children understand emotions through storytelling Opportunity to grow into a larger paid role Ideal Candidate: A student or intern located near Frisco, Allen, Plano, or surrounding North Texas communities. To Apply: Send a short introduction, resume (if available), and why this role interests you. Come grow with us. Help us build joy, healing, and opportunity for children and families. How to Apply: If you are interested and meet the qualifications, please send your resume or letter of interest and a brief cover letter to: Email: gloryabolden@tgcncorporate.org

opportunity_title: Fairview, TX: Host an Exchange Student-Volunteer families needed Fairview area for new school year!!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Feed My Starving Children Packing Sessions - Richardson, TX
description_short: Volunteers are needed for 1.75 hour packing sessions to hand-pack life saving meals for starving children around the world. We can take up to 100 people per packing session. We are open on Monday through Saturday each week. To volunteer, you must…
description_long: Volunteers are needed for 1.75 hour packing sessions to hand-pack life saving meals for starving children around the world. We can take up to 100 people per packing session. We are open on Monday through Saturday each week. To volunteer, you must register at https://www.fmsc.org/get-involved/volunteer Christian non-profit Feed My Starving Children (FMSC) has tackled world hunger since 1987 by sending volunteer-packed, nutritious meals to 70 countries. These meals are specifically designed to reverse and prevent malnutrition and are used in schools, orphanages, clinics and feeding programs to break the cycle of poverty. During your packing session you will be participating in an assembly line process. With the help of volunteers, dry ingredients are poured into bags, the bags are weighed and sealed, then boxed and put onto pallets. If we have volunteers that are unable to stand for long periods of time, they are welcome to sit at tables and label bags. Your packing session includes a brief orientation and video followed by packing instructions. Your packing shift will conclude with a short closing. During that time you will learn how many meals you and your fellow volunteers have packed.

opportunity_title: Finance & Compliance Lead (Strategic)
description_short: Job Title: Finance & Compliance Lead (Strategic) Department: Operations Department Location: Remote (Preference for candidates familiar with cross-border operations: U.S., Bali, Mexico) Type: Volunteer / Unpaid position Commitment: 6-10…
description_long: Job Title: Finance & Compliance Lead (Strategic) Department: Operations Department Location: Remote (Preference for candidates familiar with cross-border operations: U.S., Bali, Mexico) Type: Volunteer / Unpaid position Commitment: 6-10 hours/week for 3–6 months About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is a non-profit organization deeply committed to global orphanage support, guided by the Menzies 5 Keys of Needs. Our overarching mission is to lead the way in creating eco-regenerative conscious communities, serving as secure sanctuaries for orphans and vulnerable families. With a focus on addressing trauma and promoting holistic healing, we strive to foster environments where individuals can thrive and flourish. As part of the Menzies Mission Non-profit Organization, My Source Network stands as an innovative digital platform dedicated to holistic healing. By connecting individuals with a diverse network of practitioners and resources, we facilitate access to various healing modalities and transformative events. Our platform empowers users to embark on personalized wellness journeys, fostering growth and well-being in every individual we serve. Join our team and help us create exceptional digital experiences for our users! About the Role We are seeking a Strategic Finance & Compliance Lead to serve as the financial backbone of our marketplace platform. This role is ideal for someone with a startup mindset —comfortable setting up financial structure and governance from the ground up, without the bureaucracy of a corporate CFO environment. You’ll advise leadership on financial strategy, compliance, and efficient operational processes to ensure sustainable growth and clean financial operations. Key Responsibilities Financial Oversight: Manage and optimize all financial systems, including Stripe, payouts, and accounting tools. Governance & Structure: Establish standardized financial processes, approval limits, and reporting templates to ensure transparency and accountability. Compliance Management: Ensure compliance with local tax, data, and payout regulations, especially for cross-border transactions (U.S., Bali, Mexico). Cross-Functional Coordination: Partner with legal and operations teams to align payment terms, refund policies, and host agreements. Accounting & Reporting: Liaise with chartered accountants or external accounting firms to maintain accurate books and ensure timely tax filings. Internal Controls: Develop and enforce robust internal controls—such as payout approvals, refund verifications, and transaction audits—to minimize financial risk. Strategic Advisory: Provide insights on financial models, cost optimization, and long-term sustainability strategies aligned with business growth. Qualifications 5+ years of experience in finance, accounting, or compliance, ideally within a startup, fintech, or marketplace platform. Strong understanding of cross-border transactions, digital payments, and multi-currency operations. Familiarity with financial and accounting tools (e.g., Stripe, QuickBooks, Xero, or similar). Demonstrated ability to build governance frameworks and streamline financial processes. Knowledge of international tax compliance (especially U.S., Indonesia, and Mexico) is highly desirable. Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate cross-functionally with operations and legal teams. Strategic thinker with a hands-on approach to execution. Why Join Us? Innovative Projects: Work on cutting-edge projects that make a real impact. Growth Opportunities: Opportunities for professional growth and development. Collaborative Environment: Join a team of passionate and talented professionals. Flexible Work: Enjoy a flexible work environment with options for remote work. Application Process To apply, review the job description and submit your resume, portfolio, and cover letter to hr@menziesmission.org . Ensure accurate contact information for application status updates. If selected, be prepared to discuss qualifications and alignment with the organization's mission. For questions, contact hr@menziesmission.org. We look forward to reviewing your application. Equal Opportunity Employer: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Accommodation for the application process is available upon request.

opportunity_title: Foster Coordinator, Dogs Matter (REMOTE)
description_short: Foster Coordinator (Dogs Matter & Project Street Vet Initiative) Remote Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and…
description_long: Foster Coordinator (Dogs Matter & Project Street Vet Initiative) Remote Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability, and provide a healthy and loving environment for their pet. Dogs Matter is the only non-profit agency in Texas offering free temporary foster care placement and aftercare support services specifically for pets of people with addictions receiving substance abuse treatment. Dogs Matter has become a department of Dallas Pets Alive (DPA). With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to Dogs Matter, you directly impact our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dogs Matter visit https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ As our Foster Operations Coordinator, you’ll be the behind-the-scenes superhero who ensures every pet entering the Dogs Matter + Project Street Vet program receives safe, compassionate, and well-coordinated temporary care. You will coordinate placement logistics, support foster parents, and maintain the systems that keep our foster network running smoothly. Your work directly supports unhoused individuals seeking treatment by keeping their beloved pets safe, helping save lives and keeping families together. Team: Dogs Matter Location: Virtual from anywhere, understanding our clients and fosters are in the Pacific time zone. Geographical knowledge of Los Angeles is a plus. Time Commitment: 10-15 hours per week. The schedule varies depending on when a pet gets fostered. Volunteer hours must be flexible to coordinate swiftly at times with the Recovery Coach, client and foster family. Must be able to commit to a minimum of 6 months of volunteer service. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: Dogs Matter Director of Operations and Executive Director Works with: Will have regular and continuous contact with foster parents, the Dogs Matter Foster Team, Recovery Coaches, and Dogs Matter leadership and staff. Primary Responsibilities: Process foster applications, responding in a timely and professional manner Input and maintain data in our data management system Answer questions about our foster program Match animals with appropriate homes based on Dogs Matter guidelines Send welcome email to approved fosters Provide fosters with information regarding the pet’s care requirements Regularly check in and provide ongoing support and education for foster parents during the pet’s stay Conduct interviews and foster relations. Coordinate with the Foster Relationship Developer, as well as appropriate Dogs Matter and DPA staff to ensure dogs receive necessary medical care and assessments Maintain detailed records of animal’s progress and medical treatment Help overcome obstacles and escalate issues when appropriate Qualifications: We are seeking an experienced foster coordinator (those with related skills and experience as a foster parent may also qualify). Additional qualifications/skills: Skill in handling delicate situations and sensitive information with professionalism, empathy, and care Networking skills; social media a plus Highly organized and detail-oriented; project management skills a plus Flexibility in responding to evolving needs A team and service-oriented mentality Embrace Dogs Matter’s and Project Street Vet’s mission and values Ability to work virtually with regular access to a computer Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Experience with ShelterLuv or similar platform Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Must be at least 18 years of age Ability to operate a computer or mobile device Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Foster Success Partner, Onboarding (Welcoming Committee)
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As an Onboarding Foster Success Partner, you will be making a huge impact on this mission by assisting our foster families throughout this process as a member of our Welcome Committee. Team: Foster Location : Remote/Virtual - Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST timezone. Time Commitment: Under 5-10 hours a week Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Position Responsibilities: Foster Success Partners will work to ensure foster parents are set for success. They will check in with foster parents day one, and one week after picking up their new foster . They will field questions about crate training, decompression, how to make appointments, pet introductions, how to get supplies, etc. Checking in with foster parents at scheduled times via email. If no response within 48 hours, following up with a phone call or text then escalating to appropriate parties Collecting marketing information for foster animals and uploading the same to ShelterLuv internal database Assisting in troubleshooting any issues with the foster pet including training advice and medical issues Maintaining notes of correspondence with foster parents regarding the pet(s) Referring issues beyond the scope of this function to other volunteers, team leaders, or Foster Director for further processing Developing relationships with foster parents, and professionally managing potential complaints Assisting with any other duties needed to help drive Dallas Pets Alive!’s mission and support our values Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Ability to work virtually. Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: DPA! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and the communities we serve. We embrace challenges and lead with positive intent, welcoming all people who have a caring and compassionate dedication to animal welfare. Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. The golden rule is expected: Treat all living beings as you would like to be treated. DPA! has a zero tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Foster for the SPCA of Texas
description_short: The SPCA of Texas is searching for Foster Heroes who are ready to open their hearts and homes to animals in need. By becoming a Foster Hero, you'll provide one animal with a nurturing, loving environment while another takes their place at the…
description_long: The SPCA of Texas is searching for Foster Heroes who are ready to open their hearts and homes to animals in need. By becoming a Foster Hero, you'll provide one animal with a nurturing, loving environment while another takes their place at the shelter. We offer a variety of foster opportunities to fit any lifestyle, and we'll provide everything you need-all you need to bring is love! The more animals fostered, the more lives saved! Visit www.spca.org/foster to fill out an application, or www.spca.org/fosterme to see animals in need. We offer three types of foster programs: Adoption Ambassador Open your home to adoptable animals and help them find forever homes. Send us great photos and bios for online profiles. Promote animals online, around town, and at adoption events. Communicate with potential adopters to find the perfect match. Average foster time: 2-3 weeks. Benefits include better matches, more space in the shelter, and a rewarding experience for all involved. Trust Building & Socialization Foster Provide a safe, loving environment for animals with difficult pasts to regain trust in people. Typically animals from hoarding, neglect, cruelty cases, or those needing obedience work. Benefits include offering a second chance to animals, insight into their true behavior, and preparing them for adoption. Healing & Comfort Foster Provide loving care and attention for animals needing extra support. Includes mother and litter, kittens/puppies, orphaned babies, animals recovering from sickness or surgery, and fospice care for elderly or terminal animals. Benefits include ensuring comfort during recovery, insight into the animal's personality, and a rewarding experience for foster parents.

opportunity_title: Fostering
description_short: Paws in the City is looking for Foster Volunteers! This role provides temporary, in-home care for rescued animals, helping them transition from rescue to a forever home. Commitment: Timeframe varies widely; some animals are adopted within weeks,…
description_long: Paws in the City is looking for Foster Volunteers! This role provides temporary, in-home care for rescued animals, helping them transition from rescue to a forever home. Commitment: Timeframe varies widely; some animals are adopted within weeks, while others may take years to find their forever home. Responsibilities: Provide a safe, nurturing home environment for foster animals Follow care guidelines, including feeding, medication (if needed), and basic training Transport animals to vet appointments or adoption events as needed Communicate regularly with PAWS on animal progress and needs Help promote adoptable animals through photos, videos, and updates

opportunity_title: Fosters Needed! Calling All Super Heroes!
description_short: Imagine how you feel when you are out of your element. It’s hard to show your true self. The same can be said of animals that are separated from their homes and placed in a foreign environment with a lot of commotion. These animals may never have…
description_long: Imagine how you feel when you are out of your element. It’s hard to show your true self. The same can be said of animals that are separated from their homes and placed in a foreign environment with a lot of commotion. These animals may never have the ability to find their forever home. This is where being a foster assists greatly in getting an animal placed. We are able to tell potential adopters about the personality of the animal, whether they get along well with other animals, children, are house-trained, etc. FRAs will provide food, medicine and vet care necessary for you to foster an animal in your home. Go to http://frastx.org/foster/ and complete the Foster Application to get started making a difference.

opportunity_title: Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. Board Member Opportunity – Help Empower Dallas Texas Students Through Mentorship & Career Readiness
description_short: Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. is a Dallas-based nonprofit dedicated to empowering underserved students through one-to-one mentorship, career readiness, and educational support programs. We are currently seeking passionate and community-minded board…
description_long: Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. is a Dallas-based nonprofit dedicated to empowering underserved students through one-to-one mentorship, career readiness, and educational support programs. We are currently seeking passionate and community-minded board members who want to make a meaningful impact in the lives of North Texas students. Board members play an active role in supporting our mission through fundraising, community engagement, strategic planning, advocacy, and relationship-building. Ideal candidates are professionals who are passionate about youth development, education, workforce readiness, nonprofit growth, or community impact. Experience in fundraising, corporate partnerships, finance, marketing, legal, education, or nonprofit leadership is especially valuable, but not required. Board members are expected to attend quarterly board meetings, support fundraising efforts, help expand community connections, and serve as ambassadors for the organization throughout the Dallas community. We are especially interested in individuals who are excited to help grow a rapidly expanding nonprofit focused on mentorship, college and career readiness, financial literacy, and real-world opportunities for students. Through strong community partnerships and in-school programming, Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. helps students build confidence, discover opportunities, and create successful futures.

opportunity_title: Friendly visitors needed for hospice patients in Plano/Frisco/McKinney area
description_short: Volunteers are an essential part of our hospice team. They are our extra set of helping hands and eyes and hearts. Our volunteers add quality to a patient's time at the end of life's journey. No special skills needed. Typical companionship…
description_long: Volunteers are an essential part of our hospice team. They are our extra set of helping hands and eyes and hearts. Our volunteers add quality to a patient's time at the end of life's journey. No special skills needed. Typical companionship activities include visiting, reading, listening to music and providing support to the patient's family. Training is provided, quarterly on a Saturday in our Flower Mound office. The next training will take place in winter/spring 2018. Volunteers visit patients in their homes or elder-care facilities in the DFW metroplex, several of which are in the Plano/Frisco/McKinney area. This is where we need more help! After the training and one follow-up visit to our office, it won't be necessary to come to Flower Mound on a regular basis. If you think you might have the heart for hospice work, please join us at our next orientation.

opportunity_title: Frisco / Hospice Patient / Caregiver Companionship Volunteer
description_short: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily…
description_long: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. opportunity_title: Frisco TX :  Volunteer families needed to host Exchange Students
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Front Desk Volunteer
description_short: Front Desk Volunteers provide families at Ronald McDonald House with a pleasant and comfortable stay by assisting staff with daily operations of the House and assisting families as needed. You will be greeting our residents and visitors and helping…
description_long: Front Desk Volunteers provide families at Ronald McDonald House with a pleasant and comfortable stay by assisting staff with daily operations of the House and assisting families as needed. You will be greeting our residents and visitors and helping them check in as they enter the building, answering and directing phone calls, giving tours of the house, handing out supplies like toilet paper or laundry pods and other small tasks that make our building their home. 5 shifts available: 07:00 to 07:00 CST 09:00 to 12:00 CST 12:00 to 15:00 CST 15:00 to 18:00 CST 18:00 to 21:00 CST

opportunity_title: Front page editor for cutting edge daily news site (also marketing and other openings)
description_short: DailySource.org is looking for an individual with a nose for news to help update our site with great content and lay out the front page. Our nonprofit focuses on news about making the world a better place, particularly news related to topics such as…
description_long: DailySource.org is looking for an individual with a nose for news to help update our site with great content and lay out the front page. Our nonprofit focuses on news about making the world a better place, particularly news related to topics such as global poverty, nonprofits, climate change and human rights. All volunteering is via the computer -- you can be anywhere in the world. The Daily Source is truly a 21st century journalism site. Our online software is incredibly easy to use and automates most of the layout process. Just drag and drop articles from one location to another and the page adjusts itself automatically. Editing the headlines is as easy as using Microsoft Word or sending an e-mail. You will receive guidance from our senior editors who are some of the top newspaper journalists in the U.S. Besides laying out the front page, you will find articles, videos, columns and other material from around the Internet for use on the site and make the final decision of what goes on the site. You will be doing real stuff that directly impacts our front page and our nonprofit's mission to help the world. You will also gain invaluable experience that will build your skills and look great on a resume. Some of our volunteers have gone on to work in the journalism field. Two of our volunteers started their own non-profit organization after eight months of volunteering with us. One of our editors was hired full-time by Wired.com and another was hired by Congress as the official congressional photographer of senators at public events. We have an extensive training process that is about a month long, so if we are going to train you, we need you to do a certain amount of hours in all to make it worth the time we put into it. Therefore, the Front Page Editor role is a minimum of 15 hours per week for a minimum of six months. If you can volunteer 20 hours or higher a week, then the minimum is only five months, or you can do 12 hours a week for seven and a half months. We tend to give preference to applicants who can do a sizable number of hours. All editors are credited at the bottom of the site each day, and daily editions are archived so your friends, family and future employers will see what you were part of creating. To view our front page, visit - http://dailysource.org/ To read more about us, view the links here - http://dailysource.org/about/site We usually also have the following openings (minimum hours per week and minimum number of months are in parenthesis). All these positions are telecommute so you don’t have to drive or dress up, and can work from the comfort of your home. You might also be able to use our local Internet location, though there is some cost for that so virtual is preferred. If you apply for a position, you should view your commitment to it the same as if it were a paid position. We treat volunteers like normal staff and rely on them like staff. Our site is 99% volunteer run and we all depend on each other to make it a success. Please do not apply if you are viewing this opportunity as "just a volunteer position" and would treat it as less important than paid positions or other commitments you have. If you are in school, volunteering with us can function as an internship. Interns can usually receive credit with their school for their internships. Content Editor (10 hours a week for a minimum of 6 months): Searches the Internet for high quality articles, columns, editorials and videos, adds them to our system, and ranks them by quality. The front page editors pick from these to publish on the front page. Also does research and fact-checking when needed. This requires Internet surfing skills, strong understanding and judgment of the news, and attention to detail. Marketing and Social Media (6 hours a week for a minimum of 5 months): Help research potential media outlets and blogs to cover our site, e-mail our press releases to them, and keep track of coverage of our site that has occurred. We have a great website and need to get the word out about it. Help promote the website by updating our Facebook, Twitter and other social media pages, attracting new followers, and interacting with our existing followers. No marketing experience required, but you must be familiar with social media and be good at communicating. If you're interested in volunteering, please email us at volunteer@dailysource.org and let us know: * Which position or positions are you interested in? * How many hours per week and how many months can you do? We tend to give preference to people who can do more hours. * A description of your past work/volunteer experience and education, or a resume or a link to a LinkedIn profile. You can also apply directly through this website by clicking the button for that, and giving us the same information. Sometimes emails sent through this volunteer website are routed to spam folders, so we recommend you check your spam folder during the coming weeks in case our reply to you ends up there. We look forward to doing great work together to make the world better with you.

opportunity_title: Fuel progress by joining your local Multiple Myeloma Research Foundation® (MMRF®) Walk event- Dallas
description_short: Be Part of Something Bigger! The Multiple Myeloma Research Foundation (MMRF) is hosting a City Walk fundraiser at the beautiful Fair Park in Dallas on the morning of May 9, and we need enthusiastic volunteers to help make the day unforgettable! Your…
description_long: Be Part of Something Bigger! The Multiple Myeloma Research Foundation (MMRF) is hosting a City Walk fundraiser at the beautiful Fair Park in Dallas on the morning of May 9, and we need enthusiastic volunteers to help make the day unforgettable! Your energy and enthusiasm will mean the world to our walkers and their families. This is a meaningful morning where every cheering voice, every handed-out T-shirt, and every marshalled corner helps bring hope to those fighting multiple myeloma. Responsibilities include cheering on walkers, course marshalling, t-shirt distribution, refreshment coordination, gift bag distribution, and set up/takedown. If you have experience in photography, instructing zumba/yoga, cheerleading, or face painting, we would love to hear from you!

opportunity_title: Fundraising
description_short: Description - Stronger Than Cancer is seeking a dedicated Fundraising Volunteer to help expand our impact and support individuals and families affected by cancer. This role helps sustain our care kit program, where we prepare and deliver care kits…
description_long: Description - Stronger Than Cancer is seeking a dedicated Fundraising Volunteer to help expand our impact and support individuals and families affected by cancer. This role helps sustain our care kit program, where we prepare and deliver care kits to breast cancer patients, along with our outreach initiatives and educational efforts. Responsibilities - • Organize simple community fundraisers such as setting up a table at a public location (with permission) like a school, gym, library, park, or health fair and fundraising through cookies, lemonade, bracelets, or other handmade items • Research and reach out to potential donors and sponsors, including local businesses and corporate employees • Support peer to peer and online fundraising efforts • Track outreach efforts and help maintain organized donor records • Represent the organization professionally during community outreach Qualifications - • Strong communication and organizational skills • Comfortable with basic online research and outreach • Self motivated and able to work independently • Passion for community service and supporting people affected by cancer • Prior fundraising or nonprofit experience is helpful but not required

opportunity_title: Fundraising Coordinator & Campaign Designer (Campaign Focus)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking a creative, strategic, and highly organized individual to join our team as a Fundraising Coordinator & Campaign Designer (Campaign Focus) on a volunteer basis. This remote role is ideal for someone passionate about crafting compelling campaigns and driving fundraising success, with a specific focus on the Dallas, TX area for local engagement opportunities. As the Fundraising Coordinator & Campaign Designer, you will be instrumental in conceptualizing, developing, and executing impactful fundraising campaigns that resonate with our audience and inspire action. Your efforts will directly empower our ability to generate vital resources, expand our reach, and deepen our impact in the communities we serve. Position Overview: As the Fundraising Coordinator & Campaign Designer (Campaign Focus) for SelfSoulLiberate, you will lead the charge in designing and implementing innovative fundraising campaigns. This role is crucial for developing engaging narratives, coordinating campaign logistics, and driving donor participation, directly supporting our mission to achieve health equity and reduce mental health stigma in under-resourced communities. Your creativity and strategic thinking will directly empower our fundraising initiatives, enabling us to expand our reach and maximize our impact, particularly within the Dallas, TX region. Key Job Duties: Develop and implement comprehensive fundraising campaign strategies, including setting goals, identifying target audiences, and defining messaging. Design and create compelling campaign materials across various channels (e.g., email, social media, web content, print). Coordinate the execution of fundraising campaigns from start to finish, ensuring timely delivery and effective outreach. Collaborate with the communications team to ensure consistent branding and messaging across all campaign assets. Plan and support virtual or local in-person fundraising events and activities related to campaigns in Dallas, TX. Monitor campaign performance, analyze data, and generate reports to assess effectiveness and identify areas for improvement. Research fundraising trends and innovative campaign strategies to enhance future efforts. Work closely with the Board of Directors and executive team to align campaign goals with overall organizational strategy. Qualifications: Proven experience in fundraising, marketing, communications, or campaign management, with a strong portfolio of successful campaigns. Demonstrated ability to think creatively and strategically about fundraising challenges and solutions. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a talent for crafting persuasive and emotionally resonant content. Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple campaigns and deadlines simultaneously. Proficiency in graphic design tools (e.g., Canva, Adobe Creative Suite) is a plus. Experience with CRM systems and email marketing platforms is highly preferred. Exceptional organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail. Ability to work independently, take initiative, and collaborate effectively within a remote team environment. Passion for SelfSoulLiberate's mission and a commitment to health equity and mental wellness. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration, with a preference for individuals based in or familiar with the Dallas, TX area for potential local engagement.

opportunity_title: Fundraising Team Member
description_short: We are seeking motivated volunteers to join our Fundraising Team . This role directly fuels our mission to rescue women and children from trafficking by helping us secure the donations, sponsors, and resources our task force needs to operate.…
description_long: We are seeking motivated volunteers to join our Fundraising Team . This role directly fuels our mission to rescue women and children from trafficking by helping us secure the donations, sponsors, and resources our task force needs to operate. Volunteers assist with donor outreach, campaign promotion, grant research, community partnerships, and basic administrative tasks that support our ongoing rescue operations. Ideal volunteers are reliable, professional, and passionate about humanitarian work. No fundraising experience is required, just good communication skills, basic computer familiarity, and a willingness to learn. This is a very flexible (work when you want!), remote-friendly role where your efforts directly impact our ability to respond to real victims in crisis. By joining the fundraising team, you help provide the lifeline that makes every rescue possible.

opportunity_title: GET TESTED GRAB A BAG (GTGB) A National HIV Testing Day Community Activation Hosted by Women’s Health & Evolutionary Wellness (W.H.E.W.)
description_short: Event Overview Get Tested Grab a Bag (GTGB) is a community-driven HIV testing and prevention event designed to make testing accessible, supportive, and stigma-free. Participants who receive HIV testing or prevention education receive a care bag…
description_long: Event Overview Get Tested Grab a Bag (GTGB) is a community-driven HIV testing and prevention event designed to make testing accessible, supportive, and stigma-free. Participants who receive HIV testing or prevention education receive a care bag filled with hygiene items, prevention tools, and health information, along with a prepared meal, while connecting with local organizations providing health, housing, and social support services. The event also integrates voter registration and civic education, recognizing that public health and policy decisions directly impact community health outcomes. Why This Matters Despite decades of prevention work, Black communities—particularly Black women—continue to experience disproportionate HIV vulnerability while facing barriers to testing, prevention resources, and healthcare access. GTGB addresses these barriers by combining: Free HIV testing • Prevention education (PrEP, DoxyPEP, condoms) • Community resource navigation • Food and hygiene support • Civic engagement opportunities

opportunity_title: GRANT DEVELOPMENT SPECIALIST
description_short: HELP US BUILD SOMETHING THAT MATTERS Volunteer Grant Writers Wanted Do you want to join a company and mission that believes in giving more than we receive? The Grandparents Community Network is seeking committed individuals interested in learning…
description_long: HELP US BUILD SOMETHING THAT MATTERS Volunteer Grant Writers Wanted Do you want to join a company and mission that believes in giving more than we receive? The Grandparents Community Network is seeking committed individuals interested in learning and growing in the dynamic field of grant writing, funding development, sponsorship outreach, and organizational growth. This is not simply a “resume-building opportunity.” This is a ground-floor opportunity to grow with a mission-driven organization focused on long-term impact, leadership development, and future pathways to compensation as funding and operational capacity expand. What We Are Looking For We are seeking individuals who are: organized and punctual responsive and professional willing to work under leadership and supervision able to follow expectations and Standard Operating Procedures comfortable attending meetings and communicating effectively willing to communicate beyond text and chat when necessary committed to confidentiality and organizational integrity open to coaching, training, and accountability interested in long-term growth and development Important Expectations All team members must sign: Confidentiality Agreement Work Product & Intellectual Property Agreement Organizational Standards & Expectations Agreement Our work products, grant strategies, systems, templates, and organizational materials may not be shared, copied, or used for personal gain outside of the organization. What You Will Learn Grant writing Proposal development Funding research Sponsorship strategy Organizational development Revenue systems Professional communication Nonprofit growth strategy Please Do NOT Apply If: you are only looking for quick resume experience you are unwilling to communicate professionally you struggle with accountability or follow-through you are not open to structure, expectations, or teamwork Step In If You Are Looking For: long-term growth mentorship and development meaningful impact leadership opportunities a pathway toward future paid opportunities as the organization expands Spread Your Wings. Embrace Something New. To express interest, contact: Rose Payton gloryabolden@tgcncorporate.org Website: TGCN Corporate Website

opportunity_title: GRC Data Protection Analyst (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Location: Remote Reports to: GRC Manager Time commitment: minimum 20 hours weekly Summary: As we grow, the protection of our customers’ and clients’ data is paramount. We are looking for a skilled and proactive Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC)…
description_long: Location: Remote Reports to: GRC Manager Time commitment: minimum 20 hours weekly Summary: As we grow, the protection of our customers’ and clients’ data is paramount. We are looking for a skilled and proactive Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) Analyst to help build and mature our data protection aspect. This role will ensure our products and business operations comply with global data privacy regulations and security best practices from launch onward. Job Descriptions: Monitor and enforce compliance with global data protection regulations, including GDPR, CCPA, and other relevant privacy laws, across the events tech platform. Conduct data protection impact assessments (DPIAs) for new features and technologies. Maintain the company’s risk register, specifically identifying, assessing, and mitigating privacy-related risks. Develop, review, and maintain data protection policies, standards, and guidelines for the organization. Ensure policies are communicated and implemented across all relevant teams. Working comprehensively on data privacy challenges and requirements specific to the events technology sector, including attendee data collection, sponsor data sharing, and platform security. Maintain an inventory of data processing activities and analyze user data movement through the company as well as third parties systems. Candidate Qualifications: A bachelor’s or master’s degree in a related field such as Information Technology, Computer Science, Law, or Business Administration Several years of hands-on experience in data protection, risk management, and compliance is often expected for experienced roles. Precision in handling data, documenting findings, and tracking compliance requirements is critical. Proven ability to analyze complex issues, identify trends, and develop effective solutions.

opportunity_title: GRC Legal Analyst (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Location : Remote Reports to : GRC Manager Time commitment : minimum 20 hours weekly Headcount: 2 people Summary: The GRC analyst with a legal background is a critical hire for our rapid team. You will be responsible for building and maintaining the…
description_long: Location : Remote Reports to : GRC Manager Time commitment : minimum 20 hours weekly Headcount: 2 people Summary: The GRC analyst with a legal background is a critical hire for our rapid team. You will be responsible for building and maintaining the Governance, Risk Management, and Compliance aspects from the ground-up ensuring alignment with global legal requirements and business objectives. Job Descriptions: Deep knowledge of national and international regulations relevant to the events and technology sectors legal sector, regulations, and accessibility standards. Proven experience conducting comprehensive risk assessments to identify, evaluate, and mitigate potential threats and vulnerabilities to the business Prior experience in a tech company, especially a startup, helps demonstrate an understanding of fast-paced, agile environments. Understanding of potential legal and regulatory liabilities associated with the tech platform (website, mobile website, and/or applications) Knowledge of laws concerning digital rights, content usage, and accessibility for virtual and hybrid event platforms. Candidate Qualifications: A bachelor’s or master’s degree in a related field such as Information Technology, Computer Science, Law, or Business Administration Proficient with complex legal and technical concepts clearly to both technical and executive audiences. Ability to communicate complex legal and technical concepts clearly to both technical and executive audiences. Knowledge of assessing and managing risks associated with third-party vendors and platform security
