opportunity_title: GRC Technical Incident Response Analyst (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Location: Remote Reports to: GRC Manager Time commitment: minimum 20 hours weekly Headcount: 1 person Summary: We are looking for a highly motivated and detail-oriented Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) Analyst with a strong background in…
description_long: Location: Remote Reports to: GRC Manager Time commitment: minimum 20 hours weekly Headcount: 1 person Summary: We are looking for a highly motivated and detail-oriented Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) Analyst with a strong background in technical incident response. The ideal candidate will help build and mature our security programs, focusing specifically on our event technology infrastructure, while also playing a key role in responding to and mitigating technical security incidents. Job Descriptions: Perform security risk assessments, including third-party and vendor risk management, to identify vulnerabilities and mitigate threats related to events, data handling, and technology infrastructure. Conduct risk modeling and incident response exercises specific to live events, such as mitigating risks from public Wi-Fi, phishing during registration, and protecting sensitive users and clients data. Serve as an escalation point for the Security team, leading the end-to-end incident response process from detection and analysis to containment, eradication, and recovery. Assist technical security requirements into effective, actionable security policies, standards, and procedures. Develop and report on key security metrics, including those related to incident response performance (e.g., detection time, resolution time), for management and governance committees. Candidate Qualifications: A bachelor’s degree in a technical field like Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or Information Technology is typically required. Have a strong foundational knowledge of IT and cybersecurity principles, including security controls, networking concepts, and operating systems. Skills in digital and network forensics for investigating data breaches and analyzing malware. In-depth knowledge of major security frameworks(NIST, ISO) is critical.

opportunity_title: Garland TX Host a Foreign Exchange Student Garland, TX and surrounding areas!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student ! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student ! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Garland, TX: a neighbor could use a hand with lawn and yard care · Ref IWTMYL-MR8
description_short: A neighbor in Garland, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A neighbor in Garland, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and well-maintained. You're bringing a smile to a neighbor's face with your thoughtful gesture. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Garland, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-MR8&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-MR8

You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-MLM&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-MLM

opportunity_title: Garland, Tx!!! Seeking Compassionate Volunteers for Hospice Patients
description_short: Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening…
description_long: Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening ear to patients and families, assist with daily household activities for patients, provide grief support, serve as a companion to loved ones or share their personal expertise to help promote hospice care, the time given to Harbor Hospice families by our volunteers can be the most rewarding experiences of their lives. As a volunteer for Harbor Hospice's Patient Care Services, you "do good" every day. It takes a very special, caring person to give of themselves as unselfishly as our Harbor Hospice patient care volunteers. If you'd like to be a part of the Harbor Hospice team, here are some examples of the activities you can assist with: reading to a patient, or holding a hand staying with a patient while family members take a needed break or keep an appointment shopping or doing errands for a patient listening helping patients write or tell their life stories light household chores providing help with makeup and hair Veterans needed to honor Veteran patients at bedside/share stories together working on crafts or other projects clerical and other office duties If you would like to volunteer for Harbor Hospice, please follow these steps: Step 1. Complete online Application (Click Here) and submit form. Step 2. Wait and Volunteer Coordinator will process and contact you in 24-48hrs If you have any questions, contact us TF (855) 542-7267 or email us by clicking here

org_name: Medical City Hospice and Family Care
opportunity_title: Gift Your Time & Friendship to a Hospice Patient - North Richland Hills
description_short: Enrich your life with the experience of befriending a senior on hospice. Volunteer with Medical City Hospice and Family Care and provide companionship and caring presence to hospice patients living at a DFW community or at home in the Bedford area.…
description_long: Enrich your life with the experience of befriending a senior on hospice. Volunteer with Medical City Hospice and Family Care and provide companionship and caring presence to hospice patients living at a DFW community or at home in the Bedford area. You will receive free orientation and training and will be able to meet patients to read, share stories, listen to music, or provide respite for family members. Your companionship and caring presence ensures that patients spend the end of their life in dignity and comfort. Medical City Hospice and Family Care has many other locations in the DFW area and surrounding cities. Training and orientation will be arranged to fit to your schedule. Contact Medical City Hospice and Family Care 469-872-4642 PLEASE CHECK YOUR SPAM INBOX.

org_name: GIRLS ON THE RUN OF THE DFW METROPLEX
opportunity_title: Girls on the Run Coach-Richardson YMCA
description_short: Girls in Our Community Need YOU More than Ever! By volunteering as a coach, you can make a difference in the lives of girls by strengthening their physical & emotional health, helping them connect with each other & express their joy and…
description_long: Girls in Our Community Need YOU More than Ever! By volunteering as a coach, you can make a difference in the lives of girls by strengthening their physical & emotional health, helping them connect with each other & express their joy and optimism! At an age when they need it most, girls can learn critical social emotional skills. Our programming empowers girls to: Stand up for themselves and others Make an impact in their community Practice empathy, inclusion and acceptance Now more than ever, girls need opportunities to develop skills in cooperation and collaboration. They need to feel supported in identifying and expressing their emotions. They need to be equipped with the ability to create and foster strong, healthy relationships with their peers. We recognize the important connection between body and mind. Our program centers around fun and accessible physical activity, ensuring we are strengthening physical - as well as emotional - health. Girls on the Run can strengthen girls’ physical and emotional health. Through a simple online training course, you will gain access to all the resources you need to lead a small group of elementary or middle school girls in developing important life skills and healthy habits. It is our goal to ensure you are comfortable and equipped in supporting girls during each practice. Over the course of 10 weeks, twice a week for 90 minutes, you will lead your team through fun and interactive lessons. Our dedicated staff will provide everything you need to make a real difference in the lives of girls in our community. Did you know that volunteering has been shown to reduce feelings of social isolation and lower stress levels? At Girls on the Run, we teach girls to stand up for themselves and others, reminding them that they are the authors of their powerful stories. By creating an inclusive and action-oriented environment, we’re empowering the next generation of strong, empathetic women. If you would like to be a part of our movement, and learn what it's like to be called "coach", please join us! Watch our video to learn more: Girls on the Run DFW

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Give the Gift of Time -  Be a Hospice Companion and Respite Caregiver
description_short: Come join the Encompass Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other…
description_long: Come join the Encompass Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Volunteer Opportunity: Direct Patient Care - Sitting with patients, reading, working puzzles, watching tv/movies and sharing life stories. This may also include Caregiver Respite where you will visit with the patient while the caregiver runs errands, takes time for themselves or goes to appointments. Must be able to pass a Drug screen, Background check, have your Covid Vaccine, TB test, and be at least 17 years old. If you are interested in this wonderful volunteer opportunity, please email Faith Oates at Faith.Oates@ehab.com Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description Enhabit Hospice is looking for a [specify what type of volunteer] to provide [specify services] to patients during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, caregiver respite, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. Serving the following areas: Texas cities - Allen, Plano, Prosper, Melissa, McKinney, Fairview, Frisco and the surrounding areas For more information, please contact: Faith Oates - Volunteer Coordinator & Bereavement Services 214.383.7443 Faith.Oates@ehab.com

description_long: We need someone to reach out to individuals and businesses that can donate goods and services to support our mission such as proposal writing, social media management, influencer ad donations, auction items, venues to hold events, storage units to house donated items, pickup or box truck to pick up donated items, accounting and tax services,etc.. description_short: We are seeking volunteers to support the Grace Women's Wellness Fair, a free community event focused on health, wellness, and access to local resources. Volunteers will play a key role in helping the event run smoothly and creating a welcoming,…
description_long: We are seeking volunteers to support the Grace Women's Wellness Fair, a free community event focused on health, wellness, and access to local resources. Volunteers will play a key role in helping the event run smoothly and creating a welcoming, supportive environment for all attendees. Responsibilities may include: Greeting guests Assisting with event check-in Guiding participants to screenings and sessions Supporting vendors and resource tables Distributing materials and giveaways Helping with general event setup and cleanup This event will feature free health screenings, mental health resources, fitness and wellness sessions, and access to valuable community services. Volunteers will help ensure participants can easily navigate these offerings and have a positive, engaging experience. Ideal volunteers are friendly and reliable, and a willingness to help and support others. Join us in making a meaningful impact by helping families take steps toward healthier, stronger, and more confident lives.

opportunity_title: Grand Prairie, TX: Seeking Host Families: Host a Foreign Exchange Student in/around Grand Prairie!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Grant Research & Development Specialist (Volunteer)
description_short: TWCUYC is looking for a volunteer who can help identify and secure funding opportunities that support our programs and mission. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys research, writing, and helping nonprofits grow their impact. What You’ll Do…
description_long: TWCUYC is looking for a volunteer who can help identify and secure funding opportunities that support our programs and mission. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys research, writing, and helping nonprofits grow their impact. What You’ll Do Research foundation, corporate, and government grant opportunities Identify funding opportunities that align with our programs Review grant guidelines and determine eligibility Assist with grant applications and supporting documents Track deadlines and maintain a grant calendar Help maintain a pipeline of prospective funders Assist with reporting and other fundraising projects as needed What We’re Looking For Strong research skills Strong writing and proofreading skills Ability to interpret funding guidelines Strong attention to detail Ability to manage deadlines and work independently Experience with grant writing, fundraising, nonprofit development, or professional writing is preferred but not required Time Commitment A minimum of 8 hours per week is required. Additional availability is welcomed based on project needs and grant deadlines.

opportunity_title: Grants Department Lead / Fund Development Specialist
description_short: (opportunity will be considered for future paid staff) Organization: Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) Position Type: Volunteer Location: Remote About Us: The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to…
description_long: (opportunity will be considered for future paid staff) Organization: Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) Position Type: Volunteer Location: Remote About Us: The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting grandparents raising their grandchildren. Our mission is to advocate for, empower, and celebrate this unique community through education, resources, and partnerships. Position Overview: We are seeking a dynamic and detail-oriented Grant Writer / Fund Development Specialist to secure financial resources and build meaningful relationships that will sustain and grow TGCN’s mission. This role combines grant writing with active engagement in donor relations and advocacy, making it ideal for someone passionate about using their skills to make a real difference. Responsibilities: Grant Research and Identification: Identify and research grant opportunities that align with TGCN’s mission. Stay informed on funding trends and potential new opportunities. Proposal Development and Submission: Write, edit, and submit high-quality, tailored grant proposals and applications. Ensure all proposals align with funder guidelines and mission priorities. Donor Engagement and Advocacy: Attend meetings with potential donors and funding organizations to present TGCN’s mission and initiatives. Act as a spokesperson for TGCN, advocating for grandparents raising grandchildren and highlighting the importance of our work: Collaboration and Partnership Development: Work closely with TGCN’s Board of Directors, leadership team, and partners to create collaborative strategies for increasing funding. Represent TGCN at community meetings, events, and networking opportunities. Relationship Building and Stewardship: Build and maintain relationships with current and prospective funders to increase existing funding streams. Provide funders with impact reports and updates to ensure continued engagement and support. Administrative Tasks: Maintain organized records of funding opportunities, submissions, and outcomes. Track deadlines and manage multiple projects simultaneously. Community and Event Participation: Participate in community events and initiatives to raise awareness of TGCN’s mission. Collaborate with other TGCN volunteers to create innovative fundraising campaigns and events: Qualifications: Proven experience in grant writing, fundraising, or a related field, with a successful track record of securing funding preferred. Strong understanding of nonprofit funding language and financial needs. Exceptional communication and presentation skills. Ability to collaborate effectively with leadership, donors, and partners. Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to meet deadlines. Comfortable attending meetings and representing TGCN in professional settings. A passion for TGCN’s mission and a heart for supporting grandparents raising grandchildren . Why This Role Matters: Your work as a Grant Writer / Fund Development Specialist will directly impact TGCN’s ability to provide resources, advocacy, and support to grandparents who are primary caregivers. By securing vital funding, you’ll help ensure these families thrive. How to Apply: If you’re ready to use your skills to make a difference, we want to hear from you! Please send your resume and a brief description of your experience and interest to gloryabolden@tgcncorporate.org Join Us: This is more than a volunteer role—it’s an opportunity to be the voice and advocate for an underserved community. Help us grow and fulfill our mission of empowering grandparents and the grandchildren they raise.

opportunity_title: Guided Trail Program Coach
description_short: The Guided Trail Program Coach is a volunteer leadership role responsible for guiding "Guided Consultants" (learners) through a rigorous, hands-on Salesforce implementation project. You will move beyond teaching basic "clicks" to instilling a…
description_long: The Guided Trail Program Coach is a volunteer leadership role responsible for guiding "Guided Consultants" (learners) through a rigorous, hands-on Salesforce implementation project. You will move beyond teaching basic "clicks" to instilling a consultant mindset grounded in the RESOLVE methodology . Your goal is to ensure learners solve the problem outlined in our case study. Responsibilities Facilitate the Rhythmic Cycle : Lead weekly Stand-ups to review user stories from the Case Study backlog Host weekly Campfire Sessions for peer reviews and build validation. Mentor on Methodology : Guide learners through the RESOLVE Method (Recognize, Explore, Select, Outline, Launch, Verify, Evolve) to ensure every technical decision is rooted in a business requirement. (will be trained in this methodology) Technical Oversight & Auditing : Oversee and assess program execution to ensure consistency, quality, and compliance with established standards and policies. Agile Management : Help learners decompose the "Case Study" into Epics, Features, and User Stories , ensuring all work passes the INVEST litmus test and follows proper naming conventions. Career Coaching : Guide learners in documenting their work and preparing them for Employer Interview Simulations with Technical Architects. Benefits Leadership Development : Refine your skills as a Solution Architect and mentor while managing a high-stakes digital transformation. Impact : Directly contribute to a mission that saves staff time and improves outcomes for people identified in our case study. Networking : Connect with a community of dedicated Salesforce professionals and emerging talent. Portfolio Contribution : Recognition for coaching a successful cohort through a professional-grade Cast Study Portfolio Showcase Qualifications Advanced Salesforce Expertise : Deep proficiency in Service Cloud , Flow Builder , Security Models (OWDs, Roles, Permissions), and Experience Cloud . Architectural Mindset : Ability to explain complex logic, such as Relational Data Mapping (Household Account Model) and Escalation Logic vs. Flow . Facilitation Skills : Experience leading agile teams, managing backlogs, and providing constructive feedback during technical peer reviews. Values-Driven : A commitment to data security as a means of saving lives and a passion for mentoring the next generation of Salesforce professionals. Time Commitment Program Duration : 12 weeks per cohort. Weekly Live Sessions : Stand-up : ~1 hour to kick off the week's sprint. Campfire : ~2 hours for peer presentations and "Definition of Done" verification. Asynchronous Support : 1–2 hours weekly for reviewing Admin Decision Logs and answering technical "Spike" questions

opportunity_title: HIGH PRIORITY NEED! Art with Unhoused Children (Fridays, Dallas)
description_short: You don't need to be an artist to volunteer for this opportunity! We are a nonprofit organization that helps children facing Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) realize their dreams through the power of art. They can be anything they want to be!…
description_long: You don't need to be an artist to volunteer for this opportunity! We are a nonprofit organization that helps children facing Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) realize their dreams through the power of art. They can be anything they want to be! The Dallas Shelter Volunteer provides vital support to children at a local shelter and youth development program, helping them feel safe, valued, and supported. Volunteers will actively contribute to creating a positive, nurturing, and welcoming environment where children can thrive. Dedicate a few hours to help children express themselves through the use of art! For this program, we are on the Northside of Dallas on Fridays from 4:00-5:40 pm. We do art therapy-based programming with the children to have fun and create with us while forgetting their daily stresses. We focus on empowerment, using only nice words and collaboration. It's so much fun and full of positivity! We bring all the supplies, you come to hang out and help them with the activity! We are looking for volunteers over 18. Please fill out our volunteer application at drawchange.org/volunteerapp - that's the first step! Thank you in advance!

If you love to utilize your creative and artistic abilities in a positive and powerful way, please contact us about becoming an Art Therapy Volunteer at Hope Health Care! As an art therapy volunteer, at Hope Health Care you would be sharing your art and assisting seniors and their families create their own art such as: drawing, painting or collage. Time Commitment is very flexible and based on your availability. We have patients located all over greater Dallas area. All ages over 16 are welcome! Please email volunteer.program@hopehealthcareus.com or call (972) 366-5030 for more information!

opportunity_title: HR Team Member (Recruiter): Help solve the literacy crisis
description_short: Have you ever wanted to do something AMAZING to change the world but didn't know where to start? Here is an opportunity to join the American Youth Literacy Foundation, an all- volunteer organization, founded in 2008 and dedicated to providing a…
description_long: Have you ever wanted to do something AMAZING to change the world but didn't know where to start? Here is an opportunity to join the American Youth Literacy Foundation, an all- volunteer organization, founded in 2008 and dedicated to providing a gateway to a future in which all children, in every classroom in America, learn how to read by the end of 3rd grade. Our mission is to save millions of children from a lifetime of illiteracy. We started as a tutoring organization in 2008 and, throughout 2020 we created an app, based on our patented reading curriculum, that takes kids from the ABC's to a 2nd grade reading level through an amazing journey that's fully narrated and tastefully animated, and gamified just enough to create an immersive, interactive environment that captures kids' attention and imagination and launches them to literacy in a fun and easy way. With the FUNetix app, 100% free download and use, any child can learn how to read in 12 hours, and in America’s public schools today, there are millions of children who can’t read or are struggling to read. Right now, we are looking to expand our incredible Team FUNetix and we are looking for a skilled HR Team Member . The HR Team Member will be responsible for meeting hiring goals by filling open volunteer positions with talented and qualified candidates. This entails sourcing and screening candidates, conducting interviews, and coordinating the interview process, all while ensuring candidates have a pleasant experience. We hope this opportunity finds you at the right place and the right time in your career to make a difference and an impact, in an amazing way, for millions of children who are waiting for someone, or something, to lead them out of a life of impoverishment and into a future of unlimited possibility and potential. So, if this sounds like something that you have the time for and interest in, and if you have the confidence and ability to work in a virtual environment using select networking and communications tools and platforms, then please reply to this listing. We look forward to hearing from you and speaking with you about the future of literacy in America. You can read more about our story and about us on our website at https://funetix.org/ Mr. R. Kali Woodward Founding, Executive Director

opportunity_title: HUMAN RELATIONS MANAGER (VOLUNTEER A FEW PER WEEK)
description_short: HELP US BUILD OUR HUMAN RELATIONS DEPARTMENT! Volunteer a Few Hours Per Week (pathway to compensation role in the future) Help Us Build Something Meaningful from the Ground Up The Grandparents Community Network is seeking motivated individuals who…
description_long: HELP US BUILD OUR HUMAN RELATIONS DEPARTMENT! Volunteer a Few Hours Per Week (pathway to compensation role in the future) Help Us Build Something Meaningful from the Ground Up The Grandparents Community Network is seeking motivated individuals who are interested in helping us strengthen and grow our Human Relations / People Operations Department. This is a unique opportunity for someone who: wants hands-on experience enjoys organization and people support is interested in Human Resources, Operations, Leadership, or Organizational Development wants to grow professionally while making a real impact Whether you are a student, career changer, retired professional, emerging leader, or simply someone who enjoys helping organizations grow, we would love to hear from you. Volunteer Commitment Flexible Opportunity Volunteer a few hours per week based on your availability. We understand life and work schedules vary and are looking for dependable individuals willing to contribute consistently. Areas You May Assist With Recruiting & Outreach Posting opportunities Screening applicants Scheduling interviews Following up with candidates Onboarding Support Welcome communication Organizing onboarding packets Tracking required forms Supporting new team members Team Culture & Communication Team engagement Volunteer appreciation Internal communication support Encouraging accountability and responsiveness Administrative Support Maintaining records Organizing documents Updating dashboards Tracking tasks and follow-ups Workforce Development Helping improve systems Assisting with training coordination Supporting operational growth Building organizational structure Roles & Responsibilities Volunteers may assist with: workforce coordination personnel support scheduling onboarding task tracking internal communications document organization project support follow-up management accountability systems What You Will Gain Real-world leadership experience Human Relations / HR exposure Organizational development experience Operations and management skills Training and mentorship Experience building systems from the ground up Opportunity to grow with a mission-driven organization We Are Looking For Individuals Who Are (IF THIS IS NOT YOU DO NOT APPLY) dependable organized responsive professional willing to learn adaptable to change comfortable stepping into new challenges passionate about helping people and strengthening organizations Ideal For Human Relations / HR majors Business students Administrative professionals Retired professionals Operations-minded individuals Emerging nonprofit leaders People looking for meaningful volunteer experience Important Note This is currently a volunteer opportunity with flexible participation; however, as organizational growth and funding expand, some positions may develop into future paid opportunities. Spread Your Wings. Embrace Something New. Help us build systems that help families, grandparents, caregivers, and communities thrive. To express interest, contact: Rose Payton gloryabolden@tgcncorporate.org Website: www.tgcncorporate.org

opportunity_title: Hair Stylist for Hospice (Volunteer)
description_short: Are you a licensed stylist with a few extra hours in your week? We need you! A couple times a month, we receive requests from our patients for hair cuts. It would be wonderful to have a few talented stylists for us to call! We take care of people…
description_long: Are you a licensed stylist with a few extra hours in your week? We need you! A couple times a month, we receive requests from our patients for hair cuts. It would be wonderful to have a few talented stylists for us to call! We take care of people who are at the end of their lives. They aren't able to make it to the salon. Often they're in wheelchairs. Sometimes, they are bed bound. When needed, you would visit patients in their homes or elder-care facilities in the DFW metroplex. We have patients all over the metroplex but the most common locations are Flower Mound, Lewisville, Plano, and Denton. You tell us how far you're willing to drive and we'll keep it within that range. Although this would be a "when needed" role, we still ask that you attend our training program. Training is provided in a group setting on a Saturday OR on a one-on-one basis in our Flower Mound office for a few hours during a weekday. We'll work around your schedule - please contact us for details.

opportunity_title: Head of Fundraising & Partnerships (Volunteer)
description_short: Position Type Volunteer Leadership Position Time Commitment Approximately 6 hours per week Location Remote (United States-based preferred, open to global African Diaspora applicants) Reports To Board Chair About The African Think Tank The African…
description_long: Position Type Volunteer Leadership Position Time Commitment Approximately 6 hours per week Location Remote (United States-based preferred, open to global African Diaspora applicants) Reports To Board Chair About The African Think Tank The African Think Tank (TATT) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to fostering belonging and facilitating the professional, entrepreneurial, and economic transformation of the African Diaspora. Through educational programming, networking opportunities, cultural initiatives, and strategic partnerships, TATT empowers Africans, African Americans, and Friends of Africa to thrive and create lasting impact within their communities. Position Summary The Head of Fundraising & Partnerships serves as a strategic volunteer leader responsible for advancing TATT's fundraising and partnership development efforts. This individual will oversee the Fundraising Committee, Partnerships Committee, and Grant Writing Team, helping to build sustainable revenue streams, strengthen organizational relationships, and expand TATT's impact. Working closely with the Executive Director, Board Members, and committee volunteers, the Head of Fundraising & Partnerships will develop and execute fundraising strategies, cultivate mission-aligned partnerships, and guide grant development efforts that support the organization's long-term growth and sustainability. Key Responsibilities Fundraising Leadership Lead and coordinate TATT's Fundraising Committee. Develop and implement annual fundraising strategies and goals. Identify opportunities to diversify revenue streams, including individual giving, corporate sponsorships, grants, workplace giving, and fundraising campaigns. Support donor cultivation, stewardship, and retention efforts. Assist in the planning and execution of fundraising initiatives and special events, including the Ubuntu Gala and other fundraising activities. Partnership Development Lead and oversee the Partnerships Committee. Identify, cultivate, and maintain strategic relationships with corporations, foundations, educational institutions, community organizations, and mission-aligned partners. Develop partnership proposals and sponsorship opportunities. Strengthen existing partnerships while identifying new opportunities for collaboration and resource sharing. Support regional leaders in developing local partnerships and sponsorships. Grant Development & Oversight Provide leadership and direction to the Grant Writing Team. Identify relevant grant opportunities aligned with TATT's mission and programs. Coordinate grant research, proposal development, submission timelines, and reporting requirements. Review grant applications and ensure high-quality submissions. Collaborate with program leaders to gather data, impact stories, and program outcomes needed for grant proposals. Strategic Planning & Reporting Work with organizational leadership to establish annual fundraising and partnership goals. Monitor progress against fundraising targets and partnership objectives. Provide monthly updates to leadership regarding fundraising activities, grant opportunities, partnership development, and committee performance. Maintain a pipeline of prospective funders, sponsors, and partners. Qualifications Preferred Experience Experience in nonprofit fundraising, development, grant writing, sponsorship acquisition, business development, partnership management, or related fields. Demonstrated success securing grants, sponsorships, donations, or strategic partnerships. Experience leading teams, committees, or volunteer groups. Strong relationship-building and communication skills. Excellent organizational and project management abilities. Desired Characteristics Passion for empowering the African Diaspora and advancing community-centered solutions. Strategic thinker with an entrepreneurial mindset. Collaborative and relationship-oriented leader. Self-motivated and able to work independently. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and community empowerment. Expected Outcomes Within the first 12 months, the Head of Fundraising & Partnerships will help: Strengthen and organize TATT's fundraising and partnerships infrastructure. Increase the number of strategic partnerships supporting TATT's programs and events. Expand grant prospecting and proposal submissions. Support growth in sponsorship and donor revenue. Enhance coordination and effectiveness across fundraising, partnership, and grant-writing efforts. Benefits of Volunteering Opportunity to help shape one of the fastest-growing African Diaspora organizations in the United States. Gain nonprofit leadership and board-level experience. Expand your professional network across the African Diaspora ecosystem. Contribute directly to programs that empower communities through education, entrepreneurship, culture, and economic advancement. Work alongside passionate leaders committed to creating lasting impact. Volunteer Position & Performance Incentives This is a volunteer leadership position requiring an estimated commitment of 6 hours per week. While the role is unpaid, the organization may offer incentive-based compensation, stipends, or performance bonuses tied to the achievement of specific fundraising, partnership, and organizational growth milestones, subject to available funding and approval by the Executive Leadership Team.

opportunity_title: Heart to Heart Hospice Volunteer
description_short: At Heart-to-Heart Hospice, our volunteers play a vital role in bringing comfort, connection, and care to patients and families during life’s most meaningful moments. Whether you enjoy sharing stories, reading aloud, singing, or simply offering a…
description_long: At Heart-to-Heart Hospice, our volunteers play a vital role in bringing comfort, connection, and care to patients and families during life’s most meaningful moments. Whether you enjoy sharing stories, reading aloud, singing, or simply offering a listening ear, your presence can make a lasting difference. Volunteers can provide companionship to patients, support caregivers, help with bereavement, or assist in creative projects like life-story journals. For those who prefer an office setting, opportunities are also available to help with light administrative work, phone calls, community outreach, and event support. Every act of service—big or small—helps bring warmth and dignity to those we serve. Join us in making a heartfelt impact today.

opportunity_title: Help Abuse Victims By Driving!
description_short: It takes a lot to leave an abuser -- Courage. Planning. Resources. Help. We get calls for service everyday from survivors trying to restart life after violence, but we need more volunteer drivers to keep up with the demand. We need an awesome person…
description_long: It takes a lot to leave an abuser -- Courage. Planning. Resources. Help. We get calls for service everyday from survivors trying to restart life after violence, but we need more volunteer drivers to keep up with the demand. We need an awesome person like you! Assist victims making their break from abuser by driving them to safety! You choose the distance you're willing to travel - whether across town or across the country - and Families to Freedom handles the rest. You just need to be 21+, have a good driving record, pass a background check, and a heart to help. We ONLY allow our volunteers to pick-up survivors in a safe public place such as a retail store, shelter, hospital, hotel lobby or police station. Volunteer safety is our #1 priority. There is no contact with an abuser. Transporting a survivor traveling with children? We prepare the car seat for you. Worried about the cost of gas, tolls, food and lodging? We pay it. Don't want to use your personal car? Use one of ours. Not sure you'll be up for a trip when one comes along? Decline any drive you wish with no pressure from us. Weekday daytime availability is most needed! Start the process at FamiliestoFreedom.org/Volunteer.php For more details or answers to specific questions call us at 972-885-7020. Orientation and training can be conducted remotely over video, or in our Dallas office.

opportunity_title: Help Adults Earn Their GED
description_short: In Dallas today, there are thousands of adults who are not able to reach their potential because they need an alternate pathway to a high school diploma. Aspire volunteers and students share an amazing experience as both parties engage in learning.…
description_long: In Dallas today, there are thousands of adults who are not able to reach their potential because they need an alternate pathway to a high school diploma. Aspire volunteers and students share an amazing experience as both parties engage in learning. Students have a deep desire to improve and volunteers get the joy of experiencing their students grow. We welcome both experienced and novice instructors. If you have not taught adults, we will help you gain the skills to be an effective instructor. We provide curriculum, training, and support. Instructors will guide and support students as they build core academic knowledge and essential skills necessary for success in most jobs and postsecondary programs in today’s economy. An alternative path to a high school credential Knowledge and skills necessary for success in today’s workplace A safe environment to explore their strengths and weaknesses, academically, and professionally Qualifications: HSE (also known as GED) teaching positions do not require previous teaching experience. Our staff will provide the lesson plans and materials. Other qualifications are as follows: Willingness to demonstrate or aspire to a growth mindset Must be fluent in English and easy to understand Math background helpful, but not required Ability to dedicate a minimum of 3 hours each week to teaching, class preparation, and occasional training or peer support sessions. Patience, understanding, flexibility, and a positive attitude Must be able to pass a background check

opportunity_title: Help Convert a Garage into a Community Learning Space
description_short: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is seeking volunteers, skilled trades professionals, contractors, businesses, and community partners to help transform an underutilized garage at our Dallas transitional housing property into a…
description_long: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is seeking volunteers, skilled trades professionals, contractors, businesses, and community partners to help transform an underutilized garage at our Dallas transitional housing property into a Community Learning Space. This project will create a dedicated area for workshops, workforce development, life skills training, peer support meetings, virtual learning, community engagement, and other educational opportunities that help individuals build stability and move toward their goals. We are looking for volunteers and partners who can assist with: Construction & Facility Improvements HVAC installation Electrical work and additional outlets Lighting installation Flooring Insulation Drywall and finishing Painting General carpentry Shelving installation Furniture & Equipment Tables Chairs Desks Storage cabinets Bookshelves Whiteboards Office furniture Technology Computers and laptops Large TV or display screen Webcam and livestream equipment Printers Networking and internet equipment Outdoor Storage Storage shed Shed assembly and installation Outdoor storage solutions Why This Project Matters The Community Learning Space will provide a dedicated environment where individuals can: Participate in workforce development activities Build digital and employment skills Attend workshops and educational programming Access peer support and resources Participate in virtual learning opportunities Engage in community meetings and events By creating a dedicated space outside the house's living areas, residents and community members will have a more professional and comfortable environment for learning, collaboration, and growth. Volunteer Opportunities We welcome: Skilled trades professionals Contractors Construction crews Trade schools and vocational programs Corporate volunteer teams Community organizations Individual volunteers with relevant experience Whether you can donate time, materials, services, equipment, or professional expertise, your support can help bring this vision to life. Project Location Dallas, Texas About TWCUYC The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is a nonprofit organization that works to embolden communities through housing stability, education, community engagement, and advocacy. Through our transitional housing program and supportive services, we help individuals build the foundation needed for long-term success. As a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, qualifying donations are tax-deductible. If you are interested in supporting this project, please contact: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) 📞 972-559-9201 📧 info@twcuyc.org Together, we can help ensure that individuals in our program have a safe and secure place to call home.

opportunity_title: Help End Addiction! Volunteer at Shatterproof Walk Dallas 2026
description_short: The Shatterproof Walk is an opportunity for communities to come together by walking and volunteering to end addiction while surrounded by friends, family, and loved ones. We'll honor those we have lost, celebrate those who are living in recovery,…
description_long: The Shatterproof Walk is an opportunity for communities to come together by walking and volunteering to end addiction while surrounded by friends, family, and loved ones. We'll honor those we have lost, celebrate those who are living in recovery, and bring hope to the millions living with substance use disorder. Together, we can save lives. Together, we are Shatterproof. The 2026 Dallas Walk offers meaningful, high-impact volunteer opportunities for individuals and groups who want to support this powerful community event. Help play a critical role in creating a welcoming and organized experience for all Walk participants through: set-up, check-in, spirit team, route support, mission specific areas, and more ! What to Expect Volunteer t-shirt provided Snacks and beverages available Community service hours can be verified Training and support from the Shatterproof Volunteer Team Submit your interest to be contacted about available roles and next steps. We're excited to connect with you and help find the opportunity that's the best fit. Watch our video to learn more: Shatterproof Walk

opportunity_title: Help Replace the Fence at Our Dallas Transitional Housing Program
description_short: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is seeking support to replace a severely deteriorated fence surrounding our transitional housing property in Dallas. The current fence is rotted and no longer provides the safety, privacy, and…
description_long: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) is seeking support to replace a severely deteriorated fence surrounding our transitional housing property in Dallas. The current fence is rotted and no longer provides the safety, privacy, and security our residents deserve. This project will help create a more stable and welcoming environment for individuals working toward housing stability, recovery, and long term self sufficiency. We are seeking: Fence installation services Fencing materials Labor donations Skilled volunteers Corporate or community partners willing to support the project Contractors, fence companies, builders, suppliers, trade professionals, volunteer groups, and community organizations are encouraged to connect with us. As a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, qualifying donations are tax deductible. If you are interested in supporting this project, please contact: The Wright Cause Urban Youth Conservation (TWCUYC) 📞 972-559-9201 📧 info@twcuyc.org Together, we can help ensure that individuals in our program have a safe and secure place to call home.

opportunity_title: Help a Neighbor in the Arlington TX area with a Homemade Lasagna
description_short: Lasagna Love is a global grassroots movement powered by thousands of volunteers cooking and delivering lasagna to neighbors in need. Our purpose is simple yet profound: feed families, spread kindness, and strengthen communities. We are united by our…
description_long: Lasagna Love is a global grassroots movement powered by thousands of volunteers cooking and delivering lasagna to neighbors in need. Our purpose is simple yet profound: feed families, spread kindness, and strengthen communities. We are united by our commitment to confront the rising tide of food insecurity and to offer a tangible act of love, compassion, and hope in a world often marked by uncertainty and stress. Together, we are proving that kindness has the power to nourish not only bodies but also hearts and communities everywhere. As a volunteer Lasagna Chef, you choose your level of participation—once a month, several lasagnas a week, or anything in between. You also decide how far you’re able to drive to deliver and whether you can accommodate special diets. Volunteers purchase the ingredients and cook the lasagna, and the Lasagna Love team handles the matching. We also provide online training to make you comfortable in your volunteer role. If you are looking for a flexible opportunity, or a way for your family to volunteer together, making a lasagna for a neighbor in need is a great opportunity to spread some kindness!

opportunity_title: Help a disabled senior with yardwork in Wilmer, TX
description_short: Help with lawn care including mowing, edging/trimming and a light cleanup. They do not have their own equipment so you will have to bring your own. They also need help with handyman tasks. We suggest that you bring your own tools. If you are working…

They also need help with handyman tasks. We suggest that you bring your own tools. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we can provide documentation for your hours.

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Help a neighbor with yard care in Dallas, TX · Ref IWTMYL-LJJ
description_short: A military veteran in Dallas, TX would welcome help keeping their yard in good shape this season — a practical, visible way for the community to give back to someone who served our country. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A military veteran in Dallas, TX would welcome help keeping their yard in good shape this season — a practical, visible way for the community to give back to someone who served our country. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming. You're helping a neighbor maintain a beautiful outdoor space. This is a home shared by a household of three, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. A tidy, well-maintained yard means safer footing and less to worry about for a veteran who has earned an easier season, and it is a direct way for neighbors to show their gratitude. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Dallas, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-LJJ&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-LJJ

opportunity_title: Help us grow students' reading skills! Become a morning Summer Reading Buddy!
description_short: Reading Buddies are a key part of our programs and provide the essential reading comprehension assistance to a student once a week. As a Reading Buddy, you will help a child develop their comprehension skills through reading high-quality literature…
description_long: Reading Buddies are a key part of our programs and provide the essential reading comprehension assistance to a student once a week. As a Reading Buddy, you will help a child develop their comprehension skills through reading high-quality literature to them. Above all, you will be a positive role model who encourages them and makes reading fun! Summer Reading Buddies are asked to attend at least 5 sessions during our 6 week summer camp program. While we prefer the same session every week, you can choose any 5 sessions that work for you. When: June 15th - July 22nd, Monday-Wednesday.Morning session (9:15-11:00am) or Afternoon session (1:15-3:05pm) Where: Readers 2 Leaders Learning Center (Booktown): 2800 N Hampton Rd, Suite 120, Dallas, TX 75212 How To Get Started: Once you know what day(s) and times work best for you, please visit the following link to submit your contact information and availability. We will be in contact with you regarding scheduling. Sign up here: https://volunteer.bloomerang.co :443/JE/d2glvnd6rh1g6q Questions: If you have any questions please contact us via email, volunteer@readers2leaders.org , or at the R2L office (214) 905-0095.

Your profile will be searchable on our site, and you will be contacted by a family as needs arise in your community! Get started by applying today at ElderHelpers.org

opportunity_title: Highland Park, TX - Drive cancer patients to and from treatment
description_short: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their…
description_long: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their appointments. Most rides are Monday-Friday during the day. You choose the rides that fit your schedule, with a suggested minimum of 2 rides per month. Volunteer drivers must: Be age 18-84 Have a valid driver’s license and safe driving record Use a reliable, insured vehicle Pass background and motor vehicle records checks Complete online training and use our online ride scheduling tool Learn more at cancer.org/drive. We need you now more than ever. Watch our video to learn more: Hope Behind The Wheel

opportunity_title: Honor Our Heroes: Become a Veteran-to-Veteran Hospice Volunteer with Trinity Hospice
description_short: Have you served in the military? Do you have a deep respect for those who have? Trinity Hospice is seeking compassionate Veteran volunteers to provide specialized support to fellow Veterans in hospice care. Your shared experiences and understanding…
description_long: Have you served in the military? Do you have a deep respect for those who have? Trinity Hospice is seeking compassionate Veteran volunteers to provide specialized support to fellow Veterans in hospice care. Your shared experiences and understanding can create a unique bond and offer comfort during a challenging time. What does a Veteran-to-Veteran Hospice Volunteer do? Our Veteran-to-Veteran Volunteers provide companionship, emotional support, and a sense of camaraderie to Veteran patients. Your service can include: Sharing stories and experiences: Connecting with Veterans through shared military experiences and understanding their unique perspectives. Providing a listening ear: Offering a safe and non-judgmental space for Veterans to share their stories, concerns, and reflections on their service. Honoring their service: Acknowledging and appreciating the sacrifices made by Veteran patients. Offering support to families: Providing support and understanding to the families of Veteran patients, recognizing the unique challenges they may face. Reading military-related materials: Sharing books, articles, or poems related to military history or experiences. Simply being present: Offering a comforting presence and a sense of connection with someone who understands their background. Collaborating with the hospice team: Working alongside nurses, social workers, and other professionals to ensure the specific needs of Veteran patients are being met. Why Volunteer with Trinity Hospice as a Veteran-to-Veteran Volunteer? Meaningful Impact: Your shared experience and understanding can provide invaluable support to fellow Veterans during their end-of-life journey. Unique Connection: Build a special bond with Veteran patients based on shared military service. Personal Fulfillment: Experience the deep satisfaction of giving back to those who have served our country. Flexible Scheduling: We offer flexible volunteer opportunities to accommodate your availability. Specialized Training: We provide training on working with hospice patients, including sensitivity training, communication skills, and understanding the unique needs of Veterans. Be Part of a Team: Join a compassionate community of volunteers and staff dedicated to providing exceptional care. Who are We Looking For? We welcome Veterans from all branches of service who: Have a deep respect for fellow Veterans. Are compassionate, empathetic, and patient. Are good listeners. Are respectful and sensitive to the needs of hospice patients and their families. Are reliable and dependable. Are committed to maintaining confidentiality. How to Get Involved: If you are a Veteran interested in becoming a Veteran-to-Veteran Hospice Volunteer, we would be honored to hear from you! Call us: 469.726.4402 Visit our website: www.TrinityHospice.com Email us: bkaiser@TrinityHospice.com Honor their service. Provide comfort and companionship. Become a Veteran-to-Veteran Hospice Volunteer with Trinity Hospice Trinity Hospice - Providing Comfort and Care in DFW

opportunity_title: Hopestone Hospice Volunteer Opportunity- Dallas
description_short: Hopestone Hospice is seeking dedicated and compassionate volunteers to join our team in Dallas, Texas. Our volunteers play an invaluable role in enhancing the quality of life for patients and families, providing companionship, support, and care…
description_long: Hopestone Hospice is seeking dedicated and compassionate volunteers to join our team in Dallas, Texas. Our volunteers play an invaluable role in enhancing the quality of life for patients and families, providing companionship, support, and care during a sensitive time. If you’re looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community, volunteering with Hopestone Hospice offers an opportunity to make a profound impact. As a hospice volunteer, you will be a source of comfort and kindness to individuals facing end-of-life journeys. Whether you’re engaging in conversations, reading to a patient, offering respite to caregivers, or simply being a calming presence, your time and compassion can make a world of difference. Volunteer Activities Include: Patient Companionship: Spend time with patients, providing companionship and emotional support through conversation, reading, or simply being present. Family Support: Offer support to family members by providing a listening ear and reassurance, helping them navigate this challenging time. Respite for Caregivers: Give caregivers a much-needed break by sitting with patients so caregivers can rest and recharge. Why Volunteer with Hopestone Hospice? Comprehensive Training: All volunteers receive thorough training to feel comfortable and prepared for their roles. Flexible Scheduling: We offer flexible hours to accommodate your availability, making it easy to give back on your schedule. Personal Fulfillment: Volunteering with Hopestone Hospice is not only rewarding but also provides valuable insights into the power of compassion, care, and connection. If you have a heart for service and a desire to help others, join our Hopestone Hospice family. Your kindness and support can bring peace, dignity, and comfort to patients and their families. Start your volunteer journey with Hopestone Hospice in Dallas-apply today to make a meaningful impact!

opportunity_title: Hospice Admin Support
description_short: At Halcyon Home, we believe we were called for something greater - we are called to care. Every patient is a person with hopes, dreams, and dignity. Every day, every patient in our care deserves someone to be present in the moment providing a higher…
description_long: At Halcyon Home, we believe we were called for something greater - we are called to care. Every patient is a person with hopes, dreams, and dignity. Every day, every patient in our care deserves someone to be present in the moment providing a higher level of end-of-life care. Care that comes from the heart. We are seeking volunteers to help with administrative tasks, such as calling patients and families to ensure they have enough supplies and medications to last through the weekend, creating patient and volunteer binders, filing patient documentation and more. Please note this opportunity is required to be in-person at this time. Training and orientation are provided for this opportunity. For more information, please contact our Volunteer Coordinator, at tford@halcyonhome.com
