opportunity_title: Hospice Administrative Volunteer- Garland
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Interested in applying your skills in Microsoft Office to a good cause? Hope Health Care is looking for individuals that are interested in administrative service at our office in Garland, TX.…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Interested in applying your skills in Microsoft Office to a good cause? Hope Health Care is looking for individuals that are interested in administrative service at our office in Garland, TX. Administrative duties would include but are not limited to: Microsoft Excel spreadsheets. Answering phones and taking messages. Documentation maintenance. Brochure creation. Mailing letters. Organization and file maintenance. If this opportunity interests you, please contact Volunteer Coordinator at volunteer.program@hopehealthcareus.com.

opportunity_title: Hospice Animal Assisted Therapy Volunteer
description_short: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and…
description_long: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and enhance our unique programs and services. Faithful Paws Volunteers (animal assisted therapy) share their four-legged friends with patients who enjoy the companionship of an animal. These four-legged volunteers must be certified through an animal certification program. Patients are located in their own homes/assisted living facilities throughout the Dallas/Ft. Worth metroplex.

opportunity_title: Hospice Birthday Program Volunteers Needed!
description_short: Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking volunteers to participate in our Birthday Celebration Program to honor our hospice patients on their special day. We are looking for kind, reliable volunteers to help bring joy, recognition, and comfort to patients…
description_long: Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking volunteers to participate in our Birthday Celebration Program to honor our hospice patients on their special day. We are looking for kind, reliable volunteers to help bring joy, recognition, and comfort to patients through thoughtfully prepared birthday cards, gifts bags, and option in-person greetings. Your participation helps ensures every patient feels remembered and valued. What You'll Do: Create or sign birthday cards with warm, uplifting messages Assemble birthday gift bags (small comfort items, crafts, treats - provided by our team) Deliver birthday bags/cards to patients' homes or care facilities Provide a brief, friendly visit to wish the patient and family Assist the Bristol Hospice team with tracking upcoming birthdays and preparing materials Help maintain organization of birthday supplies at the office Communicate updates through our volunteer documentation system Who We're Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a desire to serve hospice patients Volunteers who are dependable and comfortable working independently or with others Crafters, creative individuals, students, retirees, and community members Must be at least 14 years old to apply, but 18 years old to visit directly with our patients Must complete hospice volunteer onboarding and training (provided at no cost) Must pass background check and TB screening (standard for all hospice volunteers) Time Commitment & Scheduling: Very flexible - Volunteers can help weekdays or weekends We will work with your schedule and availability! Delivery volunteers may choose assignments near their home To learn more information about this opportunity, please contact our Regional Volunteer Coordinator, Mackensie Moore, at mackensie.moore@bristolhospice.com

opportunity_title: Hospice Caregiver Relief Volunteer-Volunteer Where You Live!
description_short: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and…
description_long: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and enhance our unique programs and services. A Caregiver Relief Volunteer sits with the patient while their primary caregiver attends to errands, appointments, or self-care. Patients are located in their own homes/assisted living facilities throughout the Dallas/Ft. Worth Metroplex and volunteers can choose the area they would like to volunteer in.

opportunity_title: Hospice Chaplain Volunteer
description_short: "To minister is to walk beside someone at their most vulnerable moments and remind them peace is still possible." Volunteer hospice chaplains often become a steady, comforting presence for patients and families as they navigate emotional and…
description_long: "To minister is to walk beside someone at their most vulnerable moments and remind them peace is still possible." Volunteer hospice chaplains often become a steady, comforting presence for patients and families as they navigate emotional and spiritual moments. Through compassionate support, chaplains help bring peace, comfort, and reassurance during times of uncertainty. Why should you volunteer for this opportunity? Pastoral Experience: Ideal for seminary students, clergy, or retired ministry. Flexible Scheduling: Volunteer weekly, monthly, or as needed. Documented Hours: For seminary, CPE, or community service. No-Cost Training: Full hospice training and ongoing support.

org_name: Medical City Hospice and Family Care
opportunity_title: Hospice Companion - Grand Prairie
description_short: Enjoy spending time with seniors? Want to give back? Volunteer with Medical City Hospice and Family Care and provide companionship and caring presence to hospice patients living at a DFW community or at home in the Grand Prairie area. No experience…
description_long: Enjoy spending time with seniors? Want to give back? Volunteer with Medical City Hospice and Family Care and provide companionship and caring presence to hospice patients living at a DFW community or at home in the Grand Prairie area. No experience required. Listen to stories, watch TV, listen to music, talk about sports or movies, hold hands, and make a difference in a person's day. You will receive free orientation and training and will be able to meet patients to read, share stories, listen to music, or provide respite for family members. Your companionship and caring presence ensures that patients spend the end of their life in dignity and comfort. Medical City Hospice and Family Care has many other locations throughout the DFW area and surrounding cities. Training and orientation will be arranged to fit to your schedule. Contact Medical City and Family Care 469-872-4642 PLEASE CHECK YOUR SPAM INBOX.

No medical experience is required - just a caring heart! Flexible schedule and full volunteer training is provided. Requirements: Must be 18 years or older Must have reliable transportation Must complete all hospice volunteer onboarding, screenings, and training prior to starting volunteer activities Must be reliable and have excellent communication skills To learn more of have any questions regarding our volunteer program, please contact our office at 972-923-2436

opportunity_title: Hospice Companion Volunteers Needed in Mesquite, TX!
description_short: Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking compassionate and caring individuals to join our volunteer team. Our volunteers provide companionship and support to patients and families during their hospice journey. What You'll Do: Offer companionship through…
description_long: Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking compassionate and caring individuals to join our volunteer team. Our volunteers provide companionship and support to patients and families during their hospice journey. What You'll Do: Offer companionship through friendly visits, reading, sharing stories, or simply being present Provide emotional support to patients and families Provide caregiver relief support for families/primary caregivers Engage in light activities such as playing music, doing puzzles, or writing letters Requirements: Must be 18 years old or older Have a heart for service and compassion for those at end of life Reliability and commitment to visit patients regularly (schedule is flexible) Completion of hospice volunteer training Completion of background check and TB test (arranged by Bristol Hospice) Why Volunteer with Us? Make a lasting impact in someone's life Gain meaningful experience in hospice care Receive ongoing support and training from our hospice team Flexible schedules that fit your availability

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Hospice Companion and Respite Support
description_short: Looking for a rewarding Volunteer Position...look no further! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a…
description_long: Looking for a rewarding Volunteer Position...look no further! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description Enhabit Hospice is looking for a volunteer to provide friendship to patients during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, caregiver respite, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. For more information, please contact: Faith Oates - Volunteer Coordinator - Bereavement Services

opportunity_title: Hospice Friend | DFW South (Duncanville, Cedar Hill, Mansfield...)
description_short: Do you have about an hour a week to be a friend to someone with a terminal diagnosis? Many hospice patients have outlived their family and friends or have very little support at the end of their life. Did you see the story on FB about the…
description_long: Do you have about an hour a week to be a friend to someone with a terminal diagnosis? Many hospice patients have outlived their family and friends or have very little support at the end of their life. Did you see the story on FB about the 90-year-old woman who left a note asking her neighbor to be her friend? It was heart wrenching to me. Her note said: "...Would you consider becoming my friend? I am 90 years old - live alone and all my friends have passed away. I am so lonesome and scared." Below is a link to another FB story that touched my heart. "When I'm alone, I can look at photos of my family and memories I brought from home. And that's all." We can help change that! We can be the friend, the visitor they long to have. There is no greater gift than the gift of presence. Someone to walk beside. We offer training and then assign you a patient. Most of our volunteers visit their patient once a week for about an hour. Volunteers visit, read, play games, play music, etc. No hands-on care, just be a friend. If you are interested, please email to hold a spot in our next training. Our primary criteria is that you have a heart to serve others. I look forward to serving this precious population with you.

I am 90 years old - live alone and all my friends have passed away. I am so lonesome and scared." There is no greater gift than the gift of presence. Someone to walk beside. We offer training and then assign you a patient that lives near you. Most of our volunteers visit their patient once a week for about an hour. Volunteers visit, read, play games, play music, etc. No hands-on care, just be a friend. If you are interested, please email to hold a spot in our next training. Our primary criteria is that you have a heart to serve others. Volunteers | Legend Hospice - Providing hospice care for Dallas-Fort Worth. I look forward to serving this precious population with you.

opportunity_title: Hospice Massage Therapist Volunteer
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Massage Therapist Volunteer Massage therapy is proven to reduce stress, blood pressure anxiety, insomnia and Fibromyalgia. It is also known to promote relaxation, circulation, posture and…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Massage Therapist Volunteer Massage therapy is proven to reduce stress, blood pressure anxiety, insomnia and Fibromyalgia. It is also known to promote relaxation, circulation, posture and strengthen body’s immune system. These are all things that could benefit a person who are caregiving for a loved one with a disability, disease or are at the end of life stage. Would you be willing to give your skilled services to a caregiver? Licensed massage therapist volunteer would offer their massage services to caregivers in their homes or facilities. Time Commitment is very flexible and based on your availability. We have patients located all over greater Dallas area. Please email volunteer.program@hopehealthcareus.com or call (972) 366-5030 for more information!

opportunity_title: Hospice Memory Bear Team
description_short: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and…
description_long: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and enhance our unique programs and services. Our Memory Bear Team sews teddy bears and pillows for our families who are grieving a loss. The family provides clothing from their loved one to be used as the fabric. The Memory Bear Volunteers work from their own home. Volunteer time varies as the requests for bears are submitted but typically 1-2 hours per week.

opportunity_title: Hospice Office Intern Needed in Fort Worth
description_short: Kindful Health is a privately owned hospice company that is focused on providing patients and their families with the full promise of the hospice benefit in the State of Texas. We are quickly growing and in need of office administrator volunteers…
description_long: Kindful Health is a privately owned hospice company that is focused on providing patients and their families with the full promise of the hospice benefit in the State of Texas. We are quickly growing and in need of office administrator volunteers like you to help us with the most important aspect of hospice: community. If you are interested in making a positive difference in our patients lives by assisting with our office tasks, event planning & client/community outreach then this is the volunteer opportunity for you! Please let us know if you are interested and I will reach out promptly to tell you more about our amazing company. - David B

opportunity_title: Hospice Office Volunteer
description_short: Help our patients get the best care possible! Our patients are the most important thing to us and we want to set them (and ourselves) up for success! So much goes in to running a hospice and ensuring that everything goes as smoothly as possible for…
description_long: Help our patients get the best care possible! Our patients are the most important thing to us and we want to set them (and ourselves) up for success! So much goes in to running a hospice and ensuring that everything goes as smoothly as possible for patients at the end of life. Part of our commitment to care is assuring that our field staff are supported by the hospice office and we need your help! Hospice administrative volunteers assist us with so many aspects of hospice care including patient information packets, marketing materials, hospice office telephone reception, tuck in calls to our patients, human resources and bereavement! Our office, located in North Dallas (off Belt Line road) always has more projects than we have hands to tackle them and we could really use your help! If you're interested in assisting us in the office, please contact shauntae' Harris , Volunteer Coordinator at 972-482-4382 or by email shauntae.harris@goodshepherdhospice.com today to get started! Available to people 18 years or older.

description_short: We are in need of an office volunteer to come in once or twice a week to help with our staffing paperwork. Packets that our nursing staff use when meeting and admitting new patients and families to Hospice. We are the largest hospice provider in the…
description_long: We are in need of an office volunteer to come in once or twice a week to help with our staffing paperwork. Packets that our nursing staff use when meeting and admitting new patients and families to Hospice. We are the largest hospice provider in the country and have a consistent need for help in this department as we are helping new patients daily. The VITAS Values Patients and families come first. We take care of each other. I'll do my best today and do even better tomorrow. I am proud to make a difference.

opportunity_title: Hospice Office Volunteering in the Hurst Area
description_short: Do you value the work of hospice, but rather not visit patients? Share your office or organization skills with Three Oaks Hospice and assist us with mailing letters, newsletters, greeting cards, help answer phones, make calls to Caregivers, create…
description_long: Do you value the work of hospice, but rather not visit patients? Share your office or organization skills with Three Oaks Hospice and assist us with mailing letters, newsletters, greeting cards, help answer phones, make calls to Caregivers, create packets/binders, organize activities and events for hospice patients and their families. No experience required and some opportunities can be completed at home. Three Oaks Hospice has many other opportunities for volunteering, as well. Training and orientation will be arranged to fit to your schedule. This is a flexible schedule and can be as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Feel free to contact Tonya Choate at 682-777-0722 or send an email to tchoate@ThreeOaksHospice.com to learn more.

opportunity_title: Hospice Patient Care Volunteer: South Dallas
description_short: Do you want to make a direct impact in someone's life? Good Shepherd Hospice is seeking compassionate, caring, empathetic, and dependable volunteers to work directly with hospice clients in their homes. Volunteers will provide client support…
description_long: Do you want to make a direct impact in someone's life? Good Shepherd Hospice is seeking compassionate, caring, empathetic, and dependable volunteers to work directly with hospice clients in their homes. Volunteers will provide client support services that contribute to the quality of life for clients and their families, such as respite, companionship, preparing meals or snacks, engaging the clients in hobbies and/or light chores such as dog walking or shopping. If you are compassionate, warm hearted and dedicated to service to others, this is the role for you! This is an excellent role for retired people who would like to support others as well as those looking to gain experience in medical or spiritual career paths. If this opportunity interests you, contact our Volunteer Coordinator at 972-482-4382 or email at shauntae.harris@goodshepherdhospice.com. Visit our website to fill out an application today!

Families on service have a need for increased interaction or the primary caregiver needs to get out of the house for a few hours. Families need a friendly face and a kind neighbor during a difficult time. Won’t You Be My Neighbor? Song by Fred Rogers

opportunity_title: Hospice Patient Companion
description_short: At Faith Presbyterian Hospice, we are dedicated to making each moment matter for the patients and families we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of…
description_long: At Faith Presbyterian Hospice, we are dedicated to making each moment matter for the patients and families we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian Hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers, like you, to provide and enhance our unique programs and services. A Patient Companion Volunteer offers socialization and companionship to a Faith Presbyterian Hospice patient. Patients are located in their own homes or facilities throughout the Dallas/Ft. Worth Metroplex. NO EXPERIENCE NECESSARY! Each volunteer is thoroughly trained before any patient assignment. The only requirement is that you must have a heart to serve and a desire to help make each moment matter for our patients and their families. As a Companion volunteer, you would be matched up with a patient who lives in the area you prefer to serve in. Your visits will provide companionship and socialization to increase the patient's quality of life, as well as provide a little time of respite relief for the patient's family/caregiver if needed. Visit frequency would be AT LEAST once per week. We understand that most people, retired or not, have a busy schedule, so the days and times you visit are flexible, based on your availability.

opportunity_title: Hospice Patient Companionship and/or Respite Volunteer
description_short: Hospice volunteers make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and caregivers. In this role, direct patient volunteers work in the home or at a senior living facility with terminally ill patients and their families. Duties may include: Patient…
description_long: Hospice volunteers make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and caregivers. In this role, direct patient volunteers work in the home or at a senior living facility with terminally ill patients and their families. Duties may include: Patient companionship: Talking, playing games, reading, engaging in hobbies. Caregiver Respite: Sit with a patient giving a caregiver the opportunity for self-care and complete needed activities. Other duties may include light household chores, shopping, transportation. All volunteers are required to go through an application process and complete volunteer training. Volunteers are encouraged to commit to volunteer for 2 hour shifts weekly or bi-weekly. We will work with your schedule and provide ongoing support for a fulfilling experience!

opportunity_title: Hospice Pet Therapy Volunteer
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Pet Therapy Pets have an innate ability for faithful companionship and unconditional love for those around them. As a pet owner you know this! You know the amazing feeling you feel when your pet…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Pet Therapy Pets have an innate ability for faithful companionship and unconditional love for those around them. As a pet owner you know this! You know the amazing feeling you feel when your pet loves on you! Would you be willing to share your pets love, warmth and humor to those who are at the end stage of their life? Pet therapy, also known as animal-assisted therapy, in the hospice setting uses the natural bond between humans and animals to provide comfort, peace, and soothing companionship to terminally ill patients. Time commitment is very flexible and based on your availability. We have patients located all over greater Dallas area. All ages over 16 are welcome! If your pet has been certified as a therapy animal through organizations such as Pet Partners, we are the place for you! Please email volunteer.program@hopehealthcareus.com or call (972) 366-5030 for more information!

opportunity_title: Hospice Quilters and Memory Bear Makers Volunteer
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Hope Health Care is looking for volunteers who would make home-sewn bears to help ease the loss after the death of a patient. The Memory Bears are sewn from the clothing of a patient (a favorite…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Hope Health Care is looking for volunteers who would make home-sewn bears to help ease the loss after the death of a patient. The Memory Bears are sewn from the clothing of a patient (a favorite shirt, dress, uniform, etc.). The Memory Bears create a lasting keepsake and offer comfort to the family (such as a child who feels alone after the loss of a parent, grandparent or sibling). Time Commitment is very flexible and based on your availability. We have patients located all over greater Dallas area. Please email volunteer.program@hopehealthcareus.com or call (972) 366-5030 for more information!

opportunity_title: Hospice Tuck In Caller
description_short: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and…
description_long: Faith Presbyterian Hospice is dedicated to making each moment matter for the people we serve. The needs of those we serve go beyond medical care alone. Faith Presbyterian hospice relies on the talents and dedication of volunteers to provide and enhance our unique programs and services. Tuck-In Volunteers make phone calls to our at-home patients to identify needs and make sure they are comfortable and have everything the need going in to the weekend. Tuck-In Calls are made on Thursdays during normal business hours.

opportunity_title: Hospice Tuck In Calls
description_short: Hope Health Care seeks Esteemed Volunteers to complete Tuck In Calls every Wednesday or Thursday to our Hope Health Care home patients and/or caregivers to assess any needs they might have for the weekend. This provides our staff with the…
description_long: Hope Health Care seeks Esteemed Volunteers to complete Tuck In Calls every Wednesday or Thursday to our Hope Health Care home patients and/or caregivers to assess any needs they might have for the weekend. This provides our staff with the opportunity to ensure that those needs are met before the weekend begins and assures our patients and/or caregivers have the very best care provided by Hope Health Care and our team members.

opportunity_title: Hospice Tuck-in Volunteers Needed!
description_short: Would you like to help ensure a hospice patient who is living independently has everything they need? Good Shepherd Hospice is looking for compassionate, reliable volunteers to make weekly "tuck in" calls to our hospice patients who are living on…
description_long: Would you like to help ensure a hospice patient who is living independently has everything they need? Good Shepherd Hospice is looking for compassionate, reliable volunteers to make weekly "tuck in" calls to our hospice patients who are living on their own. A Tuck-In call is just a quick check in to make sure that our patients and their families have everything they need to get through the weekend such as supplies and medications. Hospice volunteers can perform this service from the hospice office in North Dallas. This opportunity only requires about an hour or so a week and we'll provide you with training and instructions. If you can donate a little time to make sure our patients are comfortable, contact our Volunteer Coordinator today at 972-482-4382 or by email at shauntae.harris@goodshepherdhospice.com ! We hope to hear from you soon!

opportunity_title: Hospice Veteran Volunteers Needed in Dallas, TX
description_short: Are you a military veteran looking to give back? Many of our hospice patients are veterans who would love the companionship of someone who understands their service experience. As a Veteran Volunteer, you will: Provide companionship and emotional…
description_long: Are you a military veteran looking to give back? Many of our hospice patients are veterans who would love the companionship of someone who understands their service experience. As a Veteran Volunteer, you will: Provide companionship and emotional support to veteran patients Participate in recognition ceremonies honoring their service Offer a listening ear and camaraderie from shared experiences Requirements: Must be a US military veteran Compassionate, respectful, and reliable Completion of hospice volunteer training and onboarding Join our veteran-to-veteran volunteer program and make a lasting difference in the lives of our patients. Contact Mackensie Moore, RVC, with any questions or concerns at mackensie.moore@bristolhospice.com.

opportunity_title: Hospice Veteran-to-Veteran Volunteer
description_short: Our Veteran-to-Veteran Program seeks volunteer who are willing to share time with the nation's heroes in our care. We provide training and help Veterans understand their role as hospice volunteers - a valued part of our care team. We connect Veteran…
description_long: Our Veteran-to-Veteran Program seeks volunteer who are willing to share time with the nation's heroes in our care. We provide training and help Veterans understand their role as hospice volunteers - a valued part of our care team. We connect Veteran patients with Veteran volunteers who have a special ability to honor and connect, encouraging peace and healing. Volunteers may provide: Companionship Emotional support Respite for caregivers Additional opportunities include: Pinning ceremonies Community events Educational outreach through the We Honor Veteran Program

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteer
description_short: There are two ways someone can volunteer with hospice, being an administrative volunteer or a direct patient care volunteer. Administrative helps in the hospice office with clerical tasks such as Answering phones, filing, data entry, and organizing…
description_long: There are two ways someone can volunteer with hospice, being an administrative volunteer or a direct patient care volunteer. Administrative helps in the hospice office with clerical tasks such as Answering phones, filing, data entry, and organizing supplies at the hospice office. They can also work on special projects such as Making blankets, cards, or items for patients. they can help withcommunity outreach such as helping at health fairs, fundraising events, or community displays. The second way someone can volunteer with hospice is direct patient care which usually comprises of Visiting patients in homes, nursing homes, or assisted living to read, play music, watch TV, or share stories. Sitting with patients to allow family members to run errands. If you are someone who loves people and loves giving back in a meaningful way, come be a hospice volunteer with us.

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteer
description_short: The Hospice Volunteer serves as an essential member of the interdisciplinary hospice team. By providing regular, non-medical companionship and respite care, the volunteer enhances the quality of life for patients facing terminal illnesses and offers…
description_long: The Hospice Volunteer serves as an essential member of the interdisciplinary hospice team. By providing regular, non-medical companionship and respite care, the volunteer enhances the quality of life for patients facing terminal illnesses and offers crucial emotional support to their families and caregivers. Provide Presence : Sit quietly bedside, offer a listening ear, hold hands, or engage in meaningful conversations based on patient comfort. Social Engagement : Read books, play music, watch favorite movies, work on puzzles, or complete light crafts with the patient. Document Life Legacies : Assist patients in writing down memories, dictating letters, or compiling photo journals for their families. Relieve Caregivers : Remain with the patient for 2 to 4 hours per visit so family members can run errands, attend doctor visits, or take a nap. Emotional Comfort : Offer a compassionate, non-judgmental presence to families experiencing caregiver burnout or active grief. Assemble Packets : Put together the physical folders given to new patients, including consent forms, rights documents, and educational booklets. Scan Documents : Convert paper medical charts, signatures, and physician orders into electronic health record (EHR) systems. Audit Files : Double-check discharged patient files to ensure all required signatures are present before final archiving.

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteer - Light Housekeeping Support
description_short: Silverado Hospice is seeking compassionate and dependable volunteers to provide light housekeeping support for a hospice patient. Maintaining a clean, calm environment can greatly enhance comfort and quality of life for patients receiving…
description_long: Silverado Hospice is seeking compassionate and dependable volunteers to provide light housekeeping support for a hospice patient. Maintaining a clean, calm environment can greatly enhance comfort and quality of life for patients receiving end-of-life care. This is a meaningful opportunity to make a direct and personal impact through simple acts of service. Volunteer Responsibilities: Light tidying and organizing Sweeping, mopping Washing a small number of dishes Basic household upkeep (no deep cleaning or heavy lifting) Ideal Volunteer: Compassionate and respectful Reliable and able to maintain patient confidentiality Comfortable working independently At least 18 years old

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteer - Red Oak
description_short: Patient visits, life review, discuss hobbies & interests, current events, or read to patient, accompany patient outside if appropriate, companionship & socialization, monitor & report progress to hospice team. May sit with patient while…
description_long: Patient visits, life review, discuss hobbies & interests, current events, or read to patient, accompany patient outside if appropriate, companionship & socialization, monitor & report progress to hospice team. May sit with patient while caregiver runs errands, but no hands-on care. Dependability for patients and families. Compassionate, caring, good listening skills, able to sit quietly at times if needed. Interest in others & their life stories, own transportation, able to complete & send in documentation after each visit. Background check, drug screen, orientation, we cover costs associated to onboarding.

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteer Hair Stylist
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer If you look good, you feel good! But unfortunately some are unable to care for their appearance as often as they use too due to disability, maybe they are a caregiver or caring for someone at the…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer If you look good, you feel good! But unfortunately some are unable to care for their appearance as often as they use too due to disability, maybe they are a caregiver or caring for someone at the end of their life. In this case a simple: haircut, shave, painted nails or a make up touch up can go a long way! A hairstylist has the ability to make powerful difference in making others feel good about themselves! Come make a powerful difference in the lives of others! Licensed hair stylist volunteer would assist with haircuts and styles for patients in their homes or at our inpatient units. Time Commitment is very flexible and based on your availability. We have patients located all over greater Dallas area. All ages over 16 are welcome! Please email volunteer.program@hopehealthcareus.com or call (972) 366-5030 for more information!

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteer – Provide Comfort, Companionship, and Dignity
description_short: At Arc Hospice & Palliative Care, we are united by a mission to bring comfort, dignity, and compassionate support to every patient and family we serve. Join a team where your work has purpose, and your growth is supported. At Arc Hospice &…
description_long: At Arc Hospice & Palliative Care, we are united by a mission to bring comfort, dignity, and compassionate support to every patient and family we serve. Join a team where your work has purpose, and your growth is supported. At Arc Hospice & Palliative Care, we are united by a mission to bring comfort, dignity, and compassionate support to every patient and family we serve. Join a team where your service has purpose and your compassion makes a meaningful difference. Whether you are looking to provide direct patient companionship, support families, assist with administrative projects, or share a special talent, there is a place for you at Arc Hospice. Don't see a volunteer opportunity that matches your specialty, skills, or passion? Let us know what interests you. We believe everyone has something valuable to contribute, and we will work with you to find a meaningful volunteer role that aligns with your strengths and interests while supporting our patients, families, and community. Volunteer Opportunities We offer a variety of Direct Patient Care and Administrative support, including: Design Team Caregiver Respite Bereavement Support Nightingale Death Doula/Vigil Volunteers Care Connection Calls Office & Administrative Support Patient Engagement/Socialization Pet Therapy & Massage Therapy (licensed/certified volunteers) Qualifications Compassionate and empathetic demeanor Strong communication and listening skills Ability to maintain boundaries and patient confidentiality Emotional maturity and sensitivity and the ability to remain calm in difficult situations Reliable transportation for patient visits if required Demonstrates a strong commitment to delivering high-quality, person-centered care that reflects Arc Hospice & Palliative Care’s mission, values, and standards of dignity, respect, compassion, and integrity Requirements Minimum Age Requirement 18+ years old Completion of hospice orientation and training as well as competency validation, ongoing training, and performance evaluation as required by hospice policy Background check and health screening (including TB testing) Adherence to HIPAA and confidentiality policies Reliable attendance and communication, commitment to scheduled volunteer hours Adheres to infection control and safety procedures in accordance with hospice policy. Ability to sit, stand, and walk for extended periods May require local travel Arc Hospice & Palliative Care is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to join us in delivering compassionate, inclusive care. Explore the impact you can make with Arc Hospice today. Arc Hospice & Palliative Care is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to join us in delivering compassionate, inclusive care. Explore the impact you can make with Arc Hospice today.

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers
description_short: Compassionate, Caring, Giving Support to hospice patients. This can include visiting, reading, taking walks, writing letters, bringing in music, supervising visits with pets, even massage therapy for volunteers with the necessary skills. What is…
description_long: Compassionate, Caring, Giving Support to hospice patients. This can include visiting, reading, taking walks, writing letters, bringing in music, supervising visits with pets, even massage therapy for volunteers with the necessary skills. What is your special talent? Can you lend an ear, hold a hand or talk to someone about sports or pets? Can you read a few pages of a favorite book or listen to favorite songs? Can you offer companionship for an hour to allow a family member a break? There are so many opportunities available to volunteer with Elysian hospice. We service the DFW Metroplex area and extend a warm welcome to volunteer opportunities in Allen, Carrollton, Cedar Hill, Dallas, Denison, Frisco, Ft. Worth, Lancaster, South Lake, Richardson and Seagoville. Give us a call to find out how you can make a difference.

This can include visiting, reading, taking walks, writing letters, bringing in music, supervising visits with pets, even massage therapy for volunteers with the necessary skills. What is your special talent? Can you lend an ear, hold a hand or talk to someone about sports or pets? Can you read a few pages of a favorite book or listen to favorite songs? Can you offer companionship for an hour to allow a family member a break? There are so many opportunities available to volunteer with Elysian hospice. We service the DFW Metroplex area and extend a warm welcome to volunteer opportunities in Allen, Carrollton, Cedar Hill, Dallas, Denison, Frisco, Ft. Worth, Lancaster, South Lake, Richardson and Seagoville. Give us a call to find out how you can make a difference.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers Make the World Better!
description_short: Looking for amazing individuals to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients…
description_long: Looking for amazing individuals to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers Needed - Caregiver Respite and Companion
description_short: Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other…
description_long: Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Volunteer Opportunity: Direct Patient Care - Sitting with patients, reading, working puzzles, watching tv/movies and sharing life stories. This may also include Caregiver Respite where you will visit with the patient while the caregiver runs errands, takes time for themselves or goes to appointments. Must be able to pass a drug screen, background check, and be at least 17 years old. If you are interested in this wonderful volunteer opportunity, please email Faith Oates at Faith.Oates@ehab.com. Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description Enhabit Hospice is looking for a volunteer to provide friendship to patients during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. For more information, please contact: Faith Oates - Volunteer Coordinator and Bereavement Services 469.677.3100 Faith.Oates@ehab.com

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers Needed - Join Our Compassionate Team
description_short: Are you looking for a meaningful way to make a difference? Our hospice team is seeking caring and dedicated volunteers to provide comfort and companionship to patients and families during their most tender moments. Ways You Can Help: Provide…
description_long: Are you looking for a meaningful way to make a difference? Our hospice team is seeking caring and dedicated volunteers to provide comfort and companionship to patients and families during their most tender moments. Ways You Can Help: Provide emotional support and companionship Sit with patients so caregivers can rest Share your talents (reading, music, crafts, etc.) Assist with office support or community outreach No medical experience needed - just compassion. Training provided! This is also a virtual opportunity. Please see attachment below. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held at a volunteer group location (e.g. office, park) Event can be held at organization location(s) No Donation

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers Needed!
description_short: We are a young, forward-thinking Hospice company serving all over Denton, Collin and Cook County. We can accommodate any volunteer living in the area. We understand the importance of fostering education. No skills necessary, we provide training.…
description_long: We are a young, forward-thinking Hospice company serving all over Denton, Collin and Cook County. We can accommodate any volunteer living in the area. We understand the importance of fostering education. No skills necessary, we provide training. Trio hospice is looking for great volunteers to add to our dedicated team to support the needs of our staff, our patients and their loved ones. We offer patient/family volunteering, special event volunteering in our community, administrative/office support and remote volunteering. Whether you are looking for ways to give back to your community or wanting to step into healthcare and see if you are interested in it as a career choice, we are interested in hearing from you. As a hospice volunteer you will have the opportunity to bring meaningful interaction and support by providing some or all the following Companionship Clerical support in the office Customer service Weekly calls or tuck calls to patients and families Interpreting Reading/writing Shopping, running errands Game and other activities Art therapy Music therapy Delivering supplies to patients Visiting with patients Helping host community events Light household chores or food preparation Sitting services while family run errands or other respite needs Dog walking Special skills (i.e. yard maintenance, musicians, beauticians, etc.) Bereavement support Remote volunteering Please contact us for more information and to learn how you can become a volunteer!

opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers Several Opportunities ARLINGTON, TX
description_short: Our hospice team is looking to expand our team. We are looking for caring, compassionate people who love to help others. We have several opportunities available between our 3 offices. We are looking for people for the following opportunities. Weekly…
description_long: Our hospice team is looking to expand our team. We are looking for caring, compassionate people who love to help others. We have several opportunities available between our 3 offices. We are looking for people for the following opportunities. Weekly phone calls to check on our families and report to supervisor if any needs are not met. Volunteer companions to listen, talk, read, do an activity Volunteers to provide respite for a few hours so that family can run errands If any of these things interest you then we would like to talk to you. We service a very large area. We would love to have you join our team. We believe in putting the patients first. Our entire staff is dedicated to giving the best end of life care to our patients and their families. That includes giving them a life with quality until that day comes.

description_short: Needing volunteers in the Ft. Worth/Arlington areas to visit our hospice patients in skilled nursing facilities, assisted living, or private home settings. How does a volunteer support hospice? Providing respite to family Socialization: reading,…
description_long: Needing volunteers in the Ft. Worth/Arlington areas to visit our hospice patients in skilled nursing facilities, assisted living, or private home settings. How does a volunteer support hospice? Providing respite to family Socialization: reading, playing cards, sewing, simply being a friendly visitor to a patient Office and administrative support Offering their unique talents in any way toward supporting patients, families, and the community at-large Volunteer with our team in the following capacities: PATIENT CARE VOLUNTEER Provide patient care and support services that contribute to the quality of life for patients and their families, such as socialization and active listening, providing companionship, or engagement in music or interested hobbies OFFICE VOLUNTEER Assist the clinical team with day-to-day operations within the office, such as assisting with patient charts, answering the telephone, filing, copying, and other supportive tasks. This opportunity would be at our office in Addison. We look forward to having you as part of our team!

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice Fort Worth
opportunity_title: Hospice Volunteers needed in HEB, Hurst, Euless, Bedford
description_short: H ospice provides support for persons in the last phases of incurable disease so that they may live as fully and as comfortably as possible. Hospice recognizes dying as part of the normal process of living and focuses on maintaining the quality of…
description_long: H ospice provides support for persons in the last phases of incurable disease so that they may live as fully and as comfortably as possible. Hospice recognizes dying as part of the normal process of living and focuses on maintaining the quality of remaining life. Hospice affirms life and neither hastens nor postpones death. Hospice exists in the hope and belief that though appropriate care, and the promotion of a caring community sensitive to their needs patients and their families may be free to attain a degree of mental and spiritual preparation for death that is satisfactory to them. Hospice, to the maximum extent possible, accepts patients regardless of ability to pay.

opportunity_title: Hospice Vounteers Wanted - Do You Have a Heart for Hospice?
description_short: We're looking for friendly people to make hour-long visits once a week. Hospice volunteers help add to the quality of a patient's time at the end of life's journey. Typical activities include - visiting, reading, listening to music and providing…
description_long: We're looking for friendly people to make hour-long visits once a week. Hospice volunteers help add to the quality of a patient's time at the end of life's journey. Typical activities include - visiting, reading, listening to music and providing support to the patient's family. Visits to patients are made in their homes or elder-care facilities in the DFW metroplex. Although our office is in Flower Mound, we have patients all over the DFW area and need people to help in all areas. Volunteers are matched to patients located near them. Recommended commitment level: one-hour visits each week, hopefully 3-4 visits per month. No special skills needed. Training takes about three hours and occurs in our office at 700 Parker Square Road in Flower Mound. The next training session will take place on Thursday, November 21 from 1 - 4 p.m. Most hospice volunteers find this work to be meaningful and rewarding. We have a robust volunteer program with plenty of support. We look forward to hearing from you!

opportunity_title: Hospitality Cart Medical City Frisco
description_short: Be a smiling face and a helpful hand as you stroll through the halls of the Women's and Children's wings of Medical City Frisco! Our Hospitality Cart Volunteers play an important role in helping patients and their family members feel comfortable…
description_long: Be a smiling face and a helpful hand as you stroll through the halls of the Women's and Children's wings of Medical City Frisco! Our Hospitality Cart Volunteers play an important role in helping patients and their family members feel comfortable during their visit here. You will be handing out snacks and beverages, activity items and comfort items, such as blankets, etc. to folks in the waiting rooms and patient rooms from the cart. You will also be responsible for keeping records of clients and items served on a check list. This is a weekday only opportunity. Volunteers must be 18 We can not verify hours for court ordered community service. To apply contact Melissa Martin mmartin@rmhdallas 1 shift available: Shift Days: Tuesdays and Thursdays only, Must be at least 18: 10:00 to 12:00 CST

opportunity_title: Host a Donation Drive for Baby & Mom Essentials in Your Community!

description_long: Host a donation drive for baby and mom essentials in your community! Your work would be benefiting mothers and babies in-need. Fill out the form below and we'll send you an email from caringformomsus@gmail.com with more information shortly.

opportunity_title: Host a Foreign Exchange Student in Keller, TX and surrounding areas!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in Keller ISD and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in Keller ISD and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student!

org_name: SHARE! High School Student Exchange Program, Exchange Students
opportunity_title: Host an International High School Exchange Student (Arlington, TX)
description_short: SHARE! Your Heart & Home with a high school exchange student Host an international exchange student! Host families and adults are needed to host exchange students for a school year or semester. SHARE! students, boys and girls who are 15 -18…
description_long: SHARE! Your Heart & Home with a high school exchange student Host an international exchange student! Host families and adults are needed to host exchange students for a school year or semester. SHARE! students, boys and girls who are 15 -18 years old from countries like France, Germany, Spain, China, Brazil, Poland, Italy, Mexico, Japan, and South Korea are waiting for a family or adult(s) to choose them as their exchange student! SHARE! students speak English, have medical insurance, and spending money for personal expenses. They will attend their host family's local high school and want to become a member of your family! SHARE! families are diverse- traditional two-parent families (with or without children), single parents or adults, retired couples and empty-nesters all make wonderful host families! Call or contact us for more information and available student applications! 800-941-3738 sharesouthwest.org/contact Watch our video to learn more: SHARE! High School Exchange Program

SHARE! students, boys and girls who are 15 -18 years old from countries like France, Germany, Spain, China, Brazil, Poland, Italy, Mexico, Japan, and South Korea are waiting for a family or adult(s) to choose them as their exchange student! SHARE! students speak English, have medical insurance, and spending money for personal expenses. They will attend their host family's local high school and want to become a member of your family! SHARE! families are diverse- traditional two-parent families (with or without children), single parents or adults, retired couples and empty-nesters all make wonderful host families! Call or contact us for more information and available student applications! 800-941-3738 sharesouthwest.org/contact Watch our video to learn more: SHARE! High School Exchange Program

org_name: SHARE! High School Student Exchange Program, Exchange Students
opportunity_title: Host an International High School Exchange Student (Nationwide)
description_short: SHARE! your heart & home. Change a Life. Open your home to a high school exchange student and experience the world without leaving home! SHARE! High School Exchange Program is seeking caring individuals and families to host international…
description_long: SHARE! your heart & home. Change a Life. Open your home to a high school exchange student and experience the world without leaving home! SHARE! High School Exchange Program is seeking caring individuals and families to host international exchange students for a semester or a full school year. Students are 14–18 years old and come from countries including France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Poland, Japan, South Korea, China, Brazil, Mexico, and many others. Our students are excited to experience American family life, attend their local high school, and become a true member of your family while sharing their own culture and traditions. Every SHARE! student: Speaks English Has comprehensive medical insurance Has spending money for personal expenses Is carefully screened and selected before arriving in the United States There is no "perfect" host family. We welcome: Two-parent families (with or without children) Single parents Single adults Retired couples Empty nesters If you have a caring heart, a stable home, and a willingness to welcome a student into your family, you may be the perfect host. To learn more or view available student profiles, contact us today. 📞 800-941-3738 🌐 sharesouthwest.org/contact

opportunity_title: Housing & Resource Navigator Volunteer
description_short: TWCUYC is seeking dedicated volunteers to assist community members with housing navigation and support with identification documents. This role is ideal for individuals who are organized, resourceful, and comfortable navigating multiple systems to…
description_long: TWCUYC is seeking dedicated volunteers to assist community members with housing navigation and support with identification documents. This role is ideal for individuals who are organized, resourceful, and comfortable navigating multiple systems to help people overcome barriers to stability. Volunteers will work directly with participants to identify resources, complete applications, gather required documentation, and connect with opportunities that support housing stability and self-sufficiency. Responsibilities Assist participants with obtaining essential identification documents, including birth certificates, state IDs, driver’s licenses, and Social Security cards. Navigate local, state, and community resource systems to identify available services and opportunities. Support participants in completing applications, submitting documentation, and following up with agencies. Assist with housing navigation by identifying rental opportunities, housing programs, and property owners willing to consider applicants facing barriers such as low credit scores, prior evictions, limited rental history, or criminal backgrounds. Conduct research and outreach to community partners, service providers, and housing resources. Maintain accurate participant records and track progress. Communicate professionally with participants, landlords, agencies, and community partners. Help participants problem-solve barriers and identify next steps toward stability. Preferred Skills Strong computer proficiency and internet research skills. Ability to navigate multiple online systems, databases, and application portals. Strong organizational, follow-up, and time management skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to manage multiple cases and competing priorities. Strong problem-solving and critical thinking abilities. Ability to work independently and identify solutions with minimal supervision. Ability to maintain confidentiality and professional boundaries. Experience in case management, housing services, workforce development, social services, nonprofit operations, customer service, or administrative support is preferred but not required. Ideal Volunteer Characteristics Compassionate and patient. Dependable and professional. Resourceful and solution-oriented. Comfortable advocating on behalf of others. Able to build rapport with diverse populations. Committed to helping individuals overcome barriers and achieve stability. Time Commitment Volunteers should be available for a minimum of 8 hours per week. Additional availability is strongly encouraged, given the volume of participant requests and the ongoing nature of housing and resource navigation services. Impact By helping individuals obtain identification documents, navigate complex systems, and access housing opportunities, volunteers play a direct role in building a foundation for long-term stability and self-sufficiency for community members.

opportunity_title: ICR Discovery Center Exhibit Hall Docent
description_short: Within the exhibit hall, there are two main areas to docent. Volunteers will have the opportunity to interact and engage with guests. Depending on the day, needs may vary. Volunteers must be at least 16 years old. All volunteers are required to pass…
description_long: Within the exhibit hall, there are two main areas to docent. Volunteers will have the opportunity to interact and engage with guests. Depending on the day, needs may vary. Volunteers must be at least 16 years old. All volunteers are required to pass a background check. Volunteers under the age of 18 will need to have a parent or guardian fill out a consent form. Volunteers must provide a testimony of salvation and faith in Jesus Christ, and agree with ICR’s tenets and values. Volunteer shifts are between 3-4 hours each and will require standing, walking, and climbing a few stairs. We ask that you commit to serve at least one shift per month to be a regular docent.

opportunity_title: ISF Mentoring Opportunity
description_short: ****Upcoming Virtual Information Session on March 31st. Please apply for more details**** About the ISF Mentorship Program ISF (Investing in the Success of Foster Youth) Combines scholarships, leadership development, and mentorships to former foster…
description_long: ****Upcoming Virtual Information Session on March 31st. Please apply for more details**** About the ISF Mentorship Program ISF (Investing in the Success of Foster Youth) Combines scholarships, leadership development, and mentorships to former foster youth. Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these students face challenges such as instability, limited family support, and gaps in life-skills training. As mentors, individuals serve as trusted adults who provide encouragement, guidance, and practical assistance, helping students navigate the complexities of college life and early adulthood. The primary aim of mentoring is not to resolve all issues, but rather to empower students, foster their confidence, and assist them in developing the skills and networks necessary for long-term success. Mentors are not expected to be experts in all areas, but should be resourceful and willing to connect students with available resources. ISF offers support and resources to mentors as needed. Key Responsibilities Build a Supportive Relationship Provide Practical Life Guidance Support Student Growth Maintain Program Partnership Mentor Qualities Successful mentors demonstrate the following qualities: Empathy and trauma-informed awareness Reliability and consistency Respect for diverse backgrounds and experiences Strong listening and communication skills Encouragement of student autonomy and growth Vibrant commitment to personal and spiritual growth Mentors recognize that trust is established through patience, consistency, and respect. Preferred Backgrounds (Not Required) Mentors may come from a variety of professional or personal backgrounds, including: Professionals with career experience or networks Individuals who were first-generation college students Adults with experience in education, counseling, or youth development Individuals with lived experience overcoming adversity or foster care The most important qualification is a genuine commitment to supporting a student’s success. ***Upcoming Virtual Information Session on March 31st. Please apply for more details****

opportunity_title: Immediate Need: Be Someone's Steady Presence — Hospice Companion Volunteer (Allen, TX)
description_short: Right now, in Allen, there's a hospice patient who would love some company. Not medical care — our nurses handle that. Company & companionship. Someone to sit with them, listen to their stories, or watch an old movie. Concho Hearts Hospice, a…
description_long: Right now, in Allen, there's a hospice patient who would love some company. Not medical care — our nurses handle that. Company & companionship. Someone to sit with them, listen to their stories, or watch an old movie. Concho Hearts Hospice, a locally owned, nurse-founded Texas hospice, has an immediate need for companion volunteers in the Allen/Garland area. The commitment: about 1–2 hours a week, on your schedule. We provide full training and ongoing support — no healthcare experience needed. Just a warm heart & consistency. Many volunteers tell us it becomes the most meaningful hour of their week.

opportunity_title: Influencer Marketing Manager
description_short: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. Influencer Marketing Manager: Manages…
description_long: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. Influencer Marketing Manager: Manages relationships with influencers to promote products and expand brand reach. Mission: As a national organization, Rebuilding Together is a leading nonprofit in safe and healthy housing. Across the country, Rebuilding Together’s affiliates complete ~10,000 rebuild projects each year. Rebuilding Together North Texas revitalizes neighborhoods by bringing volunteers and communities together to repair homes and rebuild the lives of low-income homeowners in the Denton, Dallas, Tarrant, Collin, and Rockwall counties.

opportunity_title: Intake Recovery Specialist (REMOTE)
description_short: Intake Recovery Specialist (Dogs Matter & Project Street Vet Initiative Program) Remote Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain…
description_long: Intake Recovery Specialist (Dogs Matter & Project Street Vet Initiative Program) Remote Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability, and provide a healthy and loving environment for their pet. Dogs Matter is the only non-profit agency in Texas offering free temporary foster care placement and aftercare support services specifically for pets of people with addictions receiving substance abuse treatment. Dogs Matter has become a department of Dallas Pets Alive (DPA). With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to Dogs Matter, you directly impact our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dogs Matter visit https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ As our Recovery Intake Specialist, you will serve as a resource guide to support our unhoused applicants/clients in Recovery through the Dogs Matter & Project Street Vet collaboration. You will expand our connections and resources throughout the L.A. area through research and engagement. With the support of Dogs Matter Recovery Coaches, you’ll help assess their needs, explain how the program works, guide them through the intake process, and support their journey toward treatment. Team: Dogs Matter Location : Remote/Virtual: Virtual from anywhere, understanding our clients are in the Pacific time zone and Dogs Matter staff are primarily on Central time. Geographical knowledge of Los Angeles is a plus. Time Commitment : 10 – 15 hours per week. Volunteer hours must be flexible to coordinate swiftly based on client needs. Must be available to commit to a minimum of 6 months of volunteer service. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: Dogs Matter Lead Recovery Coach, Director of Operations and Executive Director Works with: You’ll work directly (virtually) with our clients as well as the Dogs Matter, Project Street Vet, and PASS program staff and volunteers. Position Responsibilities: Screening & Intake Conduct outreach and intake conversations (via phone, in person, or virtually) with unhoused individuals interested in receiving services from Dogs Matter. Collect and document basic demographic, housing, and pet-ownership information sensitively and respectfully. Assess eligibility for the joint program, including medical, behavioral, or logistical barriers Explain the structure and services of the program and next steps Record-Keeping & Reporting Maintain accurate, confidential records of intake conversations, assessments, and follow-ups. Provide regular reports to Dogs Matter leadership on intake volume, trends, bottlenecks, and client outcomes. Collaborate with Project Street Vet teams to ensure alignment in client tracking and resource allocation. Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Demonstrated empathy, excellent communication, and active listening skills. Experience in social services, case management, recovery coaching, or nonprofit outreach preferred. Comfort working with individuals experiencing homelessness and/or substance use challenges. Organizational skills and ability to manage multiple concurrent intakes. Basic familiarity with data entry/case management tools (or willingness to learn). Commitment to the mission of Dogs Matter and Project Street Vet (keeping people and pets together, no judgment). Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device The following activities are required: Ability to operate a computer and/or mobile device. The ability to observe details at close range (within a few feet of the observer). The ability to communicate information and ideas so others will understand. What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Intern at Techinance (Various Opportunities Available for Business & Computer Science Majors).
description_short: Techinance is proudly accepting applications for various different internships! Learn more below. Content Writer Information As a Content Writer, you will be expected to do the following: Spend at least four (4) hours per week creating…
description_long: Techinance is proudly accepting applications for various different internships! Learn more below. Content Writer Information As a Content Writer, you will be expected to do the following: Spend at least four (4) hours per week creating blogs/articles related to the technology, neuroscience, business, and finance fields, aimed at youth in grades 5-9. Use Instagram to increase our online presence in coordination with the Outreach Coordinators. Gather information from the Research Analysts and create content based on their work. Keep a log of your hours worked each week if you are seeking volunteer hours . Research Analyst Information As a Research Analyst, you will be expected to do the following: Spend at least three (3) hours per week researching topics related to the technology, neuroscience, and finance fields, aimed at youth in grades 5-12. Identify relevant data and generate visual representations , such as bar graphs, scatter plots, and line graphs, to support your research. Submit your research findings to the Content Writers for the creation of articles and blogs . Keep a log of your hours worked each week if you are seeking volunteer hours . Outreach Coordinator Information As an Outreach Coordinator, you will be expected to do the following: Spend at least two (2) hours per week creating social media content and seeking partnerships with other organizations. Market the organization on various platforms (ex: TikTok, YouTube, Instagram). Assist in expanding the organization to reach a broader range of students and regions worldwide . Keep a log of your hours worked each week if you are seeking volunteer hours . Website Developer As a Website Developer, you will be expected to do the following: Spend at least three (3) hours per week working on making our website more comprehensive for prospective students and parents. Collaborate with the Research Analysts to ensure course content is accurate and engaging on the website. Working with WordPress to create a more functional, professional website. Keep a log of your hours worked each week if you are seeking volunteer hours .

opportunity_title: Intern for a non-profit!
description_short: C.H.O.I.C.E.: Consider How One Individual Changes Everything Here at Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. we are preparing for a great school year, pairing volunteer mentors with underserved students for post-secondary success! We are ready for the first…
description_long: C.H.O.I.C.E.: Consider How One Individual Changes Everything Here at Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. we are preparing for a great school year, pairing volunteer mentors with underserved students for post-secondary success! We are ready for the first step: YOU! Want to give back? It only takes a little time to do a lot of good! A CHOICE volunteer offers multiple ways you can get involved. We are always looking for eager individuals who want learn more about our mission and use their skills and education to help us grow as an organization. A CHOICE internship is a partnership. Your goal as a student or young professional is taken into consideration. We are proud to highlight all the work our interns have accomplished. Internship Opportunities include: Newsletter writing Event Planning General Operation Mentor Relations Student Relations Watch our video to learn more: Consider How One Individual Changes Everything
