opportunity_title: Intern of Volunteer ON-AIR COMMUNITY CORRESPONDENT & RADIO PERSONALITY . OBS, AZURACAST,  SERVER KNOWLEDGE
description_short: A GREAT OPPORTUNITY TO LEARN BROADCASTING AT YOUR SPEED The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) AND GRANGEL MEDIA Do people naturally open up to you? Do you enjoy talking with people, telling stories, attending community events, and being the…
description_long: A GREAT OPPORTUNITY TO LEARN BROADCASTING AT YOUR SPEED The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) AND GRANGEL MEDIA Do people naturally open up to you? Do you enjoy talking with people, telling stories, attending community events, and being the voice that helps others feel seen and heard? TGCN Radio is seeking an energetic, outgoing, and community-minded On-Air Community Correspondent & Radio Personality to help us share the stories, voices, resources, and impact of grandparents and kinship families across America. This is not a sit-behind-a-desk role ALL DAY ROLE We are looking for someone who enjoys being in the community, meeting people, asking meaningful questions, attending events, conducting interviews, and helping create engaging radio content that informs, inspires, and connects families to resources. What You'll Do Conduct live and recorded interviews Attend community events, conferences, and outreach activities Interview grandparents, caregivers, community leaders, and partners Record audio and video content in the field Create human-interest stories and feature segments Assist with TGCN Radio programming and content development Help identify stories that deserve greater visibility Collaborate with the TGCN Media Team Support podcast and internet radio broadcasts Represent TGCN professionally within the community Preferred Qualifications Experience in radio, podcasting, broadcasting, journalism, media, communications, public relations, or related fields Knowledge of internet radio platforms and podcasting tools Comfortable conducting interviews and speaking with people from diverse backgrounds Ability to record quality audio and video using smartphones, microphones, or recording equipment Strong communication and storytelling skills Social media experience is a plus Ability to work independently and meet deadlines Reliable transportation for community events preferred Ideal Personality We are looking for someone who is: Friendly and approachable Naturally curious Outgoing and engaging Professional and dependable Passionate about helping people tell their stories Comfortable speaking on-air and in public settings A strong listener who knows how to make others feel comfortable Volunteer Commitment Minimum 5-10 volunteer hours per month Flexible scheduling Some evening and weekend events may be required Opportunity for future paid opportunities as TGCN grows Why Join TGCN Radio? At TGCN Radio, every interview has the potential to change a life. Every story shared can connect a family to resources, hope, advocacy, and community support. If you love media, storytelling, community engagement, and making a difference, we would love to meet you. Join us as we amplify voices, strengthen communities, and ensure that no grandfamily walks alone. About TGCN The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) is a national nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting grandparents and kinship caregivers raising children due to family crisis through advocacy, resources, direct support, education, and community connection. Serving Grandfamilies Through Advocacy, Love, and Community.

opportunity_title: Interviewers and Storytellers needed in Plano for Hospice Patients & Families
description_short: Kindful Health is a privately owned hospice company that is focused on providing patients and their families with the full promise of the hospice benefit in the State of Texas. We are quickly growing and in need of volunteers like you to help us…
description_long: Kindful Health is a privately owned hospice company that is focused on providing patients and their families with the full promise of the hospice benefit in the State of Texas. We are quickly growing and in need of volunteers like you to help us with the most important aspect of hospice: community. If you are interested in making a positive difference in the Plano community by participating in our brand new story book project this is the opportunity for you! I will promptly reach out when you apply to tell you more about this amazing project and how you can help.

opportunity_title: Irving, TX area: Seeking Host Families: Host a Foreign Exchange Student Irving, TX and surrounding areas!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Join OSLC’s Family Service Initiative! Serve anytime, anywhere, and commemorate your impact with a Free Family Service Learning Certificate.
description_short: Omenala Service Learning Collaborative (OSLC) Everyday Service. Make an Impact. Serve Anytime, From Anywhere Omenala Service Learning Collaborative (OSLC) is a community service group and giving circle dedicated to encouraging everyday acts of…
description_long: Omenala Service Learning Collaborative (OSLC) Everyday Service. Make an Impact. Serve Anytime, From Anywhere Omenala Service Learning Collaborative (OSLC) is a community service group and giving circle dedicated to encouraging everyday acts of service and collaborative giving. We believe that when families serve together, they grow stronger and create lasting change in their communities. Introducing Our Virtual Year-Round Family Service Learning Initiative We’re excited to launch a new initiative inviting families to participate in community service activities, no matter where you are! This flexible, virtual program allows families to serve their communities on their own schedule, from their own neighborhood. How It Works Choose a Service Activity anytime during Fall, Winter, Spring and/or Summer terms. Examples include: Organizing a neighborhood clean-up Assembling care packages for shelters Writing letters to seniors or frontline workers Donating gently used clothes, books, or food Volunteering virtually with local nonprofits Take Action Get your family involved and complete the activity whenever and wherever it works for you. Share Your Impact Email us at omenalaservice@yahoo.com to commemorate your service with a FREE Family Service Certificate ! In your email, please include: A brief description of your service activity (you can also include the total number of service hours completed by your family.). How you'd like your family's name to appear on the certificate (e.g., The Johnson Family) Why Participate? Inspire a culture of giving in your home Connect with your community Celebrate your family’s impact with a keepsake certificate Participate as often as you'd like — no limits! Ready to Get Started? Visit: https://omenalaservice.weebly.com/start-your-own-family-service-club.html Here you will learn how to launch your first community service activity today. Let’s build a better world— one family, one act of service at a time .

org_name: THE HAPPY PET PROJECT, POWERED BY TEXAS GOOD DEEDS PROJECT INC
opportunity_title: Join Our Rescue Community!
description_short: Dogs don’t thrive alone — and neither does rescue. Every dog, every foster, every adoption relies on a community of caring people showing up, helping however they can, and making an impact together. Whether you want to help at adoption events,…
description_long: Dogs don’t thrive alone — and neither does rescue. Every dog, every foster, every adoption relies on a community of caring people showing up, helping however they can, and making an impact together. Whether you want to help at adoption events, assist behind the scenes, or work hands-on with dogs, there’s a place for you. No experience is required — but if you have it, we welcome it! — just a big heart and willingness to be part of something meaningful. When you volunteer, you’re not just helping dogs. You’re helping build a stronger, more connected community. Ready to make a difference?

opportunity_title: Join our Volunteer Team or our Board of Directors!
description_short: Lambie’s Love is a nonprofit organization serving Dallas–Fort Worth and surrounding communities by supporting families grieving the loss of a baby or child through compassionate bereavement care and support programs. We are currently seeking…
description_long: Lambie’s Love is a nonprofit organization serving Dallas–Fort Worth and surrounding communities by supporting families grieving the loss of a baby or child through compassionate bereavement care and support programs. We are currently seeking compassionate volunteers and individuals interested in serving on our Volunteer Team or Board of Directors to help carry our mission forward. This is a meaningful opportunity to make a lasting impact in the lives of families navigating unimaginable grief. We especially welcome individuals with experience or interest in areas such as: Mental health or counseling Fundraising and donor relations Public relations or marketing Finance or accounting Ministry or community outreach Nonprofit leadership and development Volunteers and board members contribute approximately 7 hours per month and may serve remotely. They play an important role in outreach efforts, donor engagement, organizational planning, and supporting our Bereavement Box Program — helping ensure grieving families receive comfort, care, and support when they need it most. If you are passionate about serving others and would like to learn more about getting involved, we would love to connect with you. Our Board Members are individuals with personal experience in child loss, former NICU parents, or nurses. Our amazing volunteers come from many different backgrounds — all are welcome to join.

description_long: Join the Future In Our Eyes Hospital Giving Team! If you can crochet, sew, or you just want to donate toys in general, sign up as a volunteer. Crocheters make toys and plushies, and sewists make various needed items such as G-tube pads, NICU beds, and blankets. Instructions are sent out to each volunteer regarding what items you can make and how many service hours each item made will earn.

Winters Park Trailhead Sat, 10/24, 8:30AM - 10:00AM - Cheer Station - Paddock Park Sat, 10/24, 9:30AM - 12:00PM - Cheer Station - Veterans Memorial Park Sat, 10/24, 11:30AM - 3:30PM - Cheer Station - TCU Bailey Building Sun, 10/25, 9:00AM - 11:00AM - Cheer Station - Craddock Park Sun, 10/25,10:00AM - 12:30PM - Cheer Station - Turpin Stadium Sun, 10/25,10:30AM - 1:30PM - Cheer Station - Cole Park Sun, 10/25, 1:00PM - 5:00PM - Finish Line Festival Volunteers You can view select a volunteer role and sign up here: https://komen.volunteermatters.org/project-catalog/207

opportunity_title: LEWISVILLE: Visit a Lonely Senior Citizen in Need of Companionship!
description_short: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody…
description_long: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody remember important moments in their lives. You can be one of our Volunteers When you give us the precious gift of your time and interest, we give you a chance to make a real difference in someone’s life. We train you, find you the right assignment and stand behind you as you grow. Patient Volunteer Weekly visits to offer companionship for Patients. Examples include: o Conversation o Reading to the Patient o Listening to music or watching videos of their favorite singer/actor o Watching a TV show together o Watching funny cat and dog reels together o Assisting with a hobby Volunteers are matched with 1 Patient at a time to someone within a 15-minute radius to where you live. You can volunteer for as little as 2 - 4 hours a month, and you can set a schedule that works for you. You can learn at your own pace, and we provide ongoing support until you feel completely comfortable working on your own. Requirements: Must be able to commit to Volunteer for one year (or 1 school year) Either 1 x per week or 2 x per month for about 1 hour No resume needed but an excellent skill and resume builder A caring heart to help people combat loneliness & depression. The process is simple! 1)Once you contact us, the Volunteer Coordinator will reach out via email to welcome you! 2)Then, you & the Volunteer Coordinator will set up a time to chat via phone so that you can introduce yourself & ask any questions. We look forward to you joining our Awesome Volunteer Team! It's truly an amazing and humbling experience and we walk with you the entire way!

opportunity_title: Laundry and Dish Cleaners
description_short: Volunteers are crucial to our shelter's operations, especially for behind-the-scenes tasks. This includes dishwashing, laundry assistance, and cleaning duties, all of which are essential for maintaining a safe and sanitary environment for our furry…
description_long: Volunteers are crucial to our shelter's operations, especially for behind-the-scenes tasks. This includes dishwashing, laundry assistance, and cleaning duties, all of which are essential for maintaining a safe and sanitary environment for our furry residents. Keeping our shelter clean directly impacts the health and well-being of our animals. The cleaner the environment, the healthier they can stay. We offer flexible shifts every day of the week, from 9 am to 6 pm. Anyone aged 10 and above is welcome to volunteer. For volunteers under 15, participation alongside a parent or guardian, aged 18 or older, is required. For younger volunteers, aged 10 to 11, opportunities are primarily focused on cat handling after training, while those aged 12 and older can handle both dogs and cats. To get started, you'll need to create a volunteer account and complete a few initial steps. There's a $15 volunteer fee per person to cover the initial costs of onboarding new volunteers. Once these steps are completed, you'll be eligible to sign up for an Orientation session, where you'll receive further training and information.

opportunity_title: Lead Conversion & Email Marketing Assistant
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Position Overview: As our Lead Conversion & Email Marketing Assistant , you will be instrumental in nurturing relationships with our supporters and potential advocates. You'll leverage the power of email marketing to engage our audience, guide them through their journey with SelfSoulLiberate, and inspire action, whether it's volunteering, donating, or participating in our programs. Role Description: We are seeking a detail-oriented, data-savvy, and creative Lead Conversion & Email Marketing Assistant to help optimize our digital outreach efforts. This remote volunteer position is crucial for transforming interest into engagement and support. You will work closely with the Communications/Marketing team to design, execute, and analyze email campaigns that drive lead conversion, retain existing supporters, and effectively communicate SelfSoulLiberate's impact and opportunities. Key Responsibilities: Develop and Implement Email Campaigns: Assist in the creation, scheduling, and deployment of various email marketing campaigns (e.g., newsletters, fundraising appeals, event invitations, educational series). Audience Segmentation: Support the segmentation of email lists to ensure targeted and personalized communication to different audience groups. Content Collaboration: Collaborate with the Communications/Marketing team to ensure email content is compelling, accurate, on-brand, and aligned with overall marketing strategies. A/B Testing: Assist in setting up and analyzing A/B tests for subject lines, content, calls-to-action, and send times to optimize email performance. Performance Analysis: Monitor and report on key email marketing metrics (e.g., open rates, click-through rates, conversion rates, unsubscribe rates) to identify trends and areas for improvement. Lead Nurturing Support: Contribute to strategies for nurturing leads through automated email sequences and personalized communications. Platform Management: Help maintain and organize our email marketing platform/CRM, ensuring data integrity and efficient campaign management. Compliance: Ensure all email marketing activities comply with relevant data privacy regulations (e.g., CAN-SPAM, GDPR). Qualifications: Proven interest or experience in digital marketing, email marketing, or a related field. Familiarity with email marketing platforms (e.g., Mailchimp, Constant Contact, HubSpot, etc.) is a plus. Strong written communication skills with an ability to craft clear, concise, and persuasive messages. Basic analytical skills, with an eagerness to learn how to interpret data to inform marketing decisions. Detail-oriented and highly organized, capable of managing multiple tasks and deadlines. Proactive, self-motivated, and able to work independently in a remote environment. A strong commitment to SelfSoulLiberate's mission of health equity and mental health stigma reduction. Comfortable with virtual collaboration tools and communication. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration.

opportunity_title: Lead Generation Specialist- Online (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Location: Remote Reports To: Marketing Manager Lead Generation Specialist will be a proactive and detail-oriented person responsible to build and manage a steady pipeline of high-quality leads in the holistic wellness, spiritual healing, and…
description_long: Location: Remote Reports To: Marketing Manager Lead Generation Specialist will be a proactive and detail-oriented person responsible to build and manage a steady pipeline of high-quality leads in the holistic wellness, spiritual healing, and conscious events space. This role will focus on sourcing, qualifying, and enriching contact data for practitioners, facilitators, retreat organizers, and wellness entrepreneurs who align with our platform's mission.The person will work closely with the marketing, and sales teams to ensure a consistent flow of leads that convert into engaged users, partners, subscribers, and collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Research and identify potential leads in the holistic and spiritual wellness space (practitioners, retreat/event organizers, wellness businesses). Build and maintain a lead database segmented by service type, region, modality, or niche. Qualify leads based on defined criteria (location, digital presence, audience size, service offerings, etc.). Use tools like GoHighLevel, LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Google, and event directories to source and track lead data. Enrich lead profiles with relevant contact info, social handles, service offerings, and engagement history. Monitor lead quality and provide feedback to improve targeting strategies. Update CRM and other Databases and Maintain accurate records and reporting on lead pipeline status, conversion rates, and outreach metrics. Required Skills & Qualifications: 1–3 years of experience in lead generation, list building, or CRM/data management (ideally in wellness, events, or digital services). Strong research and data entry skills. Experience with GoHighLevel or similar CRM/automation platforms/Affiliate programs. (Key skill) Comfortable using tools like Google Sheets , LinkedIn , Instagram ,etc. Understanding of digital marketing funnels and lead nurturing basics. Organized, consistent, and able to work independently on tight timelines. Passion for the holistic/spiritual/wellness industry is a major plus! Preferred Qualifications: Prior experience building leads for wellness coaches, retreats, or digital marketplaces. Familiarity with email marketing tools, lead scoring, and segmentation strategies. Knowledge of conscious community platforms, retreat centers, or event networks.

You MUST live in the United States to apply . If you are interested in the position, please apply for consideration. We will be accepting applicants on a rolling admission basis. Thank you!

opportunity_title: Legal & Compliance Volunteer / Coordinator (Pro-Bono Lawyer)
description_short: Job Title: Legal & Compliance Volunteer / Coordinator (Pro-Bono Lawyer) Department: Operations Department Location: Remote (with occasional coordination across US, Mexico, Bali, and other early international operations) Commitment: 6–10…
description_long: Job Title: Legal & Compliance Volunteer / Coordinator (Pro-Bono Lawyer) Department: Operations Department Location: Remote (with occasional coordination across US, Mexico, Bali, and other early international operations) Commitment: 6–10 hours/week for 3–6 months Employment Type: Volunteer / Unpaid position About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is a non-profit organization deeply committed to global orphanage support, guided by the Menzies 5 Keys of Needs. Our overarching mission is to lead the way in creating eco-regenerative conscious communities, serving as secure sanctuaries for orphans and vulnerable families. With a focus on addressing trauma and promoting holistic healing, we strive to foster environments where individuals can thrive and flourish. As part of the Menzies Mission Non-profit Organization, My Source Network stands as an innovative digital platform dedicated to holistic healing. By connecting individuals with a diverse network of practitioners and resources, we facilitate access to various healing modalities and transformative events. Our platform empowers users to embark on personalized wellness journeys, fostering growth and well-being in every individual we serve. Join our team and help us create exceptional digital experiences for our users! About the Role: We are seeking a motivated Legal & Compliance Volunteer / Pro-Bono Lawyer to support the launch of our international operations. This role will assist the COO and Finance & Compliance Lead with legal and compliance tasks across multiple jurisdictions, including the US, Mexico, and Bali. You will gain hands-on experience in startup legal operations, tech, and events law while helping establish the foundational legal framework for a growing organization. Key Responsibilities: Assist in drafting and setting up legal documentation such as Terms of Service, Privacy Policies, Event Host Agreements, and Refund & Payout Policies. Coordinate with external advisors (Chartered Accountant, legal consultant, or pro-bono lawyer) for document reviews, registrations, and compliance checks. Maintain a compliance tracker for US and Mexico, including tax forms (e.g., W-8BEN-E, 1099-K). Review partnership MoUs and collaboration agreements prior to sign-off. Collaborate with the Operations team to ensure event listings and contracts align with policy templates. Document and organize all signed agreements, compliance certificates, and SOPs in Google Drive. Stay updated on data protection and payment compliance standards, including CCPA, GDPR basics, and Stripe Connect terms. Qualifications: Law graduate or final-year law student (LLB or JD program). Strong interest in tech, events, or startup law. Comfortable drafting and reviewing legal templates. Familiarity with contracts, intellectual property basics, and data privacy regulations. Ability to commit 6–10 hours per week for 3–6 months. Preferred Experience: Participation in a law incubator, legal aid group, or startup legal clinic. Experience with pro-bono or volunteer projects for early-stage startups is highly valued. Why Join Us: Gain hands-on experience in international startup legal operations. Work closely with leadership and gain mentorship from experienced legal and compliance professionals. Make a meaningful impact by shaping the legal framework of a growing organization. Application Process To apply, review the job description and submit your resume, portfolio, and cover letter to hr@menziesmission.org . Ensure accurate contact information for application status updates. If selected, be prepared to discuss qualifications and alignment with the organization's mission. For questions, contact hr@menziesmission.org. We look forward to reviewing your application. Equal Opportunity Employer: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Accommodation for the application process is available upon request.

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Lend a neighbor a hand with lawn and yard care in Dallas, TX · Ref IWTMYL-MSY
description_short: A neighbor in Dallas, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming.…
description_long: A neighbor in Dallas, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming. Your kindness makes a real difference in a neighbor's life. This is a home shared by a household of two, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Dallas, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-MSY&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-MSY

opportunity_title: Live Content Creator (in-front of camera) - Dallas TX National Rebuilding Day (April 25, 2026)
description_short: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. Live Content Creator (in-front of camera):…
description_long: Calling All Creative Volunteers — We Need Your Talent to Make a Difference! Rebuilding Together North Texas is looking for help to scale our marketing efforts . Your creativity can make a real difference. Live Content Creator (in-front of camera): Captures photos, short videos, and behind-the-scenes moments during the event for immediate posting. Focuses on documenting key moments, speakers, activities, and attendee engagement. Mission: As a national organization, Rebuilding Together is a leading nonprofit in safe and healthy housing. Across the country, Rebuilding Together’s affiliates complete ~10,000 rebuild projects each year. Rebuilding Together North Texas revitalizes neighborhoods by bringing volunteers and communities together to repair homes and rebuild the lives of low-income homeowners in the Denton, Dallas, Tarrant, Collin, and Rockwall counties.

opportunity_title: Local Community Cat Care and Feeder Team Member
description_short: 🐾 Local Cat Care Volunteer (Friendly Team, Training Provided) Join a caring local team helping feed and care for community cats in the Farmers Branch / Addison area. We're looking for dependable volunteers to assist with feeding visits in the late…
description_long: 🐾 Local Cat Care Volunteer (Friendly Team, Training Provided) Join a caring local team helping feed and care for community cats in the Farmers Branch / Addison area. We're looking for dependable volunteers to assist with feeding visits in the late afternoon or early evening (times vary by season). Our colony includes approximately 10 cats cared for at six feeding stations located within 2-3 blocks of each other. During a typical visit, you'll provide food and water, check on the cats, and spend a little time getting to know their unique personalities. Sometimes they even bring guests to dinner. 😊 We're looking for reliable, committed caregivers who understand how important consistency is to the cats who depend on us each day. No experience is necessary—we'll provide training and support. If you love animals and want to make a meaningful difference right here in your local community, we'd love to hear from you.

opportunity_title: Looking for Volunteers
description_short: Hello, If you have a passion for serving others, becoming a hospice volunteer can be a fulfilling and meaningful way to make difference in the lives of others. Hospice volunteers are the heart of hospice and help patients and families make the most…
description_long: Hello, If you have a passion for serving others, becoming a hospice volunteer can be a fulfilling and meaningful way to make difference in the lives of others. Hospice volunteers are the heart of hospice and help patients and families make the most of every moment. Our volunteers serve in many ways including visiting with patients and families, assisting with tasks in the office, and working with special projects. Some of our volunteer opportunities include: Patient visits for companionship and support Spending time with patients while their family runs errands Assisting with household chores or errands Supporting patients and families in the patient’s last hours Providing veteran to veteran support Providing specialized services such as pet therapy or reiki with appropriate certifications Making tuck-in phone calls to patients or their family to make sure they have supplies, medications, and support that they may need Assisting with bereavement program tasks, bereavement support groups, and memorial services Assisting with administrative or clerical tasks in the office such as answering telephones, assisting with mailings, putting together packets or training manuals, and more o Teens between the ages of 16-17 may also volunteer in the office with administrative tasks. Working on special projects such as making quilts, blankets, fidget aprons, birthday cakes or cards, and more Volunteer days and times are flexible! Please contact me directly with any questions or if you are interested in learning more about our volunteer opportunities. All volunteers receive the training and support needed to carry out their assigned duties. Being a Hospice Volunteer is the best job you'll ever have. Sharing some of your time and your heart with those in need is so helpful and appreciated! If you are part of a community organization or church, please share these opportunities with others who may be interested as well. I look forward to hearing from you. Sincerely, Ralph Forrest, BA Volunteer Coordinator Gentiva Hospice 7557 Rambler Rd # 560, Dallas, TX 75231 O (214) 231-3914 F ( 214) 630-4032 gentivahs.com Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held at a volunteer group location (e.g. office, park) Event can be held at organization location(s) Event can be held virtually No Donation

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Love Music? Share your musical talent with a Hospice patient
description_short: Are you interested in singing or playing the piano, guitar, violin, or other instruments to our patients? If so, please let me know! I am looking to recruit volunteers who can give a two to three hours a week or every other week to brighten up…
description_long: Are you interested in singing or playing the piano, guitar, violin, or other instruments to our patients? If so, please let me know! I am looking to recruit volunteers who can give a two to three hours a week or every other week to brighten up someone’s day with music. You would be playing and singing in nursing homes and assisted living facilities. We will work with your schedule! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Volunteer Opportunity: Direct Patient Care - Sitting with patients, playing music, singing, reading, working puzzles, watching tv/movies and sharing life stories. This may also include Caregiver Respite where you will visit with the patient while the caregiver runs errands, takes time for themselves or goes to appointments. Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. For more information, please respond to this inquiry or contact: Faith Oates | Volunteer Coordinator/Bereavement Care 214.383.7443 www.ehab.com

opportunity_title: Love dogs and like helping people? Help a service dog in training!
description_short: Dogs for Better Lives has a unique opportunity to transform shelter dogs into service dogs for people with disabilities. You can play an important part in this transformative and life changing journey by fostering a shelter dog that has been…
description_long: Dogs for Better Lives has a unique opportunity to transform shelter dogs into service dogs for people with disabilities. You can play an important part in this transformative and life changing journey by fostering a shelter dog that has been evaluated and selected for this important work. What does this opportunity look like? Welcome a carefully selected shelter or rescue dog into your home for up to 6 months Dogs train with Dogs for Better Lives (DBL) professional service dog trainers during the day Monday - Friday at the DBL training location in Carrollton, TX. They spend the evenings and weekends relaxing with you at home Work with Dogs for Better Lives staff on helping reinforce training at home Coordinate with DBL staff on drop-off and pickup at the training location or your home Dogs for Better Lives provides all necessary resources including veterinary care, dog food, toys and equipment (food and water bowl, crate, etc.) Be happy knowing that you've not only given a second chance to a dog in need, but have also helped provide safety and independence to a child or adult with a disability This is a great way for the whole family to be engaged in something special For more information on this exciting program, please visit: https://dogsforbetterlives.org/from-shelter-to-service/ Watch our video to learn more: Shelter Hearing Dog

opportunity_title: Lucas, TX: Host an Exchange Student-Volunteer families needed for upcoming school year!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: MADD Helpline Volunteer Advocate
description_short: Life is forever changed after an impaired driving crash. Victims/survivors are often left bereaved, injured, and financially devastated. With the emotional, financial, and legal issues they face on a daily basis, victims/survivors need support to…
description_long: Life is forever changed after an impaired driving crash. Victims/survivors are often left bereaved, injured, and financially devastated. With the emotional, financial, and legal issues they face on a daily basis, victims/survivors need support to confront the challenges that lie ahead. In 2005, MADD launched the MADD Helpline, a telephone support service available to victims of drunk driving 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. The MADD Helpline depends on volunteers to fill the night, weekend, and holiday hours when someone needs to reach out. To become a MADD Helpline volunteer all applicants must completed online and live training. We are looking for Helpline Volunteers to provide victims with immediate emotional support, information, and referrals to MADD entities nationwide. This Volunteer Opportunity can be done remotely from the comfort of your home. If you or someone you know is interested in being a Helpline volunteer, please contact Vanessa Marquez at vanessa.marquez@madd.org. Required Skills and Qualifications: Background check Completion of the Beginning Victim Assistance Training Institute (VATI) Ability to communicate effectively Sensitivity to the needs of victims/survivors of impaired driving Ethical behavior and respect for confidentiality Computer to access the schedule, communicate referrals and maintain records Time Commitment Required: 5-10 hours per week, more if desired for 1 year. Length of Assignment: At least one year post training. In this MADD volunteer position of Helpline Volunteer, you must be at least 18 years or older. Out of respect for the victims, survivors, and families MADD serves, MADD does not accept those interested in volunteering who are currently involved in ongoing impaired driving offenses. Upon the resolution of your case please reach out to your local MADD office about potential volunteer opportunities.

description_short: Volunteers are an integral part of our care team as a friend and companion to the patient. Volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily responsibilities and stress. Making a difference in the lives…
description_long: Volunteers are an integral part of our care team as a friend and companion to the patient. Volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily responsibilities and stress. Making a difference in the lives of our patients and families is what YOU will do. Volunteers can also work in our offices doing administrative type tasks. 16 years old and up to volunteer in office, 18 years old and up to volunteer directly with our patients and families. I look forward to meeting you soon. Call with any questions 972-239-0907 or email at jwilliams@newcenturyhospice.com.

opportunity_title: MATI-X Volunteer
description_short: Interested in helping MATI-X assist communities in Southern Dallas? If you have a truck you are welcome to assist with delivering fresh produce. If you are strong and confident you can help by loading and unloading meals from cold storages to tables…
description_long: Interested in helping MATI-X assist communities in Southern Dallas? If you have a truck you are welcome to assist with delivering fresh produce. If you are strong and confident you can help by loading and unloading meals from cold storages to tables in need. We also grow food hubs within the community if gardening is your thing!

opportunity_title: METHODIST CHARLTON MEDICAL CENTER
description_short: WE WANT YOU! YES YOU! Volunteering can be an exciting and rewarding opportunity. You can learn new skills, use existing skills and meet new friends while providing important services to our staff, patients and their families. The rewards for…
description_long: WE WANT YOU! YES YOU! Volunteering can be an exciting and rewarding opportunity. You can learn new skills, use existing skills and meet new friends while providing important services to our staff, patients and their families. The rewards for volunteering are: The satisfaction of doing something for others Making a difference by serving our community Staying active after retirement The enjoyment of making new friends and having fun! How can you become a Volunteer at Methodist Charlton Medical Center? We invite you to contribute your special talents to our volunteer services program. Apply online at methodisthealthsystem.org/volunteer and select Methodist Charlton Medical Center or contact the Volunteer Services office at 214-947-7676. Current opportunities include: Outpatient/Transport Ambassador Information Desks The Breast Center Adult volunteers serve a minimum of 100 hours over six months. Volunteers commit to serving one 4-hour shift per week. The shifts are Monday - Friday. 8 a.m. - 12 p.m. or 12 p.m. - 4 p.m. We do not have weekend volunteer opportunities. We would love to hear from you!

opportunity_title: MIDLOTHIAN: Visit a Lonely Senior Citizen in need of a Friend!
description_short: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody…
description_long: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody remember important moments in their lives. You can be one of our Volunteers When you give us the precious gift of your time and interest, we give you a chance to make a real difference in someone’s life. We train you, find you the right assignment and stand behind you as you grow. Patient Volunteer Weekly visits to offer companionship for Patients. Examples include: oConversation oReading to the Patient oListening to music or watching videos of their favorite singer/actor oWatching a TV show together oWatching funny cat and dog reels together oAssisting with a hobby Volunteers are matched with 1 Patient at a time to someone within a 15-minute radius to where you live. You can volunteer for as little as 2 - 4 hours a month, and you can set a schedule that works for you. You can learn at your own pace, and we provide ongoing support until you feel completely comfortable working on your own. Requirements: Must be able to commit to Volunteer for one year (or 1 school year) Either 1 x per week or 2 x per month for about 1 hour No resume needed but an excellent skill and resume builder A caring heart to help people combat loneliness & depression. The process is simple! 1) Once you contact us, the Volunteer Coordinator will reach out via email to welcome you! 2) Then, you & the Volunteer Coordinator will set up a time to chat via phone so that you can introduce yourself & ask any questions. 3) The first step is to complete the application form via this link: https://rb.gy/3r2jta 4) Or you may email BethanyHarrell@AlphaOmegaHospice.com We look forward to you joining our Awesome Volunteer Team! It's truly an amazing and humbling experience and we walk with you the entire way!

opportunity_title: MIDLOTHIAN: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most
description_short: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most Become a Hospice Patient Volunteer Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an…
description_long: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most Become a Hospice Patient Volunteer Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an Alpha Omega Hospice Patient Volunteer, you'll become an important part of a patient's care team by offering companionship, support, and a caring presence to individuals facing serious illness. Why Hospice Volunteering? Build meaningful relationships with patients and families Make a positive impact during an important time in someone's life Develop communication, empathy, and active listening skills Share your time, talents, and compassion with others Experience the personal fulfillment that comes from serving your community Help reduce loneliness and improve quality of life for patients What Do Volunteers Do? Provide companionship through: Conversation and life-story sharing Reading, music, games, and activities Watching television together Listening and offering emotional support Simply being a caring presence No healthcare or volunteer experience is required. Volunteer Commitment Flexible scheduling Visit 1 time per week or 2 times per month Typical visits last 30–60 minutes Volunteer opportunities available near your preferred area What We Provide One-on-one patient matching within approximately 15 minutes of your preferred location Volunteer Coordinator accompaniment on your first visit Ongoing support and guidance Comprehensive training provided at no cost Requirements provided for FREE: Online Application Background Check TB Test (Skin or Blood Test) Drug Screening (THC not tested) Driver's License Online Volunteer Training "You are not just volunteering your time—you are providing comfort, dignity, companionship, and human connection to someone who truly needs it."

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Make a Difference and Impact Someone's Life - Hospice Companion
description_short: Looking for a rewarding volunteer position...look no further! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence,…
description_long: Looking for a rewarding volunteer position...look no further! Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Come join the Enhabit Hospice Volunteer TEAM! Be a part of our special team of Enhabit Hospice Volunteers. We care for our patients in the place they call home -- a private residence, skilled nursing facility, assisted living facility, or other inpatient setting. Just a few hours a week can make a lasting impact on the lives of others. Volunteer Opportunity: Direct Patient Care - Sitting with patients, reading, working puzzles, watching tv/movies and sharing life stories. This may also include Caregiver Respite where you will visit with the patient while the caregiver runs errands, takes time for themselves or goes to appointments. Must be able to pass a drug screen, background check, and be at least 17 years old. If you are interested in this wonderful volunteer opportunity, please email Faith Oates at Faith.oates@ehab.com Mission Statement At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description Enhabit Hospice is looking for a volunteer to provide friendship to patients during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, caregiver respite, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. For more information, please contact: Faith Oates - Volunteer Coordinator - Bereavement Services 469.677.3100 Faith.Oates@ehab.com

description_short: Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers can read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients…
description_long: Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers can read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: Make a difference in the life of a Hospice Patient!
description_short: Spend one-on-one time in person with a patient who seeks socialization. Use your personal skills and talents to be a source of light for individuals nearing the end of their life. We offer the following opportunities and need volunteers in these…
description_long: Spend one-on-one time in person with a patient who seeks socialization. Use your personal skills and talents to be a source of light for individuals nearing the end of their life. We offer the following opportunities and need volunteers in these areas: Direct Patient Care Compassion Calls Administrative Work Arts and Crafts Life Review/Journaling/Letters Message Therapist Physical Therapist Social Work

opportunity_title: Make a difference in the life of a Hospice Patient!
description_short: If you are compassionate, caring, and have a calling to help others, becoming a hospice volunteer might be the perfect opportunity for you. Our volunteers give their time, talent and hearts generously and unselfishly - yet commonly feel that they…
description_long: If you are compassionate, caring, and have a calling to help others, becoming a hospice volunteer might be the perfect opportunity for you. Our volunteers give their time, talent and hearts generously and unselfishly - yet commonly feel that they receive more than they give. Volunteers may choose the area in which they prefer to function and the hours they are willing to donate. We serve all of North Richland Hills and surrounding areas, Fort Worth, and Dallas. Some areas to volunteer include: Direct Patient Care: Providing Companionship to the Patient and/or Caregiver Relief Nursing home activities Reading to patients and socializing with them Musicians encouraged to apply Students who need internships or volunteer hours encouraged to apply Administrative: Data Entry, Filing and Office Assistance Bereavement Support

opportunity_title: Making a Meaningful Difference in Rockwall, TX!
description_short: Do you have a caring heart and desire to make a difference in someone's life? Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking compassionate individuals to serve as companion volunteers for patients in their final stages of life. What You'll Do: Visit patients in…
description_long: Do you have a caring heart and desire to make a difference in someone's life? Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking compassionate individuals to serve as companion volunteers for patients in their final stages of life. What You'll Do: Visit patients in their homes, assisted living, or nursing facilities Offer companionship through conversation, reading aloud, playing games, or simply sitting quietly together Provide a comforting presence to patients who may not have family nearby Support emotional well-being through kindness and attentiveness Requirements: Must be at least 18 years old Complete Bristol Hospice's volunteer training and onboarding process Pass background and TB screening (provided at no cost) Have reliable transportation Time Commitment: Flexible with your schedule - Just 1-2 hours a week can make a meaningful impact! How to Apply: If you're ready to share your time, heart, and compassion, we'd love to hear from you! Please contact our Volunteer Coordinator, Mackensie Moore at mackensie.moore@bristolhospice.com for more information!

opportunity_title: Mansfield Patient Care
description_short: Our patient's need YOU!! We are looking for volunteers who love spending time with our valuable and precious Seniors. Our patients enjoy visits from our caring community that will spend time with them, working puzzles, reading to them, watching…
description_long: Our patient's need YOU!! We are looking for volunteers who love spending time with our valuable and precious Seniors. Our patients enjoy visits from our caring community that will spend time with them, working puzzles, reading to them, watching their favorite TV shows or simply talking with them about thir interests. Our goal is to ensure no one ever feels alone.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Mansfield, TX - Hospice Volunteer
description_short: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the…
description_long: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: March for Babies TX Dallas - Event Volunteer
description_short: Depending on your availability and your volunteer experience, volunteers may assist with event set-up, registration and accepting donations, route management, leading activities during the event and post event break down.
description_long: Depending on your availability and your volunteer experience, volunteers may assist with event set-up, registration and accepting donations, route management, leading activities during the event and post event break down.

opportunity_title: Market Research Analyst (Unpaid Volunteer Position)
description_short: Location: Remote Reports To: Marketing Manager The Market Research Analyst will be responsible for researching and analyzing trends within the wellness community, healing modalities, and events industry. This role will gather insights on healers,…
description_long: Location: Remote Reports To: Marketing Manager The Market Research Analyst will be responsible for researching and analyzing trends within the wellness community, healing modalities, and events industry. This role will gather insights on healers, wellness practitioners, holistic businesses, and event organizers to guide our platform’s growth strategy. The analyst will support marketing, business development, and product teams by providing data-driven insights that help connect practitioners with seekers in the wellness community. Key Responsibilities: Conduct research on wellness, various healing modalities, wellness rituals markets to identify business opportunities, audience behavior, and emerging trends. Collect and interpret data on wellness/spiritual event organizers, practitioners, and holistic businesses to understand their needs, pricing models, and digital adoption. Monitor competitor platforms, event directories, and wellness marketplaces to benchmark performance and uncover gaps. Analyze consumer preferences, including participation in retreats, workshops, and online events. Provide insights to support platform development, subscription models, and marketing campaigns. Prepare reports, dashboards, and presentations for management, summarizing market trends and recommending actionable strategies. Support partnerships team with data insights to build collaborations with event organizers, healers, and wellness centers. Required Skills & Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Statistics, or related field (Master’s preferred). Prior experience as a Market Research Analyst, preferably in wellness, events, or digital platforms. Strong knowledge of qualitative and quantitative research methods. Proficiency in tools such as Excel, Tableau/Power BI, or similar analytics platforms. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate data into insights for non-technical teams. Passion for holistic wellness, spirituality, and community-driven platforms. Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple research projects simultaneously. Preferred Qualifications: Experience working in wellness, holistic health, or event-based industries. Hands-on experience with GoHighLevel CRM, automations, reporting, and campaign tracking. Familiarity with digital marketing metrics, SEO trends, and social media analytics. Understanding of customer behavior in subscription-based platforms.

opportunity_title: Marketing Coordinator - Adoption Container (HYBRID)

org_name: Big Brothers Big Sisters Greater Tarrant
opportunity_title: Mentor a Child in Arlington with Big Brothers Big Sisters!
description_short: At Big Brothers Big Sisters of Greater Tarrant County we believe it takes Little to be Big . Our mission is to create and support 1:1 mentoring relationships that ignite the power and promise of youth. Currently, there are over 100 children in…
description_long: At Big Brothers Big Sisters of Greater Tarrant County we believe it takes Little to be Big . Our mission is to create and support 1:1 mentoring relationships that ignite the power and promise of youth. Currently, there are over 100 children in Arlington waiting for a mentor. With just a couple hours a month, you can positively transform a child's future. Littles in our program report 90% improvement in family connectedness 98% improved attitude towards risky behavior and 97% are enrolled in university, employed, or enlisted within 1-year of high school graduation. In our Community-Based program, Bigs (mentors) and Littles (mentees) are matched based on similar interests, personalities, and backgrounds and spend time together out in the community doing things they both enjoy. Outings can be as simple as grabbing something to eat or as exciting as exploring a new hobby together! Matches typically see each other on evenings or weekends. Community-Based Expectations: See your Little 2-4 times a month. Be matched of at least 1 year. Applicants must be at least 18 y/o and have a valid driver's license and auto insurance. Are you ready to get started? Complete online application Submit 3-4 references, copy of valid driver's license, and a copy of auto insurance. Complete criminal background and driving record checks. Complete online training Complete a virtual interview

opportunity_title: Mentor a High School Student! Help start bright futures with us.
description_short: C.H.O.I.C.E.: Consider How One Individual Changes Everything Here at Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. we are preparing for a great school year, pairing volunteer mentors with underserved students for post-secondary success! We are ready for the first…
description_long: C.H.O.I.C.E.: Consider How One Individual Changes Everything Here at Foundation for C.H.O.I.C.E. we are preparing for a great school year, pairing volunteer mentors with underserved students for post-secondary success! We are ready for the first step: YOU! Each year, our nonprofit program gives volunteer mentors an opportunity to meet once a week for an hour with a hand-picked, at-risk high school junior or senior that would not otherwise have access to the guidance they need to make a future for themselves. Our mentors are provided with a complete online curriculum to lead students through college prep, researching a business, and preparing for senior year and beyond. This year we will be working hand-in-hand with select Dallas ISD high schools and plan to return to in-person meetings with students as much as possible. We are reaching out to ask you to commit to a student for the school year, and reap the benefits of changing a young life, your community, and the world. The average high school student receives less than 45 minutes of college counseling over four years. Fill the gap in their journey! Give the lasting gift of your own story and experience to a student eager for guidance and support that is not present in many young lives.

description_long: My Village Library is an enrichment program that helps HEB ISD students with homework, reading, math, self-confidence, but mostly puts time and value into building relationships. We would love to have more adult mentors to volunteer for one hour a week to encourage and challenge students to grow as learners. Our after-school program is Mondays - Thursdays from 4pm - 6pm.
