opportunity_title: Mentorship
description_short: At The Circle of SistAhood, we are seeking dedicated mentors to provide one-on-one guidance, advocacy, and support for our teen girls. Mentorship involves regular check-ins monthly, both virtual and every other month in-person ( you must be able to…
description_long: At The Circle of SistAhood, we are seeking dedicated mentors to provide one-on-one guidance, advocacy, and support for our teen girls. Mentorship involves regular check-ins monthly, both virtual and every other month in-person ( you must be able to connect with your mentee in person monthly) , to foster meaningful connections. Our girls are located in the Tarrant/Ellis County area. Through mentorship, we aim to enhance the mental, emotional, and physical stability of our teens while serving as advocates during their times of need. Joining as a mentor is a rewarding opportunity to make a lasting impact on the lives of young women in our community. More information can be discussed during an virtual meeting with our CEO. Please click on the link below to sign up!

opportunity_title: Mentorship Program Lead
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking a dedicated and supportive Mentorship Program Lead to play a key role in our mentorship initiative. This volunteer position focuses on direct engagement with participants and supporting the successful delivery of the mentorship experience under the guidance of the Program Supervisor. This is a 9-12 month minimum commitment , with the opportunity to renew. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy mentorship, facilitation, and community-building, and who want to contribute meaningfully to a structured, mission-driven program. Key Responsibilities / Tasks Support the day-to-day functioning of the mentorship program in collaboration with the Program Supervisor. Act as a point of contact for mentors and mentees for routine questions and guidance. Assist with participant onboarding, orientation sessions, and program communications. Help facilitate group check-ins, community-building activities, or structured discussions. Monitor participant engagement and flag concerns or challenges to the Program Supervisor. Encourage accountability and participation among mentors and mentees. Support documentation related to attendance, engagement, and feedback. Contribute ideas to improve the mentorship experience and overall program flow. Qualifications Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to build rapport and foster a supportive, inclusive environment. Organized and reliable, with the ability to follow established program guidelines. Comfortable working independently within a remote team structure. Prior experience as a mentor, peer leader, facilitator, or volunteer is an asset but not required. Interest in mentorship, personal development, mental health, or community support is highly valued.

opportunity_title: Mentorship Program Mentor
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking compassionate and committed Mentors to support participants in our structured mentorship program. This volunteer role focuses on building supportive, goal-oriented relationships and contributing to a positive, engaging mentorship experience. This is a 9–12 month minimum commitment, with the opportunity to renew. Mentors play a vital role in guiding participants through regular check-ins, shared learning, and personal development over the course of the program cycle. Key Responsibilities: Provide consistent, supportive mentorship to assigned participant(s) throughout the program. Participate in regular one-on-one or small-group mentorship sessions as scheduled. Support mentees in setting goals, building skills, and navigating challenges. Foster a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment during all mentorship interactions. Maintain clear and timely communication with participants and program leadership. Document session participation and engagement as required. Attend periodic mentor check-ins, trainings, or program meetings. For the on-call Mentor: provide flexible coverage support when additional mentor capacity is needed. Qualifications: Required languages: Fluency in English OR English + Spanish Strong interpersonal, active listening, and communication skills. Ability to commit reliably to scheduled mentorship sessions. Comfort working within program guidelines and maintaining confidentiality. Prior mentorship, coaching, or volunteer experience is an asset but not required. Genuine interest in mental health, personal development, and community support.

opportunity_title: Mentorship Program Supervisor
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking a committed and empathetic Mentorship Program Supervisor to support our structured mentorship initiative. This volunteer role involves overseeing a small cohort of 9-12 mentorship participants and ensuring a positive, organized, and supportive experience for both mentors and mentees. This is a 9-12 month minimum commitment , with the opportunity to renew. The role is ideal for individuals with strong leadership, communication, and mentoring skills who are passionate about community support, personal development, and mental well-being. Key Responsibilities: Serve as the primary supervisor for a mentorship cohort, supporting mentors and mentees throughout the program cycle. Facilitate onboarding and orientation for participants, including setting expectations, timelines, and program guidelines. Provide ongoing guidance, check-ins, and support to ensure mentorship relationships remain productive and respectful. Monitor participant engagement, attendance, and overall progress within the program. Address questions, concerns, or challenges that arise, escalating issues when necessary. Support communication between mentors, mentees, and the broader program team. Maintain organized records related to participant participation, schedules, and progress. Contribute to program evaluations, feed back collection, and end-of-cycle reporting. Collaborate with internal teams to support continuous improvement and future cohorts. Qualifications: Fluency in English and Spanish (spoken and written). Strong interpersonal, organizational, and communication skills. Ability to manage multiple participants while maintaining confidentiality and professionalism. Comfortable working independently in a remote environment while collaborating with a team. Prior experience in mentorship, supervision, coaching, education, or community programs is an asset. Interest in mental health, youth development, or community-based programming is strongly valued.

opportunity_title: Merchandise Manager
description_short: Paws in the City is looking for a Merchandise Manager! This role is responsible for building and managing the online merchandise store, including product strategy, availability, and overall performance using WooCommerce and Printify. Commitment:…
description_long: Paws in the City is looking for a Merchandise Manager! This role is responsible for building and managing the online merchandise store, including product strategy, availability, and overall performance using WooCommerce and Printify. Commitment: Approximately 3–6 hours per month, with flexibility based on product launches and campaign cycles. Responsibilities: Build, manage, and maintain the online store using WooCommerce and Printify Develop merchandise strategy, including what products to offer and when to release them Coordinate with team members on branding, campaigns, and seasonal promotions Monitor store performance, inventory levels, and customer engagement Optimize product listings, descriptions, and pricing to support fundraising goals Identify new merchandise opportunities that align with the PAWS mission and audience

opportunity_title: Mesquite-Visiting home patient
description_short: COVID testing and vaccinations not required. Will instruct how to use PPE safety equipment, and provide the PPE items. We visit in homes with patients that are terminally ill, not contagious diseases. The patient visit is for companionship and…
description_long: COVID testing and vaccinations not required. Will instruct how to use PPE safety equipment, and provide the PPE items. We visit in homes with patients that are terminally ill, not contagious diseases. The patient visit is for companionship and friendship only. We are seeking those who might read to the patient, watch TV with the patient, make puzzles, play games, make crafts, whatever the patient is able and willing to do. Our goal is to be there for the patient, and provide conversation and companionship. We do not prepare meals, spend more than 2 hours at a time, or run errands for the patients. This is time they asked for a person so they can avoid loneliness and depression. Visits should be 1-2 hours maximum and once or twice a week as determined by your availability and your schedule. You are the volunteer and we want to make this assignment easy for you, by keeping you close to where you work or live, so travel to a minimum. We appreciate your willingness to help however you can.

opportunity_title: Military Veteran Food Distribution Support
description_short: Make an Impact in the Lives of Veterans! Join us at our DalllasMilitary & Veteran Food Distribution events, held on the second Friday of each month, where we serve 150+ Veterans with essential food items. This is a day filled with community…
description_long: Make an Impact in the Lives of Veterans! Join us at our DalllasMilitary & Veteran Food Distribution events, held on the second Friday of each month, where we serve 150+ Veterans with essential food items. This is a day filled with community spirit, gratitude, and smiles all around! Want to see community in action? Be part of the team that prepares, greets, and loads food for our Veterans. Volunteer Times: 8:30 AM - 1:30 PM Upcoming Dates: February 14 March 14 April 11 May 9 June 13 July 11 August 8 September 12 October 10 November 14 December 12 We Need Key Leader Volunteers! Help us ensure the success of this event by taking on a leadership role: MVFD Assistant Coordinator Station Leaders Traffic Coordinator Registration Team If you have strong organizational skills and a passion for making a difference, these roles are a perfect fit! Ready to step up? Reach out today and join us in supporting our Veterans. Together, we can make a lasting impact! Register now to volunteer with this event: https://soldiersangels.formstack.com/forms/dallasmvfd_vol If you haven't created a profile in our Volunteer Database, please sign up now to stay up to date with other great ways to get involved: https://soldiersangels.my.site.com/s/login/

opportunity_title: Mother's Day Angel Blessing Bags
description_short: 🌷 Volunteer Opportunity: Mother’s Day Angels Blessing Bags Event Soup Angels Outreach Program – Dallas, TX Event Date: Saturday, May 9, 2026 Time: 1:00–3:00 p.m. Location: Chef Irvin’s House — 6919 Lindsley Ave, Dallas, TX 75223 💐 About the…
description_long: 🌷 Volunteer Opportunity: Mother’s Day Angels Blessing Bags Event Soup Angels Outreach Program – Dallas, TX Event Date: Saturday, May 9, 2026 Time: 1:00–3:00 p.m. Location: Chef Irvin’s House — 6919 Lindsley Ave, Dallas, TX 75223 💐 About the Opportunity Join Soup Angels for a joyful, hands‑on community event as we assemble 100+ Mother’s Day Blessing Bags and prepare 400 homestyle chicken plates for mothers, grandmothers, families, seniors, and unhoused neighbors across Dallas. This gathering honors the strength and resilience of the women in our community while ensuring every neighbor — male or female — receives care with dignity. This year, we’re thrilled to welcome Lakewood Art Lab as a community partner. Their young artists are creating heartfelt, hand‑drawn Mother’s Day encouragement cards that will be tucked into each Blessing Bag — a beautiful, uplifting touch that will mean so much to the women receiving them. Whether you bring donated items or simply bring your time and heart, you’ll be helping us prepare hope, nourishment, and kindness for our neighbors. 🧺 Volunteer Responsibilities Volunteers will support a variety of hands‑on tasks, including: Assembling Mother’s Day Blessing Bags with essential and comfort items Adding Lakewood Art Lab children’s encouragement drawings to each bag Sorting and organizing donated supplies Portioning homestyle chicken plates for distribution Packing outreach meals for Sunday delivery Assisting with setup, cleanup, and flow of the event Optional: Joining Chef Gary for a ride‑along outreach from 3:00–3:30 p.m. to distribute the first 100 Blessing Bags 💗 Items You May Bring (Optional) If you’d like to donate supplies, we are collecting: Socks Adult wipes Toothbrush sets Bottled water Healthy snacks Disposable hygiene kits Disposable shave kits Lip balm Extra Love for the Ladies: Travel‑size lotion, feminine hygiene products, pink/floral self‑care items, hair ties/headbands Amazon Wishlist for ideas: https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/1B4N14VCEV5SU?ref_=wl_share We also welcome pink bags for the women’s baskets and any reusable bags for the full outreach. ⭐ Qualifications No experience required — just a willing heart. Ideal volunteers: Enjoy hands‑on service and teamwork Are comfortable standing, sorting, or packing for short periods Appreciate warm, community‑centered environments Are reliable, kind, and respectful of neighbors experiencing hardship Families, teens, and groups are welcome 🧡 What Volunteers Gain A meaningful way to honor Mother’s Day through service Connection with a joyful, community‑driven outreach team The chance to directly support neighbors across Dallas A warm, inclusive environment where every contribution matters Optional real‑world outreach experience during the ride‑along 🚗 Optional Outreach Ride‑Along From 3:00–3:30 p.m. , volunteers may join Chef Gary to distribute the first 100 Blessing Bags to neighbors in need. Additional bags will be delivered on Sunday, May 🤝 Accessibility & Support No resources required — your time is just as valuable. All materials and instructions will be provided. Volunteers of all experience levels are welcome. 💙 About Soup Angels Soup Angels nourishes our Dallas neighbors with dignity, compassion, and joy — one bowl, one basket, one plate at a time. We serve families, seniors, and unhoused neighbors across sixty Dallas areas through weekly outreach, special events, and community partnerships.

org_name: PLANO CONSERVANCY FOR HISTORIC PRESERVATION INC
opportunity_title: Museum Volunteers Needed
description_short: Help local history come to life as a Tour Guide at the Interurban Railway Museum in downtown Plano! Volunteer Tour Guides greet scheduled groups and walk-ins, keep accurate visitor records and educate on museum exhibits and railway travel history.…
description_long: Help local history come to life as a Tour Guide at the Interurban Railway Museum in downtown Plano! Volunteer Tour Guides greet scheduled groups and walk-ins, keep accurate visitor records and educate on museum exhibits and railway travel history. Ideal for cheerful, proactive individuals with a passion for customer service. Interest in trains, engineering or Plano history is a plus, but not a requirement. The most important requirement is interest in engaging and educating visitors of all ages. Similarly, if you're a student interested in history or museum studies, this is a great opportunity to get relevant experience! Additional requirements: Must be 18 or older Complete a background check Must understand and recall written and verbal instructions Interested in volunteer a minimum of 1 day/week on an ongoing basis (weekdays) OR volunteering every other Saturday

opportunity_title: Music Hospice Volunteers Needed in North Dallas!
description_short: Do you play an instrument or sing? Your talent can bring comfort, peace, and joy to those at the end of life. Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking Music Volunteers to share the gift of music with our patients. What You'll Do: Play live music or sing…
description_long: Do you play an instrument or sing? Your talent can bring comfort, peace, and joy to those at the end of life. Bristol Hospice Dallas is seeking Music Volunteers to share the gift of music with our patients. What You'll Do: Play live music or sing for patients in their homes or care facilities Create a soothing atmosphere that promotes relaxation and emotional connection Offer personalized song selections based on patient preferences and history Requirements: Must be at least 18 years old Comfortable performing for individuals or small groups Complete Bristol Hospice's volunteer training and onboarding process Pass background check and TB screening Time Commitment: Flexible with your schedule, visits are typically between 30-60 minutes Apply here or contact our Volunteer Coordinator, Mackensie Moore at mackensie.moore@bristolhospice.com if you are interested or have questions regarding the volunteer program. We look forward to speaking with you soon!

opportunity_title: Music Volunteers Needed in Mesquite, TX!
description_short: Do you play an instrument or sing? Share your gift of music with patients and families receiving hospice care. Music brings joy, peace, and connection at life's most meaningful moments. As a Music Volunteer, you will: Provide soothing live music at…
description_long: Do you play an instrument or sing? Share your gift of music with patients and families receiving hospice care. Music brings joy, peace, and connection at life's most meaningful moments. As a Music Volunteer, you will: Provide soothing live music at bedside or in facilities Offer comfort through favorite songs or gentle instruments Create uplifting experiences for patients and their loved ones Requirements: Must be comfortable playing or singing in an intimate setting Reliable and compassionate presence Completion of hospice volunteer training and onboarding Join our volunteer team to bring peace and comfort to our hospice patients. Contact Mackensie Moore, RVC, with any questions or concerns at mackensie.moore@bristolhospice.com.

opportunity_title: Music Volunteers Needed- Share your love of music with hospice patients!
description_short: Looking to make a difference in the life of someone in your community? Become an Elara Caring Hospice Volunteer! Our volunteers enhance the lives of those receiving our services by providing compassionate visits. If you have an interest in serving…
description_long: Looking to make a difference in the life of someone in your community? Become an Elara Caring Hospice Volunteer! Our volunteers enhance the lives of those receiving our services by providing compassionate visits. If you have an interest in serving persons facing end of life issues and being a part of a fantastic hospice team, we want to hear from you! Please consider giving a little time each week to a hospice patient who would enjoy social visits or the gift of music! Read a book with them, help write a letter to a friend, share memories, listen. Individuals receiving hospice care appreciate the additional care and support of a volunteer and the joy of music.

description_long: We’re looking for motivated and enthusiastic high school and college leaders who are willing to start a chapter of our national organization! Responsibilities Organize and facilitate regular chapter meetings and community events Recruit, welcome, and support new chapter members Serve as the main point of contact between the local chapter and the national organization Communicate updates, opportunities, and resources to chapter members Build relationships with local partners, volunteers, and community stakeholders Track chapter activity and submit periodic reports to regional leadership Qualifications Passion for our mission and commitment to the community Strong communication and interpersonal skills Ability to motivate and bring people together Basic organizational and time management skills

opportunity_title: Need Volunteer Hours?  We can help!  Urgently need non-medical hospice volunteers at area facilities or private homes in Plano, Frisco, Allen, Wylie, Sachse, Garland, Etc.
description_short: Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening…
description_long: Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening ear to patients and families, assist with daily household activities for patients, provide grief support, serve as a companion to loved ones or share their personal expertise to help promote hospice care, the time given to Harbor Hospice families by our volunteers can be the most rewarding experiences of their lives. As a volunteer for Harbor Hospice's Patient Care Services, you "do good" every day. It takes a very special, caring person to give of themselves as unselfishly as our Harbor Hospice patient care volunteers. If you'd like to be a part of the Harbor Hospice team, here are some examples of the activities you can assist with: reading to a patient, or holding a hand staying with a patient while family members take a needed break or keep an appointment. shopping or doing errands for a patient. listening helping patients write or tell their life stories. light household chores providing help with makeup and hair Veterans needed to honor Veteran patients at bedside/share stories together. working on crafts or other projects clerical and other office duties If you would like to volunteer for Harbor Hospice, please contact Tiffany at tpowell@harborhospice.com

opportunity_title: New Digs Program Volunteer (REMOTE)
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As a New Digs Rehoming Volunteer, you'll help manage an innovative responsible rehoming Positive Alternatives to Shelter Surrender (PASS) Program. Team: PASS Location: Remote/Virtual - Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST time zone. Time Commitment: 2-4 hours/week Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: The PASS Director and New Digs Rehoming Program Director Works with: The general public, other PASS volunteers, the PASS Director, Foster Team, Adoption Team and members of Dallas Pets Alive! (DPA) leadership Position Responsibilities: Assist in reviewing candidate applications and collecting information from North Texas families for participation Ensuring that all pets participating in the program are altered, up to date on vaccines and microchipped and assist with coordinating resources to do so if not Ensuring that approved pets are placed on the DPA website with applicable information and removed post adoption Review prospective adopters and provide support to families who are looking to rehome their pets Ensure adoption agreements are completed between pet owners and adopters Tracking and follow up of all pets in and out of the rehoming program Ensure emails are responded to within 48 hours Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Professional and empathetic attitude Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Must exhibit compassion and non-judgement for individuals considering rehoming their pets Google Drive experience required PASS, counseling, social work or rescue experience is helpful Bilingual language ability helpful but not required Ability to operate a computer or mobile device The following activities are required: The ability to communicate information and ideas so others will understand. What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values. Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Newsletter Manager Marketing Internship OR Team Member
description_short: We are looking for a creative rising talent to help manage our monthly newsletter! As the newsletter manager, you would be able to take a lead role in organizing reading material for our audience of 700+ email recipients. This position can be done…
description_long: We are looking for a creative rising talent to help manage our monthly newsletter! As the newsletter manager, you would be able to take a lead role in organizing reading material for our audience of 700+ email recipients. This position can be done remotely, however, you MUST live in the United States to be considered . You also must be available at least 5 hours per week during the duration of the internship. Some responsibilities this internship requires will be: Organizing what material will appear in our monthly newsletter Leading the newsletter team which will consist of other interns and volunteers Regularly communicating with organization president and staff on newsletter projects Reviewing email campaign analytics for future strategy Some things preferred, but not required, in applicants are: Basic knowledge in using Mailchimp Experience in newsletter management or project management Preferably are currently majoring in OR have a degree in either marketing, communications, or any other relevant business major Flexible availability You MUST live in the United States to apply . If you are interested in the position, please apply for consideration. We will be accepting applicants on a rolling admission basis. Thank you!

Some responsibilities this internship requires will be: Organizing what material will appear in our monthly newsletter Leading the newsletter team which will consist of other interns and volunteers Regularly communicating with organization president and staff on newsletter projects Reviewing email campaign analytics for future strategy Some things preferred, but not required, in applicants are: Basic knowledge in using Mailchimp Experience in newsletter management or project management Preferably are currently majoring in OR have a degree in either marketing, communications, or any other relevant business major Flexible availability You MUST live in the United States to apply . If you are interested in the position, please apply for consideration. We will be accepting applicants on a rolling admission basis. Thank you!

opportunity_title: Non Profit Board Member
description_short: Seeking Passionate Leaders: Join the Board of Rebuilding Together North Texas! Are you ready to use your skills and network to create lasting change in your community? Rebuilding Together North Texas, a highly regarded nonprofit dedicated to…
description_long: Seeking Passionate Leaders: Join the Board of Rebuilding Together North Texas! Are you ready to use your skills and network to create lasting change in your community? Rebuilding Together North Texas, a highly regarded nonprofit dedicated to providing critical home repairs for low-income homeowners, is looking for four new board members with expertise in: Senior Citizens Military Service Real Estate Public Relations Social Media Business Development Dallas Philanthropy Finance Attorney/Legal Volunteer Engagement/Management Our Values At Rebuilding Together North Texas, our work is driven by our core values: ✅ Accountability - We honor our commitments and take responsibility for making a meaningful impact. ✅ Communication - Open, honest, and clear dialogue is essential to our success. ✅ Teamwork - We collaborate to maximize our effectiveness and strengthen our community. ✅ Passion - We are deeply committed to our mission and those we serve. ✅ Growth Mindset - We embrace learning, innovation, and continuous improvement. ✅ Integrity - We uphold the highest ethical standards, ensuring trust, transparency, and respect in all we do. What We’re Looking For: We’re seeking dedicated, experienced professionals who: Have a strong network of potential donors and sponsors. Connections within Dallas professional sports zones (football, hockey, basketball, cricket) are a significant plus! Thrive on action. We are a working board-every member takes on essential fundraising and development tasks. Deliver on commitments. A high "Say/Do" ratio is key to success on our board. Embrace our mission and values. You should feel comfortable building relationships with sponsors, sharing our stories, and making or securing financial contributions of at least $5,000 annually. Board Member Expectations: Active Participation - Attend monthly board meetings (held on the 4th Tuesday in Plano/Allen/McKinney area). Committee Engagement - Serve on at least one committees to help drive our mission forward. Event Support - Attend all major events and bring potential new donors to the table. Fundraising Leadership - Cultivate relationships with sponsors and donors, confidently advocate for higher levels of support, and help expand our network. Why Join Us? Impact - Be part of a nonprofit that transforms lives by ensuring safe, healthy homes for those in need. Growth - Collaborate with a talented and passionate board, build connections, and expand your reach in the community. Fun - While we work hard, we also make time to celebrate our successes and enjoy the journey together. Ideal Candidates Will Have: Nonprofit experience (preferred but not required). Strong interpersonal skills and a passion for making a difference. A willingness to lead, contribute, and inspire others to join our cause. You’ll receive a comprehensive board membership packet outlining all commitments and opportunities to make an impact. Ready to Rebuild Hope? Apply today! Watch our video to learn more: National Rebuilding Day

opportunity_title: Non-Musician Dallas Volunteers: Bring Music to Patients with Musicians On Call!
description_short: Are you someone who loves music and helping others? Apply to be a Volunteer Guide (non-musician) with Musicians On Call today! Musicians On Call (MOC) is a nonprofit organization that brings live and recorded music to the bedsides of patients,…
description_long: Are you someone who loves music and helping others? Apply to be a Volunteer Guide (non-musician) with Musicians On Call today! Musicians On Call (MOC) is a nonprofit organization that brings live and recorded music to the bedsides of patients, families and caregivers in healthcare environments. During each shift, a Volunteer Guide escorts a Volunteer Musician room to room offering a live performance to patients. MOC needs YOU to help us spread the healing power of music at Children’s Health Dallas and UT Southwestern Clements ! UT Southwestern Clements - Tuesdays @ 5:30 pm CT Children's Health Dallas - program day/time TBD Volunteers must complete one 90 minute shift once a month for at least one year Watch our video to learn more: Charles Esten Delivers the Healing Power of Music Click the "Go To Website" link below to complete your volunteer application and help us deliver the healing power of music in Dallas!

opportunity_title: Non-profit Fundraiser
description_short: Gateway Alliance Project is a startup non-profit 501(c)(3) organization geared at improving the roads of re-entry to formerly incarcerated women, and by providing diversion alternatives for at-risk youth prior to engagement with the carceral system.…
description_long: Gateway Alliance Project is a startup non-profit 501(c)(3) organization geared at improving the roads of re-entry to formerly incarcerated women, and by providing diversion alternatives for at-risk youth prior to engagement with the carceral system. We offer a variety of life skills trainings, and are happy to announce the opening of our transitional home for women April 2024. We are in need of a fundraiser to organize events and campains to raise money and other kinds of donations .

opportunity_title: NonProfit Grant Writer
description_short: Texas CARES is a dedicated nonprofit organization committed to rescuing and rehabilitating cats in need, finding them loving forever homes, and promoting animal welfare education within our community. With over 20 years of experience, our…
description_long: Texas CARES is a dedicated nonprofit organization committed to rescuing and rehabilitating cats in need, finding them loving forever homes, and promoting animal welfare education within our community. With over 20 years of experience, our organization relies heavily on the support of generous donors and the community to continue our mission. We are seeking a skilled and passionate Grant Writer to join our team. The ideal candidate will have experience in securing funding for nonprofit organizations, with a deep understanding of animal welfare issues and the unique challenges faced by rescue organizations. The Grant Writer will be responsible for researching, writing, and managing grant proposals and reports to secure financial support for our rescue programs and initiatives.

description_short: Rae's Hope is looking to recruit board members who would be interested in serving on our non-profit board. We are a grassroots organization working in underserved communities. We are looking for individual who are committed to see change in the life…
description_long: Rae's Hope is looking to recruit board members who would be interested in serving on our non-profit board. We are a grassroots organization working in underserved communities. We need board members that can help with Marketing, Funds Development, Governance, Public Relations, Strategic Planning and Community engagement. We also need someone that can help us with our website and the updates. Look at Rae's Hope Inc. as the non-profit for change. Reviving Communities one Child at a Time

opportunity_title: Nonprofit Volunteer Coordinator
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking an organized and dedicated Volunteer Manager to lead our volunteer program. The Volunteer Manager will be responsible for recruiting, and coordinating volunteers, ensuring they are effectively engaged in supporting our mission. This position is essential in building a strong and motivated volunteer network to expand our impact. Key Responsibilities: Screen, interview, and onboard new volunteers. Organize sessions to equip volunteers with the necessary skills and knowledge. Coordinate volunteer schedules and assignments to support various programs. Maintain accurate records of volunteer activities and contributions. Foster a positive and inclusive volunteer culture. Provide ongoing support and recognition to volunteers. Collaborate with leadership to identify volunteer needs and opportunities. Qualifications: Experience in volunteer coordination, human resources, or nonprofit management preferred. Strong organizational and leadership skills. Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. Passion for mental health advocacy and community engagement. Ability to work independently and in a team setting.

opportunity_title: Nursing Students | Lifted Hospice
description_short: Opportunity Description: Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Your time can make a…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: * Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. * Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. * Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: * Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. * Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. * A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? * Make a lasting impact in your community. * Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life. Contact Hannah Morris at hannah.morris@liftedhospice.com or 214-656-8402 for more information. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held at a volunteer group location (e.g. office, park) Event can be held at organization location(s) No Donation

opportunity_title: Nursing Students/Allied Health/Pre-Med:  Do you need volunteer hours for the 2018-2019 school year?
description_short: I Want To Help! Hospice Nursing students/Allied Health/Pre-med: Do you need volunteer hours for your program for 2023-2024 school year? Inquire today to find out how you can be a part of the Elysian Hospice volunteer team!! We have many…
description_long: I Want To Help! Hospice Nursing students/Allied Health/Pre-med: Do you need volunteer hours for your program for 2023-2024 school year? Inquire today to find out how you can be a part of the Elysian Hospice volunteer team!! We have many opportunities in the DFW area, including Direct Patient Companionship and Educational opportunities/in-services/Covid19 precautions as well. Volunteer hours are logged weekly and letters to your program are provided upon request. How does a volunteer support hospice? Providing respite to family Socialization: reading, playing cards, sewing, simply being a friendly visitor to a patient Office and administrative support Offering their unique talents in any way toward supporting patients, families, and the community at-large Volunteer with our team in the following capacities: PATIENT CARE VOLUNTEER Provide patient care and support services that contribute to the quality of life for patients and their families, such as socialization and active listening, providing companionship, or engagement in music or interested hobbies OFFICE VOLUNTEER Assist the clinical team with day to day operations within the office, such as assisting with patient charts, answering the telephone, filing, copying, and other supportive tasks. This opportunity would be at our office in Addison. We look forward to having you as part of our team!

opportunity_title: OVILLA:  ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most
description_short: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most Become a Hospice Patient Volunteer Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an…
description_long: ❤️ Make a Difference When It Matters Most Become a Hospice Patient Volunteer Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an Alpha Omega Hospice Patient Volunteer, you'll become an important part of a patient's care team by offering companionship, support, and a caring presence to individuals facing serious illness. Why Hospice Volunteering? Build meaningful relationships with patients and families Make a positive impact during an important time in someone's life Develop communication, empathy, and active listening skills Share your time, talents, and compassion with others Experience the personal fulfillment that comes from serving your community Help reduce loneliness and improve quality of life for patients What Do Volunteers Do? Provide companionship through: Conversation and life-story sharing Reading, music, games, and activities Watching television together Listening and offering emotional support Simply being a caring presence No healthcare or volunteer experience is required. Volunteer Commitment Flexible scheduling Visit 1 time per week or 2 times per month Typical visits last 30–60 minutes Volunteer opportunities available near your preferred area What We Provide One-on-one patient matching within approximately 15 minutes of your preferred location Volunteer Coordinator accompaniment on your first visit Ongoing support and guidance Comprehensive training provided at no cost Requirements provided for FREE: Online Application Background Check TB Test (Skin or Blood Test) Drug Screening (THC not tested) Driver's License Online Volunteer Training "You are not just volunteering your time—you are providing comfort, dignity, companionship, and human connection to someone who truly needs it."

description_short: Office volunteers assist in our main office Monday through Friday from 9am until Noon. We currently are in need of volunteers able to commit to working every Monday or every Thursday 9:00am until noon. Duties include: Answer phones Process new…

opportunity_title: Open Board Position for Special Needs Non-Profit
description_short: We are looking to 2 individuals interested in joining our Board. Our non-profit is committed to building a strong community where individuals with disabilities are valued, respected and included. We believe every person, regardless of ability,…
description_long: We are looking to 2 individuals interested in joining our Board. Our non-profit is committed to building a strong community where individuals with disabilities are valued, respected and included. We believe every person, regardless of ability, deserves dignity, opportunity and a nurturing environment to thrive. Our non-profit hosts 3 signature events yearly, plus 3 community outreach activities. The Board position will be for 1yr (may be extended) and requires a 30min to 1hr weekly commitment. However, the 2 week window prior to and during an upcoming event or activity, that commitment will temporarily increase to 4 to 6hrs. If you're passionate about serving and supporting children and adults with physical or intellectual disabilities, and interested in learning more about the position then reply and you'll be invited to an PRIVATE informational meeting on Saturday 7/25 at the Frisco Library. This is a working board, volunteer non-paid position.

opportunity_title: Operations Manager
description_short: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer)** About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation (Menzies Mission) is a non-profit dedicated to supporting orphans and vulnerable families through resources, education, and…
description_long: Location: Remote Employment Type: Part-Time, Unpaid (Volunteer)** About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation (Menzies Mission) is a non-profit dedicated to supporting orphans and vulnerable families through resources, education, and holistic development rooted in the Menzies 8 Keys of Needs: Housing, Health, Nutrition, Education, Family, Community, Faith, and Love. We believe that self-actualization is a powerful pathway to breaking cycles of poverty and trauma. Through mental and emotional support, healing-centered guidance, and community-rooted programs, we strengthen connection and belonging, empowering individuals to realize their potential and transform their future. In partnership with our social-impact company, Me Is We, we are developing a regenerative financial ecosystem to support the long-term sustainability and expansion of our mission. At the heart of this ecosystem is My Source Network, a global holistic wellness community platform devoted to healing and mindful connection. Through practitioners, conscious events, and transformational resources, My Source Network supports individuals on their journeys toward balance, empowerment, and belonging while cultivating a worldwide community rooted in healing, authenticity, and collaboration. We are an impact-driven organization rooted in integrity, accountability, collaboration, and service. Our work is guided by a commitment to collective healing and empowering the communities we serve, knowing that meaningful, lasting change is created together. If you feel called to bring your skills, presence, and purpose into our mission, we would be honored to welcome you into the Menzies Mission community. Role Overview We are building a global, holistic community marketplace that brings people together through wellness, learning, community, and conscious experiences. As we prepare for our MVP launch, we are seeking a highly capable Operations Manager to design, build, and run the operational foundation of the platform. The Operations Manager is the central owner of marketplace operations, responsible for the systems, workflows, and standards that keep the platform stable, trusted, and running smoothly. This role owns core operations systems, CRM infrastructure, and the full event and organizer lifecycle—from onboarding and publishing through delivery, issue resolution, and post-event follow-up. This role ensures organizers move smoothly and reliably through the platform while maintaining clear operational standards, quality control, and consistency across the marketplace. The Operations Manager is responsible for protecting marketplace health by identifying friction points, resolving issues and edge cases, coordinating escalations across teams, and ensuring problems are addressed quickly and thoughtfully before they impact the broader community. At the MVP stage, this role is both builder and operator—establishing workflows, documenting lightweight processes, tracking operational health signals, and improving systems in real time. The Operations Manager acts as the connective tissue between organizers, internal teams, and leadership, translating day-to-day platform reality into clarity, stability, and readiness to scale. This role is mission-critical to the success of our MVP launch and early growth. Success in this position directly impacts platform stability, organizer confidence, community trust, and the long-term health of the marketplace. What You Will Own You will have clear ownership over: The end-to-end organizer and event lifecycle, from onboarding to post-event completion Core operations systems and CRM, including data accuracy and workflow integrity Marketplace operational standards and quality control Issue resolution, edge cases, and escalation pathways Launch readiness and go-live coordination Operational visibility and performance signals You are the person who ensures nothing falls through the cracks. Key Responsibilities Design, implement, and continuously improve operational workflows that support organizers, events, and marketplace integrity Own and manage CRM systems, ensuring clean data, clear stages, and reliable handoffs Define and uphold organizer readiness standards, event publishing requirements, and quality checks Ensure organizers move smoothly through onboarding, approval, publishing, delivery, and follow-up Identify friction points, bottlenecks, and breakdowns in the system and resolve them proactively Serve as the primary owner of issue resolution, including edge cases, organizer questions, and operational problems Coordinate escalations across teams (Product, Tech, Support, Leadership) when needed Track operational health signals such as onboarding completion, event readiness, turnaround times, and recurring issues Maintain lightweight documentation of workflows and SOPs as systems evolve Coordinate MVP launch readiness, including operational checklists and go-live validation V2 Partner closely with leadership to surface risks, insights, and opportunities for improvement What Success Looks Like In this role, success means: Organizers feel supported, clear, and confident using the platform Events move through the system with minimal friction or confusion Issues are resolved quickly, calmly, and consistently The marketplace feels stable, trustworthy, and well-run Leadership has clear visibility into operational health Who You Are You are a systems thinker who thrives on creating clarity out of complexity You are calm under pressure and comfortable owning problems end-to-end You balance structure with adaptability, especially in early-stage environments You communicate clearly and respectfully with a wide range of people You care deeply about trust, quality, and the human experience behind systems You are comfortable building while operating, not waiting for perfect conditions Experience & Skills Experience in operations, marketplace operations, platform operations, or systems management Hands-on experience with CRM tools and workflow design Comfort working in MVP or early-stage environments Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills Ability to document processes simply and clearly High integrity, reliability, and ownership mindset Experience in marketplaces, events, platforms, or community-based products is a strong plus. Application Process: To apply, review the job description and submit your resume, portfolio, and cover letter to hr@menziesmission.org. Please ensure that your contact information is accurate for any status updates regarding your application. If selected, you will be contacted to discuss your qualifications and how they align with the organization's mission. For any questions, feel free to reach out to hr@menziesmission.org. We look forward to reviewing your application. Equal Opportunity Employer: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Accommodation for the application process is available upon request.

opportunity_title: Orant Charities 2026 Mudzi Gala
description_short: I am excited to invite you to volunteer for Orant Charities' Mudzi Gala on Saturday, August 29th, 2026, at Perot Museum of Nature and Science. Support our team as guests enjoy private access to the Perot Museum after dark. The evening will include…
description_long: I am excited to invite you to volunteer for Orant Charities' Mudzi Gala on Saturday, August 29th, 2026, at Perot Museum of Nature and Science. Support our team as guests enjoy private access to the Perot Museum after dark. The evening will include music, raffle, live auctions, and heads or tails game. All proceeds will expand access to healthcare, water and sanitation, education, business development, agriculture, and energy and environment for those who need it most in Malawi. Volunteers are critical to event success. We ask that volunteers be 18 + years or older and are eager and excited to learn more about our organization. Volunteers will be asked to assist with set-up, manage the registration process, fundraising pulls, answer basic questions, help with guest movement throughout the gala, and support staff where needed most. All training will be provided. Dinner is included for the volunteers. Please feel free to contact Amy Reiter for more information. Email: areiter@orantcharities.org Phone: (940) 765-38755 We look forward to meeting you soon!

opportunity_title: Our Shared Journeys Org Volunteer
description_short: We are currently seeking dedicated, ongoing volunteers to join our community and support the core operations of our organization. We have a variety of needs ranging from administrative assistance and data management to web design and digital…
description_long: We are currently seeking dedicated, ongoing volunteers to join our community and support the core operations of our organization. We have a variety of needs ranging from administrative assistance and data management to web design and digital marketing. If you are looking to play a long-term role in sustaining our mission, we would love to connect with you. Please share your interests and availability with us; while we may not have an immediate opening, we keep all volunteer applications on file and will reach out as specific opportunities arise that match your unique skills. All Our Shared Journeys roles are remote, and for in-person events, the specific location will be provided upon assignment. For those joining us in remote volunteer roles, our dress code is business casual. For in-person events, specific dress code details will be provided within your volunteer invitation.

opportunity_title: Our Southlake Area Volunteer Team Needs YOU!
description_short: Our Southlake Area patients need YOU! Become a patient companion volunteer providing companionship, active listening, reading or other activities with our Southlake area hospice patients. This is a very rewarding opportunity to support the patient…
description_long: Our Southlake Area patients need YOU! Become a patient companion volunteer providing companionship, active listening, reading or other activities with our Southlake area hospice patients. This is a very rewarding opportunity to support the patient and their family at this juncture of their life journey. Requirements; A commitment to visit assigned patient(s) a minimum of 2-4 times per month. You choose the number of patients you are able to take on and the location of those patients. Orientation & Training provided at no charge to the volunteer.

opportunity_title: Outreach Coordinator/Community Engagement Liaison (Dallas)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Position Overview: As our Outreach Coordinator/Community Engagement Liaison in Dallas, you'll be the vital link between SelfSoulLiberate and the communities we serve. You'll build meaningful relationships, foster collaborations, and directly connect individuals with our resources to advance health equity and mental wellness. Role Description: We are seeking a passionate, empathetic, and highly collaborative Outreach Coordinator/Community Engagement Liaison to build and nurture relationships within under-resourced communities in Dallas, Texas. This remote volunteer position is fundamental to understanding community needs, expanding our reach, and ensuring our programs are accessible and culturally responsive. You will be responsible for identifying key community leaders, organizations, and events, and for directly engaging residents to promote SelfSoulLiberate's mission and initiatives. Key Responsibilities: Identify and establish relationships with key community leaders, local organizations, and influential groups in under-resourced Dallas communities. Represent SelfSoulLiberate at community meetings, events, and forums, actively engaging with residents and stakeholders. Coordinate and conduct outreach activities , including informational sessions, workshops, and resource fairs, to promote mental health literacy and SelfSoulLiberate's services. Gather feedback and insights from community members to inform program development and ensure our initiatives meet local needs effectively. Distribute educational materials and resources about mental health equity, stigma reduction, and SelfSoulLiberate's mission. Collaborate with the SelfSoulLiberate team to align outreach efforts with organizational goals and program offerings. Maintain accurate records of outreach activities, community contacts, and engagement metrics. Qualifications: Proven experience in community outreach, engagement, social work, or a related field, ideally within a non-profit or public health context. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to build trust and rapport with diverse community members. Deep understanding of and passion for the Dallas, TX community, particularly its under-resourced areas. Ability to work independently, take initiative, and manage multiple outreach activities simultaneously. Strong organizational skills with attention to detail for tracking engagement and resources. Empathetic, culturally sensitive, and committed to SelfSoulLiberate's mission of health equity and mental health stigma reduction. Comfortable and effective working in a remote environment, with excellent virtual communication and collaboration skills.

opportunity_title: Outreach Engagement Lead
description_short: Lyfe Journey Unfolding (LJU) is a Texas-based nonprofit empowering individuals experiencing homelessness through community outreach, transitional housing initiatives, and wrap-around support services. We’re seeking a passionate Outreach &…
description_long: Lyfe Journey Unfolding (LJU) is a Texas-based nonprofit empowering individuals experiencing homelessness through community outreach, transitional housing initiatives, and wrap-around support services. We’re seeking a passionate Outreach & Engagement Lead to help expand our community presence and partnerships. What You’ll Do: Build relationships with community partners, volunteers, and supporters Represent LJU at outreach events and community activities Support volunteer engagement and community partnerships Assist with outreach efforts for programs, events, and initiatives What We’re Looking For: Passion for community service and social impact Strong communication and relationship-building skills Reliable, organized, and comfortable engaging with diverse communities Nonprofit, outreach, or event experience is a plus (not required) Time Commitment: Approximately 5–10 hours per week (flexible; evenings/weekends as needed) Why Volunteer With Us: Gain leadership and nonprofit experience Expand your community network Make a meaningful impact in the lives of others Opportunity to grow with a mission-driven organization

opportunity_title: Outreach Representative - Support Veterans!
description_short: Get out and about in the community to support veterans through Soldiers’ Angels! Join our team today and begin building community relationships to support veterans! If you have an outgoing personality and you love meeting new people, this is the…
description_long: Get out and about in the community to support veterans through Soldiers’ Angels! Join our team today and begin building community relationships to support veterans! If you have an outgoing personality and you love meeting new people, this is the volunteer position for you. We are looking for someone to help spread the mission of Soldiers’ Angels through tables at local events, presenting at local business, briefing other military nonprofits and more. Are you ready to put your people skills to use? Reach out now to get more information!

opportunity_title: Owenwood Farm Hands
description_short: Want to get outside while also helping to grow fresh, nutritious produce? Come join us at Owenwood Farm on the 2nd Saturday of every month to get your hands in the dirt. In cooler months, we start at 9am and in warmer months at 8am. No experience…
description_long: Want to get outside while also helping to grow fresh, nutritious produce? Come join us at Owenwood Farm on the 2nd Saturday of every month to get your hands in the dirt. In cooler months, we start at 9am and in warmer months at 8am. No experience needed, but a love of good food, attention to detail (like, weeds and young plant shoots can look alike), and a desire to grow good food in Dallas are the requirements. Closed-toed shoes needed. Please wear clothes you can get dirty and that are appropriate for the weather that day. Feel free to bring your own gloves, though we do have some onsite. Bring a water bottle. We will provide a refill for it. To register go to https://grownorthtexas.org/events and scroll to find Farm Hands Volunteer Day. Groups who would like a specific volunteer day can contact us to schedule. Owenwood Farm is located in Far East Dallas at part of the Owenwood Farm & Neighbor Space collaborative. Currently the farm has about 1 acre in production. Fruits and vegetables grown here are sold in various ways including GNTX's WIC Farm Stand program, at local farmers markets, and at our weekly onsite farm stand every Friday from 9a-12p. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 20 Event can be held at organization location(s) Donation Requested We always appreciate when volunteer groups can provide a donation to GROW North Texas. This supports the work of Owenwood farm and to covers our staff time to provide an excellent experience.

opportunity_title: PECAN HILL: Visit a Lonely Senior Citizen
description_short: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody…
description_long: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody remember important moments in their lives. You can be one of our Volunteers When you give us the precious gift of your time and interest, we give you a chance to make a real difference in someone’s life. We train you, find you the right assignment and stand behind you as you grow. Patient Volunteer Part of a Patient’s Care Team. You will make weekly visits to offer companionship for Patients. Examples of Patient care Volunteer opportunities include reading to the Patient, assisting with a hobby, conversation, listening to music or even watching a TV show together. Work with the Volunteer Coordinator to enter facilities & visit your Hospice Patient. Volunteers are matched with 1 Patient at a time to someone within a 15-minute radius to where you live. Requirements: Must be able to commit to Volunteer for 6 months - one year @ 2 - 4 x per month for up to 1 hour. No resume needed but an excellent skill and resume builder. Volunteer Coordinator always goes along with you on your 1st Patient visit.

opportunity_title: PFF Walk - Dallas
description_short: The PFF Walk unites the pulmonary fibrosis (PF) and interstitial lung disease (ILD) community in raising funds and awareness for the Pulmonary Fibrosis Foundation. Join us as we walk together toward a cure! Held at The Sound at Cypress Waters on…
description_long: The PFF Walk unites the pulmonary fibrosis (PF) and interstitial lung disease (ILD) community in raising funds and awareness for the Pulmonary Fibrosis Foundation. Join us as we walk together toward a cure! Held at The Sound at Cypress Waters on Saturday, October 24, 2026, the PFF Walk - Dallas offers two course options (one or two miles) along the tranquil Cypress waters Trail. After the walk, we invite participants to stay for a celebration with family-friendly activities and light refreshments. Provide support for the PFF Walk by volunteering! Sign up to volunteer using our SignUpGenius form or contact us at PFFWalk@pulmonaryfibrosis.org to learn more about our day-of volunteer opportunities which include: Setup and cleanup Registration tent support Food/beverage tent team Route support and cheer squad

opportunity_title: Paint Nails for Hospice Patients in Arlington TX (76011)
description_short: Direct patient care volunteers are needed to provide companionship and assist with painting nails of the group home residents. All supplies will be provided. No nail experience or any other experience required, you will just be painting nails. Tasks…
description_long: Direct patient care volunteers are needed to provide companionship and assist with painting nails of the group home residents. All supplies will be provided. No nail experience or any other experience required, you will just be painting nails. Tasks may include: Companionship for patients Painting nails Activities Entertainment Requirements: Minimum age: 16 In-office or zoom interview In-office orientation/training Valid driver's license/ID Car insurance Qualifications: A commitment to the mission and values of the hospice organization. Strong organizational and communication skills. Attention to detail and the ability to handle confidential information. Proficiency in basic computer skills, including word processing and data entry. Friendly and compassionate demeanor. Reliable and punctual. Ability to work collaboratively in a team and take direction from staff members. Time Commitment: This is an ongoing opportunity. You tell us your schedule and we'll work with you. Please let us know if you need different or shorter times, we will work with your schedule! This opportunity will help develop skills such as: Compassion Understanding patient care Understanding how a hospice business operates. Benefits: Volunteer retention. Gain knowledge of healthcare and how a hospice is run. Attain credit hours (if required by your instructors and/or institution) Work closely with patients, nurses, social workers, hospice staff, etc. The opportunity to make a meaningful impact in the lives of hospice patients and their families. Gain valuable experience in end-of-life care. Ongoing training and support to enhance your skills and knowledge. A supportive and compassionate team environment. If you or someone you know might be interested, please contact Divinity Healthcare at 817-438-7463 or tazi@divinityhospice.net.

description_long: Sewa International Dallas Chapter invites you to be part of the "Don't Mess with Texas/Great American Cleanup & Canned Food Drive" Join hands with us as we clean up our community and support families in need! This event is part of the "Don't Mess with Texas Trash-Off", Texas’ signature initiative for the Great American Cleanup, the nation’s largest community improvement program. Why Volunteer? Help remove litter from the Trinity River Watershed, which provides water for over 40% of Texans. Make an impact in your community by keeping Irving clean and beautiful. Support local families by donating non-perishable food items to Crisis Ministries Food Pantry. Volunteers of all ages are welcome ! However, those 17 and younger must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.

opportunity_title: Pathways Summit 2026 Event Onsite Volunteers
description_short: Be a Part of the Journey: Volunteer at the Pathways Summit! Are you passionate about empowering women entrepreneurs? Do you want to make a real difference in your community? Then we invite you to join us as a volunteer for the Pathways Summit! The…
description_long: Be a Part of the Journey: Volunteer at the Pathways Summit! Are you passionate about empowering women entrepreneurs? Do you want to make a real difference in your community? Then we invite you to join us as a volunteer for the Pathways Summit! The Pathways Summit is a dynamic event designed to equip under-resourced women entrepreneurs with the tools, resources, and connections they need to thrive. From inspiring speakers and interactive workshops to invaluable networking opportunities, the summit is a catalyst for growth and success. We're looking for enthusiastic and dedicated volunteers to help us make the Pathways Summit a truly transformative experience. Your support is essential to ensuring the smooth operation of the event and creating a welcoming and supportive environment for our attendees. Volunteer Opportunities We have a variety of volunteer roles available, including: Registration & Check-In: Welcome attendees, distribute materials, and ensure a smooth registration process. Event Logistics: Assist with setup, room management, and ensuring the venue runs smoothly. Workshop Support: Help facilitators with materials, technology, and attendee assistance. Networking Event Support: Assist with event setup, food service, and ensuring a fun and engaging atmosphere. Social Media & Photography: Capture the energy of the summit through photos and social media updates. Productions: Assist with audio/visual setup and operation, stage management, and other production needs. Guest Support: Provide assistance to speakers, VIPs, and other special guests, ensuring they have a positive experience. Speakers Support: Coordinate with speakers, manage their schedules, and ensure their needs are met. Hospitality Support: Provide information to attendees, answer questions, and create a welcoming and helpful environment. 7 shifts available: Thursday June 04, 2026 - 11:00 to 03:00 PM CST Thursday June 04, 2026- 1:00 PM - 6:00 PM CST Friday June 05, 2025 - 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM CST Friday June 05, 2025 - 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST Friday June 05, 2025 - 1:00 PM to 6:00 PM CST Saturday June 07, 2025 - 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM CST Saturday June 07, 2025 - 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST Saturday June 07, 2025 - 2:00 PM to 6:30 PM CST Ready to get involved? Sign up to volunteer today here or become a volunteer for Our Shared Journeys at https://dash.pointapp.org/organizations/3196. We appreciate your time and commitment to supporting women entrepreneurs. Together, we can create pathways to success! Questions? Contact us at volunteer@pathwayssummit.com. We look forward to welcoming you to the Pathways Summit volunteer team!

opportunity_title: Pathways Summit 2026 Event Planning Team
description_short: Help Us Build the Path to "Grow Beyond Limits" At Our Shared Journeys, we believe that when women entrepreneurs thrive, entire communities rise. But a summit this transformative doesn't just happen—it’s built by a dedicated team of visionaries and…
description_long: Help Us Build the Path to "Grow Beyond Limits" At Our Shared Journeys, we believe that when women entrepreneurs thrive, entire communities rise. But a summit this transformative doesn't just happen—it’s built by a dedicated team of visionaries and doers. We are looking for passionate individuals to join the Pathways Summit Planning Team . If you’re ready to lend your talents to empower women, build meaningful connections, and help others grow beyond their limits, we want you in the room. Let’s create something life-changing together. The Pathways Summit is a multi-day event that helps women entrepreneurs with limited resources start, grow, and expand their businesses. It's a supportive place where they can connect with mentors, investors, and other experts. Through workshops and networking, attendees learn practical skills and gain the confidence to succeed. Time Commitment The time commitment is flexible as some meetings may be during business hours and others will happen after hours, depending on the volunteer teams' availability. Weekly commitment is approximately 2-3 hours. Volunteer Opportunities We have a variety of volunteer roles available, including: Website & App Team: Digital Presence Team (Website & App Development & Management) Sales & Marketing Team: Sales & Marketing (Promoting the Event & Generating Revenue) Operations Team: Event Operations (Overall Event Management & Logistics) Vendor Team: Event Vendors (Vendor Management) Volunteer Team: Event Volunteers (Volunteer Coordination): Experience Team: Event Experience (Attendee Engagement & Experience Design): Speaker & Partnership Team: Speakers & Partners (Content & Speaker Management): Production Team: Event Productions (Technical Production & Stage Management): Ready to get involved? Sign up to volunteer today here or become a volunteer for Our Shared Journeys at https://dash.pointapp.org/events/564729 We appreciate your time and commitment to supporting women entrepreneurs. Together, we can create pathways to success! Questions? Contact us at volunteer@pathwayssummit.com. We look forward to welcoming you to the Pathways Summit volunteer team!

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Patient Care Services
description_short: Mission statement: At Enhabit Health and Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages…
description_long: Mission statement: At Enhabit Health and Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description: Enhabit Health and Hospice is looking for VOLUNTEERS to provide CAREGIVER RESPITE AND COMPANIONSHIP TO PATIENTS during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. For more information, please contact: Faith Oates Volunteer Coordinator - Bereavement Care 214.383.7443 . 940334.0970 faith.oates@ehab.com Hospice Community Health & Medicine Seniors Veterans & Military Families
