opportunity_title: Patient Care Volunteers Needed in the Arlington Area (76002, 76011 and 76016)
description_short: Zip codes: 76002, 76011 and 76016 As a Hospice Patient Care Volunteer, you will play a vital role in providing comfort, companionship, and support to individuals nearing the end of their life journey. You will work closely with the hospice care team…
description_long: Zip codes: 76002, 76011 and 76016 As a Hospice Patient Care Volunteer, you will play a vital role in providing comfort, companionship, and support to individuals nearing the end of their life journey. You will work closely with the hospice care team to ensure patients and their families receive compassionate care and assistance during this challenging time. Your presence and dedication will help improve the quality of life for our hospice patients and their loved ones. Patient volunteers are needed to provide companionship, assist with light household chores, read books, listen to life stories, work on puzzles, play music, play games and/or assist in special projects. Tasks may include: Light housework Pet care Shopping Entertainment Activities Any other hobbies/interests that applicant may have. Key Responsibilities: Patient Companionship: Spend quality time with hospice patients, engaging in conversations, listening to their stories, and providing emotional support. Respite Care: Offer caregivers a much-needed break by staying with the patient, allowing them time for self-care or errands. Assistance with Activities of Daily Living (ADLs): Assist patients with tasks such as meal preparation, light housekeeping, and personal grooming, depending on their needs and preferences. Emotional Support: Be a source of comfort and empathy, offering a listening ear and a caring presence to patients and their families. Reading and Entertainment: Read to patients, play music, or engage in activities that bring joy and mental stimulation to patients. Record Keeping: Maintain accurate and confidential records of your visits and interactions with patients and their families. Collaboration: Communicate regularly with the Hospice Volunteer Coordinator and the hospice care team to stay informed about patient needs and concerns. Training and Education: Attend orientation and ongoing training sessions to enhance your understanding of hospice care, end-of-life issues, and volunteer responsibilities. Adhere to Policies and Procedures: Follow the hospice organization's policies, procedures, and code of ethics. Requirements: Minimum age: 16 In-office interview In-office orientation/training Valid driver's license/ID Car Insurance Qualifications: A commitment to the mission and values of the hospice organization. Strong organizational and communication skills. Attention to detail and the ability to handle confidential information. Proficiency in basic computer skills, including word processing and data entry. Friendly and compassionate demeanor. Reliable and punctual. Time Commitment: No experience required & flexible schedules ! You decide who you want to volunteer with and the hours you're available. We are very flexible. Volunteers are expected to commit to a minimum of 1 hour per week. This opportunity will help develop skills such as: Compassion Understanding patient care Understanding how a hospice business operates. Working in an office setting Verbal and written communication Basic computer skills Benefits: Volunteer retention Gain knowledge of healthcare and how a hospice is run. Attain credit hours (if required by your instructors and/or institution) Work closely with patients, nurses, social workers, hospice staff, etc. The opportunity to make a meaningful impact in the lives of hospice patients and their families. Gain valuable experience in end-of-life care. Ongoing training and support to enhance your skills and knowledge. A supportive and compassionate team environment. If you or someone you know might be interested, please contact Divinity Hospice at 817-438-7463 or tazi@divinityhospice.net

opportunity_title: Patient Care Volunteers Needed in the Arlington Area (76002, 76011 and 76016)
description_short: Zip codes: 76002, 76011 and 76016 As a Hospice Patient Care Volunteer, you will play a vital role in providing comfort, companionship, and support to individuals nearing the end of their life journey. You will work closely with the hospice care team…
description_long: Zip codes: 76002, 76011 and 76016 As a Hospice Patient Care Volunteer, you will play a vital role in providing comfort, companionship, and support to individuals nearing the end of their life journey. You will work closely with the hospice care team to ensure patients and their families receive compassionate care and assistance during this challenging time. Your presence and dedication will help improve the quality of life for our hospice patients and their loved ones. Patient volunteers are needed to provide companionship, assist with light household chores, read books, listen to life stories, work on puzzles, play music, play games and/or assist in special projects. Tasks may include: Light housework Pet care Shopping Entertainment Activities Any other hobbies/interests that applicant may have. Key Responsibilities: Patient Companionship: Spend quality time with hospice patients, engaging in conversations, listening to their stories, and providing emotional support. Respite Care: Offer caregivers a much-needed break by staying with the patient, allowing them time for self-care or errands. Assistance with Activities of Daily Living (ADLs): Assist patients with tasks such as meal preparation, light housekeeping, and personal grooming, depending on their needs and preferences. Emotional Support: Be a source of comfort and empathy, offering a listening ear and a caring presence to patients and their families. Reading and Entertainment: Read to patients, play music, or engage in activities that bring joy and mental stimulation to patients. Record Keeping: Maintain accurate and confidential records of your visits and interactions with patients and their families. Collaboration: Communicate regularly with the Hospice Volunteer Coordinator and the hospice care team to stay informed about patient needs and concerns. Training and Education: Attend orientation and ongoing training sessions to enhance your understanding of hospice care, end-of-life issues, and volunteer responsibilities. Adhere to Policies and Procedures: Follow the hospice organization's policies, procedures, and code of ethics. Requirements: Minimum age: 16 In-office interview In-office orientation/training Valid driver's license/ID Car Insurance Qualifications: A commitment to the mission and values of the hospice organization. Strong organizational and communication skills. Attention to detail and the ability to handle confidential information. Proficiency in basic computer skills, including word processing and data entry. Friendly and compassionate demeanor. Reliable and punctual. Time Commitment: No experience required & flexible schedules ! You decide who you want to volunteer with and the hours you're available. We are very flexible. Volunteers are expected to commit to a minimum of 1 hour per week. This opportunity will help develop skills such as: Compassion Understanding patient care Understanding how a hospice business operates. Working in an office setting Verbal and written communication Basic computer skills Benefits: Volunteer retention Gain knowledge of healthcare and how a hospice is run. Attain credit hours (if required by your instructors and/or institution) Work closely with patients, nurses, social workers, hospice staff, etc. The opportunity to make a meaningful impact in the lives of hospice patients and their families. Gain valuable experience in end-of-life care. Ongoing training and support to enhance your skills and knowledge. A supportive and compassionate team environment. If you or someone you know might be interested, please contact Divinity Hospice at 817-438-7463 or tazi@divinityhospice.net

opportunity_title: Patient Care and Administrative
description_short: Our Richardson office is looking for volunteers for administrative office volunteers and for patient care volunteers to visit with patients in home and facilities. Minimum age for administrative volunteers is 16 and for patient care volunteers is 18.
description_long: Our Richardson office is looking for volunteers for administrative office volunteers and for patient care volunteers to visit with patients in home and facilities. Minimum age for administrative volunteers is 16 and for patient care volunteers is 18.

opportunity_title: Patient Companionship Volunteer - Veteran's needed!
description_short: Patient companionship volunteers needed to share their compassion with hospice patients a 1-2 hours a week, allowing caregivers time to run errands or get much needed rest. Give Vet to Vet companionship visits for our beloved Veteran patients.…
description_long: Patient companionship volunteers needed to share their compassion with hospice patients a 1-2 hours a week, allowing caregivers time to run errands or get much needed rest. Give Vet to Vet companionship visits for our beloved Veteran patients. Training classes will provide you with the knowledge and confidence to provide this much appreciated gift of your time. Please call Cheri Spurrier, Volunteer Coordinator at Community Hospice of Texas at 682-540-2196 or email at cspurrier@chot.org www.chot.org Community Hospice of Texas is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization and does not discriminate against any person on the basis of race, color, national origin, disability, or age in admission, treatment, or participation in programs, services and activities, or in employment.

org_name: Enhabit Home Health  & Hospice DFW metroplex
opportunity_title: Patient Companionship Volunteers and Caregiver Relief Volunteers
description_short: Mission statement: At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life.…
description_long: Mission statement: At Enhabit Hospice, we believe hospice is not a place, but rather a philosophy of care. Our goal is to bring dignity and comfort home by offering compassionate care to patients and their families during the final stages of life. Description: Enhabit Hospice is looking for VOLUNTEERS to provide CAREGIVER RESPITE AND COMPANIONSHIP TO PATIENTS during their final months and weeks of life. Volunteers provide non-medical services and respite time for caregivers and family members. As a hospice volunteer, you will be given choices as to how much and what types of things you want to do. A few ways you can help as a hospice volunteer is by running errands for patients and families, playing games or reading with patients, caregiver respite, walking and caring for family pets, or even creating a photo album of the patient and their family. For more information, please contact: Faith Oates - Volunteer Coordinator - Bereavement Services 469.677.3100 Faith.oates@ehab.com Hospice Community Health & Medicine Seniors Veterans & Military Families

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice of Texas
opportunity_title: Patient Support Allen
description_short: Bring your positive attitude and concern for others to those facing end of life challenges. Brighten their day with regular visits, listen to their stories, read, assist with life review, play games, watch sports, assist them with activities in the…
description_long: Bring your positive attitude and concern for others to those facing end of life challenges. Brighten their day with regular visits, listen to their stories, read, assist with life review, play games, watch sports, assist them with activities in the building, assist the family. Work directly with patients and families in your own community as a committed listener providing companionship and support, translation, light housekeeping and/or relief for the caregiver. This is provided anytime you are available and the patient/family are agreeable. Could be weekdays, evenings, weekends, etc. Usually, volunteers see patients once a week or every other week.

opportunity_title: Patient Visits & Activities: Volunteer with Lifted Hospice | Flower Mound/Lewisville
description_short: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate…
description_long: Opportunity Description: Although our headquarters is in Plano, we serve the majority of DFW, and you can volunteer near where you live. Are you looking for a way to make a meaningful impact in your community? Lifted Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers to visit elderly patients, provide companionship, and offer encouragement. Our volunteers sit and visit with patients, one on one activities, or group activities at the Memory Care/Assisted Living facilities. Your time can make a profound difference in the lives of those nearing the end of their journey. Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. Options of activities to do with patient: playing games/cards, listening to music (or singing/playing instruments), Veteran services, arts & crafts, reading to patient, etc... Group activities at facility: arts & crafts, games, music Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life.

Whether it’s through a heartfelt conversation, sharing a laugh, or simply sitting together, your presence brings comfort, hope, and dignity to someone in need. What You’ll Do: Spend quality time with elderly patients in their homes or care facilities. Offer companionship, encouragement, and emotional support. Options of activities to do with patient: playing games/cards, listening to music (or singing/playing instruments), Veteran services, arts & crafts, reading to patient, etc... Group activities at facility: arts & crafts, games, music Brighten their day by being a kind and caring presence. What We’re Looking For: Compassionate individuals with a heart for serving others. Good listeners who can provide support and comfort. A commitment of a few hours per week or as your schedule allows. No medical experience is required-just a willingness to share your time and kindness. Comprehensive training and support are provided. Why Volunteer with Lifted Hospice? Make a lasting impact in your community. Build meaningful connections with patients and families. Experience the joy of knowing you’ve brought comfort and care to someone’s life.

opportunity_title: Peer Support Group Facilitator
description_short: The Peer Support Group Facilitator is responsible for leading structured, trauma-informed peer support groups that promote emotional wellness, personal accountability, and community connection. This role creates a safe, respectful, and…
description_long: The Peer Support Group Facilitator is responsible for leading structured, trauma-informed peer support groups that promote emotional wellness, personal accountability, and community connection. This role creates a safe, respectful, and non-judgmental environment where participants can share lived experiences, build coping skills, and support one another in their recovery and personal development journeys. The facilitator serves as both a guide and a peer ally, modeling healthy communication, emotional regulation, and problem-solving while maintaining professional boundaries.

opportunity_title: Peer Support Group Facilitator
description_short: TWCUYC is seeking compassionate volunteers to facilitate weekly peer support groups for individuals working toward stability, recovery, and personal growth. Groups may be held virtually or in person. What You’ll Do Facilitate one peer support group…
description_long: TWCUYC is seeking compassionate volunteers to facilitate weekly peer support groups for individuals working toward stability, recovery, and personal growth. Groups may be held virtually or in person. What You’ll Do Facilitate one peer support group per week Create a welcoming and supportive environment Encourage participant engagement and discussion Share resources and coping strategies when appropriate Track attendance and participation Coordinate with TWCUYC leadership regarding group needs What We’re Looking For Strong listening and communication skills Ability to facilitate discussions in a respectful and supportive manner Lived experience, peer support experience, recovery coaching, mentoring, or group facilitation experience is highly valued Certification as an RSPS, MHPS, CPRS, CPS, Peer Recovery Coach, or similar peer support credential is preferred. Candidates without certification must have documented experience facilitating support groups, recovery groups, or peer-led programs. Time Commitment At least one group session per week, plus preparation time.

opportunity_title: Peer to Peer Grief Support Volunteer - 1 Year Commitment
description_short: Hello! We are looking for volunteers in the community for our Peer-to-Peer program to provide ongoing grief support to our newly bereaved families. Our volunteers, or Villagers as we call them, are not licensed counselors. They are individuals who…
description_long: Hello! We are looking for volunteers in the community for our Peer-to-Peer program to provide ongoing grief support to our newly bereaved families. Our volunteers, or Villagers as we call them, are not licensed counselors. They are individuals who have experienced a personal loss of a loved one and have been able to find adjustment to their grief. They provide a sense of comfort, reassurance and the opportunity for newly bereaved individuals to talk and learn about their grief. They act as supporters and meet regularly with their peers to be an additional layer of care. You can apply here right now: https://carsonsvillage.org/get-involved/become-a-villager/ Our Villagers must participate in 20 hours of training and bi-monthly meetings through Carson’s Village. We match our Villagers with a Peer with intention, hoping to help form meaningful and supportive connections. We ask our Villagers to commit to 1 year of supporting their peer, through phone calls, facetime, or even in-person meet ups for coffee. Carson’s Villagers play a special role in serving our communities, where grief support is not often found. Many walk this journey alone. Becoming a Villager is a commitment, and we believe that the time and energy that is put into becoming a Villager is returned in the meaning that is found from the peer connection. If you are interested in supporting another individual on their grief journey, please check out the requirements below. Requirements Desire to assist those who are grieving from a recent loss with empathy and compassion Ability to communicate well, both over the phone and/or virtually Successful adjustment to a personal loss, with no personal loss within the past 24 months Ability to successfully complete our 20 hours of initial training (virtual) and self-study Be at least 21 years of age and able to pass a background check Review quarterly virtual support trainings and maintain 2-3+ check-ins with assigned peer per month Bi-monthly check-ins with Carson’s Village staff (virtual or phone) Interview and completed application, including agreement for a Criminal Background Check

opportunity_title: Perfect for UT Southwestern & College Students: Make Human Connection Matter
description_short: Looking for hands-on experience that actually means something? Hospice volunteering offers a rare opportunity to support older adults at one of the most meaningful stages of life—through simple, human connection. What You’ll Do As a hospice…
description_long: Looking for hands-on experience that actually means something? Hospice volunteering offers a rare opportunity to support older adults at one of the most meaningful stages of life—through simple, human connection. What You’ll Do As a hospice volunteer, you’ll provide companionship and emotional support to elderly patients by: Visiting and talking with patients Listening to life stories and sharing conversation Reading, playing games, or sitting quietly together Offering comfort and presence to patients and families No medical tasks—just being human. Why This Is Ideal for Students ✔ Meaningful experience for healthcare, medical, nursing, social work, psychology, and pre-health tracks ✔ Builds empathy, communication skills, and emotional intelligence ✔ Flexible scheduling that works around classes ✔ Volunteer hours for resumes, grad school, and med school applications ✔ Eye-opening exposure to end-of-life care and geriatric populations What We’re Looking For Compassionate, dependable college students Strong listeners who enjoy connecting with others No prior hospice or medical experience required Training and ongoing support provided Why Hospice Volunteering Is Different You won’t just “log hours.” You’ll make someone feel seen, heard, and valued —sometimes becoming the highlight of their week. “This experience changed how I see patients, aging, and myself.” – Former Student Volunteer Ready to Make a Real Impact? If you’re seeking meaningful experience that aligns with your future career—and your values—we’d love to hear from you. 📩 Apply today through Idealist 📍 Serving the Dallas area (flexible placements)

opportunity_title: Pet Adoption Event Volunteers Needed!
description_short: The Seagoville Texas Animal Advocates and Rescue (STAAR) is partnered with the Seagoville Animal Shelter to host adoption events at locations in Seagoville, Mesquite, Terrell and Forney. STAAR is in need of volunteer adoption counsellors to help out…
description_long: The Seagoville Texas Animal Advocates and Rescue (STAAR) is partnered with the Seagoville Animal Shelter to host adoption events at locations in Seagoville, Mesquite, Terrell and Forney. STAAR is in need of volunteer adoption counsellors to help out on Saturdays at these off-site locations! We also need help with transporting animals to and from the events. Make a difference in the life of homeless pets by volunteering to help them find forever homes! One day per month makes a huge difference to these wonderful animals.

opportunity_title: Pet Photography & Marketing
description_short: We are currently seeking volunteers to assist with photographing and filming our shelter dogs and cats. Updated visuals significantly contribute to these animals finding loving homes more quickly! Volunteers must be at least 12 years old to…
description_long: We are currently seeking volunteers to assist with photographing and filming our shelter dogs and cats. Updated visuals significantly contribute to these animals finding loving homes more quickly! Volunteers must be at least 12 years old to participate. Those under 15 years old must be accompanied by a parent or guardian who is at least 18 years old. To begin, you'll need to register for a volunteer account and fulfill a few initial requirements. There is a $15 volunteer fee per person to help cover the initial expenses associated with onboarding new volunteers. Once these steps are completed, you'll be eligible to enroll in an Orientation session, during which you'll receive further training and details.

opportunity_title: Pet Therapy Volunteers Needed!
description_short: Bring comfort, joy, and unconditional love to patients and families through the healing presence of your certified therapy animal. Hospice patients often find peace and happiness in the companionship of animals, and your visits can brighten their…
description_long: Bring comfort, joy, and unconditional love to patients and families through the healing presence of your certified therapy animal. Hospice patients often find peace and happiness in the companionship of animals, and your visits can brighten their day in a truly meaningful way. What You'll Do: Visit patients in their homes or facilities with your therapy animal Provide comfort, companionship, and emotional support Create moments of joy and connection through animal interaction Support families by offering a calming and uplifting presence What We're Looking For: Volunteers with a certified therapy dog (or other approved therapy animal) Compassionate, dependable individuals with good communication skills Willingness to complete hospice volunteer training and background screening Comfort with visiting patients in various care settings Time Commitment: Flexible with your schedule! Why Volunteer with Us: Share the unique bond of your therapy animal to bring joy and comfort Be part of a caring and supportive hospice team Make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and families Interested? Please contact our Regional Volunteer Coordinator, Mackensie Moore, to learn more on how you and your furry friend can join our hospice volunteer team. Email: mackensie.moore@bristolhospice.com

opportunity_title: PetSmart Cat Adoption Ambassadors
description_short: When the Friends of Rescue Animals foster cats and kittens are ready to be adopted, we place them at one of three PetSmart Cat Adoption Centers in Rockwall, Rowlett or Wylie Texas. We need daily assistance with cleaning, feeding, and playing with…
description_long: When the Friends of Rescue Animals foster cats and kittens are ready to be adopted, we place them at one of three PetSmart Cat Adoption Centers in Rockwall, Rowlett or Wylie Texas. We need daily assistance with cleaning, feeding, and playing with the animals. In addition, cats get adopted and go home faster when a volunteer is there to show them to potential adopters. A Cat Adoption Ambassador commits to being at the store for 2 hours to clean and socialize with the cats while helping potential adopters.

opportunity_title: Petsmart Cat Program - City Place
description_short: We deeply appreciate your interest in volunteering at one of our PetSmart locations. Volunteers play a crucial role in our efforts to care for, socialize, and facilitate adoptions for our cats. Here are the key details about volunteering: Age…
description_long: We deeply appreciate your interest in volunteering at one of our PetSmart locations. Volunteers play a crucial role in our efforts to care for, socialize, and facilitate adoptions for our cats. Here are the key details about volunteering: Age Requirements: Volunteers must be 18 years or older to volunteer independently. For individuals aged 12-17, volunteering is permitted with a parent or guardian who is at least 18 years old. Time Commitment: This opportunity involves a regular weekly shift lasting 2 hours and requires a long-term commitment of at least 2 months. Adoption Volunteer Shifts : Volunteers assist in finding forever homes for cats during these shifts, which are available at various times throughout each day. Cleaning Volunteer Shifts: Volunteers assist with cleaning kennels, providing enrichment, and socializing with the cats. Shifts are available Mondays through Saturdays, with AM shifts from 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM and PM shifts from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM. Sunday shifts vary due to store opening and closing times. Our locations include: City Place (75204) Greenville Ave. (75206) Lakewood (75214) Denton (76205) Whether you can volunteer once a week or more frequently, the choice is entirely yours and depends on your availability and commitment. Your dedication is invaluable to us!

opportunity_title: Photography Volunteer
description_short: As a Photography Volunteer, your role is crucial in capturing the essence of Soldiers' Angels events. You'll be working closely with our staff to photograph special occasions such as our Veteran Food Distribution, volunteer fairs, holiday events,…
description_long: As a Photography Volunteer, your role is crucial in capturing the essence of Soldiers' Angels events. You'll be working closely with our staff to photograph special occasions such as our Veteran Food Distribution, volunteer fairs, holiday events, fundraisers, and more. Depending on our calendar of events, the volunteer work may vary, but regardless of how frequently you help out, your contributions are invaluable to our cause. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES to include but not limited to: Be able to attend events as needed with notification ranging from well in advance or short term. Assist Soldiers’ Angels staff and Key Role volunteers at events by being on-hand to capture photos and videos Take clear, engaging, and useful photographs. Learn and demonstrate best practices. Ensure photo release forms are filled out and turned into Key Role volunteers at events. Immediately following events, upload photos and notify the Marketing Team that the photos have been uploaded. In some instances, basic photo editing or enhancing may be needed or requested. If you haven't set up your volunteer profile in the Soldiers' Angels Volunteer Database yet, please do so by clicking on the following link: https://soldiersangels.my.site.com/s/login/ Watch our video to learn more: Soldiers' Angels Orlando MVFD: Behind the Bag

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Pitch in on lawn care for a neighbor in Arlington, TX · Ref IWTMYL-NOV
description_short: A neighbor in Arlington, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A neighbor in Arlington, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and welcoming. Your kindness makes a real difference in a neighbor's life. This is a home shared by a household of two, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Arlington, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-NOV&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-NOV

A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-LCU&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-LCU

description_short: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily…
description_long: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily responsibilities and stress. Making a difference as a hospice volunteer begins with the desire to help the dying and their families. Hospice volunteers also provide a number of services such as bereavement support and help in the office. 16 yrs and up for Administrative Volunteer and 18 yrs and Older for Patient Support Volunteer. Please provide your phone number for contact to you. Call with any questions or email me at jwilliams@hospiceplus.net. Jennifer Williams Volunteer Coordinator 972-239-0907

opportunity_title: Plano Area Companion Volunteer - Silverado Hospice
description_short: Silverado Hospice is seeking compassionate Companion Volunteers to provide comfort and connection to patients during their hospice journey. Volunteers spend time with patients through conversation, reading, music, life review, or simply offering a…
description_long: Silverado Hospice is seeking compassionate Companion Volunteers to provide comfort and connection to patients during their hospice journey. Volunteers spend time with patients through conversation, reading, music, life review, or simply offering a peaceful presence. Your time and kindness can make a profound difference for individuals and their families. Responsibilities: Visit patients in their homes or care communities Provide companionship and emotional support Engage in simple activities such as talking, listening, reading, or playing music Maintain professional boundaries and uphold patient confidentiality Complete orientation and required training (provided at no cost) What We Offer: Flexible volunteer scheduling Comprehensive training and ongoing support Opportunities to make a meaningful impact A caring and supportive volunteer community Ideal Candidate: Kind, reliable, and empathetic Good listener with a calming presence Comfortable working with individuals at end of life Able to commit to regular visits after training

opportunity_title: Plano, TX: Host an Exchange Student-Volunteer families needed Plano area!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

This is a flexible schedule and can be as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Feel free to contact Tonya Choate at 682-777-0722 or send an email to tchoate@ThreeOaksHospice.com to learn more.

opportunity_title: Post Admission Patient Follow-Up Volunteer
description_short: Hope Health Care is seeking individuals in the Dallas area who can volunteer to contact our newly admitted home hospice patients, to ensure all needs have been met at admission. These phone call must be made in accordance with our timeframe,…
description_long: Hope Health Care is seeking individuals in the Dallas area who can volunteer to contact our newly admitted home hospice patients, to ensure all needs have been met at admission. These phone call must be made in accordance with our timeframe, therefore volunteers must reside in Texas or Central time zone. Volunteers can make these calls remotely or at our Garland or Greenville office. Thorough training is provided for this position in our Garland office.

opportunity_title: Product Owner
description_short: Job Title: Product Owner Reports to: CEO Location: Remote (WFH) / Unpaid Volunteer Position About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation (Menzies Mission) is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting orphans and vulnerable families through…
description_long: Job Title: Product Owner Reports to: CEO Location: Remote (WFH) / Unpaid Volunteer Position About the Organization: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation (Menzies Mission) is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting orphans and vulnerable families through resources, education, and holistic development rooted in the Menzies 7 Keys of Needs (Housing, Health & Nutrition, Education, Safety, Family, Faith, and Love). Believing that self-actualization can break cycles of poverty and trauma, we provide mental and emotional support, healing-centered guidance, and programs that help individuals realize their potential and transform their future. In partnership with our social-impact company, Me Is We , we are developing a regenerative financial ecosystem to ensure long-term sustainability and expansion for our mission. At the heart of this ecosystem is My Source Network —a global holistic wellness community platform for healing and mindful connection. Through practitioners, conscious events, and transformational resources, MSN supports individuals on their journeys toward balance, empowerment, and belonging, cultivating a worldwide community rooted in healing, authenticity, and collaboration. We are an impact-driven organization that values innovation, accountability, and collaboration. If you are passionate about meaningful work and creating scalable positive change, we would be honored to have you join our team. Job Overview: The Product Owner at the project My Source Network will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our digital platform. You will be responsible for defining and driving the product vision, managing the product backlog, and ensuring that the development team delivers features and enhancements that align with the organization’s mission. Working closely with the CEO, stakeholders, and development teams, you will ensure the successful delivery of digital solutions that facilitate healing and growth for users across the platform. Key Responsibilities: Product Vision & Strategy : Define and communicate a product vision that supports the organization’s mission of holistic healing and eco-regenerative communities. Ensure alignment with the needs of our users and stakeholders, particularly within the non-profit sector. Backlog Management : Prioritize and maintain the product backlog, ensuring that user stories, epics, and acceptance criteria are clearly defined and aligned with the organization's goals. Agile Leadership : Collaborate with the development team in an Agile environment. Participate in sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives to guide product development and ensure alignment with strategic priorities. Stakeholder Collaboration : Act as the primary point of contact for internal and external stakeholders, including practitioners, users, and non-profit partners. Communicate progress, gather feedback, and align product development with user needs and organizational objectives. Release Planning & Delivery : Plan and manage product releases to ensure the timely delivery of new features and improvements. Coordinate with the marketing and outreach teams to successfully launch and communicate new capabilities to the community. Customer & Market Feedback : Regularly gather and analyze user feedback, market trends, and data to continually refine and improve the product. Focus on enhancing user experiences related to holistic healing and wellness journeys. Risk Management : Identify and mitigate risks associated with product development and delivery, balancing the need for innovation with organizational stability and sustainability. Skills and Qualifications: Education : Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business, or a related field. Experience : 3-5+ years of experience as a Product Owner in a digital or software development environment, preferably with experience in non-profit, wellness, or community-focused platforms. Proven experience working within an Agile framework (Scrum or Kanban). Technical Skills : Strong understanding of software development processes and the ability to work effectively with technical teams. Experience with backlog management tools (e.g., Jira, Trello, etc.). Communication & Leadership : Exceptional communication and leadership skills, with the ability to inspire and guide cross-functional teams. Ability to effectively communicate technical and non-technical concepts to stakeholders at all levels. Customer Focus & Empathy : Deep understanding of customer needs, particularly in the fields of holistic healing, wellness, and trauma-informed care. Ability to translate these needs into actionable product requirements. Analytical & Strategic Thinking : Strong problem-solving abilities, data-driven decision-making, and the ability to navigate complex stakeholder environments. Experience with gathering and analyzing user data to drive product enhancements. Application Process To apply, review the job description and submit your resume, portfolio, and cover letter to hr@menziesmission.org. Ensure accurate contact information for application status updates. If selected, be prepared to discuss qualifications and alignment with the organization's mission. For questions, contact hr@menziesmission.org. We look forward to reviewing your application. Equal Opportunity Employer: Menzies Philanthropic Foundation is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Accommodation for the application process is available upon request. Google Meet . Please note that the session will be recorded to support our evaluation and documentation process.*

opportunity_title: Professional Hospice Volunteers
description_short: Hospice volunteers make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and caregivers. Our direct patient volunteers work in the home or at a senior living facility with terminally ill patients and their families throughout Dallas, Collin County and…
description_long: Hospice volunteers make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and caregivers. Our direct patient volunteers work in the home or at a senior living facility with terminally ill patients and their families throughout Dallas, Collin County and surrounding areas. Professional Volunteers (must have valid license or certification): Hair stylist; pet therapy; aroma therapy; massage therapy; interpreters, etc. Other Opportunities: Patient companionship : Talking, playing games, reading, engaging in hobbies. Caregiver respite : Sit with a patient for a set period giving a caregiver the opportunity for selfcare and complete needed activities. Other duties may include light household chores, shopping, transportation. Professional Volunteers (must have valid License or certifications): Hair stylist; pet therapy; aroma therapy; massage therapy; interpreters, etc. Administrative assistance: Assist with special projects, general office duties and patient outreach.

opportunity_title: Project Manager Internship OR Team Member
description_short: We are seeking a motivated individual that is searching for an internship that focuses on leadership and project management skills. This position is also open to non-students that want to become a CBATES Team Member. This position can be done…
description_long: We are seeking a motivated individual that is searching for an internship that focuses on leadership and project management skills. This position is also open to non-students that want to become a CBATES Team Member. This position can be done remotely, however, you MUST live in the United States to be considered . Our volunteer program consists of a few teams that are still in the process of being formed. We are actively recruiting volunteers for these teams. As a project management Intern/Team Member, you will have the chance to learn how to manage and supervise multiple teams, network with notable professionals from bigger companies, and more. You will also, be responsible in organizing details for programming and specail events, scheduling and detailing logistics. So, if you are looking for an experience to showcase leadership skills on your resume, we encourage you to apply! Some typical responsibilities for this position will consist of: Organize details for special events an programming Scheduling and Detailing Logistics Moderating volunteer group chats Managing volunteer teams Assisting volunteers with their questions and requests Helping recruit and interview volunteers Networking with various companies and high level professionals to create beneficial relationships for the organization and volunteer program Some things we prefer, but not require, in applicants are: Basic knowledge in software such as Google docs and Google excel Preferably majoring in business administration (or any other relevant business major) Flexible availability You MUST live in the United States to apply . If you are interested in the position, please apply for consideration. Thank you!

org_name: American Red Cross - North Texas Region
opportunity_title: Provide Assistance During Disasters: Shelter Team Member
description_short: PLACEMENT PURPOSE Volunteers in this role provide a variety of basic services to Red Cross clients in a shelter. OPEN TIME DAYS Varies. 8-hour shifts during Disaster Relief Operations As a trained Mass Care volunteer you will be added to our active…
description_long: PLACEMENT PURPOSE Volunteers in this role provide a variety of basic services to Red Cross clients in a shelter. OPEN TIME DAYS Varies. 8-hour shifts during Disaster Relief Operations As a trained Mass Care volunteer you will be added to our active roster of personnel. And whether we have 20 shelters open or just one, you might be called upon to volunteer! Typically there are three, 8-hour shifts to choose from each day: 7am - 3:30pm 3pm - 11:30pm 11pm - 7:30am Interested in traveling to work on a Disaster Relief Operation? State-wide or National deployments of 2-3 weeks are available by signing up with our Disaster Services Human Resources (DSHR) program. REQUIRED TRAINING Online Orientation Disater Services: An Overview (DSO) Shelter Fundamentals Basic Food Safety ｠

description_short: As Course Marshalls at Fair Park, you will play a vital role in the event's success by directing participants along the 1-mile and 2.5-mile routes, managing water stations, and providing encouragement to our walkers. Training provided Please note…

opportunity_title: Recovery Rides-Transport Driver (HYBRID)
description_short: Recovery Rides - Transport Driver Hybrid Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability and provide a healthy and…
description_long: Recovery Rides - Transport Driver Hybrid Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability and provide a healthy and loving environment for their pet. Dogs Matter is the only non-profit agency in Texas offering free temporary foster care placement and aftercare support services specifically for pets of people with addictions receiving substance abuse treatment. Dogs Matter has become a department of Dallas Pets Alive (DPA). With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to Dogs Matter, you directly impact our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dogs Matter visit https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ Are you ready to hit the road and be the hero for pet families in need? As our Transport Driver, you'll be the essential link that gets pets to their temporary homes while their owner prepares for their own recovery journey. You’ll ensure a safe and comfortable ride from one stop to the next - whether it’s a short drive or a long haul. While transporting is the main goal, it’s also about being the face of our rescue on the move. A love for animals is a must, but photography skills are a huge plus! If you’ve got a clean driving record, a heart for rescue and recovery, and maybe even a flair for pet photography, we’d love to have you join our pack! Team: Dogs Matter - Transport Location: Hybrid, located in Dallas-Forth Worth (DFW) and able to drive between Austin, San Antonio, and Houston. Time Commitment: Hours vary. Up to 15 hours a week. A flexible schedule is most important. Transports are needed any day of the week, including weekends. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position. However, we are willing to reimburse for gas/mileage. Local transports pay up to $35. Longer drives TBD, depending upon time, gas, and number of pets. Reports to: Dogs Matter Director of Operations and Executive Director Works with: Owners, temporary foster parents, Recovery Coaches, Foster Coordinator, Director of Operations, and the Executive Director. Primary Responsibilities: Coordinate with the team to ensure schedules run smoothly and dogs arrive on time Transport dogs to their temporary foster home, prioritizing their safety and comfort Capture those unforgettable moments on the road, sharing photos to keep everyone’s spirits high Qualifications: A valid Texas driver’s license Current vehicle insurance A clean driving record Reliable and safe transportation Must complete DPA’s handler training Animals must be transported in a crate or carrier (most pets come with a crate, or can be borrowed from Dogs Matter if needed) Skill in handling delicate situations and sensitive information with professionalism, empathy, and care. Show compassion to owners who are temporarily parting with their dogs and struggling with addiction. Organized, reliable, and prompt Photography skills are a plus! Flexibility and a sense of adventure - every day is a new road trip! A team and service-oriented mentality Embrace Dogs Matter’s mission and values Regular access to and ability to access a computer or mobile device Fluency in English required; bilingual with fluency in Spanish is a major bonus! Must be at least 18 years of age What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values. Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org. Watch our video to learn more: Dallas Pets Alive!

opportunity_title: Red Oak, TX Hospice Volunteer
description_short: Patient visits in Senior Living, Nursing home, or private home. Become a friend and talk about life review, discuss hobbies & interests, current events, or read to patient, accompany patient outside if appropriate, companionship &…
description_long: Patient visits in Senior Living, Nursing home, or private home. Become a friend and talk about life review, discuss hobbies & interests, current events, or read to patient, accompany patient outside if appropriate, companionship & socialization, monitor & report progress to hospice team. May sit with patient while caregiver runs errands, but no hands-on care. Dependability for patients and families. Compassionate, caring, good listening skills, able to sit quietly at times if needed. Interest in others & their life stories, own transportation, able to complete & send in documentation after each visit. Background check, drug screen, orientation, we cover costs associated to onboarding.

opportunity_title: Remembrance Bears Volunteer: Good Shepherd Hospice
description_short: Do you have a talent for sewing or crafting? Good Shepherd Hospice would love volunteers to sew/create Remembrance Bears for our patients! Remembrance Bears are created for the families dealing with a loss of a loved one. Our sewing volunteers use a…
description_long: Do you have a talent for sewing or crafting? Good Shepherd Hospice would love volunteers to sew/create Remembrance Bears for our patients! Remembrance Bears are created for the families dealing with a loss of a loved one. Our sewing volunteers use a special piece of clothing that belonged to the patient that can be made into a Remembrance Bear. It can be a shirt, dress, pants or bathrobe. This bear is a treasured keepsake for children, grandchildren, or other family members who would like a huggable reminder of the person that they love. It is such a wonderful gift to our patient's families! If you have a talent for sewing and want to give back, contact our Volunteer Coordinator and give the gift of comfort to a grieving family. 972-482-4382 or shauntae.harris@goodshepherdhospice.com

opportunity_title: Remote Administrative Hospice Office Volunteer
description_short: As a Hospice Administrative Volunteer, you will play a crucial role in supporting the administrative functions of our hospice organization. This role is an excellent opportunity for college students looking to gain real-world experience in a…
description_long: As a Hospice Administrative Volunteer, you will play a crucial role in supporting the administrative functions of our hospice organization. This role is an excellent opportunity for college students looking to gain real-world experience in a healthcare setting while contributing to a mission of compassion and care. You will work closely with our administrative team to help ensure the smooth operation of our hospice services. Tasks may include: Answering phones Filing Scanning Faxing Data entry Mailings Arts and craft projects Key Responsibilities: Phone and email communication: Handle incoming calls and emails, providing courteous and helpful responses, and directing inquiries to the appropriate staff members. Filing and organization: Maintain organized and easily accessible filing systems for administrative documents, ensuring efficiency in record retrieval. Administrative tasks: Perform general office tasks such as photocopying, scanning, faxing, and maintaining office supplies. Data entry and record keeping: Assist in maintaining and updating patient records, volunteer logs, and other essential documents accurately and confidentially. Assist with mailings: Prepare and send out mailings, including thank-you letters, newsletters, and informational materials. Assist with projects: Assist volunteer coordinator with special projects for our patients such as making cards, writing letters, putting gifts together, etc. Support staff meetings: Attend staff meetings and assist in preparing agendas, taking minutes, and distributing meeting materials. Event support: Assist in planning and organizing hospice events, including fundraisers, awareness campaigns, and volunteer appreciation events. Requirements: Minimum age: 16 In-office interview In-office orientation/training Valid driver's license/ID Qualifications: A commitment to the mission and values of the hospice organization. Strong organizational and communication skills. Attention to detail and the ability to handle confidential information. Proficiency in basic computer skills, including word processing and data entry. Friendly and compassionate demeanor. Reliable and punctual. Ability to work collaboratively in a team and take direction from staff members. Time Commitment: No experience required & flexible schedules ! You decide who you want to volunteer with and the hours you're available. We are very flexible. Volunteers are expected to commit to a minimum of 1 hour per week. This opportunity will help develop skills such as: Compassion Understanding patient care Understanding how a hospice business operates. Working in an office setting Verbal and written communication Basic computer skills Benefits: Volunteer retention Gain knowledge of healthcare and how a hospice is run. Attain credit hours (if required by your instructors and/or institution) Work closely with patients, nurses, social workers, hospice staff, etc. The opportunity to make a meaningful impact in the lives of hospice patients and their families. Gain valuable experience in end-of-life care. Ongoing training and support to enhance your skills and knowledge. A supportive and compassionate team environment. If you or someone you know might be interested, please contact Divinity Hospice at 817-438-7463 or tazi@divinityhospice.net.

opportunity_title: Representative, Adoption Operations (Adoptline)- Fridays
description_short: Representative, Adoption Operations (Adoptline) Remote Volunteer Position Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is…
description_long: Representative, Adoption Operations (Adoptline) Remote Volunteer Position Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As an Adoptline Representative, you'll manage our adoption mailbox to make sure our potential parents have all the information they need to comfortably pursue the pet of their dreams! Team: Adoption Location: Remote/Virtual: Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST time zone. Time Commitment: Approximately 2 hours per week. This position will have a specific day of the week for volunteering. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: Adoption Hotline Team Lead and Adoption Director Works with: Position involvescorrespondence with potential adoptive families, other adoption hotlines and adoption team members on a regular basis. Correspondence with foster families and foster team volunteers required on a semi-regular basis. Correspondence with other teams as needed. Position Responsibilities: Respond to all inquiries received regarding adoptable pets within 24 hours of receipt Forward applications to adoption counselors for review and approval Forward any other correspondence received to appropriate volunteer or team Log and update applications received and adoption application status Submit end-of-day report, logging items completed along with any pertinent remaining items to team prior to logging off Assist with any other duties needed to help drive DPA’s mission and support our values Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Ability to work virtually via email and phone at least 1 day per week on a consistent basis. Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values. Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org. Watch our video to learn more: Dallas Pets Alive!
