opportunity_title: Rescue Agent
description_short: *CURRENTLY ONLY ACCEPTING VOLUNTEERS FROM TEXAS, OKLAHOMA, LOUISIANA, MISSOURI, ARKANSAS, KANSAS, OR NEBRASKA Position Overview: A Rescue Agent with Gideon's Army serves as a trained volunteer, providing humanitarian assistance to survivors of human…
description_long: *CURRENTLY ONLY ACCEPTING VOLUNTEERS FROM TEXAS, OKLAHOMA, LOUISIANA, MISSOURI, ARKANSAS, KANSAS, OR NEBRASKA Position Overview: A Rescue Agent with Gideon's Army serves as a trained volunteer, providing humanitarian assistance to survivors of human trafficking through coordinated field work. Rescue Agents help to identify, connect with, support, and safely relocate ("rescue") survivors of exploitation, by transporting them to pre-determined aftercare services. This role involves building and executing safe, peaceful exit plans for survivors to help them covertly escape trafficking or exploitation. This is a hands-on, intervention-based role that involves detailed team coordination. This role operates within a strictly legal, non-confrontational framework and prioritizes survivor safety, dignity, and informed consent at all times. Where legally required or operationally necessary, Gideon's Army partners with law enforcement and licensed aftercare organizations to help facilitate the rescue process. This role centers on situational awareness, disciplined execution, and human connection. Operations are often low-visibility and require patience, swift adaptation, and precision. THIS ROLE IS NOT: A Hollywood action movie "Door-kicking" An ego boost THIS ROLE DOES CONSIST OF: Semi-long periods of maintaining operational readiness through self-lead training and study Sudden activation when missions pop up - short windows of time for travel and response to quickly help a survivor escape trafficking Environments that are unpredictable, emotionally charged, or unstable Key responsibilities: Maintain communication with the Gideon's Army team daily through Signal Be able and willing to travel on short notice Assist with safe, lawful transportation efforts Assist with exit planning and voluntary relocation efforts ("soft rescue") Assist law enforcement and/or other partner NGOs under the direction of Gideon's Army leadership in case building or joint-rescue operations ("hard rescue") Uphold strict confidentiality and operational integrity Support mission planning and execution Attend training on field medical care, rescue and relocation efforts, safe mission planning, trauma-informed care, and crisis stabilization Basic Qualifications: Strong emotional bearing and ability to remain calm and decisive under pressure Commitment to ethical and legal conduct at all times Ability to follow structured protocols and work in a fluid, team-based environment Strong communication and interpersonal skills Willingness to travel and complete required training Ability to pass a comprehensive, multi-step background check and vetting process Preferred (Not Required): Background in military, law enforcement, private security, or executive protection OR; Background in social work, psychology/therapy, victim advocacy OR; Background in medical or mental health services OR; Prior experience in anti-human trafficking efforts, such as outreach or aftercare OR; Prior experience working with vulnerable populations such as children Impact of this Role: Gideon's Army Rescue Agents provide direct survivor support through coordinated intervention efforts, ensuring their safe recovery and healing. This role allows you to help pull someone out of exploitation, changing their entire trajectory of life. If you are tired with just being "aware" of the problem, and you are tired of theoretical discussion without action, then this is the volunteer position for you! About the Role: This is an UNPAID, volunteer position There are no set schedules, deadlines, or daily requirements to meet This position is extremely flexible, with no requirement of relocation All activities are conducted in compliance with applicable laws. Gideon's Army is a humanitarian nonprofit organization and does not condone or promote vigilante, paramilitary, or illegal behavior.

opportunity_title: Rescue Assistant Director - Canine
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org In our rescue program, we focus on: Rescuing healthy and treatable pets at North Texas shelters at the greatest risk of being euthanized Reducing owner surrender through our Positive Alternatives to Shelter Surrender (PASS) program Opening kennel space by transporting pets to partners in the U.S. and Canada in need of adoptable animals Challenging the way our community views rescue animals. We believe that adoption is not only the right thing to do but THE thing to do! PURPOSE OF THE POSITION: The Assistant Rescue Director- Canine will focus on the canine needs of our North Texas Shelter partners. SUMMARY: Our organization is run mostly by volunteers and all of our pets are housed by dedicated foster families throughout North Texas. The Assistant Rescue Director- Canine will work closely with our rescue partners with their canine needs. They will coordinate with the Rescue Admin’s and Evaluators on what dogs or puppies need to be networked for fosters. This individual will keep the rescue team, and Animal Operations team informed of canine needs and areas of focus for our shelter partners. This role is both a remote and in person volunteer role. Team: Rescue Team Reports To: Rescue Director Time Commitment: Approximately 7-10 hours per week. Must be able to commit to a minimum of 6 months of volunteer service. Works with: DPA! Leadership, Rescue team, Shelter partners RESPONSIBILITIES: Providing leadership and guidance to Rescue team members In person evaluations of dogs at one of DPA’s shelter partners twice a month Helping to maintain a positive relationship with DPA partner shelters Attend shelter partner meetings, if availability allows Correspond with shelters about non-tag related questions including medical inquiries and paperwork requests Networking urgent dogs to Trello and FB page as needed Attending DPA leadership meetings (virtual), if availability allows CONTACTS WITH OTHERS: Correspondence with rescue team, DPA shelter partners, DPA Leadership, and DPA volunteers QUALIFICATIONS: Preferred: Knowledge of canine behavior Required: At least 18 years of age Associates or Bachelor's Degree at an accredited college or university Google Drive, Excel, Word and PowerPoint experience Ability to assess data and report on findings Must embrace the mission of Dallas Pets Alive and be able to passionately motivate dono rs to invest in our life saving mission Be organized and exhibit “follow through” on tasks and goals Must have excellent oral and written communication skills, and maintain professional level correspondence at all times Display a fun, energetic and positive attitude, show concern for people and community, demonstrate presence, self-confidence, common sense and good listening ability Maintain emotional control under stress and conduct oneself at a high level of professionalism when working with others Must exhibit strong communication skills and compassion for individuals considering surrender of their pet(s) Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Rescuers Needed on Thursdays to Rescue Food & Fight Hunger In Dallas!
description_short: This posting is specifically for a Thursday afternoon rescue. However, you're welcome to sign up reardless! "Volunteers Needed to Rescue Food & Fight Hunger In Dallas" Who We Are: Rescuing Leftover Cuisine is a 501(c)3 non-profit that…
description_long: This posting is specifically for a Thursday afternoon rescue. However, you're welcome to sign up reardless! "Volunteers Needed to Rescue Food & Fight Hunger In Dallas" Who We Are: Rescuing Leftover Cuisine is a 501(c)3 non-profit that redistributes excess food to people experiencing food insecurity. What We Do: We partner with licensed food vendors (restaurants, bakeries, etc) and businesses who order catering (offices, production companies, etc.) who know they will have excess, wholesome cuisine and link their food donations to a nonprofit recipient within their local community. Our dedicated team of volunteers collect that excess food on a set schedule (pickups can be once a week, daily, or anywhere in between) and deliver it to one of RLC's partner nonprofit recipients to help feed our neighbors who can benefit from a good meal! Who We Need: YOU!! No minimum time commitment is required - you may sign up to volunteer sporadically or on a recurring basis. Documentation for proof of volunteering is available upon request. Vehicle is required to volunteer for this event RLC is currently seeking passionate and committed leaders who are willing to take on the role of Lead Rescuer and lead food rescue events. Pick up food donations and deliver them to a nonprofit recipient, where the donations will then be distributed to the homeless and food insecure community. Each event is an hour or less. As new volunteer opportunities become available they will be added to our online calendar. How To Get Started: Create an account on our website: https://www.rescuingleftovercuisine.org/volunteer Complete the remainder of the training process (available on your RLC website dashboard) to join our leadership team of volunteers as a Lead Rescuer and lead events on your own! Sign up for any event on our calendar that fits with your schedule.

opportunity_title: Retirees who are Compassionate Help Hospice Patients and Families
description_short: Hospice volunteers make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and caregivers. Our direct patient volunteers work in the home or at a senior living facility with terminally ill patients and their families. Open Volunteer Jobs throughout Dallas…
description_long: Hospice volunteers make a meaningful impact in the lives of patients and caregivers. Our direct patient volunteers work in the home or at a senior living facility with terminally ill patients and their families. Open Volunteer Jobs throughout Dallas and Collin County include: Patient companionship : Talking, playing games, reading, engaging in hobbies. Caregiver respite : Sit with a patient for a set period giving a caregiver the opportunity for selfcare and complete needed activities. Other duties may include light household chores, shopping, transportation. Professional Volunteers (must have valid License or certifications): Hair stylist; pet therapy; aroma therapy; massage therapy; interpreters, etc. Administrative assistance: Assist with special projects, general office duties and patient outreach. There are many benefits to volunteering with hospice. Most of all you make a significant impact in the lives of hospice patients and families while experiencing personal fulfillment!

opportunity_title: Richardson / Hospice Volunteer Patient / Caregiver Companionship Volunteer
description_short: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily…
description_long: Congratulations! You are now on your way to making a difference! Volunteers are an integral part of the team. As a friend and companion to the patient, volunteers also support caregivers by allowing them some free time and relief from their daily responsibilities and stress. Making a difference as a hospice volunteer begins with the desire to help the dying and their families. Hospice volunteers also provide a number of services such as bereavement support and help in the office. 16 yrs and up for Administrative Volunteer and 18 yrs and Older for Patient Support Volunteer. Please provide your phone number for contact to you. Call with any questions or email me at susan.olivarez@newcenturyhospice.com Susan Olivarez, Volunteer Coordinator 972-239-0907

opportunity_title: Richardson, TX - Drive cancer patients to and from treatment
description_short: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their…
description_long: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their appointments. Most rides are Monday-Friday during the day. You choose the rides that fit your schedule, with a suggested minimum of 2 rides per month. Volunteer drivers must: Be age 18-84 Have a valid driver’s license and safe driving record Use a reliable, insured vehicle Pass background and motor vehicle records checks Complete online training and use our online ride scheduling tool Learn more at cancer.org/drive. We need you now more than ever. Watch our video to learn more: Hope Behind The Wheel

opportunity_title: Richardson, TX: Host an Exchange Student-Volunteer families needed Richardson area!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student !! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Richardson, TX: a neighbor could use a hand with lawn and yard care · Ref IWTMYL-LAT
description_short: A neighbor in Richardson, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A neighbor in Richardson, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and well-maintained. Your kindness makes a real difference in a neighbor's life. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Richardson, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-LAT&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-LAT

opportunity_title: SDG Represantative - Global SDG Leadership Network
description_short: The SDG Representative role serves as the foundation of our leadership structure and provides an opportunity to gain experience, demonstrate commitment, and build a record of SDG-related activities within your community. Representatives work…
description_long: The SDG Representative role serves as the foundation of our leadership structure and provides an opportunity to gain experience, demonstrate commitment, and build a record of SDG-related activities within your community. Representatives work directly with IPC leadership and contribute to awareness campaigns, community outreach, volunteer engagement, educational initiatives, and local SDG action projects. As an SDG Representative, you will receive: Official appointment within the IPC Global SDG Leadership Network. IPC Membership Fee Waiver for one (1) year. Eligibility for an additional one-year membership waiver upon successful completion of assigned responsibilities and active participation. Access to IPC global activities, campaigns, and networking opportunities. Eligibility for training, certificates, recognition awards, and future leadership advancement opportunities. Consideration for promotion to SDG Team Leader, SDG Commissioner, or SDG Ambassador based on performance, leadership capacity, and documented achievements. Key expectations include: Supporting at least one SDG-related activity or initiative each month. Promoting awareness of the Sustainable Development Goals within your community. Assisting with volunteer recruitment and community engagement. Submitting brief activity reports when requested. Upholding the values and mission of the International Peace Commission.

opportunity_title: SOUPER SATURDAYS: DISCO INFERNO
description_short: 🪩🔥 Volunteer with Soup Angels: Souper Saturdays — Disco Inferno! 🔥🪩 Saturday, May 16, 2026 • 1:00–3:00 p.m. Lakewood • Pleasant Grove • Downtown Dallas Soup Angels is looking for at least four volunteers to help us bring nourishment, dignity, and…
description_long: 🪩🔥 Volunteer with Soup Angels: Souper Saturdays — Disco Inferno! 🔥🪩 Saturday, May 16, 2026 • 1:00–3:00 p.m. Lakewood • Pleasant Grove • Downtown Dallas Soup Angels is looking for at least four volunteers to help us bring nourishment, dignity, and joy to our Dallas neighbors at this special Souper Saturday event. This week’s theme: Disco Inferno! A Build‑Your‑Own Chicken Corn Chowder Bar with glittery toppings, funky beats, and community magic — all fueling our outreach to families, seniors, and unhoused friends. ⭐ Volunteer Roles 🍲 Chowder Bar Support Help guests build their bowls, restock toppings, and keep the line moving with warmth and kindness. 🤝 Outreach Meal Prep Assist in assembling outreach chowders for distribution across Dallas neighborhoods. 🎶 Event Flow & Hospitality Welcome guests, maintain a joyful environment, and keep the disco energy alive. ⭐ Why Your Help Matters Every pot we prepare and every bowl we serve strengthens our mission to deliver nourishment, connection, and compassion — no questions, no barriers, just love and soup. ⭐ Additional Support 🍽️ We also offer FREE Soup Angels Outreach Soups for anyone who needs meals for weekly nourishment. Join Us Bring your energy, your heart, and your best disco spirit. We’ll bring the soup, the sparkle, and the soul. Chef Gary W. Irvin II Founder & Organizer, Soup Angels “We feed with love. We serve with joy. We show up with heart.”

opportunity_title: SPCA of Texas Pet Food Pantry - Community Outreach
description_short: Occasionally, even the most devoted pet owners can face financial challenges in providing for their furry companions. As a member of the Perry PRC Product Team, you'll play a vital role in assisting these pet parents by ensuring they have access to…
description_long: Occasionally, even the most devoted pet owners can face financial challenges in providing for their furry companions. As a member of the Perry PRC Product Team, you'll play a vital role in assisting these pet parents by ensuring they have access to the necessary supplies to care for their beloved pets, thereby keeping families together. We're currently seeking volunteers to join us at our Dallas Animal Care Center (2400 Lone Star Dr. Dallas, TX 75212) for our drive-thru pet food distribution event. During this event, we'll be distributing a limited supply of dog and cat food to support our community. Volunteers will assist with crowd management, registration, and transporting bags of food to our customers. Volunteer opportunities are available every third Sunday of the month, with specific times adjusted according to current conditions. Volunteers aged 10 and older are welcome to participate. However, those under 15 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian who is 18 years or older. To get started with volunteering, you'll need to create a volunteer account and complete the initial steps. There's a $15 volunteer fee per person to cover onboarding costs. Once these steps are completed, you'll be eligible to sign up for an Orientation session, where you'll receive additional training and information.

opportunity_title: STEAM Festival: Where Science, Technology, Engineering, Art & Math Become Pathways to Peace
description_short: This interactive, family friendly festival blends STEAM learning, creative expression, and holistic wellness into one unforgettable experience sponsored by Moody Fund for the Arts, TJ Hunter Engineering Firm, and Credit Union of Texas supported in…
description_long: This interactive, family friendly festival blends STEAM learning, creative expression, and holistic wellness into one unforgettable experience sponsored by Moody Fund for the Arts, TJ Hunter Engineering Firm, and Credit Union of Texas supported in part by the City of Dallas Office of Arts and Culture, and in partnership flowOURS and The Texas Discovery Gardens. Designed to uplift families, educators, frontline workers, and youth, The STEAM Festival invites the community into a day of exploration, connection, and restoration through immersive engagement. Whether you’re building, creating, breathing, dancing, or discovering something new this event is for YOU.

opportunity_title: STEM EXPO
description_short: We’re looking for passionate volunteers to help bring our STEM Expo to life! This is an opportunity to inspire the next generation of innovators by supporting hands-on activities, assisting with event setup, and engaging with students and families.…
description_long: We’re looking for passionate volunteers to help bring our STEM Expo to life! This is an opportunity to inspire the next generation of innovators by supporting hands-on activities, assisting with event setup, and engaging with students and families. Whether you have a background in STEM or simply a heart for serving youth, your presence will make a lasting impact. Join us in creating an exciting, educational experience that empowers students to explore, create, and dream bigger!

opportunity_title: STEM Mentors Needed in Arlington, TX Area
description_short: STEM for Others is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization that provides free, personalized STEM education and mentorship to students from underrepresented communities. Our volunteer mentors work one-on-one with students to build confidence, spark…
description_long: STEM for Others is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization that provides free, personalized STEM education and mentorship to students from underrepresented communities. Our volunteer mentors work one-on-one with students to build confidence, spark curiosity, and open doors to future opportunities in science, technology, engineering, and math. As a Volunteer Mentor, you will: • Provide free, personalized STEM tutoring and mentorship to K-12 students • Meet virtually or in-person with your student(s) on a flexible, recurring basis • Help students with homework, test prep, and STEM projects • Serve as a role model and guide for students interested in STEM careers • Collaborate with our team to track student progress and goals Who We're Looking For: • College students, graduate students, or professionals with a background or interest in STEM • Patient, enthusiastic, and committed to making a difference • Comfortable working with youth from diverse backgrounds • No prior teaching experience required — just a passion for STEM and mentorship! Time Commitment: • Typically 2-4 hours per week • Flexible scheduling based on your availability and your student's needs • Ongoing commitment preferred (semester or longer) Why Volunteer with STEM for Others? • Make a meaningful impact in a student's life and future • Gain teaching, mentorship, and leadership experience • Join a community of passionate STEM advocates • Receive support and training from our team STEM for Others Mission Our education system is at a crossroads where it is not preparing our students to be competitive at the global level. The issue is more pronounced for socio-economically disadvantaged families as the disparity in STEM education widens as they face substantial hurdles in accessing quality learning opportunities. We believe that advancement in STEM is the greatest equalizer that creates the greatest opportunities for upward mobility. Our mission is to make a concerted effort to provide deeper and more targeted programs that focus on equitable access to STEM education.

description_short: Save the Storks partners with Pregnancy Health Clinics [Mobile and Brick & Mortar] nationally and refers volunteers to these partner clinics. We are looking for medical professionals with the training and credentialing to perform pregnancy…

opportunity_title: Script Writer
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking creative and thoughtful Script Writers to support the launch of our Animation Program . This volunteer role focuses on developing clear, engaging, and impactful scripts that will be used for animated content aligned with our mission and educational goals. This is a 6-9 month minimum commitment, with the opportunity to renew. This position is ideal for individuals who enjoy storytelling, writing for visual media, and contributing to meaningful, purpose-driven content. Key Responsibilities: Write short-form scripts for animated videos, including educational, informational, and storytelling content. Collaborate with animators, program leads, and internal teams to align scripts with visual concepts and messaging. Adapt complex topics into clear, accessible, and engaging narratives. Revise and refine scripts based on feedback and evolving program needs. Support the development of tone, style, and storytelling guidelines for the animation program. Ensure scripts are culturally sensitive, inclusive, and aligned with organizational values. Maintain organized documentation of scripts and revisions. Qualifications: Strong writing and storytelling skills with attention to clarity and structure. Ability to write for visual and animated formats. Comfortable collaborating in a remote, feedback-driven environment. Ability to meet deadlines and manage multiple writing tasks. Prior experience in scriptwriting, content writing, or storytelling is an asset but not required. Interest in mental health, education, or mission-driven content is strongly valued. Interest in astrology and unconventional/spiritual frameworks as tools for storytelling and character development.

opportunity_title: Secretary Board position
description_short: Make a Difference Behind the Scenes 💛 Our nonprofit is looking for an organized, reliable, and heart-driven Volunteer Secretary to support our leadership team and help keep our mission moving forward. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys…
description_long: Make a Difference Behind the Scenes 💛 Our nonprofit is looking for an organized, reliable, and heart-driven Volunteer Secretary to support our leadership team and help keep our mission moving forward. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys staying organized and wants to make a meaningful impact without being in the spotlight. What You’ll Do: • Take notes and keep minutes during meetings • Help organize documents, schedules, and records • Review and organize emails • Support communication between board members and volunteers • Assist with basic administrative tasks as needed • Help keep our operations running smoothly What We’re Looking For: • Organized and detail-oriented • Comfortable with email, Google Docs, or similar tools • Reliable and responsive • Passionate about helping children and families in our community • Previous admin or secretary experience is a plus, but not required Time Commitment: About 8 - 12 hours per month Mostly virtual, with occasional in-person meetings if local Why Volunteer With Us? • Be part of a caring, mission-driven team • Gain nonprofit and leadership experience • Make a real impact behind the scenes • Flexible schedule that fits your life • Know your work directly supports children who deserve to feel celebrated Ready to Join the Adventure? If you love organization and want to use your skills to help create joyful moments for kids, we’d love to meet you! Apply today and become one of our Adventurers!

opportunity_title: Seeking Board Members
description_short: The The Circle of SistAhood is growing and we’re looking for passionate, purpose-driven leaders to join our Board of Directors! Who We Are: A nonprofit dedicated to empowering teen girls (ages 12–17) through mentorship, mental health support,…
description_long: The The Circle of SistAhood is growing and we’re looking for passionate, purpose-driven leaders to join our Board of Directors! Who We Are: A nonprofit dedicated to empowering teen girls (ages 12–17) through mentorship, mental health support, leadership development, and family engagement. We’re Looking For Individuals Who: Are passionate about youth empowerment & community impact Bring experience in leadership, nonprofit work, education, mental health, finance, or fundraising Can commit to supporting our mission, programs, and growth Want to help shape the future of young women in our community Board Member Responsibilities Include: Attending monthly meetings Supporting fundraising & community outreach Providing strategic guidance and oversight Advocating for our programs and mission Why Join Us? Make a lasting impact in the lives of teen girls Be part of a purpose-driven sisterhood Help expand programs like mentorship, P.L.U.S., and college tours

opportunity_title: Seeking Compassionate Heroes in North Dallas
description_short: Not all heroes wear scrubs. Sometimes they simply sit, listen and care. Sometimes the most powerful gift you can give is simply your presence. Freedom Hospice is looking for compassionate volunteers to support patients and families receiving hospice…
description_long: Not all heroes wear scrubs. Sometimes they simply sit, listen and care. Sometimes the most powerful gift you can give is simply your presence. Freedom Hospice is looking for compassionate volunteers to support patients and families receiving hospice care. As a hospice volunteer, you can provide comfort, companionship and dignity during life's most meaningful moments. Ways you can help: Sit and visit with patients Read listen or share conversation Capture the unique story of patients Help with small office tasks Share special skills like music, crafts, pet therapy, etc. Why volunteer with hospice? Because no one should face the end of life alone. Your time and kindness can bring peace, connection and comfort to someone who truly needs it. Flexible scheduling Free on-line training provided No medical experience required - just a caring heart. If you are ready to make a difference that truly matters, we'd love to hear from you. To apply, complete our on-line application found at https://www.volgistics.com/appform/898610724

opportunity_title: Seeking Compassionate Volunteers for Hospice Patients
description_short: Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening…
description_long: Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening ear to patients and families, assist with daily household activities for patients, provide grief support, serve as a companion to loved ones or share their personal expertise to help promote hospice care, the time given to Harbor Hospice families by our volunteers can be the most rewarding experiences of their lives. As a volunteer for Harbor Hospice's Patient Care Services, you "do good" every day. It takes a very special, caring person to give of themselves as unselfishly as our Harbor Hospice patient care volunteers. If you'd like to be a part of the Harbor Hospice team, here are some examples of the activities you can assist with: reading to a patient, or holding a hand staying with a patient while family members take a needed break or keep an appointment shopping or doing errands for a patient listening helping patients write or tell their life stories light household chores providing help with makeup and hair Veterans needed to honor Veteran patients at bedside/share stories together working on crafts or other projects clerical and other office duties If you would like to volunteer for Harbor Hospice, please follow these steps: Step 1. Complete online Application (Click Here) and submit form. Step 2. Wait and Volunteer Coordinator will process and contact you in 24-48hrs If you have any questions, contact us TF (855) 542-7267 or email us by clicking here

opportunity_title: Seeking Hair Stylist / Nail Tech Volunteer for Hospice Patients in PLANO GARLAND N DALLAS RICHARDSON FRISCO ALLEN ETC
description_short: Just because you are housebound and/or ill, does not mean you do not like to feel pretty. A nice bright nail color always brightens my day, how about you? Imagine using your special skills to brighten someone's day? Someone who may be feeling a bit…
description_long: Just because you are housebound and/or ill, does not mean you do not like to feel pretty. A nice bright nail color always brightens my day, how about you? Imagine using your special skills to brighten someone's day? Someone who may be feeling a bit depressed due to their circumstances. A nice pretty, fresh hairdo could surely make someone's day brighter. We are seeking volunteers to help brighten a hospice patient's day. Volunteers are literally, the heart of Harbor Hospice, providing a special kind of caring and compassion for our patients and their families. Every day, our volunteers make a difference in the lives of so many people. Whether they offer a listening ear to patients and families, assist with daily household activities for patients, provide grief support, serve as a companion to loved ones or share their personal expertise to help promote hospice care, the time given to Harbor Hospice families by our volunteers can be the most rewarding experiences of their lives. As a volunteer for Harbor Hospice's Patient Care Services, you "do good" every day. It takes a very special, caring person to give of themselves as unselfishly as our Harbor Hospice patient care volunteers. If you'd like to be a part of the Harbor Hospice team, here are some examples of the activities you can assist with: reading to a patient, or holding a hand staying with a patient while family members take a needed break or keep an appointment shopping or doing errands for a patient listening helping patients write or tell their life stories light household chores providing help with makeup and hair Veterans needed to honor Veteran patients at bedside/share stories together working on crafts or other projects clerical and other office duties If you would like to volunteer for Harbor Hospice, please follow these steps: Step 1. Complete online Application (Click Here) and submit form. Step 2. Wait and Volunteer Coordinator will process and contact you in 24-48hrs If you have any questions, contact us TF (855) 542-7267 or email us by clicking here

opportunity_title: Seeking Host Families: Host a Foreign Exchange Student Allen, TX and surrounding areas!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Seeking Volunteer Software Engineers & Data Scientists Global & Local
description_short: RLF Students is expanding its STEM initiative to connect students with real-world tech experts. We are looking for dedicated professionals holding degrees in Software Engineering or Data Science to volunteer their time and expertise. Whether you are…
description_long: RLF Students is expanding its STEM initiative to connect students with real-world tech experts. We are looking for dedicated professionals holding degrees in Software Engineering or Data Science to volunteer their time and expertise. Whether you are right here locally or anywhere else in the world, you can help shape the next generation of tech leaders. Time Commitment : Just 2 hours per week of virtual mentoring or support. Curriculum Creation : Volunteers will design and put together their own curriculum for their specific track.

opportunity_title: Senior Activities
description_short: At Complete TX Hospice & Palliative Care, we are currently seeking caring and dependable volunteers to help with our Bingo activities by calling out numbers and handing out prizes to our patients. These simple but meaningful tasks bring joy,…
description_long: At Complete TX Hospice & Palliative Care, we are currently seeking caring and dependable volunteers to help with our Bingo activities by calling out numbers and handing out prizes to our patients. These simple but meaningful tasks bring joy, laughter, and social connection to those we serve, creating moments our patients truly look forward to. By volunteering your time, you help create a warm and engaging environment that supports emotional well-being and companionship. If you’re friendly, compassionate, and enjoy interacting with others, we would love to have you join us in making a difference.

opportunity_title: Senior Care United Treasurer Officer Position
description_short: The Chapter Engagement Officer is responsible for building strong connections within their chapter and encouraging active participation in Senior Care United’s mission. They work to recruit and support volunteers, organize card-making and craft…
description_long: The Chapter Engagement Officer is responsible for building strong connections within their chapter and encouraging active participation in Senior Care United’s mission. They work to recruit and support volunteers, organize card-making and craft activities, and ensure members stay involved and motivated. By fostering a positive and collaborative environment, the Chapter Engagement Officer helps expand outreach efforts and strengthen the impact made in the lives of seniors.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Share Music with Hospice Patients
description_short: Use your musical talent to give quality of life to hospice patients. Whether you are a vocalist or you play an instrument, you can bring the joy of live music to patients for comfort, joy, and relaxation. Music can reduce pain, bring calm, stir the…
description_long: Use your musical talent to give quality of life to hospice patients. Whether you are a vocalist or you play an instrument, you can bring the joy of live music to patients for comfort, joy, and relaxation. Music can reduce pain, bring calm, stir the imagination, and bring back wonderful memories. Music volunteers are needed for one-on-one or duet assignment with hospice patients. Requirements: Volunteer Orientation - only two hours TB Test - free Background Check - free Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers can read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Watch our video to learn more: Hospice - Something More

opportunity_title: Share Rescue Dogs on Facebook
description_short: Do you need community service hours or simply want to help rescue dogs ? If you have a Facebook account , you can assist our rescue by posting adoptable dogs in Facebook groups and receive volunteer credit! Easy & Flexible: Post on Facebook…
description_long: Do you need community service hours or simply want to help rescue dogs ? If you have a Facebook account , you can assist our rescue by posting adoptable dogs in Facebook groups and receive volunteer credit! Easy & Flexible: Post on Facebook groups from anywhere! Make a Difference: Help dogs find forever homes. Earn Service Hours: Credit provided for each post shared. Proof of Posting - A link or screenshot must be provided to get service credit. Time Calculation - Each post = 10 minutes How It Works:Receive a List - You’ll be provided with a list of dog names and Facebook groups to post in. Find Their Photos - Get each dog’s picture from our Adopt a Pet or Petfinder page. Create a Post - In each assigned Facebook group, paste the dog's pic along with a link to our website . Help Them Get Adopted! - Your posts will bring visibility to these dogs and help them find loving homes. Thank you for volunteering-every post makes a difference!

org_name: Medical City Hospice and Family Care
opportunity_title: Share Your Friendship and Time with a Hospice Patient! - Allen
description_short: Many people at the end of their lives are unable to go out and see their friends and do the hobbies they loved so much. If you become a Hospice Volunteer, you can go to them! No matter where they live or how much they are still able to do, people on…
description_long: Many people at the end of their lives are unable to go out and see their friends and do the hobbies they loved so much. If you become a Hospice Volunteer, you can go to them! No matter where they live or how much they are still able to do, people on hospice benefit from the friendly presence of volunteers. Volunteer with Medical City Hospice and Family Care and provide companionship and caring presence to hospice patients living at a DFW community or at home in Carrollton. You will receive free orientation and training and will be able to meet patients to read, share stories, listen to music, or provide respite for family members. Your companionship and caring presence ensures that patients spend the end of their life in dignity and comfort. Medical City Hospice and Family Care has many other locations in the DFW area and surrounding cities. Training and orientation will be scheduled to fit to your schedule. Contact Medical City Hospice and Family Care 469-872-4642 PLEASE CHECK YOUR SPAM INBOX.

opportunity_title: Share the Gift of Music
description_short: Music brings comfort, calm, and connection, often when words are no longer needed. We're seeking compassionate musicians and music students to provide gentle, soothing music for hospice patients in bedside and shared spaces. Music helps ease anxiety…
description_long: Music brings comfort, calm, and connection, often when words are no longer needed. We're seeking compassionate musicians and music students to provide gentle, soothing music for hospice patients in bedside and shared spaces. Music helps ease anxiety and discomfort, brings peace to patients and families, creates meaningful moments, and offers calm through familiar music. Who can Volunteer: Vocalists Guitarist Pianists/Keyboardists String musicians Woodwind players Music therapy students Service hours for school programs, HOSA, college courses, or internships Hands-on experience with patient-centered care Flexible scheduling Make a lasting impact through music

opportunity_title: Shelter Admin (Dallas Animal Services)
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As a Rescue Shelter Admin, you provide the organization with a dedicated representative to work hand in hand with the administration side of a continued positive partnership of Dallas Pets Alive! and our shelter partners. Team: Rescue Time Commitment: Estimate 4-6 hours per week. Must be able to commit to a minimum of 6 months of volunteer service. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation Reports to: Our Rescue Director, Vice President of Animal Operations, and Executive Director. Works with: This volunteer will have regular and continuous contact virtually or over the phone with employees of Dallas Animal Services, along with Dallas Pets Alive! team leaders and fosters. This opportunity will also require consistent communication and availability in case of shelter emergency rescue needs. Responsibilities: Monitor emails from Dallas Animal Services and/or their social media for pets in need Complete brief write-ups with photos of shelter pets in need and add to DPA’s Trello networking albums (ideally daily but if not feasible as frequently as possible) Add shelter candidates to Trello “Pending” albums for review and approval to network to fosters Maintain Dallas Animal Services Trello boards (Dogs In Need & Cats In Need) for candidates, adding and removing pets based on availability Request medical and other records as needed from Dallas Animal Services for any pets added to the Trello board Qualifications/Skills: Must be at least 18 years of age Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Ability and willingness to maintain consistent availability in case of emergencies. Have some related animal/rescue group experience, preferred Ability to work virtually ( Email, Slack, Phone ). A flexible work schedule to answer shelter partner correspondence during the work day is preferred but not required. Must be organized and skilled at record-keeping What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Social Media & Marketing Volunteer
description_short: Something is happening at Be Kingdom Builders, and I want the right person to be part of it from the ground up. We are a 501(c)(3) digital discipleship ministry built around one mission: calling believers out of passive, performance-based…
description_long: Something is happening at Be Kingdom Builders, and I want the right person to be part of it from the ground up. We are a 501(c)(3) digital discipleship ministry built around one mission: calling believers out of passive, performance-based Christianity and into bold, Spirit-led obedience. The content is resonating, the community is growing, and the people finding us are genuinely hungry for something real. This is not another Christian brand chasing followers. This is a ministry doing actual formation work, and it is picking up momentum. I am looking for a volunteer social media and content creation assistant to help carry that message further. A few hours a week is all this takes. You would be creating and scheduling posts across Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn, writing copy that reflects the voice and mission of the ministry, and helping us stay consistent and visible while I focus on building the deeper formation work. You will work directly with the founder, shape how this ministry shows up publicly, and come away with real portfolio work, a professional reference, and a LinkedIn recommendation. As BKB grows, there is a genuine path for this to become a paid role with more responsibility. One thing worth mentioning: Be Kingdom Builders is a Christian ministry, and we do ask that everyone who serves on our team shares that foundation. You do not need a theology degree or a perfect track record. But you do need a genuine, active faith in Jesus Christ and a heart for what this ministry is doing. We will ask for a brief testimony as part of your application, not to put you on the spot, but because this work flows out of something real and we want everyone building it to be rooted in the same thing. If you are a marketing student, a recent grad, or someone who loves the Lord and wants their skills to count for something eternal, this is worth a conversation. To apply, send your resume, a few samples of your work, and a brief testimony to volunteer@bekingdombuilders.com . Tell us who you are, what you believe, and why this opportunity caught your attention.

opportunity_title: Social Media Manager Team Lead
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We're seeking a passionate and experienced Social Media Manager Team Lead to guide our volunteer social media team and elevate our online presence. In this remote role, you'll be instrumental in shaping our social media strategy, mentoring our coordinators, and ensuring our mission resonates across all platforms. Key Responsibilities: Guiding, training, and supporting a team of volunteer Social Media Coordinators, fostering team development and ensuring high-quality content and engagement. Developing, implementing, and refining comprehensive social media strategies aligned with organizational goals, brand messaging, and audience engagement. Overseeing the creation, scheduling, and publishing of diverse and engaging content across all platforms (e.g., Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn), ensuring brand consistency and best practices. Guiding the team in monitoring social channels, responding to comments and messages, and actively fostering a supportive and engaged online community. Establishing KPIs, analyzing social media performance data, and providing regular, insightful reports to leadership, identifying areas for optimization and growth. Staying abreast of the latest social media trends, tools, and platform updates, proactively recommending and implementing innovative strategies for a dynamic online presence. Working closely with other internal teams to ensure cohesive messaging, integrated campaigns, and a unified brand voice across all digital touchpoints. Developing and documenting best practices, workflows, and guidelines for the social media team to optimize efficiency and effectiveness. Qualifications: Proven experience in social media management, with a track record of leading teams or projects. Deep understanding of major social media platforms, analytics, and current best practices. Exceptional content creation, creative writing, and editing skills with a keen eye for detail. Strong leadership abilities, with experience in mentoring and motivating a team. Excellent organizational and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines. Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and translate insights into actionable strategies. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a remote, team-oriented environment. Passion for our organization's mission and a desire to make a significant impact through social media. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration.

opportunity_title: Souper Saturday Jambalaya & Jazz
description_short: Volunteer Opportunity: Souper Saturday — Jambalaya & Jazz with Soup Angels Date: Saturday, May 23, 2026 Time: 1:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. Location: Lakewood • Pleasant Grove • Downtown Dallas Overview Join Soup Angels for a soulful afternoon of food,…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunity: Souper Saturday — Jambalaya & Jazz with Soup Angels Date: Saturday, May 23, 2026 Time: 1:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. Location: Lakewood • Pleasant Grove • Downtown Dallas Overview Join Soup Angels for a soulful afternoon of food, music, and community care. This Souper Saturday brings the spirit of New Orleans to Dallas with a Build‑Your‑Own Jambalaya Bar and smooth, classic jazz that sets the tone for comfort, connection, and compassion. Volunteer Roles Volunteers will help prepare and serve hearty jambalaya bowls for families, seniors, and unhoused neighbors across Dallas. Tasks include: Food prep & assembly — chopping, stirring, building bowls with love Organizing toppings — Cajun‑Creole add‑ons and spice boosters Serving & packaging — meals to‑go for families and outreach Outreach support — helping distribute meals to local communities Why It Matters Every bowl served fuels the Soup Angels mission to deliver nourishment, kindness, and connection throughout Dallas. Volunteers help ensure that no one goes hungry — and that every meal is shared with dignity and heart. What to Expect 🎷 Smooth jazz classics 🔥 Smoky, soulful jambalaya 🍤 Bold Cajun‑Creole flavors 💛 Community warmth and teamwork Whether you’re stirring the pot, serving guests, or simply soaking in the music, you’ll be part of something joyful and deeply needed. How to Get Involved Bring your energy, your appetite, and your smoothest jazz spirit — we’ll bring the jambalaya, the rhythm, and the soul. No experience required; all supplies provided. Families and groups welcome. Additional Support If you or your family need meals for the week, Soup Angels offers free outreach soups — no questions, no barriers, just love and nourishment. Chef Gary W. Irvin II Founder & Organizer | Soup Angels “We feed with love. We serve with joy. We show up with heart.”

description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Role Description: We are seeking a highly motivated and results-oriented Sponsorship Outreach Lead to join our team on a volunteer basis. As a Sponsorship Outreach Lead, you will be instrumental in identifying, cultivating, and securing partnerships with organizations and individuals who align with our mission, helping to secure the vital resources needed to expand our programs and reach. Key Responsibilities: Research and identify potential corporate, foundation, and individual sponsors whose values align with SelfSoulLiberate's mission. Develop and implement outreach strategies to engage potential sponsors, including crafting compelling proposals and presentations. Initiate and manage communication with prospective sponsors, nurturing relationships through various channels (email, phone, virtual meetings). Collaborate with the marketing and event planning teams to create attractive sponsorship packages and promotional opportunities. Track and report on all sponsorship outreach activities, maintaining an organized database of contacts and progress. Assist in the negotiation and finalization of sponsorship agreements. Represent SelfSoulLiberate professionally in all interactions with potential sponsors. Qualifications: Proven experience in sales, fundraising, business development, or a related field, with a focus on outreach and relationship building. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to craft persuasive proposals and engage diverse stakeholders. Strong interpersonal skills and a professional demeanor. Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple leads and track progress effectively. Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a remote team environment. Proficiency in Microsoft Outlook and other Microsoft Office Suite applications (e.g., Word, Excel, PowerPoint), as well as Google Workspace (e.g., Docs, Sheets, Slides). Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration.

opportunity_title: Start a Community Chapter
description_short: Stronger Than Cancer is a student-led nonprofit focused on raising cancer awareness, supporting patients and survivors, and educating communities through outreach and volunteer-driven initiatives. We assemble and distribute comfort care kits for…
description_long: Stronger Than Cancer is a student-led nonprofit focused on raising cancer awareness, supporting patients and survivors, and educating communities through outreach and volunteer-driven initiatives. We assemble and distribute comfort care kits for cancer patients and work closely with hospitals and community partners to provide meaningful support to individuals and families. We are looking for passionate and self driven students to start and lead Stronger Than Cancer Community Chapters in their local areas. This is a unique leadership opportunity where you will build something meaningful from the ground up while making a real difference in your community. What You’ll Do: Recruiting volunteers from neighborhoods, friend groups, and community organizations Hosting volunteer meetups at homes, libraries, community centers, or local events Making bracelets and cards, and helping with care kit support projects Supporting outreach, fundraising, or awareness efforts, including breast cancer awareness, based on interest Tracking volunteer participation and service hours Coordinating donation drop offs with our team Representing Stronger Than Cancer with kindness and responsibility Sharing photos and short updates with the Stronger Than Cancer team Who Should Apply : High school or college students Interested in leadership, healthcare, or community impact Willing to take initiative and work with community Passionate about making a difference What You’ll Gain : A leadership role as a Chapter Founder and President Experience building, leading, and managing a student team Verified volunteer hours and official recognition Mentorship and ongoing support from the Stronger Than Cancer leadership team Hands-on experience in leadership, event planning, community outreach, and partnership development Opportunity to earn a certificate of leadership and recognition for your impact Potential eligibility for a letter of recommendation based on performance and commitment A meaningful, real-world initiative that strengthens college and scholarship applications If you have any questions, please contact us at 469 660 6087. opportunity_title: Start a bright smiles project chapter in your community
description_short: The Bright Smiles Project makes it easy for anyone to create real change—and you don’t have to do it alone. Whether you gather a team through your school or form a group within your neighborhood, starting a chapter is a powerful way to unite people…
description_long: The Bright Smiles Project makes it easy for anyone to create real change—and you don’t have to do it alone. Whether you gather a team through your school or form a group within your neighborhood, starting a chapter is a powerful way to unite people who care about supporting children in need. Chapters work together to collect, sort, and redistribute gently used books and toys, ensuring that every child—regardless of their background—has access to resources that inspire imagination, build literacy, and encourage emotional growth. Your team can host donation drives, partner with local organizations, and deliver items directly to families and community centers. Not only does this project make a meaningful difference, but it also offers volunteers the opportunity to earn service hours while contributing to something truly impactful. Every hour spent helping is an hour spent brightening a child’s future. Starting a Bright Smiles Project chapter means taking the lead in spreading kindness, opportunity, and joy. With a team by your side—school-affiliated or community-based—you can help create lasting change, one book, one toy, and one smile at a time.
