If you are serious about your participation, you can sign up at stem@rlfstudents.org. The topics covered in the program include Coding Fundamentals, Information Technology, Robotics, Drones, Arts, Cybersecurity, and much more.

opportunity_title: Student Volunteers Needed!
description_short: We are a young, forward-thinking Hospice company serving all over Denton, Collin and Cook County. We can accommodate any volunteer living in the area. We understand the importance of fostering education. No skills necessary, we provide training.…
description_long: We are a young, forward-thinking Hospice company serving all over Denton, Collin and Cook County. We can accommodate any volunteer living in the area. We understand the importance of fostering education. No skills necessary, we provide training. Trio hospice is looking for great volunteers to add to our dedicated team to support the needs of our staff, our patients and their loved ones. We offer patient/family volunteering, special event volunteering in our community, administrative/office support and remote volunteering. Whether you are looking for ways to give back to your community or wanting to step into healthcare and see if you are interested in it as a career choice, we are interested in hearing from you. As a hospice volunteer you will have the opportunity to bring meaningful interaction and support by providing some or all the following: Companionship Clerical support in the office Customer service Weekly calls or tuck calls to patients and families Interpreting Reading/writing Shopping, running errands Game and other activities Art therapy Music therapy Delivering supplies to patients Visiting with patients Helping host community events Light household chores or food preparation Sitting services while family run errands or other respite needs Dog walking Special skills (i.e. yard maintenance, musicians, beauticians, etc.) Bereavement support Remote volunteering Please contact us for more information and to learn how you can become a volunteer!

opportunity_title: Substack Content Creator (blog, newsletter)
description_short: The Substack Content Creator is the voice of the organization’s digital community. This role focuses on crafting compelling narratives, sharing organizational updates, and managing a recurring newsletter that keeps donors, volunteers, and partners…
description_long: The Substack Content Creator is the voice of the organization’s digital community. This role focuses on crafting compelling narratives, sharing organizational updates, and managing a recurring newsletter that keeps donors, volunteers, and partners engaged. You will be responsible for the end-to-end production of the Substack publication, ensuring a consistent and professional brand presence. Responsibilities Editorial Planning: Develop and maintain a monthly content calendar. Content Creation: Write, edit, and format 2–4 high-quality posts per month (articles, interviews, or news updates). Newsletter Management: Curate and send periodic email newsletters to the subscriber list. Audience Engagement: Monitor and respond to comments; implement strategies to grow the subscriber base. Analysis: Track key performance indicators (KPIs) like open rates and click-through rates to improve content strategy. Benefits Portfolio Growth: Build a public body of work on a leading publishing platform. Networking: Connect with leaders, volunteers, and stakeholders in the non-profit space. Skill Development: Gain hands-on experience in digital marketing, community management, and professional storytelling. Impact: Directly contribute to the organization’s growth by increasing visibility and donor engagement. Qualifications Writing Excellence: Strong command of grammar, tone, and storytelling. Technical Savvy: Familiarity with the Substack platform (or a willingness to learn) and basic digital formatting. Self-Starter: Ability to work independently and meet deadlines without constant supervision. Collaborative Spirit: Comfortable interviewing team members or subjects to gather information for stories.

opportunity_title: Summer Camp 2026 Volunteering
description_short: About Us: It’s a Sensory World! provides sensory-based education, recreation, and support for neurodiverse children and young adults in a joyful, inclusive environment. About Our Summer Camp: Our summer camp creates a safe, joyful, and structured…
description_long: About Us: It’s a Sensory World! provides sensory-based education, recreation, and support for neurodiverse children and young adults in a joyful, inclusive environment. About Our Summer Camp: Our summer camp creates a safe, joyful, and structured environment for children with intellectual and developmental disabilities. We are seeking caring, dependable individuals to support our campers this summer! We have two kinds of opportunities: child-facing or administrative supportive! Child Facing Opportunities: Work alongside our staff to support campers during play, special activities, water days, field trips, and transitions while helping create a safe, inclusive, and joyful summer camp experience. Administrative Support Opportunities: Support summer camp behind the scenes by helping with getting camp set-up or torn down, answering phone calls, organizing materials, decorating bulletin boards, taking pictures, heat pressing shirts, and more! Who Can Volunteer? Individuals 14 and older High school and college students seeking service hours Parents, professionals, and retirees Community, school, or corporate groups Anyone with a heart for inclusive learning and play How to Get Started Fill out our Volunteer Interest Form: bit.ly/VolunteerISW You’ll hear from an ISW team member within 2–3 business days We’ll schedule your first volunteer day and, if you’re 18 or older, provide a background check link. All volunteers will complete a short orientation before beginning.

opportunity_title: Summer Volunteer Coordinator Assistant- Volunteer Role
description_short: Summer Volunteer Coordinator Assistant (Volunteer Position) Looking for a meaningful way to build experience this summer while making a difference behind the scenes? Our hospice volunteer department is looking for a Volunteer Coordinator Assistant…
description_long: Summer Volunteer Coordinator Assistant (Volunteer Position) Looking for a meaningful way to build experience this summer while making a difference behind the scenes? Our hospice volunteer department is looking for a Volunteer Coordinator Assistant to help support volunteer programs, community outreach, office projects, and recruitment efforts. This is a great opportunity for someone interested in nonprofit work, healthcare administration, event coordination, communications, or community engagement. This role works directly with the Volunteer Coordinator and helps with the day-to-day operations that support our patients, families, and volunteers. This Opportunity Is Great For: College students Anyone looking to gain administrative or volunteer coordinator experience Those needing volunteer hours or resume-building experience Individuals who enjoy organization, teamwork, and helping others Volunteer Responsibilities May Include: Assisting with volunteer recruitment and outreach research Helping organize volunteer events and appreciation projects Preparing packets, mailings, and office materials Assisting with administrative projects and data entry Organizing volunteer resources Supporting social media or networking outreach General office and program support What We Offer: Flexible scheduling — volunteer on your schedule Hands-on experience in volunteer coordinating Training and mentorship Resume-building experience A supportive and meaningful team environment To Get Started: Application Phone Interview Background Check Orientation We provide full training in hospice philosophy and volunteer duties. Background and reference check required. Help make a difference behind the scenes while gaining valuable experience this summer!

opportunity_title: Support Volunteer (Remote)
description_short: Note: We can support reporting community service hours as needed. We have an open position for a administrative support volunteer. This position is virtual and requires use of Internet. We would love to have someone located in the Dallas-Fort Worth…
description_long: Note: We can support reporting community service hours as needed. We have an open position for a administrative support volunteer. This position is virtual and requires use of Internet. We would love to have someone located in the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex or North Texas. However, that is not a firm requirement. Volunteer should have interest in animal welfare, marketing, communications, community development, etc. Projects Available: Web Development Social/Media Administrative/Operations Desired Skills: Strong communication skills Attention to detail Willingness to learn Positive attitude and integrity Ability to work independently and think creatively Skill at prioritizing work and completing tasks Bonus Skills (We're willing to train): Software*: Graphics Design, Word Processing, Spreadsheets, Email, Slack We will provide licenses for any software needed.

opportunity_title: TNR Coordinator – Help Community Cats
description_short: Friends of Rescue Animals (FRAs) is building a community-based Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) program to humanely manage community cat populations. Caretakers, homeowners, and business owners all play a role in caring for cats in their neighborhoods, and…
description_long: Friends of Rescue Animals (FRAs) is building a community-based Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) program to humanely manage community cat populations. Caretakers, homeowners, and business owners all play a role in caring for cats in their neighborhoods, and FRAs provides the tools and support to make TNR possible. We loan traps, provide spay/neuter vouchers, offer TNR classes, and help with mentoring, staging, and transportation. For those unable to trap themselves—or in situations with large colonies—we step in directly to assist. We are looking for a TNR Coordinator to join our volunteer team. This person will: Help manage volunteer trappers and transporters. Work with Colony Managers to coordinate TNR efforts. Support communication and logistics to ensure community cats get the care they need. This is a rewarding role for someone who is organized, enjoys working with people, and wants to make a measurable impact on reducing cat overpopulation. FRAs will provide training and ongoing support. 👉 Interested in helping community cats? Apply today to be our next TNR Coordinator: www.frastx.org/volunteer

opportunity_title: TX Chapter (Dallas), Board Member
description_short: Organization & Board Member Description Gift of Adoption is a national charity that provides grants of up to $15,000 to complete the adoptions of children in vulnerable circumstances from the U.S. and around the world. Priority is placed on…
description_long: Organization & Board Member Description Gift of Adoption is a national charity that provides grants of up to $15,000 to complete the adoptions of children in vulnerable circumstances from the U.S. and around the world. Priority is placed on helping children for whom reunification is not possible and are facing what is possibly their last or only chance at adoption, including those at-risk of separation from siblings, entering foster care, aging-out of an orphanage, or with critical special needs. Gift of Adoption is the largest provider of adoption assistance grants on a nondiscriminatory basis (without regard to age, race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or marital status). Since inception in 1996, Gift of Adoption has awarded over $23 million to give 6,835 children permanent families and the chance to thrive. Gift of Adoption is recognized by Charity Navigator and the Better Business Bureau's Wise Giving Alliance. Want to make a difference? Join a Gift of Adoption Chapter Board! Our all-volunteer chapter boards engage leaders who believe that having a loving, permanent family is a necessity for all children, everywhere. Chapter board members are responsible for raising money and generating awareness. 100% of the funds raised by chapter board members goes directly toward awarding grants to unite children with their forever families in your chapter market. We are actively seeking passionate, diverse, and experienced fundraisers to join our Texas Chapter board. Our Values Honor, Amplify and Be Scrappy Gift of Adoption Needs YOU if : You are passionate about the Gift of Adoption mission. You have the time and energy to spend 6 hours a month working with your board colleagues to raise funds in a collaborative team environment. You can leverage connections, networks, and resources in achieving your chapter’s goals. We are especially interested in talking with you if : You have been a part of nonprofit boards or fundraising committees. You have experience in event planning, grant writing, or major gifts. You will work diligently to meet your personal give/get goal. You are willing to bring your unique background, talent, and passion to the table. Next Steps Gift of Adoption chapter board members serve 3-year terms. Each board member is tasked with recruiting one new board member to their chapter team by the completion of each term. This is a non-compensatory, volunteer chapter board role. Our chapter board members are impactful and game changers. Please visit our website www.giftofadoption.org for more information.

description_short: Volunteer working on the environment and climate change with our grassroots climate group! CCL volunteers work with their community and members of Congress on climate change solutions. We are organized into more than 450 local chapters across the…
description_long: Volunteer working on the environment and climate change with our grassroots climate group! CCL volunteers work with their community and members of Congress on climate change solutions. We are organized into more than 450 local chapters across the U.S. Your activities might include: Educating the community about global warming and legislative solutions Meeting with congressional leaders Writing letters to the editor Hosting film screenings and panel conversations Meeting with local media We also have options available for working on your own or virtually. Want to know more? We offer a weekly livestream Informational Session every Wednesday and there are opportunities to ask questions: https://citizensclimatelobby.org/informational-session/ Watch our video to learn more: A Reminder of Who We Are

description_long: Our letters have been swiftly accepted by courts in the US. Work will be based on YOUR background, interests and hobbies. Accepting applicants nationwide. Pursue your passions. Many service options. Easy onboarding. Enjoy flexible hours. Get inspired and gain valuable experience volunteering for our virtual library. Work X hours as ordered by court or diversion program or advised by attorney or probation officer. Work is Documentable, Ongoing, Engaging and Exciting! Work always available. Nonprofit 501(c)3 organization. Why choose Ganga Library? Ganga Library kindles a fire in you, revealing to you your hidden aspirations, abilities, and skills! Testimonials "Thank you so much!!! It was refreshing to actually learn something from my community service." - Volunteer "I greatly appreciate this opportunity to work with the Ganga Library. I have found my research on some of the most influential people on society of all time very informative. It is not something I would have woken up one day and just 'decided' to do, but I am very grateful that I did." - Volunteer who had been advised by Attorney to do community service prior to going to court. Watch our video to learn more: Ideas & Thought Processes of Nobel Laureates

opportunity_title: Take pictures at the Grace Women's Wellness Fair
description_short: We are seeking a photographer to capture the energy, impact, and community spirit of the Grace Women's Wellness Fair, a free community event focused on health, wellness, and access to local resources. You don't have to be a professional.…
description_long: We are seeking a photographer to capture the energy, impact, and community spirit of the Grace Women's Wellness Fair, a free community event focused on health, wellness, and access to local resources. You don't have to be a professional. Responsibilities may include: Capture high-quality photos and video throughout the event Document key activities, speakers, vendors, and community engagement Submit media files after the event This event will feature free health screenings, mental health resources, fitness and wellness sessions, and access to valuable community services. Volunteers must be prompt and reliable. Join us in making a meaningful impact by helping families take steps toward healthier, stronger, and more confident lives.

opportunity_title: Taste of Italy
description_short: Volunteer Roles Filled – Thank You 🇮🇹✨ Volunteer Opportunity: Support Chef Irvin’s Family‑Style Italian Dinner Help Serve, Help Build Community, Help Fuel a Mission Location: Chef Irvin’s Home Kitchen — Lakewood, Dallas Date: Tuesday, May 19 Time:…
description_long: Volunteer Roles Filled – Thank You 🇮🇹✨ Volunteer Opportunity: Support Chef Irvin’s Family‑Style Italian Dinner Help Serve, Help Build Community, Help Fuel a Mission Location: Chef Irvin’s Home Kitchen — Lakewood, Dallas Date: Tuesday, May 19 Time: 7:00 p.m. – 9:00 p.m. Volunteers Needed: 2 Roles: Water Refills • Light Event Support • End‑of‑Night Clean‑Up Perk: You’ll be fed a full Italian feast while you serve 💛 About the Event Every Tuesday night, Chef Gary W. Irvin II opens his home for a free, abundant, family‑style global dinner — a joyful gathering where friends become family. These dinners fuel the mission behind: 🌟 The Kitchen of Tomorrow Campaign 🌟 The Angels School of Culinary Arts 🌟 Soup Angels outreach feeding 1,000 neighbors weekly This week’s dinner is a Cena Tradizionale Italiana a Sette Portate — a seven‑course Italian feast served family‑style, filled with warmth, laughter, and community. 🙌 Volunteer Responsibilities Volunteers will help keep the evening flowing smoothly by assisting with: 💧 Water refills at the table 🍽️ Light event support as needed 🧼 Simple end‑of‑night clean‑up (clearing disposables, wiping surfaces) No heavy lifting. No stress. Just kindness, presence, and teamwork. 🍝 Volunteer Perks You will be treated like family — and fed like family , too. Enjoy the full seven‑course Italian dinner while you help support a mission that feeds Dallas with dignity and love. 🌟 Who We’re Looking For People who are: Friendly and community‑minded Comfortable in a warm, home‑based event setting Happy to help with simple tasks Supportive of food‑justice and community‑care work ❤️ Why Your Help Matters Every plate passed and every hand that helps makes it possible for Chef Irvin to continue feeding families, seniors, and unhoused neighbors across Dallas — seven days a week. Your presence supports the mission, the meal, and the movement. 📩 How to Sign Up Reply through Idealist to claim one of the two volunteer spots.

opportunity_title: Teach Tennis to Kids with Autism - ACEing Autism Dallas, TX - LB Houston (CFB-Irving)
description_short: Looking for volunteers with heart to help us teach tennis and pickleball to kids with autism in Dallas, TX! No tennis or pickleball experience is necessary! Our goal is to help these children learn socializing skills, fine & gross motor skills,…
description_long: Looking for volunteers with heart to help us teach tennis and pickleball to kids with autism in Dallas, TX! No tennis or pickleball experience is necessary! Our goal is to help these children learn socializing skills, fine & gross motor skills, and gain interest in fitness, which means we have a focus on fun. Volunteers receive training and are paired with a participant to help them through the motions of the game of tennis and pickleball through various drills and games. We hope that our volunteers and participants form a bond and friendship over the course of our session. Volunteers who return each week find the most value in our volunteer opportunity and will see the strides the athletes make in our program. Saturdays, February 14, 21, 28, March 7, 28, April 11 8:15 - 11:15 am Learn more about our program here.

opportunity_title: Teacher Appreciation
description_short: Teacher Appreciation Week is coming up, and we’d love your help in making it extra special for our dedicated teachers! Every day, our teachers go above and beyond to create a nurturing, enriching environment for our students, and now it’s our turn…
description_long: Teacher Appreciation Week is coming up, and we’d love your help in making it extra special for our dedicated teachers! Every day, our teachers go above and beyond to create a nurturing, enriching environment for our students, and now it’s our turn to show them just how much they mean to us. To celebrate their hard work, we’re asking for gift card donations that we can share with our teachers throughout the week. Even a small token makes a big difference in showing our gratitude. Thank you in advance for helping us make Teacher Appreciation Week unforgettable for the incredible team at Mi Escuelita Preschool! Contact Erika Graciano, egraciano@miescuelita.org or call 214-526-0220 x108 for more details. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 50+ Event can be held virtually Donation Required We kindly ask that all donations be in the form of gift cards, which allow our staff the flexibility to use them as needed.

opportunity_title: Team Hope Walk Greater North Texas - Auction Committee Member
description_short: About Team Hope Walk: The Team Hope Walk program is HDSA’s signature grassroots fundraising campaign designed to provide hope and support for those touched by this devastating disease. All proceeds support HDSA’s mission to improve the lives of…
description_long: About Team Hope Walk: The Team Hope Walk program is HDSA’s signature grassroots fundraising campaign designed to provide hope and support for those touched by this devastating disease. All proceeds support HDSA’s mission to improve the lives of people affected by HD and their families. Since their inception in 2007, Team Hope Walks have occurred in over 100 different cities and have raised more than $26 million for the cause. Role Purpose: This creative team builds the event’s live and silent auction experience. Responsibilities: Solicit and organize auction items and packages Support auction setup and display on event day Utilize auction software (with training provided) to manage bidding and logistics Collaborate with the Logistics Chair to ensure smooth execution

opportunity_title: Teen Hospice Volunteers Wanted This Summer!
description_short: Looking for a meaningful way to spend your summer? Join our volunteer program at Bristol Hospice Pathways and make a difference in the lives of hospice patients and families in your community! Our teen volunteers can assist with: Creating Cards and…
description_long: Looking for a meaningful way to spend your summer? Join our volunteer program at Bristol Hospice Pathways and make a difference in the lives of hospice patients and families in your community! Our teen volunteers can assist with: Creating Cards and Crafts Administrative Support Assist with Special Projects Remote Volunteer Opportunities Providing Companionship for Patients ( Must have a parent/guardian who is also a Bristol Volunteer attend visits) Much more! Volunteering for Bristol Hospice is a great way to: Earn volunteer/service hours Build leadership and communication skills Gain healthcare and community service experience Making lasting connections and meaningful memories! Requirements: Must be 14 years or older Must be compassionate, dependable, and eager to help others Must complete background check, TB test, and volunteer onboarding processes Time Commitment: We'll work with your schedule! For more information or to apply, please contact our main office at 972-923-2436.

opportunity_title: Texas Host Families for Amazing Exchange Students (Dallas Metro)
description_short: Opportunity for families & individuals in Dallas Metro area ! Ready to have a fun experience? Ready to enjoy some cultural diversity? Ready to get inspired? Invite an international high school student to your home for a few weeks, a semester or…
description_long: Opportunity for families & individuals in Dallas Metro area ! Ready to have a fun experience? Ready to enjoy some cultural diversity? Ready to get inspired? Invite an international high school student to your home for a few weeks, a semester or even a full school year! Hosting is an amazing experience that will enrich, not only yourself and your family, but also schools and the entire community. Host families come in all shapes and sizes. You may be a single parent, an empty-nester, a couple with or without children - All family dynamics are welcome! Our students are 15-18 years old and they come from Europe, Asia, Oceania and Latin America. They are carefully selected and come with full health insurance, spending money and they speak English well. And you can of course choose your own student. As a host family, you provide room and meals while at home, and a loving family to be a part of. International Cultural Exchange Services is a nonprofit student exchange organization, that has helped connecting international students with American families for over 30 years. Contact us today to learn more about this amazing and life-changing opportunity! You may also find us online at www.icesusa.org. Thank You!

org_name: SHARE! High School Student Exchange Program, Exchange Students
opportunity_title: Texas Host Families or Parents Needed
description_short: SHARE! Your Heart & Home with a high school exchange student Host an international exchange student! Host families and adults are needed to host exchange students for a school year or semester. SHARE! students, boys and girls who are 15 -18…
description_long: SHARE! Your Heart & Home with a high school exchange student Host an international exchange student! Host families and adults are needed to host exchange students for a school year or semester. SHARE! students, boys and girls who are 15 -18 years old from countries like FRANCE, GERMANY, SPAIN, CHINA, BRAZIL, Poland, ITALY, MEXICO, JAPAN, VIETNAM, and SOUTH KOREA are waiting for a family or adult(s) to choose them as their exchange student! SHARE! students speak English, have medical insurance, and spending money for personal expenses. They will attend their host family's local high school and want to become a member of your family! SHARE! families are diverse- traditional two-parent families (with or without children), single parents or adults, retired couples and empty-nesters all make wonderful host families! Call or contact us for more information and available student applications! 800-941-3738 sharesouthwest.org/contact Watch our video to learn more: SHARE! High School Exchange Program

description_short: *This opportunity is based in the Dallas Fort Worth area of Texas.* Make an Impact-On Your Own Time! Want to help but can’t commit to a set schedule? Our Open Volunteer role is the perfect way to make a difference-when it works for you! Jump in when…
description_long: *This opportunity is based in the Dallas Fort Worth area of Texas.* Make an Impact-On Your Own Time! Want to help but can’t commit to a set schedule? Our Open Volunteer role is the perfect way to make a difference-when it works for you! Jump in when you can! Opportunities include: Transporting dogs (freedom rides, vet visits, & more!) Helping at adoption & shelter events Supporting fundraisers & community outreach No ongoing commitment-just show up, help out, and be part of something amazing! Join our volunteer team today: thehappypetproject.org/volunteer *most but not all open volunteer opportunities are in person in the DFW area of Texas. All volunteers must be at least 18 years of age. Ready to jump in? Apply today and our Recruitment Coordinators will be in touch! https://www.thehappypetproject.org/volunteer.html

opportunity_title: The Power of Flight:  Drone Prototype
description_short: Power of Flight — Volunteer & Mentor Information About the Challenge Power of Flight is a hands-on innovation challenge where middle and high school students design an edible drone concept —a flight-enabled solution capable of delivering…
description_long: Power of Flight — Volunteer & Mentor Information About the Challenge Power of Flight is a hands-on innovation challenge where middle and high school students design an edible drone concept —a flight-enabled solution capable of delivering nutrition to disaster zones and communities in need. This experience blends engineering, food systems, humanitarian design, and leadership , helping students understand how creative problem-solving can meet real-world needs. Power of Flight is led by Girls Save The World , a leadership and innovation program of Independence Gardens . Independence Gardens’ mission: to equip and empower the next generation to grow leaders and solutions that strengthen communities through hands-on learning and real-world impact. Role of Volunteers & Mentors Volunteers and mentors serve as guides, encouragers, and thought partners —not instructors. Your role is to help students think critically, iterate boldly, and collaborate effectively as they develop their ideas. This is makers mentoring makers —sharing skills, curiosity, and confidence with the next generation. Mentor Support Areas Volunteers and mentors may assist students with: Engineering and aerodynamics Edible and sustainable materials selection Prototyping and testing Design iteration and problem-solving Systems thinking and humanitarian impact Team collaboration and communication No prior teaching experience is required. Who Should Volunteer We welcome volunteers with experience or interest in: Engineering, aerospace, or robotics Food science, nutrition, or sustainability Product design, prototyping, or fabrication STEM education or mentorship Makers, creatives, and problem-solvers of all kinds Time Commitment Event Date: March 22, 2026 Location: Dallas Makerspace Commitment Options: Half-day mentor Full-day mentor Training, schedules, and expectations will be provided in advance. Why Volunteer Empower students—especially girls—to see themselves as innovators Apply your skills to humanitarian and food security challenges Support leadership development through real-world problem-solving Be part of a purpose-driven, high-energy build experience Strengthen community connections through mentoring How to Get Involved Interested in volunteering or mentoring? 👉 Sign up to Volunteer / Mentor 👉 Learn more about Girls Save The World Together, we can help students design solutions that nourish communities—and prove that innovation with purpose can truly take flight.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Therapy Dog and Volunteer needed in Hospice
description_short: Heart to Heart Hospice is seeking friendly canines and their owners to join our volunteer team! Man's best friend and their human will visit the local assisted living facility where our hospice patients call home. We all understand the joy and…
description_long: Heart to Heart Hospice is seeking friendly canines and their owners to join our volunteer team! Man's best friend and their human will visit the local assisted living facility where our hospice patients call home. We all understand the joy and comfort that pets or cuddles from a furry friend can bring. These dynamic duos, our friendly canines and their owners, will bring this same joy and comfort to our patients. Their presence will brighten not only the patients' days but also the lives of the facility staff. Our orientation is designed to fit your schedule, and we offer flexible hours to accommodate your availability. In addition, we will write letters of recommendation and serve as a referral for you, recognizing and appreciating your valuable contribution to our cause. With our volunteer-friendly approach, you can choose to work as many hours as you are able and willing to. We understand that your time is valuable, and we want to ensure that you can make a meaningful contribution without compromising your other commitments.

opportunity_title: TikTok Social Media Manager
description_short: Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability and provide a healthy and loving environment for their dog. Dogs Matter is the only…
description_long: Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability and provide a healthy and loving environment for their dog. Dogs Matter is the only non-profit agency in Texas offering free temporary foster care placement and aftercare support services specifically for dogs of people with addictions receiving substance abuse treatment. Dogs Matter has just become a Department of Dallas Pets Alive, with over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to Dogs Matter, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dogs Matter visit: https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ Team: Operations / Marketing Location : Part time Remote/Virtual - Virtual from anywhere, understanding we are in the CST time zone. Part-time In-person, DFW area Time Commitment: Maximum 10 Hours a week Team: Marketing Reports to: Social Media Manager & Director of Operations Works with: This position has primary working relationships with the Directors of Dogs Matter, and the Marketing Director of DPA. The Social Media Manager collaborates on projects with the extended marketing team including Fundraising and Graphic Design team members. Responsibilities: Content Creation: Develop and schedule engaging posts, stories, and multimedia content tailored to the brand’s voice and audience. Social Media Strategy : Assist in creating and implementing a social media plan to align with the company’s goals and increase online presence. Community Engagement : Monitor engagement to build relationships with the audience and address inquiries promptly. Analytics & Reporting : Track performance metrics (likes, shares, clicks, reach) and provide regular reports with insights and recommendations for improvement. Platform Management : Maintain and update social media, TikTok profile Campaign Support : Collaborate with marketing teams to promote events, campaigns, or products that will help increase brand awareness, drive fostering, and generate/retain donor/support of the foundation Trend Monitoring : Stay informed about industry trends, platform updates, and competitor strategies to adapt and innovate. Ad Management : Assist in creating and monitoring paid ad campaigns to maximize ROI (if applicable). Brand Consistency: Ensure all social media content aligns with DM & DPA’s branding and messaging guidelines. Collaboration & Support: Design strategies, methodologies, and goals for communication and marketing, support with press releases Assisting: social media coordinator and volunteers on strategy to ensure consistency of message, and any other social media duties needed to help drive Dogs Matter and Dallas Pets Alive!’s mission and support our values Qualifications/Skills: Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare Must love animals! Demonstrated skills, knowledge and experience in the design and execution of marketing strategies, management of digital marketing channels, communications plans, and social media strategies. Strong creative, strategic, analytical, organizational and personal sales skills. Experience overseeing the design and production of print materials and publications. Excellent writing and editing skills Solid understanding of and experience with digital marketing channels, KPIs, and marketing to diverse audiences (donors, adopters, community at large). Ability to make decisions and follow through with minimal supervision Candidate must be a self starter and comfortable with both taking initiative and working collaboratively with a team Ability to dedicate approximately 5-10 hours per week with flexible hours. Strong understanding of branding Strong project management and multi-tasking skills Ability to work effectively with a diverse range of individuals and groups Effective communicator and troubleshooter Ability to make decisions and follow through with minimal supervision Strong knowledge and active use of social media, specifically TikTok Self-motivated, collaborative and adaptable with a strong sense of responsibility Highly collaborative; ability to work independently and as part of a larger team including supervising others Enthusiastic, Detail-oriented, Self-motivated, Positive Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities We require that you have your own computer and have access to the internet to complete the tasks listed above. Previous non-profit marketing experience is a plus. Dallas Pets Alive! Musts: Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times A motivational, can-do attitude to represent Dallas Pets Alive! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare Time Commitment: up to 10 hours per week

opportunity_title: Time Travel -  Dallas Volunteer Assignment
description_short: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and…
description_long: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and each race is a chance to create a ripple effect of positivity - encouraging families to get moving, friends to bond over fitness, and communities to come together around a shared goal: better health. We couldn’t make any of this happen without the help of incredible volunteers like you. We’re currently seeking volunteers for our upcoming event - and we need YOUR help to bring it to life. Whether you’re signing up solo, with your family, or rallying a group of coworkers or classmates, there’s a spot for everyone to get involved and make a difference. As a thank-you, every volunteer gets ONE of the following: Two future race entries (Bodies Race) A $20 Amazon gift card BONUS: Community service hours signed off Volunteer Roles - Here’s How You Can Help: Aid Station Crew - Highest Need Keep our runners fueled and focused. Hand out water, pick up discarded cups, and bring positive energy to everyone who passes by. Every high five counts! Course Monitor Be the spirit of the course! Guide runners on the right path and cheer them on with signs, music, or even a fun costume. You’ll be keeping the course safe and fun. Lead Bike Volunteer - High Priority Lead the way! Use your own bike and helmet to guide runners throughout the course. You’ll pre-ride the route in advance and be a key player on race day. Course Photographer If you love capturing special moments, this one’s for you. Bring your own camera and head to a scenic course spot to catch the smiles, the grit, and the joy of race day. Finish Line Volunteer Be part of the celebration! Hand out medals, manage the awards table, and help runners feel the reward of their hard work. We’re looking for individuals, friend groups, student organizations, businesses, and families who want to be part of something bigger. Come spend your morning making memories, supporting wellness in your city, and cheering on every single finish line victory. Let’s build a healthier community, together. Sign up to volunteer today - we can’t do it without you!

opportunity_title: Transportation  Volunteer
description_short: Serving Our Seniors (formerly known as Mid-Cities Care Corp) provides transportation for senior citizens to scheduled routine medical appointments and "quality of life" rides (grocery shopping, banking, trips to the pharmacy, etc.) within Northeast…
description_long: Serving Our Seniors (formerly known as Mid-Cities Care Corp) provides transportation for senior citizens to scheduled routine medical appointments and "quality of life" rides (grocery shopping, banking, trips to the pharmacy, etc.) within Northeast Tarrant County cities of: Bedford, Colleyville, Euless, Grapevine, Haltom City, Hurst, Keller, N Richland Hills, Richland Hills, Southlake and Watauga. Transportation is provided by caring volunteers who provide their vehicle, time, and gas to transport senior neighbors. A typical volunteer drive includes picking the client up at their residence, driving the client to the appointment, staying with them the duration of the appointment, and returning the client to their residence. An individual with 3 hours a month to spare is considered a potential volunteer candidate for SOS. The average time for a volunteer drive is 3 hours. Our clients schedule all their appointments Monday through Friday between the hours of 9a.m. and 3p.m. Volunteers have the flexibility to accept or decline drive opportunities based upon their personal schedules. All volunteers must pass a criminal background check and have a clean driving record. SOS does not income qualify clients and never charges for services because we recognize that most of our clients are on limited income.

org_name: SELF REFLECTION GIRLS & WOMEN ORGANIZATION INC
opportunity_title: Treasurer (Sponsored Board of Directors)
description_short: The Duties of the Treasurer/Head of Fundraising (Sponsored board of Directors is position you will be required to donate to assist with the functioning on the organization) 1. CoCreate budget for the organization with the President. 2. Generate…
description_long: The Duties of the Treasurer/Head of Fundraising (Sponsored board of Directors is position you will be required to donate to assist with the functioning on the organization) 1. CoCreate budget for the organization with the President. 2. Generate financial reports internally and externally. 3. Ensuring funds are readily available, and to pay bills and comply with other financial stakeholders to ensure tax compliance. 4. Monitor the organization's cash flow. 5. Oversee the organization’s financial administration. 6. Review and enforce financial policies and procedures and edit by-laws accordingly. 7. Advise the Board and staff on financial strategies and fundraising. 8. Head of Fundraising Maintains records of donors and Organizes campaigns that will lead to donations 9. Head of Fundraising cowrites funding proposals with the Executive Director and sends to potential sponsor 10. HOF shall set strategies and participate in fundraising for the organization and manages the funding committee for the Board

opportunity_title: Trinity Hospice Admin Volunteer- Help answer phones (Weekday during business hours)
description_short: Build your administrative skills while assisting in the office. Know the insights of an hospice office and learn basic computer skills. We will provide you on hand training and guidance throughout your experience. We are looking for volunteers to…
description_long: Build your administrative skills while assisting in the office. Know the insights of an hospice office and learn basic computer skills. We will provide you on hand training and guidance throughout your experience. We are looking for volunteers to help answer phones during business hours. This is a perfect for a college student that is needing volunteer hours. You can even bring school work to do in between calls. This oppurtunity is during Business hours Monday- Friday. This opportunity will be a great resume builder! Start as soon as possible. YOU build your own schedule. Assist weekly or even monthly. To Get Started: Application Phone Interview Background Check Orientation Administrative Duties: Assisting with filing, typing, and answering the phones We provide full training in Hospice philosophy and volunteer duties. Orientation and on-going training provided! Background and reference check required. We will work with your schedule! We have many ways for volunteers to engage and change the lives of our patients, follow our Volunteer Match page or visit our website www.trinityhospice.com/volunteers

opportunity_title: Turning patches into a Blanket for Breast Cancer survivors

opportunity_title: US Veteran Hospice Patient Companion Volunteer-Dallas
description_short: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Getting out of the service doesn’t mean you have to stop having someone's back or living heroic values like courage and integrity. Hope Health Care has an opportunity for you to continue to serve…
description_long: Those Who Can, Do - Those Who Can Do More, Volunteer Getting out of the service doesn’t mean you have to stop having someone's back or living heroic values like courage and integrity. Hope Health Care has an opportunity for you to continue to serve others but on your own time and in your way. Hope Health Care serves veterans who are on Hospice care. They need an ally who will assist them with completing their final mission in life. Hospice provides Medicare entitled services for the veteran to feel comfortable and free of pain. But that doesn’t mean these veterans are not full of life. The veterans we serve have amazing stories to share with those willing to listen. They can engage heartily and leave you feeling more grateful than you expected to feel when you arrived. If you want to help a veteran approach their final days with courage and dignity, please reach out to us today regarding this incredible opportunity. We are certain you will be glad you did. TRAINING Volunteers receive training free of charge as well as on-going education and support. TIME Your schedule is up to you! Most volunteers serve between 1 to 4 hours per week! LOCATION If you'd like to be a veteran to veteran patient Companion volunteer we will match you with a veteran who lives in or around your community

opportunity_title: United Way of Tarrant County at 4Saints Food Pantry
description_short: At 4Saints Food Pantry, their mission is to serve with dignity and humility the nutritional needs of those seeking assistance. As a premier partner of the Tarrant Area Food Bank, this is the largest food pantry in Fort Worth's historic and diverse…
description_long: At 4Saints Food Pantry, their mission is to serve with dignity and humility the nutritional needs of those seeking assistance. As a premier partner of the Tarrant Area Food Bank, this is the largest food pantry in Fort Worth's historic and diverse East Lancaster area. Unfortunately, East Lancaster is considered a "food desert" because access to affordable, nutritious food options is highly limited and there is no full-sized grocery store in the area making 4Saints and Friends critical to the community. Date: Friday, June 26 Time: 11am to 2:30pm Location: 4Saints Food Pantry – 2529 Mount View Ave., Fort Worth, TX 76103 Volunteers Needed: 5 Volunteer Duties: Volunteers will sort and organize food in the pantry, help clients with shopping and loading vehicles. Other: Volunteers are welcome to bring a drink/snack and take as many breaks as needed during their shift. Parking: Parking is available behind the building in the parking lot located on the corner of Mt. Vernon Ave. and Clairmont Ave. Questions? Please contact Victoria Walton, Volunteer Relations Manager at: victoria.walton@unitedwaytarrant.org

opportunity_title: United Way of Tarrant County at CEC Food Pantry
description_short: United Way of Tarrant County at CEC Food Pantry – Wed., May 27 - 8:30am to 1pm In-person volunteer opportunity For more than 30 years, CEC has been helping low-income neighbors with a variety of services including food support. The CEC Food Pantry…
description_long: United Way of Tarrant County at CEC Food Pantry – Wed., May 27 - 8:30am to 1pm In-person volunteer opportunity For more than 30 years, CEC has been helping low-income neighbors with a variety of services including food support. The CEC Food Pantry provides free grocery assistance to any household in Tarrant County and 12 additional counties with a gross household income of 180% of poverty level or less. Date: Wednesday, May 27 Time: 8:30am to 1pm Location: CEC Food Pantry, 6250 NE Loop 820, North Richland Hills, TX 76180 Volunteers Needed: 7 Volunteer Duties: Volunteers will sort and restock pantry shelves, build grocery bags for clients, assist with loading client vehicles and potentially sweeping and taking out trash. Other: Children 10 and older are welcome to participate with a parent or guardian. Volunteers are welcome to bring a drink and snack for their shift and breaks can be taken whenever needed. Parking: Parking is available in the agency parking lot. Questions? Please contact Victoria Walton, Volunteer Relations Manager at: victoria.walton@unitedwaytarrant.org Please contact me if you would like to schedule a larger volunteer team at this location.

opportunity_title: United Way of Tarrant County at GRACE Donation Station
description_short: Based in Grapevine, Texas, GRACE is a nonprofit relief agency that provides food, clothing, financial assistance, and other vital necessities to people who are struggling with a limited income or recent emergency. The Donation Station is a drive…
description_long: Based in Grapevine, Texas, GRACE is a nonprofit relief agency that provides food, clothing, financial assistance, and other vital necessities to people who are struggling with a limited income or recent emergency. The Donation Station is a drive through drop-off site where all community donations are processed. Donations are sorted to first meet GRACE client needs and the rest being prepared to stock our resale stores. With up to 300 cars pulling through each day, our volunteer team makes sure we can turn around these donations as quickly as possible. Date: Friday, July 31 Time: 8:30am to 12:30pm Location: GRACE Donation Station, 1060 Texan Trail, Grapevine, TX 76051 Volunteers Needed: 10 Volunteer duties: Volunteers will assist with unloading donations from vehicles, inspecting items, sorting by category, testing electronics, pricing and scanning and more! Parking: Parking is available in the lot next to the Donation Station. Other: Volunteers will work indoors and will only be outside to remove items from vehicles as they come arrive at the drive through. Volunteers are encouraged to dress comfortably, and closed toe shoes are required. Volunteers are welcome to take a water bottle and snack for their shift and can take as many breaks as needed. Questions? Please contact Victoria Walton, Volunteer Relations Manager at: victoria.walton@unitedwaytarrant.org Please contact victoria.walton@unitedwaytarrant.org if you would like to schedule a larger volunteer team at this location.

opportunity_title: University Park, TX - Drive cancer patients to and from treatment
description_short: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their…
description_long: Help cancer patients get to life-saving treatment. As a Road To Recovery volunteer driver with the American Cancer Society, you’ll use your personal vehicle to provide free rides for cancer patients who have no other way to get to their appointments. Most rides are Monday-Friday during the day. You choose the rides that fit your schedule, with a suggested minimum of 2 rides per month. Volunteer drivers must: Be age 18-84 Have a valid driver’s license and safe driving record Use a reliable, insured vehicle Pass background and motor vehicle records checks Complete online training and use our online ride scheduling tool Learn more at cancer.org/drive. We need you now more than ever. Watch our video to learn more: Hope Behind The Wheel

opportunity_title: Urgent: Host Families Needed for Exchange Students from Spain (Fall 2026)
description_short: Above & Beyond Education is seeking welcoming host families in the community to open their hearts and homes to international exchange students for the upcoming academic year. Hosting an exchange student is a deeply rewarding volunteer experience…
description_long: Above & Beyond Education is seeking welcoming host families in the community to open their hearts and homes to international exchange students for the upcoming academic year. Hosting an exchange student is a deeply rewarding volunteer experience that brings the world directly to your family and community. We are currently on a tight timeline to find placements for our remaining students, including two wonderful young women from Spain who are fully vetted, insured, and ready for their American adventure: Meet Sara from Spain Passions & Interests: Cooking, fitness, beach trips, crafts, and animal care Sara is a thoughtful, confident, and hardworking 15-year-old from Spain who will be entering the 11th grade. While she can be a bit reserved at first, she is incredibly kind, observant, and truly opens up once she feels comfortable. Having grown up traveling with her family, Sara is highly adaptable and open-minded. She developed a passion for cooking at a young age alongside her father and loves trying out new, healthy recipes. She values an active lifestyle, enjoys going to the gym, playing sports, and spending time at the beach or doing crafts. Fun fact: Sara dreams of becoming a future veterinarian! She is excited to build a deep connection with her host family and share her vibrant culture. (Note: Sara requests a home with no cats, please!) Meet Patricia from Spain Passions & Interests: Volleyball, choir, pop music & karaoke, and reading Patricia is a cheerful, friendly, and hardworking 14-year-old from Spain entering the 10th grade. She has a bright, social personality and absolutely loves being around people. Patricia is incredibly passionate about music—she sings in the choir, loves listening to pop music, and is always down for karaoke! She is also a dedicated athlete who loves playing volleyball and hopes to join her local American high school team. Patricia is an animal lover (with experience caring for birds and hamsters) and is fascinated by the legal system, dreaming of one day becoming a lawyer! Ever since visiting New York City at eight years old, she has dreamed of studying abroad, and she can't wait to jump into school activities and share new memories with a host family. What is required to host? Host families simply provide a safe, supportive home environment, a designated bed for the student (can share a room with a same-sex sibling close in age), and three daily meals. Families of all shapes and sizes make excellent hosts, including empty nesters, single parents, and families with young children. Above & Beyond Education provides 24/7 local coordinator support throughout the entire exchange period. How to Apply / Learn More: If you have a spare bedroom and an open heart, you can change a student's life this fall. For more information on Sara, Patricia, or our other remaining students, please contact Laura Braley immediately: Phone: 267-864-8900 Email: laurabraley@anbeducation.com Website: anbeducation.com

opportunity_title: VA Representative
description_short: Support our nation's Hero's by stepping up to oversee all VA events! The VA Representative helps plan and deliver support to our Active Duty and Veterans at non-VA events. You will be working with community leaders and civic organizations to bring…
description_long: Support our nation's Hero's by stepping up to oversee all VA events! The VA Representative helps plan and deliver support to our Active Duty and Veterans at non-VA events. opportunity_title: VENUS: Visit a Lonely Senior Citizen
description_short: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody…
description_long: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody remember important moments in their lives. You can be one of our Volunteers When you give us the precious gift of your time and interest, we give you a chance to make a real difference in someone’s life. We train you, find you the right assignment and stand behind you as you grow. Patient Volunteer Part of a Patient’s Care Team. You will make weekly visits to offer companionship for Patients. Examples of Patient care Volunteer opportunities include reading to the Patient, assisting with a hobby, conversation, listening to music or even watching a TV show together. Work with the Volunteer Coordinator to enter facilities & visit your Hospice Patient. Volunteers are matched with 1 Patient at a time to someone within a 15-minute radius to where you live. Requirements: Must be able to commit to Volunteer for one year @ 1 x per week for up to 1 hour minimum. No resume needed but an excellent skill and resume builder. Volunteer Coordinator always goes along with you on your 1st Patient visit.

opportunity_title: VETERAN TO VETERAN HOSPICE VOLUNTEERS NEEDED!-Opportunities in Dallas/Collin/Rockwall/Hunt/Kaufman Counties
description_short: THERE'S NO BETTER WAY TO SAY "THANK YOU" TO OUR VETERANS THAN TO SERVE THEM! Whether at home or abroad, during combat or peace-time, a soldier's bravery, dedication and sacrifice to help preserve our country’s safety and freedom, makes them a Hero.…
description_long: THERE'S NO BETTER WAY TO SAY "THANK YOU" TO OUR VETERANS THAN TO SERVE THEM! Whether at home or abroad, during combat or peace-time, a soldier's bravery, dedication and sacrifice to help preserve our country’s safety and freedom, makes them a Hero. Will YOU answer the call and become a Veteran to Veteran Hospice Volunteer? Hospice volunteering in your community can be a very fulfilling experience. YOU can play a vital role in caring for our veteran patients and their families. You would serve in your community for a couple of hours each week. Just by showing up and offering companionship, socialization, a listening ear, and a comforting presence to a fellow veteran, during one of their life’s most difficult times, would mean more to them than you could every imagine. Wil you have the courage to answer the call and walk alongside a fellow veteran as they live out their last days, showing them the dignity, honor and respect they deserve? If this is you and you would like to be part of our Veteran To Veteran program, please complete the interest form and I will respond very soon. THANK YOU FOR YOUR SERVICE!

opportunity_title: VOLUNTEERS NEEDED - Special Needs Egg Hunt - Volunteer Opportunity
description_short: VOLUNTEER AND MAKE A DIFFERENCE! Hello Especially Needed Volunteers! We need volunteers for our Saturday 3/28 at 11am, 12pm or 1:15pm for an Egg Hunt in Frisco TX (families arrive later). CURRENT OPEN VOLUNTEER SHIFT OPPORTUNITIES: Pre-Event…
description_long: VOLUNTEER AND MAKE A DIFFERENCE! Hello Especially Needed Volunteers! We need volunteers for our Saturday 3/28 at 11am, 12pm or 1:15pm for an Egg Hunt in Frisco TX (families arrive later). CURRENT OPEN VOLUNTEER SHIFT OPPORTUNITIES: Pre-Event Volunteer Stuff Eggs at your home 3/24-3/27 (Tues-Fri) Volunteers Pre-Event Load Truck 11am-12pm Volunteers Event Setup, 12pm-1:45pm Event Volunteers LEAD, 12:15pm-5pm Volunteers Egg Distribution & Stuffing, 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Easter Bunny & Helper, 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Wear Bunny Costume, 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Photographers, 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Registration Desk, 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Help with Booth/Games 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Parking Directors 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Event Shift, 1:15pm-4:30pm Volunteers Post-Event Load Truck 4pm-5pm Help us put on one of the largest Easter Egg Hunts for individuals with special needs. Especially Needed can't host such a fun and exciting event without the assistance of amazing volunteers like you! As always, our event is filled with fun activities for the families. We will have games, a bounce houses, face painting, a balloon artist, a resource fair and much more! Ready to be a part of the magic this year? Deadline to register is Friday 3/27/2025. We have several volunteer shifts available and know that every single role is vital to the success of the event. There are a couple of things to consider before signing up to volunteer: We ask that all volunteers are 14 and up. We ask that any students that require written validation of their volunteer hours seek out our volunteer coordinator after your volunteer shift is over so we can sign your paperwork. Be aware of the weather - you are signing up for a shift outside and if it is cold or warm, please dress appropriately. Please wear closed-toed shoes and/or anything else you may need to be comfortable. Honor your commitment - we need all hands on deck during the entire shift. Bring a positive attitude - it's a fun event and a beautiful day to be a part of! Be ready to work, have fun and make a difference with Especially Needed! We look forward to seeing familiar faces and meeting a whole lot of new ones. Please don't hesitate to e-mail us if you have any questions regarding volunteering; Our volunteer coordinator is Nechole Whitlock, you can contact her at 972-567-8005 or nechole@especiallyneeded.org. If you would like to donate to Especially Needed, sponsor this event, be part of the resource fair or inquire about anything regarding Especially Needed, please visit our website or contact Nechole or Heather at the information listed above. Thank you for being a part of such a wonderful event! It would not be a success without you! In Celebration, The Especially Needed Team

opportunity_title: VOUNTEER BASED WITH PAID PATHWAY DATA ANALYTICS AND PERFORMANCE INTELLIGENCE LEAD
description_short: The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) is seeking a mission-driven and organized individual to support data tracking, dashboard reporting, and organizational performance visibility. You will also support our digital operating systems and building…
description_long: The Grandparents Community Network (TGCN) is seeking a mission-driven and organized individual to support data tracking, dashboard reporting, and organizational performance visibility. You will also support our digital operating systems and building infrastructure for the company. This role will assist with maintaining reports, tracking measurable impact, organizing information, and helping leadership better understand program growth, community needs, and operational progress. The ideal candidate should have experience with dashboards, spreadsheets, reporting systems, or digital workspaces and should know how to use or be willing to learn Notion. You should also be willing to work with standard operating procedures and expectations. You must be a citizen of the United States and have a valid US identification. This is a volunteer leadership support opportunity designed for individuals who want to grow with a mission-focused organization while gaining experience in data analytics, reporting systems, and organizational intelligence. 🔹 POSITION TYPE & GROWTH OPPORTUNITY This position begins as a volunteer support role with a structured pathway toward paid opportunities based on: Organizational growth Funding availability Performance and consistency Leadership alignment Contribution to measurable outcomes TGCN believes in developing long-term team members by recognizing dedication, accountability, initiative, and mission impact . Submit resume to: gloryabolden@tgcncorporate.org and why you would be a good fit for this position. If you just want to volunteer to get resume get experience with no intentions of commitment, please do not apply respectfully. If you want to volunteer with pay as your only motivation to help. DO NOT APPLY.

description_short: Are you a veteran and want to help support other vets at the end of life? Veterans at the end of life deserve the best kind of support from someone who understands! Become a Vet to Vet volunteer with GOOD SHEPHERD HOSPICE! Vet to Vet volunteers…
description_long: Are you a veteran and want to help support other vets at the end of life? Veterans at the end of life deserve the best kind of support from someone who understands! Become a Vet to Vet volunteer with GOOD SHEPHERD HOSPICE! Vet to Vet volunteers spend time with hospice and palliative care patients in the patient's home. Some activities may include: Reading, playing music, or watching TV with the patient Providing temporary respite care for a veteran's family Listening, telling stories or being a comforting presence Light household tasks or errand running Pet care Assisting patients or their caregivers with things like snack preparation If this opportunity interests you, please go to our website at www.goodshepherdhospice.com or contact our Volunteer Coordinator today at 972.482.4382 or email shauntae.harris@goodshepherdhospice.com

opportunity_title: Veteran to Veteran Hospice Volunteer Program
description_short: Merit Hospice is recruiting Veteran Volunteers to work with Veteran Hospice Patients! Veterans Volunteers have a unique ability to relate and connect with hospice patients during their end of life journey. It is Merit Hospice's goal to build a…
description_long: Merit Hospice is recruiting Veteran Volunteers to work with Veteran Hospice Patients! Veterans Volunteers have a unique ability to relate and connect with hospice patients during their end of life journey. It is Merit Hospice's goal to build a thriving Veteran Volunteer program! We need your help! If you are interested in volunteering for any of the following, please reach out: -Companionship to patients and caregivers -A We Honor Veterans (WHV) Program leadership role and helping us to become a Level 5 WHV Hospice (www.wehonorveterans.org) -Assist with Veteran Community Outreach to ensure veterans are knowledgeable about VA benefits they are entilted to; assistance with resources and education on end of life care through VA and community resources! Requirements: Compassionate and Ethical; complete applications process (18 years+; Auto-insurance; TB test, at no cost; pass a background check); complete volunteer training. Scheduling is very flexible once onboarding is complete. Service Areas: Collin, Dallas and Denton Counties Watch our video to learn more: Impact Video by We Honor Veteran Program

opportunity_title: Veteran to Veteran Volunteers wanted
description_short: We are gaining our Stars from the VA, have 2 Stars and working on #3. Our company is committed to getting companionship to those terminally ill patients that have served our country. As Veterans know, you don't talk about your service to the general…
description_long: We are gaining our Stars from the VA, have 2 Stars and working on #3. Our company is committed to getting companionship to those terminally ill patients that have served our country. As Veterans know, you don't talk about your service to the general public. There are things that you have seen, done, and witnessed that you hold inside. Our Veterans are just the same. Some do not want a volunteer at all because they don't have anything to talk about, or they do not connect on the same level. Veterans are amazing, the stories they have to share, the support they can offer to their fellow soldiers. We want to provide volunteers to talk to our Veteran patients, that will make them feel comfortable, and have someone they can tell about their experiences. They want that person they can identify with. If this is an opportunity that you would be able to fill, we want to provide a patient for you to visit. We try to keep visits to once or twice a week, and at their facility, we try to match a patient close to your home, if possible. We would like to talk to you about this opportunity. Please don't hesitate to respond to this email, please. If you do pet therapy, that would be great as well.
