opportunity_title: Victim Advocate
description_short: Organization: Gideon's Army is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit focused on rescuing survivors of human trafficking through direct intervention and coordinated logistical efforts. Role Overview: A Victim Advocate is the primary support person for a survivor…
description_long: Organization: Gideon's Army is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit focused on rescuing survivors of human trafficking through direct intervention and coordinated logistical efforts. Role Overview: A Victim Advocate is the primary support person for a survivor during and immediately after their exit ("rescue") from exploitation. While the other team members manage mission-planning, movement, and logistics, the Advocate manages the human side of the operation - arguably the most important piece. This includes keeping the survivor calm, grounded, and willing to continue forward. This role requires strong interpersonal skills, emotional bearing, and empathy. Core Functions: Immediate Stabilization Establish a calm, safe presence from the first point of contact Reduce panic, fear, or distrust through tone, posture, and trauma-informed communication Help the survivor feel safe, confident, and focused on the next step towards recovery Continuous Support During Transport Remain physically present with the survivor throughout the relocation process Maintain a steady, reassuring environment inside the vehicle Monitor the survivor's emotional state, body language, and shifts in mood Intervene early with trauma-informed care if anxiety, distress, or hesitation increases Communication & Trust-Building Speak in a clear, grounded, and non-judgmental way Avoid overwhelming the survivor with too much information Answer questions honestly while keeping things simple and stabilizing Build trust professionally and appropriately Situational Awareness (Focus on Survivor) Maintain focus on survivor at all times and honor their autonomy Recognize signs of panic, shutdown, or potential fight/flight response Provide crisis stabilization without escalating tension Transition Support Help prepare the survivor mentally for arrival to the aftercare facility Provide reassurance about what comes next Maintain continuity so the survivor doesn't feel "handed off" abruptly Operational Reality You may be sitting inches from someone who is in extreme fear, confusion, or emotional conflict They may change their mind, shut down, or test your trust mid-transport The environment must stay calm and controlled Your ability to remain steady and model appropriate boundaries directly impacts whether they feel safe, and the mission continues smoothly Key Traits for a good Victim Advocate Emotional Bearing - you stay grounded no matter what they are feeling or saying Presence - model calm, steady energy that they can anchor to Active listening - being fully present, with your full attention Non-Judgmental - listening without judgement; providing neutral answers Adaptability - adjusting your approach based on their behavior and response Patience & Honoring Autonomy - giving them space when they need it, letting them talk when they need it, providing guidance when they need it, and respecting their dignity and autonomy at all times What this role is NOT Therapy or diagnosis Interrogation Controlling the individual Mission Impact This role is about supporting someone through one of the most intense transitions of their lifetime, without adding more pressure or instability onto them. The Victim Advocate if often the deciding factor in whether someone follows through with leaving or turns back. A calm, trustworthy Victim Advocate can carry someone through fear, doubt, and uncertainty all the way to lifelong healing. Preferred (Not Required) A background in social work, therapy/psychology, victim advocacy, medical or mental health fields, OR; Prior experience working in outreach/trafficking prevention, anti-trafficking intervention, or aftercare, OR; Prior experience working with vulnerable populations such as children Required Ability to pass a background check and multi-step vetting process Ability to attend one, 4-day training

opportunity_title: Video Shorts Editor Volunteer
description_short: Be Kingdom Builders is growing, the content is strong, and we are looking for someone who wants to help take it to the next level. We are a 501(c)(3) digital discipleship ministry calling believers out of passive Christianity and into real,…
description_long: Be Kingdom Builders is growing, the content is strong, and we are looking for someone who wants to help take it to the next level. We are a 501(c)(3) digital discipleship ministry calling believers out of passive Christianity and into real, Spirit-led obedience. We run a weekly live teaching stream on YouTube, and we batch record 8 to 12 short-form videos every week, each running around 60 seconds. The content has weight. It has the kind of clarity that can stop someone mid-scroll and genuinely shift how they think about their faith. We need the right editor to make sure it lands that way. We are looking for a volunteer short-form video editor comfortable working in Descript. You would be editing our batch-recorded Shorts, Reels, and YouTube Shorts for maximum impact, along with pulling clips from our weekly live teaching when the moment calls for it. Captions, pacing, cuts. The volume is consistent and the workflow is already built. You are stepping into a rhythm, not building one from scratch. This is a volunteer role, but it is not a small opportunity. You will work directly with the founder, build a portfolio of finished ministry content, and receive a professional reference and LinkedIn recommendation. As the ministry scales, this role has a real path to becoming paid. One thing worth mentioning: Be Kingdom Builders is a Christian ministry, and we do ask that everyone who serves on our team shares that foundation. You do not need a theology degree or a perfect track record. But you do need a genuine, active faith in Jesus Christ and a heart for what this ministry is doing. We will ask for a brief testimony as part of your application, not to put you on the spot, but because this work flows out of something real and we want everyone building it to be rooted in the same thing. To apply, send your resume, a few samples of your work, and a brief testimony to volunteer@bekingdombuilders.com . Tell us who you are, what you believe, and why this opportunity caught your attention.

opportunity_title: Videographer (HYBRID)
description_short: Videographer Hybrid Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability, and provide a healthy and loving environment…
description_long: Videographer Hybrid Volunteer Position Dogs Matter is a unique and innovative 501(c)(3) organization whose mission is to provide the needed services and support to gain long-term sobriety and stability, and provide a healthy and loving environment for their pet. Dogs Matter is the only non-profit agency in Texas offering free temporary foster care placement and aftercare support services specifically for pets of people with addictions receiving substance abuse treatment. Dogs Matter has become a department of Dallas Pets Alive (DPA). With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to Dogs Matter, you directly impact our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dogs Matter visit https://www.dogsmatter2.org/ As our Videographer, you’ll play an important role in capturing compelling visual content for interviews and reunions that showcase our mission and purpose. Your creative skills will contribute to raising awareness, engaging supporters, and promoting the overall impact of our work. Help us make sure every heartfelt moment gets the spotlight it deserves! Team: Dogs Matter Location: Hybrid, located in Dallas-Forth Worth (DFW). Reunions and interviews may be held at multiple locations throughout the Dallas-Fort Worth area. Time Commitment: 5-10 hours per week/month. Schedule varies based on reunions, and events. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: Dogs Matter Director of Operations and Executive Director Works with: Dog owners, foster parents, and the entire Dogs Matter team Primary Responsibilities: Plan, shoot, and edit video content for various purposes, including marketing campaigns, social media posts, product demonstrations, interviews, and corporate events. Operate video cameras, lighting equipment, and audio recording devices to capture high-quality footage, ensuring proper exposure, focus, and framing. Conduct interviews with subjects and stakeholders, asking relevant questions to elicit engaging responses and stories. Edit video footage using industry-standard software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools including Canva,, incorporating music, graphics, and other visual elements as needed. Ensure video content is optimized for various distribution channels, including websites, social media platforms, email campaigns, and digital advertising. Collaborate with the DPA Marketing team as needed. Qualifications: Proven experience as a videographer, with a strong portfolio showcasing your creative work across various video formats and styles and the ability to tell visual stories Proficiency in videography techniques, including camera operation, lighting setup, audio recording, and composition Expertise in video editing software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools, with a strong understanding of video editing principles and techniques Excellent storytelling skills and the ability to convey messages effectively through visual storytelling Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously Skill in handling delicate situations and sensitive information with professionalism, empathy, and care Embrace Dogs Matter’s mission and values A team and service-oriented mentality Understanding of animal welfare or recovery is a plus Excellent interpersonal and communication skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a non-profit environment Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; Spanish is a major plus! What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values. Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional-level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org. Watch our video to learn more: Dallas Pets Alive!

opportunity_title: Videographer (Marketing)
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As our Videographer, you’ll play an important role in capturing compelling visual content that showcases our organization's mission, animals, and activities. Your creative skills will contribute to raising awareness, engaging supporters, and promoting the overall impact of our non-profit. Team: Marketing Location : In Person - Located in Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex and be mainly in-person at our Adoption Container or Shelter. The DPA Container is located at the Dallas Community Beer Garden 3110 Commonwealth Dr, Dallas, TX 75247. Humane Society of Dallas County is located at 2719 Manor Way Dr. Dallas Texas 75235. Time Commitment: 1 project per quarter. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Position Responsibilities: Plan, shoot, and edit video content for various purposes, including marketing campaigns, social media posts, product demonstrations, interviews, and corporate events. Collaborate with the marketing team to understand project objectives, target audience demographics, and brand messaging, and translate these into compelling video concepts and scripts. Operate video cameras, lighting equipment, and audio recording devices to capture high-quality footage, ensuring proper exposure, focus, and framing. Conduct interviews with subjects and stakeholders, asking relevant questions to elicit engaging responses and stories. Edit video footage using industry-standard software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools, incorporating music, graphics, and other visual elements as needed. Ensure video content is optimized for various distribution channels, including websites, social media platforms, email campaigns, and digital advertising. Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Proven experience as a videographer, with a strong portfolio showcasing your creative work across various video formats and styles. Proficiency in videography techniques, including camera operation, lighting setup, audio recording, and composition. Expertise in video editing software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools, with a strong understanding of video editing principles and techniques. Excellent storytelling skills and the ability to convey messages effectively through visual storytelling. Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously under tight deadlines. Strong portfolio showcasing your videography skills and ability to tell visual stories Understanding of non-profit and animal welfare issues is a plus Excellent interpersonal and communication skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: DPA is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and the communities we serve. We embrace challenges and lead with positive intent, welcoming all people who have a caring and compassionate dedication to animal welfare. Dallas Pets Alive champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. The golden rule is expected: Treat all living beings as you would like to be treated. DPA has a zero tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Virtus Barber & Co. Soup Drive with Soup Angels
description_short: Volunteer Opportunity: Neighborhood Soup Drive & Kindness Pop‑Up at Virtus Barber & Co. Organization: Soup Angels Location: Virtus Barber & Co. — Parking Lot (Dallas, TX) Date & Time: Saturday, April 25, 2026 • 1:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m.…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunity: Neighborhood Soup Drive & Kindness Pop‑Up at Virtus Barber & Co. Organization: Soup Angels Location: Virtus Barber & Co. — Parking Lot (Dallas, TX) Date & Time: Saturday, April 25, 2026 • 1:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. Commitment: 2.5–3 hours Great for: Individuals, families, teens, community groups, first‑time volunteers 💛 About the Event Soup Angels and Virtus Barber & Co. are teaming up for a joyful, high‑impact Soup Drive and Kindness Pop‑Up to support our unhoused neighbors across Dallas. This is more than a donation drop‑off — it’s a mini block‑party of generosity, connection, and community spirit. We’re looking for volunteers who want to help create a welcoming, energetic, and dignifying experience for every neighbor who donates or stops by. 🙌 Volunteer Roles Volunteers will help bring the event to life through a variety of fun, interactive stations. Roles include: 🎤 Good Deeds Shout‑Out Mic Assistant Celebrate donors with live shout‑outs and keep the energy high. 📸 Barber Chair Photo Throne Host Welcome donors, help them take their “Champion of Kindness” portrait, and cheer them on. 🥣 Guess the Soup Ingredient Game Guide Run the sniff‑and‑guess game and award the title of “Soup Genius” to winners. 🕺 Dance‑Off Hype Crew Help track donation milestones and spark friendly Barber vs. Soup Angel dance‑offs. 🥫 Bag Toss for Good Attendant Manage the beanbag toss station and encourage friendly competition. ✍️ Wall of Gratitude Steward Invite guests to write messages of hope that will travel with future meal bags. 🎶 Barbershop Beats Support Assist barbers as they rotate through DJ hour and keep the vibes flowing. 🐶 Pet Parade Helper Hand out “Soup Angel Pup” tags and help pups and their humans feel celebrated. 🎨 Kids’ Kindness Corner Volunteer Support kids as they create drawings that will be included in outreach bags. 💚 Why This Matters Soup Angels is a grassroots, community‑powered mission serving warm meals, dignity, and connection to our unhoused neighbors across Dallas. Every can, packet, and hygiene item collected helps us reach more people with compassion and care. By volunteering, you help transform a simple Saturday afternoon into something meaningful — joyful service, community connection, and tangible impact. ⭐ Who We’re Looking For Friendly, welcoming volunteers People who enjoy community events and lighthearted fun Families and teens (with guardian) are welcome No experience needed — just a warm heart and willingness to help 📝 How to Get Involved Sign up through Idealist to reserve your volunteer spot. We’ll follow up with a brief orientation and role assignment before the event. Let’s fill the parking lot with joy, laughter, and bags of love — together.

opportunity_title: Voice Actor (with AI Experience)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a nonprofit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking expressive and reliable Voice Actors with AI experience to support the launch of our Animation Program . This volunteer role focuses on delivering high-quality voice recordings for animated content that supports educational storytelling and mission-driven messaging. This is a 6-9 month minimum commitment, with the opportunity to renew. This position is ideal for individuals who enjoy voice performance, narration, and experimenting with AI-assisted audio tools in a creative, collaborative environment. Key Responsibilities: Record voiceovers for animated content, including narration, character dialogue, and informational scripts. Collaborate with script writers and animators to align vocal delivery with tone, pacing, and visual storytelling. Use AI-assisted audio or voice tools to support recording, editing, or quality enhancement where appropriate. Perform multiple takes and revisions based on feedback. Maintain consistent vocal quality, clarity, and delivery across recordings. Follow project guidelines related to audio format, tone, and accessibility. Organize and submit audio files according to program standards and timelines. Qualifications: Experience in voice acting, narration, or audio recording. Familiarity with AI-powered audio or voice tools (e.g., AI-enhanced editing, voice workflows, or synthesis tools). Ability to interpret scripts and convey emotion and clarity through voice. Access to basic recording equipment or software suitable for remote work. Comfortable collaborating in a feedback-driven, remote environment. Prior volunteer or creative project experience is an asset but not required. Interest in mission-driven or educational content is strongly valued. Interest in astrology and unconventional/spiritual frameworks as tools for storytelling and character development.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Board Member (Physician)
description_short: DeDe Cares is seeking a passionate and community-minded physician to serve as a Volunteer Board Member. Our organization is committed to building self-sustaining communities and breaking the cycle of poverty by expanding access to health, education,…
description_long: DeDe Cares is seeking a passionate and community-minded physician to serve as a Volunteer Board Member. Our organization is committed to building self-sustaining communities and breaking the cycle of poverty by expanding access to health, education, and economic opportunity. We are looking for a doctor who believes in community impact and wants to use their professional expertise to help guide our health-related initiatives, strengthen our mission, and support strategic growth. A physician with experience in pathology would be especially valuable as we continue exploring health screening, specimen collection, and community-based wellness efforts. Key Responsibilities: Provide strategic guidance as a member of the Board of Directors Offer medical insight related to community health and wellness initiatives Provide expertise that may inform screening, specimen-related, or diagnostic-focused programs Support the development of health-focused programs, partnerships, and outreach efforts Attend board meetings and participate in planning discussions Serve as an ambassador for DeDe Cares within the community Preferred Qualifications: MD or DO required Background in pathology strongly preferred Passion for community service and improving access to care Interest in nonprofit leadership, health equity, preventive health, or diagnostic services Willingness to collaborate with board members, healthcare professionals, and community partners Prior board experience is a plus, but not required Time Commitment: This is a volunteer leadership role. Board members are expected to attend scheduled board meetings and provide occasional guidance and support throughout the year. Why Join DeDe Cares: By joining our board, you will help shape programs that expand access to health services and create lasting community impact. Your expertise can help strengthen our vision for meaningful, mission-driven health initiatives.

description_long: Our volunteers work one-on-one with unaccompanied migrant children to build meaningful connections and advocate for their rights and best interests. Basic volunteer requirements include: Must be at least 21 years old Speak a language other than English Preference for Spanish and Central American Indigenous Languages Access to reliable transportation to attend meetings with the child Completion of all required background checks We will be hosting an in-person Volunteer Child Advocate training on September 26 & 27, 2026 in the following location: San Antonio, TX. If you anticipate being unable to attend an in-person training, we still encourage you to apply, as alternative training dates and formats may be available throughout the year.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Content Creator
description_short: As a Volunteer Content Creator at TWCUYC, you will be instrumental in spreading awareness about our mission and impact. Your creativity and storytelling skills will help us engage the community, attract supporters, and highlight the stories of the…
description_long: As a Volunteer Content Creator at TWCUYC, you will be instrumental in spreading awareness about our mission and impact. Your creativity and storytelling skills will help us engage the community, attract supporters, and highlight the stories of the youth we serve. Your work will be vital in shaping our online presence and communicating our mission effectively. *Key Responsibilities:** Generate creative and compelling content for various platforms, including social media, blogs, newsletters, and videos. Collaborate with TWCUYC team members to gather information, stories, and data for content creation. Write and edit articles, blog posts, and press releases to communicate our mission and activities. Create engaging visual content, such as images, infographics, and videos. Manage and update content on the organization's website. Develop content calendars and schedules for timely and consistent posting. Monitor and engage with the online community by responding to comments and messages. Stay up-to-date on industry trends and best practices in content creation. Assist in the creation of fundraising and promotional materials. Support the organization in crafting a solid and consistent brand voice. *Qualifications and Qualities:** Strong writing and storytelling skills. Proficiency in graphic design, video editing, or other content creation tools. Creativity and the ability to convey messages effectively. Familiarity with social media platforms and content management systems. Attention to detail and commitment to maintaining quality content. Passion for our mission and values. Ability to work independently and meet deadlines. Effective communication and collaboration skills. A desire to make a positive impact on the community. *Benefits to You:** Gain experience in content creation and digital marketing for a nonprofit. Contribute to a meaningful cause that empowers youth and fosters personal growth. Develop skills in storytelling, graphic design, and video editing. Work with a dedicated team of volunteers and staff. Make a lasting impact on the community and the lives of underserved youth. Recognition for your role in communicating TWCUYC's mission effectively. *What You Can Expect:** Access to resources and support to excel in your role. Collaboration with a dedicated team to achieve our mission. Opportunities to network with other professionals in content creation and digital marketing. A fulfilling and rewarding experience in telling the stories of underserved youth and making a difference. *Time Commitment:** This is a flexible volunteer position. Depending on content creation needs and your availability, you can expect to dedicate an estimated 5-10 hours per week. Join us as a Volunteer Content Creator at TWCUYC and be a storyteller for our mission to empower underserved youth and create positive change. Your creativity and content creation skills will play a pivotal role in communicating our message and making a difference in the lives of young individuals.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Event Planner – Nonprofit Fundraising Gala
description_short: Description: SheBuilds Futures is seeking a passionate and organized volunteer Event Planner to help coordinate our annual fundraising gala, Boots, Bling & Building Dreams , taking place on September 26, 2026. This signature event brings…
description_long: Description: SheBuilds Futures is seeking a passionate and organized volunteer Event Planner to help coordinate our annual fundraising gala, Boots, Bling & Building Dreams , taking place on September 26, 2026. This signature event brings together business leaders, architects, developers, and community champions to support our mission of inspiring girls to explore future careers in STEAAM—Science, Technology, Engineering, Architecture, Arts, and Math. The volunteer Event Planner will help ensure a memorable and impactful experience for our guests while helping raise critical funds that support our programs. Responsibilities may include: • Assist with overall event planning and coordination • Develop a run-of-show and event timeline • Coordinate vendors (venue, catering, décor, AV, etc.) • Support sponsorship activations and VIP experiences • Assist with volunteer coordination • Provide guidance on event flow and guest experience Ideal Volunteer Skills: • Event planning or production experience • Strong organization and communication skills • Experience with nonprofit or fundraising events is a plus • Ability to collaborate with a passionate team Impact: Your expertise will help us create an unforgettable evening that supports programs empowering girls to design, build, and lead the future. About SheBuilds Futures: SheBuilds Futures is a Dallas–Fort Worth nonprofit that inspires girls to explore careers in STEAAM through hands-on learning in architecture, construction, and the skilled trades.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Graphic Artist – Web & Social Media Design
description_short: BLAQ LINQ is seeking a Volunteer Graphic Artist to support our website and social media presence through intentional, mission-aligned design. Our mission is to empower and elevate underserved ethnic groups of color—regardless of how someone may…
description_long: BLAQ LINQ is seeking a Volunteer Graphic Artist to support our website and social media presence through intentional, mission-aligned design. Our mission is to empower and elevate underserved ethnic groups of color—regardless of how someone may identify—through our four pillars: social enterprise; digital media and creative arts; education and personal development; and health and wellness. This role is ideal for a creative professional who wants to use visual storytelling to amplify community-centered programs, elevate underrepresented voices, and contribute to meaningful social impact. Key Responsibilities As a Volunteer Graphic Artist, you will: Design compelling social media graphics to promote programs, workshops, and community events aligned with BLAQ LINQ’s four pillars. Create and update website graphics , including banners, program visuals, and digital assets. Support visual storytelling rooted in creative arts, education, wellness, and social enterprise . Maintain brand consistency and accessibility across digital platforms. Collaborate with the BLAQ LINQ team to translate program goals into visually engaging content. Assist with promotional campaigns that empower and uplift underserved communities. Qualifications & Skills Ideal candidates: Have experience designing graphics for websites and social media . Are proficient in Canva, Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator), Figma, or similar tools . Understand social media formats, digital best practices, and accessibility considerations. Possess strong visual design, typography, and layout skills. Are reliable, collaborative, and able to meet deadlines. Are aligned with BLAQ LINQ’s mission of equity, empowerment, and community wellness . Preferred (not required): Experience with nonprofit or community-based organizations. Familiarity with platforms such as WordPress, Squarespace, or Wix. Interest in branding, digital media, or culturally responsive design. Time Commitment & Location Commitment: Flexible; project-based or ongoing (approximately 3–5 hours per week). Schedule: Flexible and coordinated with the team. Location: Remote; limited in-person opportunities may be available in Dallas, TX. Duration: Ongoing or short-term, depending on availability. Benefits Opportunity to apply creative skills toward empowering and elevating communities of color . Portfolio-worthy projects with meaningful community impact. Experience working with a mission-driven organization across arts, education, wellness, and social enterprise . Volunteer recognition and references upon request.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Hairdresser/Barber
description_short: Share your special talent for cutting and styling hair at Ronald McDonald House Dallas! Our families don't always have the time to get a haircut in-between visits to their child's doctor appointments and hospital stays, but receiving this self-care…
description_long: Share your special talent for cutting and styling hair at Ronald McDonald House Dallas! Our families don't always have the time to get a haircut in-between visits to their child's doctor appointments and hospital stays, but receiving this self-care makes a world of difference! We have our own Hairdressing/Barber station in house and would love to offer haircuts and styles at least twice a month. Help us provide a smile, confidence, and care for the wonderful RMHD families!

opportunity_title: Volunteer Mentor – Meet + Mentor Program
description_short: Meet + Mentor connects women professionals with middle school girls for short, structured conversations that spark confidence, broaden career awareness, and help girls imagine what’s possible. This is a no-prep, low-lift volunteer…
description_long: Meet + Mentor connects women professionals with middle school girls for short, structured conversations that spark confidence, broaden career awareness, and help girls imagine what’s possible. This is a no-prep, low-lift volunteer opportunity—perfect for busy professionals who want to make a meaningful impact in a short amount of time. ﻿Play Like a Girl brings girls together to explore STEM , build confidence through sports , and connect with real mentors who show them what’s possible for their future. You can play a key role by: Joining small groups of girls for rotating conversations Answering simple prompts about your career journey (and life!) Building rapport, encouraging curiosity, and offering advice Participating in light activities (no technical skills required) Your presence helps girls gain confidence, discover new interests, and picture themselves in fields where women are still underrepresented. Your time and energy genuinely shape how these girls think about their strengths, their goals, and what they can achieve. Please note that registration for a session does not guarantee placement. A separate confirmation will be sent to confirm your spot.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Nonprofit Grant Writer
description_short: Position : Volunteer Nonprofit Grant Writer Location : Remote Duration : Flexible Role Overview : We are looking for a dedicated and detail-oriented Volunteer Nonprofit Grant Writer to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for…
description_long: Position : Volunteer Nonprofit Grant Writer Location : Remote Duration : Flexible Role Overview : We are looking for a dedicated and detail-oriented Volunteer Nonprofit Grant Writer to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for preparing and writing grant proposals, researching grant opportunities, and supporting our nonprofit’s fundraising efforts to help sustain and expand our programs. While previous experience with grant identification and budgeting is a plus, our primary focus is on strong writing skills and the ability to develop compelling grant applications. Responsibilities : Research and identify potential grant opportunities that align with our nonprofit’s mission and objectives. Prepare, draft, and submit well-organized and persuasive grant applications, ensuring they meet all guidelines and requirements. Collaborate with program and development teams to gather relevant information and insights for strong applications. Maintain a calendar of grant deadlines, application submissions, and reporting requirements. Assist in tracking grant statuses, documenting outcomes, and reporting on funding progress to internal stakeholders. Optional (Preferred): Support budgeting aspects for grant proposals to align with funder requirements. Requirements : Proven experience in grant writing or nonprofit fundraising; experience with identifying grants and budgeting is a plus. Strong research, writing, and editing skills, with a high level of attention to detail. Ability to communicate our organization’s mission and impact clearly to potential funders. Familiarity with nonprofit funding sources, databases, and relevant tools (e.g., Microsoft Word, Excel, Google Workspace). Excellent organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple deadlines effectively. Commitment to our nonprofit’s mission and a desire to make a positive impact. Comfortable working remotely and collaborating with a virtual team. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration. Benefits : Opportunity to gain valuable experience and make a meaningful impact with a nonprofit organization. Flexible schedule and remote work environment. Enhance your grant writing and research skills, building your portfolio within the nonprofit sector. Work with a passionate, supportive team dedicated to positive change.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Nonprofit Vice President (VP)
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking a dynamic and experienced Vice President (VP) to join our leadership team on a volunteer basis. The VP will work closely with the Founder and Board Members to provide strategic direction, operational oversight, and ensure the effective implementation of our programs and initiatives. This position plays a critical role in shaping the organization’s future and expanding our reach and impact. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate with the Founder and Board of Directors to establish strategic goals and priorities. Provide operational oversight for the organization, ensuring efficiency, accountability, and mission alignment across all programs. Lead the development and implementation of new initiatives, from concept to execution. Support and help drive fundraising efforts, including grant management and donor relations. Act as a key representative and spokesperson for SelfSoulLiberate. Cultivate a positive and collaborative culture, fostering the growth and engagement of board members and volunteers. Oversee the financial health and sustainability of the organization in partnership with the board. Qualifications: Proven leadership experience, preferably in a nonprofit or social impact setting. A strong commitment to our mission of promoting mental well-being and health equity. Exceptional communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills. Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams and foster a collaborative, values-driven work environment. Experience in strategic planning, operations management, fundraising, and community engagement. Strong financial acumen and experience with budget management. A passion for our mission and a deep alignment with our core values. Core Values: Compassion - Appreciating others’ perspectives and situations without judgment. Authenticity - Living and leading in your truest form to build strong, trusting foundations. Transparency - Embracing openness, clear communication, and accountability. Accountability - Owning decisions and delivering on commitments. Innovation - Constantly seeking better ways to serve our community. Community - Uniting and moving forward together on a shared path. Passion - Expressing contagious enthusiasm for our shared goals. Adaptability - Embracing change and new ideas with an open mind. Collaboration - Fostering an environment of shared knowledge to achieve big things together. Time Commitment & Logistics: This is a remote volunteer position. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration. Financial Commitment: Board members, including the Vice President, are expected to make a meaningful annual financial contribution that is significant to them. This commitment demonstrates a personal investment in our mission and helps to support the organization's financial health.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Nonprofit Web Developer with WordPress Experience
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Overview: We are seeking a skilled and passionate Volunteer Nonprofit Web Developer with WordPress experience to join our team. As a Volunteer Web Developer, you will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing our nonprofit organization's website, ensuring it is user-friendly, visually appealing, and effectively supports our mission. Your expertise in WordPress and web development will be essential in driving our online presence and engaging with our community. Responsibilities: Manage and maintain our WordPress website, ensuring it is updated, secure, and functioning optimally. Assist in uploading, editing, and organizing website content, including blog posts, event listings, and other resources. Customize WordPress themes and plugins to meet the specific needs of our organization, enhancing website functionality and design. Identify and resolve technical issues, bugs, and errors to ensure a smooth user experience. Implement SEO best practices to improve the visibility and search engine ranking of our website. Work closely with our communications and marketing teams to align website content with organizational goals and campaigns. Continuously assess and improve the website's user experience, making it more accessible and engaging for our audience. Requirements: Strong experience with WordPress, including theme customization, plugin management, and website optimization. Proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PHP, with the ability to create and modify web elements. A good eye for design and user experience, with the ability to create visually appealing and functional web pages. Ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues independently. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical team members. A strong commitment to our nonprofit’s mission and the desire to contribute to making a positive impact. Comfortable working remotely and collaborating with a virtual team. Benefits: Opportunity to gain valuable experience and make a meaningful impact with a nonprofit organization. Flexible schedule and remote work environment. Gain valuable web development experience in a nonprofit setting, enhancing your portfolio and skills. Work with a supportive and dedicated team committed to our mission. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Opportunities in Oak Cliff, Dallas, Duncanville
description_short: Visit when it works with your schedule, have fun with the patients, bring smiles to their faces. Patients get lonely and families at home and staff at the facilities are not always able to spend quality time with the patients. This opportunity…
description_long: Visit when it works with your schedule, have fun with the patients, bring smiles to their faces. Patients get lonely and families at home and staff at the facilities are not always able to spend quality time with the patients. This opportunity allows you to spend time to help them organize their pictures, play cars, build puzzles, work with those who want to have visitors. Some patients want to go outside, some play Bingo or would like to join activites in the common area but no one will help them get there. You can make the difference in someone's day. We can offer visits and once they become comfortable with your visits, you can make progress to do more things. Trust is a valuable relationship builder. We also sign off for school credit hours and can write evaluations for you, if needed. Volunteer work means a lot for job and academic impressions. Gain personal experience in working with others and building relationships.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Opportunity: Activities & Event Support Volunteer
description_short: Do you enjoy engaging with people, helping create fun experiences, and making a difference in your community? We are looking for compassionate and energetic volunteers to assist with activities and special events. As an Activities & Event…
description_long: Do you enjoy engaging with people, helping create fun experiences, and making a difference in your community? We are looking for compassionate and energetic volunteers to assist with activities and special events. As an Activities & Event Support Volunteer, you may: Help plan and organize activity programs Assist with games, crafts, music, and social events Welcome and interact with participants Encourage engagement and socialization Help set up and clean up activity areas Support staff during community outreach and special events Whether you can give a few hours a month or volunteer regularly, your time and presence can bring joy, connection, and meaningful experiences to others. No experience necessary—training provided. Flexible scheduling available. Join us in creating memorable moments and enriching lives through activities, companionship, and community involvement! Contact us today to learn more about becoming an Activities & Event Support Volunteer. 🌟

opportunity_title: Volunteer Opportunity: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
description_short: AfriGenetry Link (AGL) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to connecting heritage, health, healing, and humanity through DNA. We work across Africa and the global African diaspora to advance genomic health education, ancestry and identity…
description_long: AfriGenetry Link (AGL) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to connecting heritage, health, healing, and humanity through DNA. We work across Africa and the global African diaspora to advance genomic health education, ancestry and identity reconnection, genetic counseling workforce development, community engagement, and equitable access to DNA and genomic resources. Our initiatives include community DNA pop-up events, identity and campus reconnection programs, genetic counseling fellowships, genomic health awareness campaigns, research collaborations, and community-centered education focused on hereditary health conditions. Position Overview AfriGenetry Link is seeking a mission-driven Chief Operating Officer (COO) to join our leadership team and Board of Directors. This is a volunteer executive leadership position for an individual based in the Dallas-Fort Worth area who is passionate about community impact, public health, operational excellence, and organizational growth. The COO will work closely with the Founder & Executive Director, Board leadership, department heads, and regional leaders to help oversee day-to-day organizational operations, improve execution, strengthen internal systems, and support the successful delivery of programs and initiatives. Key Responsibilities Provide operational leadership and strategic guidance across the organization Support organizational planning, execution, and accountability Coordinate activities across departments, teams, and leadership groups Help establish and improve operational systems, processes, and workflows Monitor organizational priorities, timelines, and deliverables Support the planning and execution of community programs and events Assist with volunteer engagement and organizational growth Participate in leadership and Board meetings Serve as a strategic advisor to the Executive Director and Board Preferred Qualifications Experience in operations, project management, program management, nonprofit leadership, business administration, healthcare administration, or a related field Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills Ability to manage multiple priorities and work collaboratively with diverse teams Passion for health equity, ancestry, genomics, education, community engagement, or nonprofit impact Experience in nonprofit organizations, startups, or mission-driven initiatives is preferred Must be based in the Dallas-Fort Worth area Why Join AGL? Help shape a growing international nonprofit organization Play a key leadership role in advancing innovative health and ancestry initiatives Build valuable executive leadership experience Work alongside professionals and leaders across Africa and the global diaspora Create meaningful impact in communities through education, health, heritage, and genomics Help build systems and programs that can improve lives for generations to come If you are passionate about leadership, service, community impact, and helping build something meaningful, we would love to hear from you.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Opportunity: Express, Create, Heal (ECH) Program Facilitator – Dallas/Fort Worth
description_short: Organization: A Moment of Magic (AMoM) Location: Fort Worth, TX (Cook Children's Medical Center) Commitment: 8 Weeks | Approximately 4 Hours Per Week Volunteer Positions Available: 4–6 Per Cohort A Moment of Magic is seeking compassionate, reliable…
description_long: Organization: A Moment of Magic (AMoM) Location: Fort Worth, TX (Cook Children's Medical Center) Commitment: 8 Weeks | Approximately 4 Hours Per Week Volunteer Positions Available: 4–6 Per Cohort A Moment of Magic is seeking compassionate, reliable volunteers in the Dallas-Fort Worth area to help facilitate our Express, Create, Heal (ECH) program, an innovative psychosocial support initiative for children and adolescents impacted by pediatric cancer and other serious medical conditions. Through art, play, storytelling, and creative expression, ECH helps young people build emotional resilience, process difficult experiences, and connect with peers in a supportive environment. This opportunity is ideal for students, aspiring healthcare professionals, educators, social workers, child life specialists, artists, and community members who are passionate about supporting children and families facing medical challenges. What You'll Do As an ECH Program Facilitator, you will: Lead and support creative, play-based activities designed to foster emotional wellbeing Build meaningful connections with children and adolescents throughout the 8-week program Help create a safe, welcoming, and inclusive environment Support small group discussions and guided activities Collaborate with fellow volunteers and A Moment of Magic staff Complete brief observational notes and program feedback after each session Participate in volunteer training and ongoing support meetings Commitment We are looking for volunteers who can commit to: One full 8-week program cycle Weekly sessions (approximately 45–60 minutes each) Volunteer preparation, debriefing, and communication Approximately 4 hours per week total Consistent attendance throughout the entire cohort Because trust and relationship-building are critical to the success of this program, volunteers must be able to attend the majority of sessions during the cycle. Preferred Qualifications We welcome volunteers from all backgrounds. Preference may be given to individuals with experience or interest in: Healthcare, medicine, nursing, psychology, counseling, social work, or child life Education, youth development, or mentoring Art, music, theater, creative writing, or other creative disciplines Pediatric oncology, chronic illness, disability, or mental health advocacy Working with children and adolescents What You'll Gain Direct experience supporting medically vulnerable children and families Training in trauma-informed, psychosocial support practices Exposure to pediatric healthcare and nonprofit program delivery Leadership, facilitation, and communication experience Volunteer service hours and professional references (upon successful completion) The opportunity to make a lasting impact on children navigating some of life's most difficult challenges About A Moment of Magic A Moment of Magic is a national nonprofit serving medically vulnerable children through creative programming, mental health support, and community connection. Since 2014, our volunteers have helped bring joy, emotional support, and healing-centered experiences to more than 185,000 children nationwide. Through Express, Create, Heal, we are helping ensure that emotional wellbeing becomes an essential part of every child's care journey. If you are passionate about supporting children, building community, and helping create moments of healing and hope, we'd love to hear from you.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Opportunity: Girls Save The World™ Global Youth Summit (April 2026 | Dallas, TX)
description_short: Overview Girls Save The World™ (GSTW) Global Youth Summit is a two-day leadership and innovation experience designed to activate the next generation of purpose-driven girls and youth allies from across the country. Powered by Independence Gardens…
description_long: Overview Girls Save The World™ (GSTW) Global Youth Summit is a two-day leadership and innovation experience designed to activate the next generation of purpose-driven girls and youth allies from across the country. Powered by Independence Gardens and Conscious Capital Foundation, the Summit brings together students, educators, innovators, and community leaders to collaborate, build prototypes, and amplify sustainable impact. We are recruiting volunteers who are excited about youth empowerment, STEAM learning, innovation, event operations, and leadership development. Volunteer Roles May Include: Registration & Check-In Support Summit Wayfinding & Guest Experience Prototype Lab Support Innovation Hub Support Classroom/Workshop Support Media & Photography Assistants School & District Liaison Support Sponsor & Partner Hospitality Event Set-Up/Tear-Down Crew Assignments will be based on availability, skills, and Summit needs. Dates Needed Tuesday, April 21 — Wednesday, April 22, 2026 Volunteers may select single-day or multi-day shifts. Requirements To support the safety of all student participants, all volunteers are required to complete a background check . ✔ You will receive a secure link to complete the background check after registration ✔ Clearance must be completed prior to Summit volunteer shifts Volunteer Benefits As a registered GSTW Summit volunteer, you will receive: ⭐ Free volunteer access to the Summit 🍽 Meals & drinks provided on volunteer days (except the Tuesday Awards Dinner + Wednesday Leadership Panel) 🎟 Event access aligned to your volunteer role 🤝 Network with educators, innovators & purpose-driven leaders 🧭 Service & leadership experience for college, job, or community records Who Should Volunteer Ideal volunteers are: College students Young professionals Educators Corporate volunteer groups Community leaders Individuals passionate about youth empowerment, innovation, or sustainability How to Register Interested volunteers will complete a brief online form. After approval, volunteers will receive: Volunteer shift scheduling link Background check link Onboarding + training instructions Questions? For more information, please contact our Volunteer Team at: gstw@independencegardens.org

org_name: Now I Lay Me Down To Sleep
opportunity_title: Volunteer Photographer – Infant Remembrance Photography | Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep
description_short: At Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep (NILMDTS), we believe every family experiencing the loss of a baby deserves the opportunity to have tangible memories to hold onto. We are seeking compassionate photographers who want to use their talent and heart to…
description_long: At Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep (NILMDTS), we believe every family experiencing the loss of a baby deserves the opportunity to have tangible memories to hold onto. We are seeking compassionate photographers who want to use their talent and heart to serve families during one of the most difficult moments of their lives. As an Affiliated Photographer, you will provide remembrance photography sessions for families experiencing the loss of a baby. Your gift of photography can provide families with timeless images they may treasure for a lifetime. What You'll Do: Respond to photography session requests in your local service area based on your availability Photograph remembrance sessions in hospitals Create beautiful and meaningful images for grieving families Work compassionately and professionally alongside healthcare teams and family members Upload images for editing and delivery according to NILMDTS guidelines Why This Role Matters: The images created through NILMDTS often become some of a family's most treasured possessions. During a time when parents leave the hospital with empty arms, these photographs provide lasting memories and tangible connections to their baby. Qualifications: Photography experience and technical proficiency Professional or advanced hobbyist photography skills Access to professional-quality camera equipment Strong interpersonal skills and compassion Ability to remain calm and professional in emotionally sensitive environments Reliable transportation Must meet organization onboarding requirements Training & Support NILMDTS provides: Specialized bereavement photography training Ongoing support and educational resources Access to a compassionate volunteer community Guidance and best practices for serving families Time Commitment Volunteer photographers serve on a flexible, on-call basis according to personal availability and geographic service area. Dispatch hours are 7am-10pm, volunteers are not expected to go at times that don't work for them. We work with volunteer schedules. About Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep is a nonprofit organization that provides remembrance photography to families experiencing the loss of a baby. Through the gift of photography, our volunteers help families preserve memories during moments that matter most.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Position: Workshop Instructor
description_short: Position Summary: Own It Financial Education is seeking passionate and knowledgeable Volunteer Workshop Instructors to deliver engaging, easy-to-understand financial literacy and business education sessions to underserved individuals and families in…
description_long: Position Summary: Own It Financial Education is seeking passionate and knowledgeable Volunteer Workshop Instructors to deliver engaging, easy-to-understand financial literacy and business education sessions to underserved individuals and families in the DFW area. These workshops equip participants with practical knowledge in budgeting, credit, taxes, investing, entrepreneurship, and more. Whether you're a professional in finance, business, education, or simply someone with a heart for teaching and helping others, this is your opportunity to make a meaningful impact-one Saturday at a time. Responsibilities: Deliver one or more of our pre-developed workshop topics (materials provided), including: Budgeting & Credit Basics Debt Elimination Taxes & Retirement Planning Business Start-Up & Bookkeeping Real Estate & Investment Education Foster a supportive, inclusive, and engaging learning environment. Encourage audience participation and answer basic questions during or after workshops. Provide feedback to staff on workshop outcomes and participant engagement. Set up and break down materials or handouts (if applicable). Arrive on time and represent Own It Financial Education professionally. Requirements: Background in finance, business, education, or a related field preferred (but not required). Strong communication and presentation skills. Comfort speaking in front of small to medium-sized groups. Commitment to equity, empowerment, and community service. Must be at least 21 years old and pass a basic background check. Time Commitment & Scheduling: Workshops are held on Saturdays , typically between 9 a.m. and 3 p.m. Volunteer Instructors can sign up for as little as 1 Saturday a month , or up to 4, with 2 to 4-hour shifts per day . Flexible scheduling-commit only to the dates that fit your calendar. Benefits: Make a direct, life-changing impact in your local community. Receive training, workshop guides, and presentation materials. Network with like-minded professionals and community leaders. Earn volunteer service hours (confirmation letters available). Enjoy a sense of purpose while helping others gain financial freedom. Let’s work together to empower our communities through education, one workshop at a time. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 10 Event can be held at a volunteer group location (e.g. office, park) Event can be held virtually No Donation

org_name: Literacy Achieves (Founded as Vickery Meadow Learning Center)
opportunity_title: Volunteer Teacher — Adult English Language Learning Program
description_short: Are you passionate about people, education, literacy, and making a meaningful difference in your community? Literacy Achieves is seeking compassionate, dependable, and enthusiastic Volunteer Teachers to support adult learners in our Adult English…
description_long: Are you passionate about people, education, literacy, and making a meaningful difference in your community? Literacy Achieves is seeking compassionate, dependable, and enthusiastic Volunteer Teachers to support adult learners in our Adult English Language Learning Program. This is a meaningful opportunity for individuals who want to give back, build community, and help adults gain the confidence and language skills they need to thrive in everyday life. No formal teaching experience is required just a heart for people, a willingness to learn, and a desire to support adults on their literacy journey. Why Volunteer with Literacy Achieves? As a Volunteer Teacher, you will help adult learners: Strengthen their English speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills Build confidence in daily communication Support their children’s education more confidently Navigate work, school, and community life with greater independence Create new opportunities for themselves and their families Who Should Apply? This opportunity is a great fit for: College students studying education, social work, ESL, linguistics, human services, or related fields Working professionals looking for a meaningful way to give back Retirees or former educators Community members passionate about literacy, adult education, language access, or equity Individuals who enjoy helping others learn, grow, and feel encouraged Preferred candidates may have experience in teaching, tutoring, mentoring, adult education, English language learning, ESL, or community-based volunteer work; however, prior experience is not required. Speaking another language is not required . Campus Information Volunteer opportunities are available at: Vickery Meadow ELM-East Dallas Wesley Rankin Community Center Online Campus Summer Classes Begin: June 1 Summer Locations: Vickery Meadow and Online Fall Classes Begin: August 31 Spring Classes Begin: January 25 Fall/Spring Locations: ELM-East Dallas, Vickery Meadow, Wesley Rankin Community Center, and Online What You’ll Do Volunteer Teachers will: Lead or support adult English language learning classes using curriculum provided by Literacy Achieves Create a welcoming, respectful, and encouraging learning environment Help adult learners practice speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills Follow lesson plans, curriculum guidance, and classroom expectations provided through training Partner with co teachers and Literacy Achieves staff to create engaging, interactive class sessions that support student learning, confidence, and participation. Communicate consistently regarding lesson planning, classroom needs, and student support Use program technology and curriculum tools as needed Support different learning styles with patience, flexibility, and creativity Commit to teaching a minimum of 2 hours per week for the semester Bring a heart for empowering adults through literacy and education Training & Support Provided You will not be doing this alone. Literacy Achieves provides: Curriculum and lesson materials Volunteer teacher training Ongoing program support Guidance from campus and program staff Documentation of completed volunteer hours when needed for approved credit-hour or service-hour requirements Credit Hours / Service Hours Eligible volunteers may be able to obtain course credit, internship hours, service learning hours, or community service hours with approval from their school, program, or institution. For volunteers seeking college credit, the following will be required: A copy of class/program requirements Approval from the class professor or school representative Approval from the Literacy Achieves Adult Program Director Completion and documentation of approved volunteer hours Additional Volunteer Opportunities Additional volunteer opportunities may also be available in: Student registration Student recognition events Special events and program support English communication practice sessions General program support How to Apply / Get Involved Ready to turn two hours a week into lasting community impact? Become a Literacy Achieves Volunteer Teacher and help adults take the next step toward confidence, opportunity, and success! Email: Volunteer@literacyachieves.org Website: LiteracyAchieves.org/Volunteer Phone: 214-265-5057 ext. 106 Flexible work from home options available.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Tour Ambassador (Internship Credit Available)
description_short: Flexible / 5-10 hours / virtual About the Opportunity: In coordination with our upcoming 2026–2027 Northeast Executive Tour , the Cognitive Institute of Dallas is seeking dynamic Volunteer Tour Ambassadors to join our regional outreach team. This…
description_long: Flexible / 5-10 hours / virtual About the Opportunity: In coordination with our upcoming 2026–2027 Northeast Executive Tour , the Cognitive Institute of Dallas is seeking dynamic Volunteer Tour Ambassadors to join our regional outreach team. This multi-city technical briefing and signing series launches this October in New York City before heading to Massachusetts, Pennsylvania, and Washington D.C. This is a highly flexible, impact-driven role perfect for students, recent graduates, or community organizers looking to gain high-level executive networking experience in corporate advertising, marketing promotion, and event management. Academic Internship Credit: We gladly partner with academic institutions. If you are currently enrolled in a college or university program, our leadership team will coordinate with your department to sign off on necessary paperwork for formal internship course credit. Responsibilities & Financial Incentives: Assist with regional marketing and corporate advertising outreach leading up to local tour stops. Engage with regional corporate networks and professional associations to promote our executive briefing series. Financial Structure: While this is a voluntary community outreach and learning role, ambassadors receive highly competitive, performance-based commission incentives tied to corporate partner acquisitions and advertising targets. Qualifications: Strong interest in business development, public relations, or marketing. Excellent communication skills and professional demeanor. Ability to work independently with flexible weekly hours. How to Apply: Interested candidates should submit their information directly through our independent intake portal. Please indicate if you are seeking academic internship credit.

opportunity_title: Volunteer Treasurer - Mental Health Non-Profit
description_short: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop…
description_long: Company Description: SelfSoulLiberate is a non-profit organization dedicated to achieving health equity and reducing stigma surrounding mental health in under-resourced communities. Our mission is to provide educational resources, develop sustainable wellness frameworks, and make care more accessible. Role Description: We are seeking a dedicated and highly detail-oriented Treasurer to join our Board of Directors on a volunteer basis. The Treasurer will be essential in safeguarding and guiding the financial health of SelfSoulLiberate, ensuring that our resources are managed effectively and ethically to maximize our impact in advancing mental health equity. Key Responsibilities: Lead the organization’s financial planning, annual budgeting process, and ongoing financial forecasting. Ensure accurate and transparent financial record-keeping, adhering to all nonprofit accounting standards and regulatory compliance. Prepare and present clear, concise financial statements and reports to the Board of Directors, offering insights and recommendations. Develop, implement, and periodically review the organization’s financial policies and procedures to ensure best practices. Work collaboratively with the Board and fundraising team to develop sustainable financial strategies that support mission growth. Manage banking relationships and oversee responsible investment activities. Serve as the primary liaison with external auditors, facilitating and overseeing the annual audit process. Provide expert financial guidance and support to other board members and staff as needed. Qualifications: Proven experience in financial management, accounting, or a closely related field, with a strong preference for experience within a non-profit environment. Comprehensive understanding of financial statements, budgeting principles, and financial reporting. In-depth familiarity with non-profit financial regulations and generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP). Exceptional organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking abilities. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex financial information clearly to both financial and non-financial audiences. Unwavering commitment to the mission and core values of SelfSoulLiberate. Core Values: Compassion - Appreciating others’ perspectives and situations without judgment. Authenticity - Living and leading in your truest form to build strong, trusting foundations. Transparency - Embracing openness, clear communication, and accountability. Accountability - Owning decisions and delivering on commitments. Innovation - Constantly seeking better ways to serve our community. Community - Uniting and moving forward together on a shared path. Passion - Expressing contagious enthusiasm for our shared goals. Adaptability - Embracing change and new ideas with an open mind. Collaboration - Fostering an environment of shared knowledge to achieve big things together. Time Commitment & Logistics: This is a remote volunteer position. Volunteers should be available to work within a U.S. time zone for effective collaboration. Financial Commitment: As a key leader in ensuring our financial stability, the Treasurer, like all board members, is expected to make a meaningful annual financial contribution that is personally significant to them. This commitment demonstrates a vital personal investment in our mission and helps to support the organization's overall financial health.

opportunity_title: Volunteer With Us on Race Day: Heatwave Half - Dallas
description_short: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and…
description_long: Make a Difference, One Mile at a Time - Volunteer with Us! Bodies Race Company is passionate about building healthier, more active communities through fun, accessible, family-friendly race events. We host events in cities all across the country, and each race is a chance to create a ripple effect of positivity - encouraging families to get moving, friends to bond over fitness, and communities to come together around a shared goal: better health. We couldn’t make any of this happen without the help of incredible volunteers like you. We’re currently seeking volunteers for our upcoming event - and we need YOUR help to bring it to life. Whether you’re signing up solo, with your family, or rallying a group of coworkers or classmates, there’s a spot for everyone to get involved and make a difference. As a thank-you, every volunteer gets ONE of the following: Two free race entries to a future Bodies Race Company event A $20 Amazon gift card BONUS: Community service hours signed off Volunteer Roles - Here’s How You Can Help: Aid Station Crew - Highest Need Keep our runners fueled and focused. Hand out water, pick up discarded cups, and bring positive energy to everyone who passes by. Every high five counts! Lead Bike Volunteer - High Priority Lead the way! Use your own bike and helmet to guide runners throughout the course. You’ll pre-ride the route in advance and be a key player on race day. Course Photographer If you love capturing special moments, this one’s for you. Bring your own camera and head to a scenic course spot to catch the smiles, the grit, and the joy of race day. Finish Line Volunteer Be part of the celebration! Hand out medals, manage the awards table, and help runners feel the reward of their hard work. We’re looking for individuals, friend groups, student organizations, businesses, and families who want to be part of something bigger. Come spend your morning making memories, supporting wellness in your city, and cheering on every single finish line victory. Let’s build a healthier community together. Sign up to volunteer today - we can’t do it without you!

opportunity_title: Volunteer as the Coffee and Doughnuts Coordinator
description_short: Bring SWEETNESS to Veterans lives and thank those who are serving and served our country in a fun but meaningful way! Become the Coffee and Donuts Coordinator for Dallas VA Medical Center. Volunteer's assignments will include, but is not limited to:…
description_long: Bring SWEETNESS to Veterans lives and thank those who are serving and served our country in a fun but meaningful way! Become the Coffee and Donuts Coordinator for Dallas VA Medical Center. Volunteer's assignments will include, but is not limited to: Order and pick up Donuts and any other needed items for the specific events. Coordinate with the sponsor company on volunteers, timing, and other logistics. Coordinate with the VA Medical Center to ensure any VA supplies are in stock and available for the event. The mission of Soldiers’ Angels is to provide aid, comfort, and resources to the military, veterans, and their families. We can only do this through the support of our volunteers! Check the link for more information on this volunteer opportunity: https://www.signupgenius.com/go/10c0b4daaae2aa2fc1-coffee6#/

Join our team to help serve fans and make game day unforgettable.
