org_name: Heart To Heart Hospice of Rockwall
opportunity_title: Volunteer at Heart to Heart Hospice of Rockwall
description_short: Volunteer with Heart to Heart Hospice Make a meaningful difference in someone's life simply by being present. At Heart to Heart Hospice, our volunteers bring comfort, companionship, and kindness to patients and families during an important time in…
description_long: Volunteer with Heart to Heart Hospice Make a meaningful difference in someone's life simply by being present. At Heart to Heart Hospice, our volunteers bring comfort, companionship, and kindness to patients and families during an important time in life. Whether it's sitting and talking, listening to music, reading, coloring, or simply offering a caring presence, volunteers make a lasting impact. You do not need medical experience to volunteer, just a compassionate heart. Volunteer Opportunities Include: Providing companionship visits Offering caregiver relief so families can rest or run errands Playing games, listening to music, reading, or sharing conversation Bringing comfort and emotional support to patients and families Every patient's situation is unique, and we work with volunteers to find the best fit for their comfort level and schedule. Why Volunteer with Hospice? Many volunteers say hospice volunteering become one of the most rewarding experiences of their lives. Your time can bring peace, dignity, comfort, and connections to someone who truly needs it. Our office is location in Rockwall, TX and serves patients throughout Rockwall, Hunt, Kaufman, Collin, and Dallas Counties. If you would like to learn more about becoming a volunteer, we would love to hear from you. Contact: Presley Barber 469-651-2171

opportunity_title: Volunteer at Heritage Farmstead Museum in 2026!
description_short: The Heritage Farmstead Museum needs You! We are always looking for enthusiastic and dedicated friends to join our volunteer team! Connect your skills and passion for history, education, farm life, nature or the outdoors to a rewarding role at the…
description_long: The Heritage Farmstead Museum needs You! We are always looking for enthusiastic and dedicated friends to join our volunteer team! Connect your skills and passion for history, education, farm life, nature or the outdoors to a rewarding role at the museum. We can’t wait to meet you! We have seasonal opportunities and ongoing. Please copy and paste the link below in your browser to read through all of the areas and let us know where you see yourself! Complete your application today! www.heritagefarmstead.org/join-give/volunteer/ All volunteers (18 years and older) must complete a volunteer application and background check before working at the museum. Volunteers 12-16 years old must have an adult assist them at the same area. Anyone under 12 years old is unable to volunteer. 2 shifts available: Visitor Center Hosts, Farmstead Guides & House Docents work 10 am - 12 pm or 12 pm-2 pm on Thursdays, Fridays and Saturdays.: 10:00 to 12:00 CST Visitor Center Hosts and Grounds Guides work 12-2 p.m. on Thursdays, Fridays or Saturdays.: 12:00 to 14:00 CST

opportunity_title: Volunteer at a Senior Facility in 75149
description_short: Works well with others, can multi task, enjoys meeting new people, adds a smile to those they meet...Are these things that you want people to say about you? You can make that happen. With a little time and some effort, we can build those…
description_long: Works well with others, can multi task, enjoys meeting new people, adds a smile to those they meet...Are these things that you want people to say about you? You can make that happen. With a little time and some effort, we can build those relationships and help overcome some obstacles, and grow from experience. It is hard to not get excited to go visit our patients. I love coming to work everyday because I know I am making a difference in the lives of others. I make people smile, they make me smile, and we find a lot of common ground to grow relationships every single day.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Volunteer at the Local Assisted Living Facility
description_short: We are looking for volunteers who can help their local assisted living facility (ALF). The ALF activity director organizes fun games, classes, and parties for the residents. Volunteers will work with the activity director to facilitate the events…
description_long: We are looking for volunteers who can help their local assisted living facility (ALF). The ALF activity director organizes fun games, classes, and parties for the residents. Volunteers will work with the activity director to facilitate the events and engage the residents. These activities include: Offering Friendship and Companionship to Residents Call B-I-N-G-O or Assist Players Participating in Monthly Birthday and Holiday Parties Lead a Bible Study Play Cards & Dominos Teach Chair Exercises Paint Fingernails and Do Hair Sing or Play an Instrument Lead Karaoke or Cooking and Gardening Classes Visit Residents and Staff with Your Certified Therapy Dog Heart to Heart Hospice provides comprehensive training and will identify the most suitable service area for you. We understand the importance of your time, so we work around your schedule; you can volunteer as little or as much as you want, giving you the power to make a difference on your terms. When you volunteer, you are ensuring that residents have quality of life. Volunteer Requirements : A Desire to Help Others Transportation to the ALF Completed Application with References At-Home Online Orientation Training at Your Own Pace (free) Tuberculosis Blood Test (free) Background Check (free)

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Volunteer for some neighborly lawn and yard care in Arlington, TX · Ref IWTMYL-KVF
description_short: A neighbor in Arlington, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help will allow a neighbor in Arlington to keep their…
description_long: A neighbor in Arlington, TX could use a hand keeping their lawn and yard maintained. This is a simple, practical request that a nearby volunteer could take care of, often in a single visit. Your help will allow a neighbor in Arlington to keep their yard looking great. You're providing a helping hand to a neighbor, making a real difference in their community. This is a home shared by a household of four, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. Help like this is practical and immediate — the kind of thing a neighbor notices and appreciates right away, and that keeps an outdoor space safe and welcoming. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Arlington, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-KVF&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-KVF

Expectation is for growing a relationship that will bring companionship and socialization to the one you visit. We provide tools to help conversations and can provide small games and puzzles if needed.

description_short: Seeking volunteers to sit with patients and provide friendship and companionship. We offer open availability and close to your location. Our goal is 1-2 hours per week, every week, no travel except to and from volunteer location. No hands on care.…

Our goal is 1-2 hours per week, every week, no travel except to and from volunteer location. No hands on care. Patients in this season of life, need support and someone to be present with them. Some patients like to garden, talk about their pets, play with the pets, laugh and be a part of something other than their condition. You can be that person making a difference for them, with an opportunity valued for years to come.

description_short: How would you like to visit with someone that wants to have a friend? Someone to talk to, watch TV with, go for a walk, make a puzzle, play a game, have coffee and do your nails? You never know what you can do to make other people happy. We work…
description_long: How would you like to visit with someone that wants to have a friend? Someone to talk to, watch TV with, go for a walk, make a puzzle, play a game, have coffee and do your nails? You never know what you can do to make other people happy. We work with terminally ill patients that would like to have a visitor, someone that they can share their live experiences with, talk about your experiences, and make experiences together. We do require some specifics, such as an application, background checks, TB testing, and drug screening. I would like to meet you for coffee or a soda and learn what you want to contribute as a volunteer. Some of our nursing homes would like to have help with activities such as painting classes, playing group games, having a teatime, or social hour. The possibilities are there if you want to make a difference. Please consider meeting me and talking about what a wonderful gift it is to give to others when they are in need of companionship. We allow pet therapy if you have a trained animal. We paint fingernails, can put on a fun fashion show, help with holiday outings or resident parties.

opportunity_title: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student Coppell, TX and surrounding areas!
description_short: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad…
description_long: Volunteer to Host a Foreign Exchange Student in your town and surrounding areas!! Benefit from opening your home and heart to an exchange student for a term during the upcoming high school year by: Making life-long friendship connections abroad Giving a young person an opportunity to immerse themselves in our American culture and your family Gaining an international son/daughter Gain new perspectives on our American way of life through the eyes of your exchange student Choose from these ways to host; just a few weeks as a short-term Welcome Family, for a Semester, or school Year. You don't have to have your own child in high school to host. You may be a Couple, with or without children, Single, Empty-nester or Grandparents. Many different family dynamics make for great hosts. And yes, you get to pick the student(s) you wish to host by reviewing our student profiles. Our exchange students, ages 15-18, have worked hard to be accepted into our program and are waiting now to be selected by their US host family. This year's NWSE exchange students come from Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, Japan, Czech Republic, Kazakhstan and more! They arrive with English fluency, travel medical insurance and their own spending money. We ask our hosts to provide a loving, caring environment, 3 meals per day and a bedroom, which can be shared with a host sibling of the same gender 12 and up. Students attend the high school your home is zoned for. #ExChange Your World...Become a NWSE Host Family today. Visit nwse.com/host We look forward to hearing from you very soon and matching you with your foreign exchange student! Watch our video to learn more: EXCHANGE YOUR WORLD with NWSE

opportunity_title: Volunteer to be a virtual mentor! (2-4 hours a month)
description_short: Position Overview Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these students face challenges such as instability, limited family support, and gaps in life-skills…
description_long: Position Overview Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these students face challenges such as instability, limited family support, and gaps in life-skills training. As mentors, individuals serve as trusted adults who provide encouragement, guidance, and practical assistance, helping students navigate the complexities of college life and early adulthood. The primary aim of mentoring is not to resolve all issues, but rather to empower students, foster their confidence, and assist them in developing the skills and networks necessary for long-term success. Key Responsibilities Build a Supportive Relationship Meet regularly with your mentee, biweekly and for any special programing from ISF (quarterly). Create a safe and non-judgmental environment for open conversation. Utilize active listening and offer encouragement. Establish healthy boundaries while developing a trusting relationship. Provide Practical Life Guidance Support mentees in budgeting and financial literacy. Assist with time management and goal setting. Help mentees navigate housing options. Guide students through internship and career exploration. Promote professional communication skills. Facilitate networking opportunities and career connections. Offer advice on graduate school or career pathways. Mentors are not expected to be experts in all areas, but should be resourceful and willing to connect students with available resources. ISF offers support and resources to mentors as needed. Support Student Growth Help mentees identify their strengths and build self-confidence. Encourage independence and support decision-making. Provide guidance during challenging times, such as exams, holidays, or housing transitions. Encourage spiritual formation and emotional health Engage in opportunities and discussions on leadership development Maintain Program Partnership Participate in mentor orientation or training sessions. Communicate with program staff if concerns arise. Refer students to program resources or professional services when necessary. Attend the annual leadership retreat (optional). Attend the annual mission trip if applicable. Mentor Qualities Successful mentors demonstrate the following qualities: Empathy and trauma-informed awareness Reliability and consistency Respect for diverse backgrounds and experiences Strong listening and communication skills Encouragement of student autonomy and growth Vibrant commitment to personal and spiritual growth Mentors recognize that trust is established through patience, consistency, and respect. Preferred Backgrounds (Not Required) Mentors may come from a variety of professional or personal backgrounds, including: Professionals with career experience or networks Individuals who were first-generation college students Adults with experience in education, counseling, or youth development Individuals with lived experience overcoming adversity or foster care The most important qualification is a genuine commitment to supporting a student’s success.

org_name: I Want To Mow Your Lawn ®
opportunity_title: Volunteer to help a Navy veteran with yard work in Garland, TX · Ref IWTMYL-N48
description_short: A Navy veteran in Garland, TX would welcome help keeping their yard in good shape this season — a practical, visible way for the community to give back to someone who served our country. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and…
description_long: A Navy veteran in Garland, TX would welcome help keeping their yard in good shape this season — a practical, visible way for the community to give back to someone who served our country. Your help ensures a neighbor's yard stays tidy and well-maintained. Your kindness makes a real difference in a neighbor's life. This is a home shared by a household of two, so keeping the yard manageable helps everyone who lives there. Specifically, this opportunity may include mowing and light yard work. They do not have equipment available, so this is best for a volunteer who can bring basic lawn tools. This is primarily a one-time relief visit. Exact timing can be coordinated based on the volunteer’s availability, and a visit like this is often the kind of task a volunteer can take care of in a single afternoon. Most requests are simple, routine yard care rather than heavy landscaping, and you can review the details before you decide whether it is a good fit for you. A tidy, well-maintained yard means safer footing and less to worry about for a veteran who has earned an easier season, and it is a direct way for neighbors to show their gratitude. About I Want To Mow Your Lawn®: we are a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing completely free lawn mowing, yard maintenance, and seasonal outdoor help to older adults, military veterans, people with disabilities, and neighbors facing financial hardship. Our mission is to preserve dignity, improve safety, and support independent living by bringing neighbors together through meaningful, hands-on service. Since 2020, our growing network of more than 1,800 volunteers has reached communities across all 50 states — and requests keep growing, including near Garland, TX. We combine practical outdoor help with environmentally conscious practices, and every service is offered at no cost to the people we serve. What a volunteer can expect: you choose the opportunities that fit your location, availability, equipment, and comfort level. Typical help includes lawn mowing, trimming and edging, leaf removal, and light seasonal yard cleanup. You will often see the difference you made before you even pack up — real, hands-on, tangible community impact. Every visit is coordinated in advance, and you decide what you are comfortable taking on — there is never any pressure to do more than the task described. Each request comes through our intake system and is shared with volunteers in or near the community. Requests may be submitted by homeowners, family members, caregivers, case managers, social workers, or community organizations, and our platform helps connect each one with available volunteers nearby. You are always in control of your own schedule and can step back at any time. What we provide volunteers: Volunteer insurance coverage while completing an approved service. Coverage may apply to eligible volunteer activity coordinated through our organization; this is not a guarantee of coverage for every situation, and our team can share details. Access to our free, mobile-friendly platform to find and claim nearby opportunities Tools to log visits, service hours, notes, and before-and-after photos Privacy-protected calling and texting, so you can coordinate without sharing your personal phone number Tracked and documented volunteer hours for school, work, or organizational requirements. If you are working court-mandated volunteer hours, we may be able to provide documentation for completed hours. Orientation materials and support from a nationally recognized nonprofit, plus potential equipment-support and upgrade opportunities for active volunteers through participating outdoor-equipment partners A welcoming community of volunteers and staff who are glad to answer questions and help you get started Bringing your own lawn equipment is helpful, but you can still register and explore available opportunities if you do not currently own equipment. No fixed schedule, ever: there are no minimum hours, meetings, fundraising requirements, or fixed schedules. Volunteer once, once a season, once a month, or whenever an opportunity fits your schedule. Our guiding philosophy is simple: we do what we can, when we can. This opportunity may be a great fit for: Individuals who can safely operate lawn or yard equipment Families looking to volunteer together, and retirees or community members Students earning community-service hours Scout troops and youth organizations with adult supervision Landscapers willing to donate an occasional service Civic clubs, faith-based groups, and corporate or team-volunteer programs Volunteers set their own terms — distance, availability, equipment, and comfort level. We protect homeowner privacy and only share exact service details once a volunteer is confirmed. This is a volunteer network coordinating neighbors who may be able to help based on location, availability, equipment, and comfort level — so while we cannot guarantee that a volunteer will be found, every offer of help genuinely matters. A quick word on safety and peace of mind: volunteers always use their own judgment, coordinate each visit in advance, and are free to decline anything that does not feel like a good fit — there is never any pressure to take on more than you are comfortable with. Because every service is arranged ahead of time, you will always know the general scope of the work and the location before you commit, and our team is available if any question comes up along the way. Whether you help with one lawn or become a regular volunteer, your time can help a neighbor remain safer, more comfortable, and more connected to their community. If you are looking for one of the most direct, hands-on ways to help your own neighborhood, this is it. Ready to help? Tell us your town or ZIP code and we will help connect you with this and other nearby opportunities as they become available. You can also sign up directly on our website — no Idealist account needed — at this link: https://iwanttomowyourlawn.com/volunteer-sign-up/?utm_source=idealist&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=opportunity_factory&utm_content=IWTMYL-N48&source=idealist Reference: IWTMYL-N48

opportunity_title: Volunteer to help a child become a stronger reader (Dallas)
description_short: Help a K-4 student in your community learn to read! Reading Partners has been pairing volunteer tutors with elementary students for 25 years to ensure that all kids have the foundational reading skills and confidence they need to be able to read at…
description_long: Help a K-4 student in your community learn to read! Reading Partners has been pairing volunteer tutors with elementary students for 25 years to ensure that all kids have the foundational reading skills and confidence they need to be able to read at grade level by fourth grade. We believe that literacy is the foundation of all learning. With strong literacy skills, students can unlock their potential and thrive in school and in life. For a little over an hour per week, you can make a huge difference in the life of young readers. Here’s what that looks like: one-on-one tutoring, once a week, with a structured curriculum. No experience is necessary, and we offer in-person and online options with flexible tutoring times and on-site or live support for volunteers! Before you take the next step to sign up, please review the following tutoring requirements: All tutors must reside within the continental United States. Tutoring runs during the school year from late September to May/June. During the school year, tutoring takes place Mondays-Fridays during the school day, typically from 8 AM-3 PM CT. Please make sure you have availability within this timeframe. Tutors commit to a little over an hour per week (same day/time each week) for a minimum of one semester, although the full school year is preferred. Consistent attendance is required. Tutors work one-on-one with a student in kindergarten through fourth grade. If you’re interested in tutoring in-person, we operate in these neighborhoods: Fort Worth Dallas and surrounding cities Volunteering with Reading Partners is one of the easiest and most accessible ways to make a lasting, proven impact not only in your community but also on the future of local students. To learn more about volunteering and to sign up, please visit our website at readingpartners.org/volunteer . Please note: we are currently recruiting volunteers for the 2026-2027 school year.

opportunity_title: Volunteer – Bridge Breast Network Annual Gala
description_short: Bridge Breast Network is seeking volunteers to assist with our Annual Gala , an evening dedicated to raising funds and awareness to support breast cancer screening, diagnosis, treatment, and survivorship services for low-income, uninsured, and…
description_long: Bridge Breast Network is seeking volunteers to assist with our Annual Gala , an evening dedicated to raising funds and awareness to support breast cancer screening, diagnosis, treatment, and survivorship services for low-income, uninsured, and underinsured individuals across North Texas. This event brings together community members, partners, and supporters to help ensure individuals facing breast cancer receive life-saving care and compassionate support. Volunteers play a key role in helping the evening run smoothly and ensuring guests have a welcoming and enjoyable experience. Volunteer responsibilities may include: Assisting with event setup and preparation Supporting guest check-in and registration Helping guide guests to their tables or event areas Assisting with silent and live auction support Helping distribute auction items during checkout Serving as floaters to assist staff where needed Assisting with event breakdown and cleanup Available volunteer shifts: Setup Shift (1:00 PM – 3:00 PM) Volunteers will assist with preparing the venue, setting up event materials, arranging registration areas, and organizing auction displays. Check-In Shift (6:00 PM – 8:00 PM) Volunteers will welcome guests, assist with registration and check-in, distribute event materials or bid numbers, and direct guests upon arrival. Event Support Shift (6:30 PM – 11:00 PM) Volunteers will assist with auction runners, checkout support, ushering guests, floating support roles, and helping with event breakdown after the program concludes. Qualifications: Friendly, reliable, and professional Comfortable interacting with guests and staff Able to stand or walk for periods of time Willing to assist in a fast-paced event environment Volunteering at the gala helps Bridge Breast Network continue providing life-saving breast health services to individuals across North Texas.

opportunity_title: Volunteer – Bridge Breast Network Survivorship Retreat
description_short: Bridge Breast Network is seeking volunteers to support our 2026 Survivorship Retreat , a special event designed to celebrate and support individuals who have been diagnosed with breast cancer. The retreat provides survivors and individuals currently…
description_long: Bridge Breast Network is seeking volunteers to support our 2026 Survivorship Retreat , a special event designed to celebrate and support individuals who have been diagnosed with breast cancer. The retreat provides survivors and individuals currently undergoing treatment with a day of encouragement, connection, education, and wellness activities. Volunteers play an important role in creating a welcoming and uplifting experience for our guests while assisting staff with event logistics throughout the day. Volunteer responsibilities may include: Assisting with event setup and preparing the event space Placing survivor gift baskets on tables prior to guest arrival Welcoming guests and assisting with check-in Guiding participants throughout the venue Assisting with lunch service Supporting activity or wellness stations Providing general support as a floater Assisting with event breakdown and cleanup Available volunteer shifts: Setup Shift (12:45 PM – 3:00 PM) Volunteers will help prepare the event space, arrange tables and materials, and place survivor gift baskets at designated tables before guests arrive. Event Support Shift (3:00 PM – 7:00 PM) Volunteers will assist with guest check-in, guiding participants throughout the venue, supporting activity stations, assisting with lunch service, providing floating support where needed, and helping with event breakdown. Qualifications: Friendly, dependable, and welcoming Comfortable interacting with guests and staff Able to stand or walk for periods of time Compassionate and respectful when working with breast cancer survivors This retreat is an important part of Bridge Breast Network’s mission to provide encouragement, community, and supportive resources for individuals navigating life during and after breast cancer treatment.

opportunity_title: Volunteer! The Dogs and Cats Will Thank You!
description_short: Volunteers are needed for the many different opportunities: (If you have skills or passion, we can find the spot for you!) Shelter Volunteer : You can volunteer at the municpal animal shelters in Garland, Rowlett, Sachse or Richardson Texas through…
description_long: Volunteers are needed for the many different opportunities: (If you have skills or passion, we can find the spot for you!) Shelter Volunteer : You can volunteer at the municpal animal shelters in Garland, Rowlett, Sachse or Richardson Texas through our program. This includes tasks such as walking dogs, caring for cats, laundry/dishes and assisting potential adopters. Offsite Dog Adoptions : this includes anything from bathing dogs before the event, transporting them to/from the event, handling them and talking to potential adopters, or setting up the event venue. PetSmart Cat Crew: Our organization rescues cats and when ready, adopts them out through one of three PetSmart Cat Adoption Centers. Our volunteers maintain the adoption center by cleaning the cat condos, replacing liter, feeding/watering, and cleaning of the adoption area. Of course there is also cat cuddling time each shift! Social Media/Online Marketing: Do you like creating social media posts, creating videos and sharing content on social media? Do you have the technology skills along with the passion to create eye catching posts? Are you Facebook, Instagram or TikTok savvy where you can cross-post to several pages/groups? This is for you! As you can see, there are many things that are involved with getting the animals adopted, so there is bound to be something that you love to do or that fits your personality! If you want to get started right away with an amazing group of people trying to make a difference you can email the Volunteer coordinator at volunteer@frastx.org or go to www.frastx.org/volunteer to learn more about our organization.

opportunity_title: Volunteers Needed for Field Day!
description_short: Mi Escuelita Preschool is gearing up for an exciting Field Day, and we need your help! We are looking for volunteers to assist with donating and managing games for Field Day at our four locations: Carolyn Strickland Early Childhood Center: 801 W.…
description_long: Mi Escuelita Preschool is gearing up for an exciting Field Day, and we need your help! We are looking for volunteers to assist with donating and managing games for Field Day at our four locations: Carolyn Strickland Early Childhood Center: 801 W. Ave. B Garland,Tx 75040 Casa View: 11540 Ferguson Rd. Dallas,Tx 75228 Cockrell Hill: 4031 W. Clarendon Dr. Dallas,Tx 75211 Frank Diaz: 4931 Bernal Dr. Dallas,Tx 75212 This fun and engaging event is a wonderful opportunity for groups of 20 or more to make a difference in the lives of our students. Your support will help us create a memorable experience for our children as they participate in games and activities that encourage teamwork, coordination, and fun! Contact Erika Graciano, egraciano@miescuelita.org or 214-526-0220 x108, for more details. Private Group Details: Group Size Range: 1 - 20 Event can be held at organization location(s) Donation Requested Donations are not required, but they are greatly appreciated and will help ensure the success of the event.

opportunity_title: Volunteers Needed for companionship in BEDFORD, TX
description_short: Making people smile is easy. Making hospice people smile is a work of heart. If you have the heart and a little time then we have some people for you to bring joy to. We have a few facilities who would love to have people come and help with their…
description_long: Making people smile is easy. Making hospice people smile is a work of heart. If you have the heart and a little time then we have some people for you to bring joy to. We have a few facilities who would love to have people come and help with their activities. If you'd prefer you can do one on one with our patients. Talk, sing, read, play games. Contact me and let's get you loving on our loved ones.

opportunity_title: Volunteers Needed for our Hospice
description_short: Volunteers Needed – Make a Meaningful Difference Lenity Light Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers who want to bring comfort, dignity, and light to patients and families during life’s most important moments. As a volunteer, you can: ✨ Provide…
description_long: Volunteers Needed – Make a Meaningful Difference Lenity Light Hospice is seeking compassionate volunteers who want to bring comfort, dignity, and light to patients and families during life’s most important moments. As a volunteer, you can: ✨ Provide companionship and emotional support ✨ Offer respite and encouragement to caregivers ✨ Share your time, talents, or special skills ✨ Help ensure no one feels alone on their journey No medical experience is required—just a caring heart and a willingness to serve. We provide training, guidance, and flexible scheduling. If you feel called to serve with compassion, kindness, and purpose, we would be honored to have you join our Lenity Light Hospice family. Message us at info@LLHTX.com or visit our website to learn more about becoming a volunteer today www.llhtx.com Because sometimes, the greatest gift is simply being there.

opportunity_title: Volunteers Needed to Rescue Food & Fight Hunger In Dallas!
description_short: "Volunteers Needed to Rescue Food & Fight Hunger In Dallas" Who We Are: Rescuing Leftover Cuisine is a 501(c)3 non-profit that redistributes excess food to people experiencing food insecurity. What We Do: We partner with licensed food vendors…
description_long: "Volunteers Needed to Rescue Food & Fight Hunger In Dallas" Who We Are: Rescuing Leftover Cuisine is a 501(c)3 non-profit that redistributes excess food to people experiencing food insecurity. What We Do: We partner with licensed food vendors (restaurants, bakeries, etc) and businesses who order catering (offices, production companies, etc.) who know they will have excess, wholesome cuisine and link their food donations to a nonprofit recipient within their local community. Our dedicated team of volunteers collect that excess food on a set schedule (pickups can be once a week, daily, or anywhere in between) and deliver it to one of RLC's partner nonprofit recipients to help feed our neighbors who can benefit from a good meal! Who We Need: YOU!! No minimum time commitment is required - you may sign up to volunteer sporadically or on a recurring basis. Documentation for proof of volunteering is available upon request. Vehicle is required to volunteer for this event RLC is currently seeking passionate and committed leaders who are willing to take on the role of Lead Rescuer and lead food rescue events. Pick up food donations and deliver them to a nonprofit recipient, where the donations will then be distributed to the homeless and food insecure community. Each event is an hour or less. As new volunteer opportunities become available they will be added to our online calendar. How To Get Started: Create an account on our website: https://www.rescuingleftovercuisine.org/volunteer Complete the remainder of the training process (available on your RLC website dashboard) to join our leadership team of volunteers as a Lead Rescuer and lead events on your own! Sign up for any event on our calendar that fits with your schedule.

opportunity_title: Volunteers Needed to Visit Veteran Patients and Families
description_short: VITAS wants to enlist your services to care for terminally ill veterans living at home and in nursing homes, assisted living communities and medical centers. VITAS Veteran Volunteers do not need to be U.S. Veterans; you need only the desire to…
description_long: VITAS wants to enlist your services to care for terminally ill veterans living at home and in nursing homes, assisted living communities and medical centers. VITAS Veteran Volunteers do not need to be U.S. Veterans; you need only the desire to remember; honor and support America's living heroes. VITAS Veteran Volunteers visit terminally ill veterans; listen to life stories or record or videotape a veteran's reminiscences; telephone veterans to ask how they are doing; attend or help plan activities celebrating Memorial Day and Veteran's Day.

opportunity_title: Volunteers Several Opportunities EULESS, TX
description_short: Our hospice team is looking to expand our team. We are looking for caring, compassionate people who love to help others. We have several opportunities available between our 3 offices. We are looking for people for the following opportunities. Weekly…
description_long: Our hospice team is looking to expand our team. We are looking for caring, compassionate people who love to help others. We have several opportunities available between our 3 offices. We are looking for people for the following opportunities. Weekly phone calls to check on our families and report to supervisor if any needs are not met. Volunteer companions to listen, talk, read, do an activity Volunteers to provide respite for a few hours so that family can run errands If any of these things interest you then we would like to talk to you. We service a very large area. We would love to have you join our team. We believe in putting the patients first. Our entire staff is dedicated to giving the best end of life care to our patients and their families. That includes giving them a life with quality until that day comes.

opportunity_title: Volunteers and Interns Needed!
description_short: Join our Team in celebrating life to its fullest potential! We support families and patients in their end-of-life journey, by providing the best possible care and experience. Being a hospice volunteer, can bring great personal satisfaction from…
description_long: Join our Team in celebrating life to its fullest potential! We support families and patients in their end-of-life journey, by providing the best possible care and experience. Being a hospice volunteer, can bring great personal satisfaction from knowing that you have made a difference in another person’s life. By giving the gift of your time and/or presence, volunteers like You play an essential role in making our patients days brighter. Our Team needs YOU! We have Direct (in patient) and Pen Pal (virtual) opportunities. We're also accepting Interns for our Internship program! Activity volunteering available Join us in our support to patients and families!!

opportunity_title: Volunteers needed for Dallas Cowboys Home Games!
description_short: Join Healing Minds Through Design, a national nonprofit organization, as we team up with us to operate concessions in Dallas. We’re looking for dedicated volunteers to help run concessions during games. This is your chance to be part of the game-day…
description_long: Join Healing Minds Through Design, a national nonprofit organization, as we team up with us to operate concessions in Dallas. We’re looking for dedicated volunteers to help run concessions during games. This is your chance to be part of the game-day action while supporting a great cause — proceeds go toward our community events like toy drives, home furnishing giveaways, and holiday donations. ✅ Perks of Volunteering: Free admission to the stadium on game days Food and beverage provided Community service hours Help fund impactful programs in your community Be a part of something bigger than soccer — help us make a difference! Dates are subject to change

opportunity_title: Volunteers needed for Mesquite, TX
description_short: Volunteer opportunities in the Mesquite area. Needing to be able to visit patients one day a week every week (as available), application, Orientation, training, TB test, background checks and drug screening. Once submitting your contact information…
description_long: Volunteer opportunities in the Mesquite area. Needing to be able to visit patients one day a week every week (as available), application, Orientation, training, TB test, background checks and drug screening. Once submitting your contact information to me, I will send an application, we can meet for coffee and then interview for about an hour. I like to cover what Hospice Plus is about, what we expect from volunteers and what your goals are for becoming a volunteer. We do need some Spanish/English speaking volunteers.

opportunity_title: Volunteers needed for St. Jude Showplace Events - March 2026 (Southlake, Texas)
description_short: We’re excited to inform you on volunteer opportunities for our upcoming St. Jude Showplace events in March in Southlake, Texas! What is a St. Jude Showplace? It is a special fundraising initiative where 5 builders (built 5 homes next to each other)…
description_long: We’re excited to inform you on volunteer opportunities for our upcoming St. Jude Showplace events in March in Southlake, Texas! What is a St. Jude Showplace? It is a special fundraising initiative where 5 builders (built 5 homes next to each other) have agreed to each raise $250,000 for St. Jude. The 5 homes are ready to be sold, and we are helping to staff the open houses. We have approx. 210 volunteer openings over 11 days and would truly appreciate your help in sharing these opportunities with anyone you think would be great at greeting and welcoming attendees. No background checks are required, but all volunteers must be 18 years or older . You can also sign up for a multitude of shifts. Below is the master link for volunteer sign-ups: Sign up link👉 https://volunteer.stjude.org/custom/515/organization_details/7907 Open House Events – 2 shifts each day (March 7, March 8, March 12, March 13, March 14, March 15, March 19, March 20, March 21 & March 22).

opportunity_title: Volunteers needed for the Simply United Together Dallas Chapter
description_short: Hey everyone, how are you doing? We are reaching out to you because we are recruiting new members for our SUT Dallas Chapter. We are expanding our organization in 2026...We are looking for a group of volunteers to help lead the chapter as we will be…
description_long: Hey everyone, how are you doing? We are reaching out to you because we are recruiting new members for our SUT Dallas Chapter. We are expanding our organization in 2026...We are looking for a group of volunteers to help lead the chapter as we will be doing community events in all of our chapters across the country once a month starting in March they vary from Feeding the Homeless to Assisting the Veterans. We also fundraise to support our Toy, Turkey & Back to School giveaways at sporting events and entertainment events, running one of their concession stands or guest services. If you are interested in joining the team please send me your number and we will be contacting you. Thanks

description_short: Hey everyone, thank you for joining us, we will be running a concession stand at the Global Life..We will be running cash registers, and this process is about 8 hours ....This is a fundraiser for our Feeding the Homeless events in November and…
description_long: Hey everyone, thank you for joining us, we will be running a concession stand at the Global Life..We will be running cash registers, and this process is about 8 hours ....This is a fundraiser for our Feeding the Homeless events in November and December, we appreciate it. We are an active advocate for increasing the awareness of Lupus, as well as supporting survivors and warriors of the battle against this debilitating disease. With your help volunteering at our fundraisers, we vastly increase the likelihood of success of our programs. Thanks

opportunity_title: Volunteers needed to brighten someone's day
description_short: Brighten someone's day by becoming a volunteer and provide companionship to hospice patients. We have direct patient care opportunities where you can interact one on one with a patient, and also administrative opportunities. Come join our team and…
description_long: Brighten someone's day by becoming a volunteer and provide companionship to hospice patients. We have direct patient care opportunities where you can interact one on one with a patient, and also administrative opportunities. Come join our team and bless our patient's as they near the end of their life journey!

opportunity_title: WAXAHACHIE: Visit a Lonely Senior Citizen in need of a Friend!
description_short: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody…
description_long: We would love to have you as a Hospice Volunteer ! Become a Hero on your own terms On your own time. In your own way. With your personality. Your ability to relate. Your own skills. Your way of solving a problem, sharing a story, helping somebody remember important moments in their lives. You can be one of our Volunteers When you give us the precious gift of your time and interest, we give you a chance to make a real difference in someone’s life. We train you, find you the right assignment and stand behind you as you grow. Patient Volunteer Weekly visits to offer companionship for Patients. Examples include: oConversation oReading to the Patient oListening to music or watching videos of their favorite singer/actor oWatching a TV show together oWatching funny cat and dog reels together oAssisting with a hobby Volunteers are matched with 1 Patient at a time to someone within a 15-minute radius to where you live. You can volunteer for as little as 2 - 4 hours a month, and you can set a schedule that works for you. You can learn at your own pace, and we provide ongoing support until you feel completely comfortable working on your own. Requirements: Must be able to commit to Volunteer for one year (or 1 school year) Either 1 x per week or 2 x per month for about 1 hour No resume needed but an excellent skill and resume builder A caring heart to help people combat loneliness & depression. The process is simple! 1) Once you contact us, the Volunteer Coordinator will reach out via email to welcome you! 2) Then, you & the Volunteer Coordinator will set up a time to chat via phone so that you can introduce yourself & ask any questions. 3) The first step is to complete the application form via this link: https://rb.gy/3r2jta 4) Or you may email BethanyHarrell@AlphaOmegaHospice.com We look forward to you joining our Awesome Volunteer Team! It's truly an amazing and humbling experience and we walk with you the entire way!

opportunity_title: WAXAHACHIE: Visit a Lonely Senior Citizen in need of a Friend!
description_short: Make a Difference Through Hospice Volunteering Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an Alpha Omega Hospice Patient…
description_long: Make a Difference Through Hospice Volunteering Provide Comfort, Connection, and Compassion Are you looking for a meaningful way to give back to your community while making a genuine difference in someone's life? As an Alpha Omega Hospice Patient Volunteer, you'll become an important part of a patient's care team by offering companionship, support, and a caring presence to individuals facing serious illness. Why Hospice Volunteering? Build meaningful relationships with patients and families Make a positive impact during an important time in someone's life Develop communication, empathy, and active listening skills Share your time, talents, and compassion with others Experience the personal fulfillment that comes from serving your community Help reduce loneliness and improve quality of life for patients What Do Volunteers Do? Provide companionship through: Conversation and life-story sharing Reading, music, games, and activities Watching television together Listening and offering emotional support Simply being a caring presence No healthcare or volunteer experience is required. Volunteer Commitment Flexible scheduling Visit 1 time per week or 2 times per month Typical visits last 30–60 minutes Volunteer opportunities available near your preferred area What We Provide One-on-one patient matching within approximately 15 minutes of your preferred location Volunteer Coordinator accompaniment on your first visit Ongoing support and guidance Comprehensive training provided at no cost Requirements provided for FREE: Online Application Background Check TB Test (Skin or Blood Test) Drug Screening (THC not tested) Driver's License Online Volunteer Training "You are not just volunteering your time—you are providing comfort, dignity, companionship, and human connection to someone who truly needs it."

opportunity_title: WOGA Banquet Fundraising Raffle Game Facilitator
description_short: We need responsible teens or adults who are willing to volunteer 2 hours to help facilitate some “yard games” such as horseshoes, ring toss, beanbag toss, etc., then hand out raffle tickets based on the child’s game play. Games will be held indoors…
description_long: We need responsible teens or adults who are willing to volunteer 2 hours to help facilitate some “yard games” such as horseshoes, ring toss, beanbag toss, etc., then hand out raffle tickets based on the child’s game play. Games will be held indoors at Southfork Ranch in Parker, TX, on Tuesday, May 12, from 5pm-7pm.

opportunity_title: Walk to Cure Arthritis-Volunteers
description_short: Join us in our mission to find a cure for arthritis by volunteering at our annual Walk to Cure Arthritis event. This nationwide event, organized by the Arthritis Foundation, unites communities across the country to make a difference in the lives of…
description_long: Join us in our mission to find a cure for arthritis by volunteering at our annual Walk to Cure Arthritis event. This nationwide event, organized by the Arthritis Foundation, unites communities across the country to make a difference in the lives of millions of people suffering from arthritis. As a volunteer, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the success of the event and helping us pave the way towards a future free of arthritis. Several volunteer positions available. Typical day of event activities include: Event Set-Up Registration Vendor and Team Tailgate Area VIP &Kids Zone Water/Food Station Event Wrap-Up The ideal people would be willing to commit several hours day of the event which will be held SATURDAY, April 25th 6:30AM- 12:00 PM at Grandscape at The Colony

org_name: Lupus Foundation of America, Lone Star Chapter
opportunity_title: Walk to End Lupus Dallas
description_short: SET UP / BREAK DOWN Assist with unpacking trucks Hanging up signs Route signs along route Take down ROUTE MARSHALLS/ CHEER LEADERS Placed along route to direct and cheer Finish line cheer leaders: Celebrate walkers GREETER First point of contact for…
description_long: SET UP / BREAK DOWN Assist with unpacking trucks Hanging up signs Route signs along route Take down ROUTE MARSHALLS/ CHEER LEADERS Placed along route to direct and cheer Finish line cheer leaders: Celebrate walkers GREETER First point of contact for participants Smile and make them feel welcome Help them with directions Hand out Warrior (purple) and Supporter (green) beads REGISTRATION Registration Process: Find Participant on List: Shows captain status, shirt earned, total raised, and team sign eligibility. Medals & Team Signs: If participant has earned a medal or team sign ($1,000+), give it to them at this table. T-Shirts: If participant raised $100+ , give them a T-shirt. Other Prizes: Certificate to redeem prize will be emailed within 6 weeks. Not Registered? Direct to QR code to register OR have them fill out a walker waiver. Donations today? Yes Cash/Checks: Fill envelope, sign/seal (walkers), and place in box. Credit Cards: Use QR code on table Volunteers will be trained on the full process the day of the event. WHY I WALK TENT & LFA TENT Setup: Arrange literature samples & advocacy materials. Display frames with QR codes. Put “Why I Walk” signs + stickers + markers on table (make a sample sign). Engagement: Encourage people to fill out & carry “Why I Walk” signs/stickers during the walk. Invite people to sign up to become more involved FOOD TENT Set Up: Help set up snack area Put water on tables Replenish as needed SPONSOR AREA & TOP TEAM TENTS Help sponsors with their equipment Decorate Top Team Tents KIDS ZONE Set up all kids games and keep an eye on the area

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: Want to be a SUPERHERO?
description_short: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the…
description_long: Looking for Superheroes with invisible capes, to visit with our hospice patients who reside through-out the metroplex in their private homes and facilities. Volunteer will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, and be a referral for you. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer.

opportunity_title: Wanted: One couch. Two hands to scratch. - Dallas
description_short: It doesn’t take a massive backyard or a complicated setup to save an animal—sometimes, it just takes a couch and a willing volunteer. By joining the Doobert community, you get access to a massive, supportive tribe of rescuers who have your back from…
description_long: It doesn’t take a massive backyard or a complicated setup to save an animal—sometimes, it just takes a couch and a willing volunteer. By joining the Doobert community, you get access to a massive, supportive tribe of rescuers who have your back from day one. We guide you through every step of the journey, offering tailored training and 24/7 answers to your questions, all while matching you with a foster animal tailored to your exact living situation. Visit doobert.com to get started today.

opportunity_title: Waxahachie Hospice Volunteer
description_short: Patient visits, life review, discuss hobbies & interests, current events, or read to patient, accompany patient outside if appropriate, companionship & socialization, monitor & report progress to hospice team. May sit with patient while…
description_long: Patient visits, life review, discuss hobbies & interests, current events, or read to patient, accompany patient outside if appropriate, companionship & socialization, monitor & report progress to hospice team. May sit with patient while caregiver runs errands, but no hands-on care. Dependability for patients and families. Compassionate, caring, good listening skills, able to sit quietly at times if needed. Interest in others & their life stories, own transportation, able to complete & send in documentation after each visit. Background check, drug screen, orientation, we cover costs associated to onboarding.

org_name: Heart to Heart Hospice - Serving Mansfield, Arlington, Midlothian, Burleson, Crowley, Cleburne, Granbury, Texas and Surrounding Areas
opportunity_title: We Need You, Yes You! - Hospice Patient Companion
description_short: Your going to love being a Heart to Heart Hospice Volunteer! Our volunteers make a positive difference in the lives of our patients and families. Volunteers provide companionship to patients and caregiver respite to their families. Patients…
description_long: Your going to love being a Heart to Heart Hospice Volunteer! Our volunteers make a positive difference in the lives of our patients and families. Volunteers provide companionship to patients and caregiver respite to their families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of volunteers. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and arranged to fit your schedule. We have flexible hours, will write letters of recommendation, be a referral, and are willing to do a Non-Paid Internship. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Volunteer Requirements: A desire to help others Orientation Training at your own pace Transportation to volunteer site Background Check (free) TB test (free) You can learn more about us at https://hearttohearthospice.com/our-difference/volunteering-heart-to-heart-hospice/ Watch our video to learn more: Hospice - Something More

opportunity_title: We need your compassion and care. Hospice volunteer: Dallas Metro
description_short: Do you want to make a direct impact in someone's life? Good Shepherd Hospice is seeking compassionate, caring, empathetic, and dependable volunteers to work directly with hospice clients in their homes. Volunteers will provide client support…
description_long: Do you want to make a direct impact in someone's life? Good Shepherd Hospice is seeking compassionate, caring, empathetic, and dependable volunteers to work directly with hospice clients in their homes. Volunteers will provide client support services that contribute to the quality of life for clients and their families, such as respite, companionship, preparing meals or snacks, engaging the clients in hobbies and/or light chores such as dog walking or shopping. If you are compassionate, warm hearted and dedicated to service to others, this is the role for you! This is an excellent role for retired people who would like to support others as well as those looking to gain experience in medical or spiritual career paths. If this opportunity interests you, contact our Volunteer Coordinator at 972-482-4382 or email at shauntae.harris@goodshepherdhospice.com Visit our website https://www.goodshepherdhospice.com/volunteers/ to fill out an application today!

opportunity_title: Web design and marketing Internship
description_short: Bright Smiles Project Internship Program Position Title Marketing & Website Development Intern Organization Bright Smiles Project Position Type Volunteer Internship About Bright Smiles Project The Bright Smiles Project is a youth-led nonprofit…
description_long: Bright Smiles Project Internship Program Position Title Marketing & Website Development Intern Organization Bright Smiles Project Position Type Volunteer Internship About Bright Smiles Project The Bright Smiles Project is a youth-led nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting children through educational, literacy, and community service initiatives. Our mission is to create opportunities that help children thrive and bring smiles to their faces through meaningful programs and community engagement. Position Overview The Bright Smiles Project is seeking motivated, creative, and passionate interns to join our team. This internship provides students with hands-on experience in nonprofit operations, digital marketing, website management, fundraising, and community outreach. Interns will work closely with leadership to help expand the organization's impact while developing valuable professional and leadership skills. Responsibilities Assist in updating and maintaining the Bright Smiles Project website. Create engaging content for social media platforms and marketing campaigns. Help promote fundraising events, donation drives, and community initiatives. Research and develop new fundraising opportunities and sponsorship ideas. Brainstorm innovative programs and projects that align with the organization's mission. Design promotional materials, flyers, graphics, and digital content. Support community outreach efforts and partnership development. Assist with event planning, coordination, and volunteer recruitment. Track engagement metrics and provide recommendations for improvement. Participate in team meetings and contribute creative ideas for organizational growth. Qualifications Current high school or college student. Strong interest in nonprofit work, community service, marketing, business, communications, or technology. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment. Strong organizational and time-management skills. Creative mindset with a willingness to share ideas and take initiative. Familiarity with social media platforms and basic digital tools. Experience with website management, graphic design, or content creation is a plus but not required. Skills Gained Nonprofit leadership and management Digital marketing and social media strategy Website development and maintenance Event planning and fundraising Community outreach and partnership building Professional communication and teamwork Project management and problem-solving Time Commitment Flexible schedule Approximately 3–5 hours per week Remote opportunities available Minimum commitment of one semester preferred Benefits Gain real-world nonprofit experience. Earn community service hours. Receive mentorship from nonprofit leaders. Develop professional and leadership skills. Build your résumé and college applications. Make a meaningful impact in the lives of children and families. Ideal Candidate We are looking for students who are passionate about making a difference, eager to learn, and excited to contribute creative ideas that help expand the reach and impact of the Bright Smiles Project. Successful interns are proactive, dependable, and committed to serving their communities through meaningful work. Join us in creating brighter futures and brighter smiles for children in our communities.

opportunity_title: Weekly Email Writer, Animals in Need
description_short: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to…
description_long: Dallas Pets Alive! is a highly respected animal rescue organization and provides the perfect place to utilize your skills to grow personally and professionally. Our mission is to promote and provide the resources, education, and programs needed to eliminate the killing of companion animals in North Texas - and volunteers make our mission a reality. With over 350 Volunteers on more than 10 teams, we have numerous opportunities for you to join our mission. By dedicating your valuable time to DPA, you are directly impacting our ability to achieve our organizational goals in a fun and supportive community. To learn more about Dallas Pets Alive! visit https://dallaspetsalive.org As our Animals in Need Email Writer, you’ll play an important role in that you will be responsible for writing and sending out the bi-weekly animals in need email that gets sent to our approved foster families highlighting featured pets in need and current events affecting animal rescue. Team: Rescue Location Remote/Virtual Time Commitment 2-3 Hours/week. Must be able to commit to a minimum of 6 months of volunteer service. Compensation: This is a Volunteer Position with no monetary compensation. Reports to: Rescue Director Works with: Rescue team, Foster team, and Marketing team Position Responsibilities: Creating intriguing content that will promote shelter pets in need of fosters Ensuring emails are sent in a timely manner and reaches our growing audience Ensuring the animals being promoted are approved by DPA and still in need of rescue Skills and Qualifications best suited for this position: Must have HS diploma or higher degree of education Must have access to Mailchimp at least twice per week Intermediate computer skills Ability to work within deadlines and manage multiple priorities Must be at least 18 years of age Proficiency with Gmail and G-Suite applications Fluency in English; bilingual a plus! Ability to operate a computer or mobile device The ability to communicate information and ideas so others will understand. What we look for in our Volunteers: Commitment to DPA!’s mission and values . Excellent oral and written communication skills to maintain professional level correspondence at all times. A motivational, can-do attitude to represent DPA! in a positive manner to our supporters and the general public. A problem-solving mindset to handle and diffuse challenging situations in a professional manner. A love for animals and dedication to the cause of animal welfare. Must not have conflicting associations with similar non-profit organizations or business(es) related to animal welfare. Our Commitment to You: Dallas Pets Alive! champions a world where: Both ends of the leash are respected and honored. All individuals are treated with dignity, compassion, and understanding. We treat all living beings as they wish to be treated. DPA is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We will lead with positive intent and foster inclusivity and belonging in all that we do. DPA! has a zero-tolerance discrimination policy. Unfair or prejudicial treatment of people and groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or sexual orientation will not be tolerated. To be considered for this position, please complete our Official Volunteer Application at https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/volunteer-application/ For general information about volunteering, visit https://dallaspetsalive.org/volunteer/ Dallas Pets Alive! is committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive culture that reflects the uniqueness of the animals and communities we serve. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation. To request reasonable accommodation, contact volunteer@dallaspetsalive.org.

opportunity_title: Wish Granting Volunteer Opportunity (Bilingual & English)
description_short: Volunteers are the heart of our mission! Together, we create life-changing wishes for children with critical illnesses. From our humble beginnings with one boy’s wish to be a police officer, we have evolved to be one of the world’s leading…
description_long: Volunteers are the heart of our mission! Together, we create life-changing wishes for children with critical illnesses. From our humble beginnings with one boy’s wish to be a police officer, we have evolved to be one of the world’s leading children’s charities, serving children in every community in the United States and its territories. With the help of generous donors and nearly 40,000 volunteers, Make-A-Wish grants a wish somewhere in the country every 34 minutes. Wish Granters work with families over the course of 12-18 months volunteering about an hour a month per wish child. Wish granters are trained individuals who meet with our children to determine their wish. These volunteers work closely with the Make-A-Wish staff to make each wish come true. Wish granters will have the opportunity to meet with wish families virtually (FaceTime, Zoom Meetings, HouseParty, Phone Calls, etc.) or in person. Wishes can be granted in just several days or may take up to a year to complete. A specialized training program is provided before contact is made with a wish family. We serve a large percentage of Spanish speaking with families, and without Spanish speaking wish granters, we are not able to quickly meet the needs of those Spanish speaking families. If you are fluent in both English and Spanish, please consider becoming a wish granter.

opportunity_title: Wix Developer: Help achieve universal literacy - Urgently Needed
description_short: Have you ever wanted to do something AMAZING to change the world but didn't know where to start? Here is an opportunity to join the American Youth Literacy Foundation, an all- volunteer 501 c 3 charitable non-profit organization, founded in 2008 and…
description_long: Have you ever wanted to do something AMAZING to change the world but didn't know where to start? Here is an opportunity to join the American Youth Literacy Foundation, an all- volunteer 501 c 3 charitable non-profit organization, founded in 2008 and dedicated to providing a gateway to a future in which all children, in every classroom in America, learn how to read by the end of 3rd grade. Our mission is to save millions of children from a lifetime of illiteracy and bring about true education parity in just a few years’ time by deploying the FUNetix(r) 12 Hour Reading App(tm) nationwide. With the FUNetix app, which is 100% free to download and use, any child can learn how to read in about 12 hours of total play time; and in America’s public schools today, there are millions of children who can’t read or are struggling to read, which has led to more than 20 million illiterate high school dropouts over the past 20 years. But it doesn’t have to be this way! We started in 2008 by taking a groundbreaking, science-based curriculum and proving that it can scale to reach and help thousands of children. Then, in 2020, during the pandemic, we created an app, based on our now patented reading curriculum, that takes kids from the ABC's to a 2nd grade reading level through an amazing journey that's fully narrated and tastefully animated, and gamified, just enough, to create an immersive, interactive environment that captures kids' attention and imagination and launches them to literacy in a fun and easy way. We are expanding and opening up FUNetix Reading Centers where children will have the opportunity to work with a tutor! Right now, we are looking to expand our incredible Team FUNetix and we are looking for a skilled Wix Developer . We are seeking an experienced Wix developer who can begin immediately to help build a new website for our Reading Centers. The ideal candidate has deep expertise in Wix and can seamlessly integrate key functionalities including scheduling calendars, payment processing, digital waivers, and online forms. Experience with WordPress and broader web development support is a strong plus, especially for future expansion and ongoing site maintenance. We hope this opportunity finds you at the right place and the right time in your career to make a difference and an impact, in an amazing way, for millions of children who are waiting for someone, or something, to lead them out of a life of impoverishment and into a future of unlimited possibility and potential. So, if you have volunteering experience, if you have the confidence and ability to work in a virtual environment using select networking and communications tools and platforms, and if this opportunity sounds like something that you have the time for and interest in, then please reply to this listing and tell us about yourself. We look forward to hearing from you and speaking with you about the future of literacy in America. You can read more about our story, and about our team, on our website at www.funetix.org . Mr. R. Kali Woodward Founding, Executive Director Watch our video to learn more: FUNetix 12 Hour Reading App
