opportunity_title: YouTube Content Creator
description_short: The YouTube Content Creator is the lead visual storyteller for Transition Trails. This role involves producing high-quality video content that educates our audience, showcases our community impact, and boosts our global visibility. You will be…
description_long: The YouTube Content Creator is the lead visual storyteller for Transition Trails. This role involves producing high-quality video content that educates our audience, showcases our community impact, and boosts our global visibility. You will be responsible for the entire video lifecycle—from conceptualization and scripting to filming and post-production—ensuring our YouTube channel serves as a professional and engaging resource for the movement. Responsibilities Video Production: Create 1–2 high-quality videos per month (educational deep-dives, "day in the life" stories, or event recaps). YouTube Shorts: Repurpose long-form content into engaging 60-second Shorts to capture mobile viewership. SEO & Optimization: Write compelling titles, descriptions, and tags; design eye-catching thumbnails to maximize click-through rates. Channel Management: Organize playlists, manage community tab posts, and respond to viewer comments. Scripting: Collaborate with leadership to draft scripts that are informative, accurate, and aligned with our mission. Benefits Professional Credits: Feature your work on a growing platform with full creative credit. Advanced Experience: Gain experience in long-form content strategy, video SEO, and audience retention analytics. Creative Freedom: Opportunity to pitch and lead original video series and creative concepts. Impact: Help Transition Trails reach thousands of potential supporters through the world's second-largest search engine. Qualifications Video Editing: Proficiency in video editing software. Storytelling Ability: Experience in "Strong writer/editor/proofreader" roles to craft engaging narratives and scripts. Visual Design: Basic "Graphic design" skills for creating high-performing thumbnails. Technical Savvy: Understanding of "Video editing skill" and "Digital outreach and engagement" specifically within the YouTube ecosystem. Reliability: Ability to "Produce high-quality, error-free work" while meeting a regular upload schedule.

opportunity_title: Your Service Matters, Your Time Matters!  Become a Veteran Volunteer Today!
description_short: Your service matters. Your time matters. Are you a Veteran looking for a meaningful way to serve other Veterans? Consider being a Veteran volunteer with Elara Caring Hospice. Hospice patients who have served have unique perspectives that only other…
description_long: Your service matters. Your time matters. Are you a Veteran looking for a meaningful way to serve other Veterans? Consider being a Veteran volunteer with Elara Caring Hospice. Hospice patients who have served have unique perspectives that only other Veterans can understand. Pairing patients and volunteers who are both Veterans gives opportunity for connection, respect, honor and camaraderie. Elara Caring Hospice, one of the nation’s largest providers of home-based care, is looking for compassionate Veteran volunteers willing to donate their time to provide exceptional end-of-life care to local hospice Veteran patients and their families. Elara Caring Hospice is looking for volunteers who will visit Veteran patients in local nursing facilities or in private homes. Veteran volunteers can also assist in honoring Veterans in pinning ceremonies, assist in Veteran-centered events, or organize Veteran volunteers for We Honor Veterans program activities. Hours are very flexible with volunteering on weekdays, evenings or weekends. Thank you for your service and your consideration!

org_name: Southwest Key Youth Mentoring Program - Dallas
opportunity_title: Youth Mentor
description_short: Program Model and Program Number: Youth Mentoring Program Voluntary Position Summary: The Mentor provides support and guidance to the assigned youth within the program for a minimum of one year, meeting an average of one hour per week (minimum 4…
description_long: Program Model and Program Number: Youth Mentoring Program Voluntary Position Summary: The Mentor provides support and guidance to the assigned youth within the program for a minimum of one year, meeting an average of one hour per week (minimum 4 hours per month). The Mentor offers friendship to the youth, serving as a role model, motivator and advocate. Qualifications: Sincere desire to be personally involved with another person to help him or her achieve goals; Ability to communicate with teenagers openly and nonjudgmentally; Strong listening skills; Ability to establish a relationship based on equal responsibility and respect; Practical problem-solving skills and ability to suggest options and alternatives; and Sensitivity to persons of different educational, economic, cultural or racial backgrounds. Eligibility Requirements: Be at least 18 years of age (21 preferred). Reside in the metropolitan area surrounding Southwest Key’s offices. Be willing to adhere to all Southwest Key Mentoring Program policies and procedures. Agree to a minimum 6-month commitment to the program. Commit to spending a minimum of 4 hours a month with the mentee (minimum 1 hour weekly preferred). Be willing to communicate and/or meet with the mentee weekly. Complete the screening procedure and criminal background check successfully. Agree to attend 2–3 hours of initial mentor training (and additional training throughout this year, if possible). Be willing to communicate regularly with the program staff and submit monthly notes about your activities with your mentee. Be willing to participate in some group activities organized by Southwest Key. Have access to a vehicle or reliable transportation. Have a current driver’s license, auto insurance, and good driving record.

opportunity_title: Youth Volleyball Life Skills Mentor for girls
description_short: Rae’s Hope is seeking committed, skills‑based volunteers to support our weekly Volleyball & Life Skills Program at Juanita Craft Recreation Center beginning August 2026. Volunteers will help youth ages 5–14 build confidence, teamwork,…
description_long: Rae’s Hope is seeking committed, skills‑based volunteers to support our weekly Volleyball & Life Skills Program at Juanita Craft Recreation Center beginning August 2026. Volunteers will help youth ages 5–14 build confidence, teamwork, discipline, and healthy habits through structured volleyball training, fitness activities, and life‑skills development. This role is ideal for volunteers who enjoy working with youth, value consistency, and want to make a meaningful impact in the South Dallas community. Responsibilities Volleyball Instruction — Assist with drills, teach basic skills, support beginners, and reinforce teamwork and discipline. Fitness Station Support — Set up and monitor youth fitness stations, encourage safe participation, and maintain positive energy. Youth Engagement — Motivate youth, model respect and teamwork, redirect behavior calmly, and help maintain a structured environment. Life Skills Facilitation — Guide youth through communication, teamwork, responsibility, and social‑skills exercises. Program Operations — Organize equipment, assist with transitions between stations, support themed program days, and help maintain a safe practice area. Mentorship — Provide encouragement, build rapport, and help youth feel valued and capable. Qualifications Experience with Youth — Comfortable working with children ages 5–14 in group settings. Basic Volleyball Knowledge — Able to assist with drills and fundamentals (advanced coaching not required). Strong Communication — Clear, positive communication with youth, volunteers, and program leaders. Patience & Positivity — Calm, encouraging presence with a commitment to youth development. Consistency — Able to attend more than 4–8 sessions to provide stability, build trust, and support program continuity. Team Orientation — Works well with coaches, volunteers, and program staff to maintain structure and safety. Additional Details Weekly sessions at Juanita Craft Recreation Center Training and orientation provided Great for volunteers who enjoy youth development, sports, fitness, and community impact

opportunity_title: 💛 Be the Bright Spot in Someone's Life 💛 (Dallas, Collin, Tarrant, Grayson, Fannin, & Cooke County)
description_short: There are moments at the end of life that no one should walk through alone. At Star City Hospice, we believe hospice is not just about how someone dies. It is about how they are cared for, honored, supported, and loved in the moments that matter…
description_long: There are moments at the end of life that no one should walk through alone. At Star City Hospice, we believe hospice is not just about how someone dies. It is about how they are cared for, honored, supported, and loved in the moments that matter most. We are currently looking for compassionate hospice volunteers who want to make a real difference in the lives of patients and families throughout our community. Star City Hospice is resetting the standard in care, and we would love to talk to you about joining our team! You do not have to be clinical to change someone’s life. Sometimes the greatest gift is simply your presence. Our volunteers help by: Providing companionship and emotional support Reading, talking, listening, playing music or simply sitting with patients Offering caregiver relief visits Assisting with administrative or office support Supporting veteran recognition and special programs Participating in bereavement and family support activities veteran to veteran support Hospice volunteers are an important part of compassionate end-of-life care because they bring something medicine alone cannot provide — human connection, dignity, comfort, and presence. Hospice volunteer guidelines include: Volunteers complete orientation and training Background check Driver's license/ Proof of insurance Volunteer services support patients, families, or organizational operations Volunteers may provide companionship, respite support, administrative assistance, bereavement support, and community outreach All volunteers follow HIPAA and patient confidentiality requirements This is not just volunteering. This is sitting beside someone during one of the most important chapters of their life. If you have a heart for people and want to serve with purpose, we would love to connect with you. " There is a Patient at the end of every decision we make and do not make."
